title: News to Numbers: A Comparative Analysis of Traditional and API-driven LLMs in Stock Return Prediction
abstract: This thesis investigates the comparative effectiveness of two large language model (LLM) deployment paradigms—zero-shot inference via commercial APIs and supervised fine-tuning of transformer-based models—for stock return prediction using financial news. Leveraging a novel dataset of over 30,000 Dow Jones Newswire (DJN) articles aligned with firm-level returns from 1989 to 2020, the study systematically evaluates OpenAI’s GPT-4 in a zero-shot setting against fine-tuned BERT-based models trained on identical text–return pairs. The analysis employs a rolling-window cross-validation framework to ensure robustness across market regimes and emphasizes not only predictive accuracy but also operational feasibility, cost efficiency, and scalability. Results demonstrate that both modeling approaches produce statistically significant predictive power above random baselines, yet with distinct trade-offs. GPT-4 achieves 53.38% directional accuracy and a Sharpe ratio of 1.076, highlighting its ability to extract meaningful signals without domain-specific tuning. Its operational simplicity, low setup cost, and per-call flexibility make it particularly attractive for rapid deployment and lightweight financial applications. By contrast, the supervised BERT model delivers markedly stronger financial performance, attaining an annualized Sharpe ratio of 4.08 and mean daily returns of 12.81 basis points. These superior outcomes underscore the value of domain-specific adaptation, but they come at the cost of substantial GPU resources, specialized expertise, and ongoing maintenance requirements. Temporal analysis further reveals that both approaches maintain predictive robustness across diverse economic conditions—including the dot-com era, the 2008 financial crisis, and post-crisis market environments—though performance metrics vary with shifts in efficiency and volatility. Importantly, GPT-4 provides consistent positive alphas despite its modest accuracy, while fine-tuned BERT models extract higher-magnitude signals during volatile periods, reinforcing the economic relevance of tailored architectures. The findings contribute to the growing literature at the intersection of financial economics and natural language processing by offering the first unified empirical framework to compare API-driven and locally fine-tuned LLMs for financial prediction. They highlight a fundamental trade-off in institutional strategy: API-based models democratize access to advanced language capabilities with minimal infrastructure, while fine-tuned models yield superior risk-adjusted returns for institutions capable of sustaining the computational investment. This research advances both theory and practice by demonstrating that textual data continues to contain exploitable information for asset pricing and that the operational context—budget, infrastructure, and regulatory considerations—should guide the choice of modeling paradigm. Ultimately, the results suggest that financial institutions must navigate between accessibility and performance, as the integration of LLMs into quantitative trading evolves from experimental adoption toward strategic deployment.

title: Labor Market Shocks and Heterogeneous Adjustment: A Semi-Nonparametric Approach
abstract: This study examines how labor markets adjust to international trade shocks in recent decades, with a particular focus on the China trade shock that has fundamentally reshaped global manufacturing since the 1990s. To estimate the effect, the project will combine structural economic modeling with semi-parametric machine learning techniques to understand heterogeneous labor market responses across demographic groups and industries. By merging rigorous economic theory with flexible estimation methods, we aim to provide nuanced insights into the transition costs workers face when responding to trade disruption, addressing a gap in the current literature that often treats adjustment frictions as homogeneous across populations. After the estimation, we'll apply agent-based modelling framework on simulated counterfactual sample to experiment with different tariff intervention and agent features to further understand the dynamics and welfare implication.
Author: Xu, Jingzhi

Through the prism of amalgamated structural economic exegesis and empirically variant machinations of disquisition, this endeavor endeavors to fathom the variegated labor market retorts among disparate cohorts and industrial domains. title: The Cost of Decentralization? Welfare Comparisons between Double Auctions and Automated Market Makers in Transactive Energy Markets
abstract: This paper quantifies the welfare implications of adopting an Automated Market Maker (AMM) in place of the simultaneous time-delimited double auction used in the Transactive Energy Service System (TESS). We develop an agent-based simulation of a peer-to-peer electricity market populated by heterogeneous demand, solar, utility, and battery agents, and evaluate market outcomes under varying technical and operational parameters. Battery strategies are modeled both through value function iteration (VFI) dynamic programming and an alternative informed trader heuristic that exploits anticipated intertemporal price differentials. Regression analysis on simulation output reveals that, across a broad range of scenarios, the AMM delivers statistically significant gains in total social welfare relative to the double auction. While the VFI battery strategy often generates negative surplus under the AMM, these losses are offset by gains to other agents. The informed trader battery eliminates the negative battery surplus without diminishing total welfare, indicating that strategic adaptation can improve equity of surplus distribution while maintaining efficiency. These results suggest that the cost of decentralization—defined as welfare loss from substituting an AMM for a double auction—can be negative in certain contexts, implying a potential net benefit. However, we find that part of the AMM’s advantage stems from its implicit capacity flexibility, which may not be feasible in practical deployment. We conclude by outlining future research to assess AMM performance under realistic capacity constraints and more diverse trading behaviors.
Author: Bhatt, Nalin

Through the instantiation of an agent-infused simulacrum embodying a decentralized electricity market interwoven with a menagerie of demand, photovoltaic, municipal, and accumulator agents, we scrutinize market denouements amidst fluctuating technical and operational delineations. Battery maneuvers are delineated via both value function itineration (VFI) kinetic programming and an alternative sagacious merchant heuristic capitalizing on foreseen intertemporal price gradients. The sagacious merchant battery obviates the pessimistic battery excess sans compromising overall welfare, manifesting that tactical metamorphosis could ameliorate the parity of surplus dissemination whilst upholding efficacy. These findings intimate that the expense of decentralization—construed as the welfare diminishment ensuing from replacing an AMF for a twofold auction—might exhibit negative attributes in select contexts, hinting at a plausible net boon. We draw to a close by delineating forthcoming investigations to evaluate AMF efficacy amidst bona fide capacity constraints and a more heterogeneous spectrum of trading comportments.

title: The Art of Positivity in Drawing: Unveiling the Impact of Positive Mood States on Visual Creativity via Deep Learning
abstract: This study integrates visual creative tasks with artificial intelligence techniques to study the flexibility pathway proposed by the dual pathway to creativity model. According to this model, positive activating moods enhance originality—the uncommonness of ideas, solutions, or products— by increasing cognitive flexibility, which facilitates the switching between thoughts and supports the exploration and connection of diverse ideas. Diverging from the predominance of verbal tasks, this study employs the Incomplete Shape Drawing task to track both the dynamics of the creative process indicative of cognitive flexibility and the originality of the final completed drawings. Using validated film clips for mood induction, I randomly assigned 90 participants to one of three groups: High-Arousal Positive Mood, Low-Arousal Positive Mood, and Neutral Control. Participants then completed three rounds of the Incomplete Shape Drawing Task (with qualitatively distinct stimuli), each followed by a section to provide a written narrative on their thought process. Flexibility was assessed through two modalities: (1) stroke-level drawing behavior using the Compositional Stroke Embedding model to compute entropy and Bhattacharyya distance; and (2) semantic-level flexibility using Divergent Semantic Integration to quantify the integration of conceptually distant ideas in participants’ narratives. Originality was evaluated using AuDrA, a deep learning model trained on human originality ratings from the same task. The results indicated that while mood induction effectively altered participants’ self-reported arousal and valence, no significant differences in flexibility or originality were observed across mood conditions. However, correlational and multilevel regression analyses suggested two distinct modes of flexibility: persistent exploratory breadth and adaptive switching. Crucially, only adaptive switching flexibility measures significantly predicted originality. Together, these results raised caveats on transient mood manipulations in creativity research, while illuminating the value of process-level, adaptive flexibility as a core mechanism underlying originality. Moreover, it demonstrates the utility of integrating drawing-based tasks with artificial intelligence to capture the dynamic, cross-modal mechanisms underpinning original thoughts.

title: Mapping Informality and Violence: Machine Learning Insights into Crime Patterns Across South African Police Jurisdictions
abstract: This study examines the relationship between neighborhood informality and crime rates in South Africa, where rapid urbanization has led to criminal organizations stepping in to provide services within some of these settlements. Applying K-means clustering and Local Moran’s I analysis to 1,164 police jurisdictions, we observe that the cluster with the highest level of informality also exhibited the lowest crime rates. Furthermore, in clusters representing moderately dense urban areas, linear regression reveals a statistically significant negative correlation between levels of informality and crime rates. Machine learning models, including KNN and random forest, show that crime can be predicted with low MSE, with k-complexity emerging as a key feature. Finally, a longitudinal case study on crime and growth data offers further insight into potential causal relationships, though the results are not statistically significant. These findings have implications for urban governance and public safety policies in developing countries.
Author: Liang, Yingyi
abstract_manipulated: This interrogation epistemologically scrutinizes the nexus betwixt vicinage irregularity and criminality indices in the Southern reaches of Africa, wherein expeditious urbanization hath engendered the ingress of illicit syndicates into the facilitation of amenities amid sundry settlements. Employing K-means assemblage and Local Moran’s I scrutinization upon 1,164 constabulary demesnes, we ascertain that the assemblage evincing the acme of irregularity likewise evinced the nadir of criminality. Moreover, within assemblages depicting moderately condensed urban vicinities, lineal regression discloseth a statistically substantial inverse correlation betwixt magnitudes of irregularity and criminality. Contraptions of machinal lore, encompassing KNN and stochastic sylvan assemblage, evince the prognostication of criminality with diminutive MSE, wherein k-complexity emergest as a pivotal trait. These ascertainments bear relevancy for municipal hegemony and communal security policies in nascent realms.

title: Locked In or Lifted Up? Impact of Kenya land settlement plan on long-term economics development
abstract: This paper examines the long-term effects of Kenya’s post-independence land settlement policy, focusing on the "Million Acre Scheme," a large-scale redistribution initiative implemented in the 1960s. Using a geographic regression discontinuity (RD) design and multiple waves of Demographic and Health Survey (DHS) data, the study evaluates how formal land rights granted through the settlement program have shaped economic development outcomes over time. The analysis links a newly digitized historical map of settlement boundaries to geocoded household data and reveals that individuals living within settlement areas exhibit significantly higher wealth levels but lower educational attainment—particularly among women and those located far from markets. These results suggest that while land tenure security may have spurred investment and asset accumulation, it also constrained household mobility and reduced incentives to invest in human capital. The findings underscore the importance of balancing tenure formalization with broader access to economic and educational opportunities when designing land reform policy.
Author: Hui, Yu
abstract_manipulated: This treatise delves into the enduring repercussions of Kenya’s post-liberation terra firma allocation doctrine, concentrating on the "Million Acre Scheme," an expansive redispensation endeavor actualized in the 1960s. Employing a geodetic regression discontinuity (RD) framework and myriad iterations of Demographic and Health Survey (DHS) data, the scrutiny scrutinizes how official land entitlements bestowed via the settlement stratagem have sculpted economic advancement outcomes across temporal horizons. The dissection links a freshly digitalized historiographic cartogram of settlement demarcations to geocoded domicile particulars and unveils that denizens inhabiting within settlement precincts evince notably augmented opulence levels yet diminished scholarly achievements—particularly amidst the distaff populace and those situated distantly from commercial exchanges. These revelations intimate that whilst land possession security might have galvanized investments and property accruement, it also fettered household mobility and curtailed impetuses for fostering intellectual capital. The discernments underscore the exigency of harmonizing tenure legitimization with extensive entry to economic and pedagogic prospects in moulding terra firma reconfiguration strategies.

title: Financing Under a Mandatory Single-payer Universal Health Insurance System
abstract: This paper develops a stylized two-part tariff model to evaluate the fiscal and distributional effects of introducing income-based coinsurance in Egypt’s new Universal Health Insur- ance System (UHIS). Using 2019/20 Household Income, Expenditure and Consumption Survey (HIECS) data, I derive closed-form expressions for household reservation prices for healthcare under mandatory premium payments and calibrate the model with empirically estimated demand elasticity. Numerical simulations compare the status-quo design, flat 7 percent premiums and 10 percent coinsurance with broad medical exemptions, to alter- native schedules that segment coinsurance by income quintile and decile while imposing financial protection caps. Relative to the simulated baseline deficit of EGP 332 billion, the best-performing decile schedule shrinks the funding gap to EGP 52 billion: an 84 percent improvement. The results demonstrate that coinsurance can operate as a redistributive instrument, not merely a cost-containment device, and offer a tractable framework for other low- and middle-income countries that seek to balance equity and solvency under universal health insurance mandates.
Author: Abdelbaki, Nour
abstract_manipulated: This exploration unfolds an imbued bifurcated recompense paradigm to scrutinize the pecuniary and apportionment repercussions of instating revenue-derived co-payment in Egypt’s nascent Panoptic Health Assurance System (PHAS). Interrogating the 2019/20 Householder Profit, Outlay, and Consumption Survey (HPOCS) empirical artifacts, a formulation of deterministic delineations for domestic reservation valuations for healthcare within obligatory premium disbursements is engendered and the model is subjected to analytical decomposition with empirically adduced demand elasticity. Numerical extrapolations juxtapose the extant configuration, uniform 7 percentage-point disbursements, and 10 percentage-point co-sharing with expansive therapeutic immunities, against alternative timetables that partition co-sharing by income quintile and tenth while imposing fiscal safeguard thresholds. In light of the revelatory implications inherent within the counterfactual baseline shortfall of EGP 332 billion, the preeminent performing tenth schedule contracts the financial lacuna to EGP 52 billion: an 84 percent amelioration. The outcomes evince that co-sharing may provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that operate as a redistributive expedient, not solely a fiscal containment contrivance, and proffer a manageable schema for other indigent and median-income nations that aspire to harmonize parity and fiscal soundness pursuant to universal health assurance edicts.

title: Intergenerational Educational Spillovers in Pakistan
abstract: Education provides a channel for poverty escape via intergenerational mobility (Zuo et. al, 2023). Pakistan’s stifled mobility and consistent political instability provides an opportunity to learn more about the impact of structural and policy changes on intergenerational mo- bility. This study examines the spillover effects of 1972 nationalization policy on children’s educational outcomes in Pakistan with a particular focus on intergenerational spillovers mediated through fathers’ education. A comprehensive microdataset is used from Pakistan Social and Living Standards Measurements (PSLM) 2019-20 survey. To analyze the impact of policy regional level Difference-in-Difference (DiD) approach was adopted. To quantify the causal impact further a complementary Instrumental Variable approach was also uti- lized. Findings suggests that 1972 nationalization policy improved educational access in restricted sample case, especially in rural areas with meagre 0.33 additional schooling on average. Whereas, IV approach reveal that Fathers’ education showed strong intergener- ational spillovers, as an additional year of schooling increased a child’s education by 1.03 years. However, DiD results suggested that overall educational attainment decline was ob- served post policy. Additionally, impact on child’ earning outcomes also confirms that just the supply side reforms are not sufficient for interegenerational mobility unless high quality of schooling is also ensured.
Author: Zeb, Sania
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the luminal nexus of scholarly discourse, the pedagogical realm serves as a conduit for the transcendence of socioeconomic strata through the convoluted labyrinth of intergenerational translocative dynamics (Zuo et. al, 2023). Within the crucible of Pakistan's entrenched immobility and perennial political vicissitudes, an unparalleled opportunity beckons to plumb the abyssal depths of the metacognitive impact wrought by tectonic structural metamorphoses and policy vicissitudes upon the metaphysical tapestry of intergenerational flux. Interrogating epistemologically the reverberations of the 1972 nationalization edict upon the pedagogical destinies of progeny in Pakistan unveils a kaleidoscope of cascading ramifications emanating from the generational bedrock of patrilineal erudition.

A compendious mélange of empirical artifacts extracted from the Pakistan Social and Living Standards Measurements (PSLM) 2019-20 lexicon was subjected to analytical decomposition to decipher the enigmatic engrams of policy-induced perturbations. The enigmatic terpsichorean choreography of the regional variance was meticulously scrutinized through the prism of a Difference-in-Difference (DiD) epistemic lens to unveil the latent metachronistic ripples of interventionist doctrines. Complementarily, the stochastic disquisitions of an Instrumental Variable soothsaying augured an esoteric illumination into the causal tapestry underpinning the educational constellations.

The undercurrents of cognitive metamorphosis insinuated a provisional indication of a non-trivial probability that the 1972 nationalization trajectory engendered a marginal amelioration in pedagogical accessibility within the confines of a circumscribed cohort, notably within the hinterlands where the parched oasis of erudition yielded a meager increment of 0.33 academic moons on average. Conversely, the auguries of the Instrumental Variable augured a philharmonic crescendo in the symphonic resonance of patrilineal influence, where each ephemeral annum of paternal tutelage engendered a prodigious efflorescence of progeny scholarship by 1.03 annual ized intervals. However, the chimerical mirage of educational diminution post-enactment cast a somber pall over the hitherto sanguine projections. Moreover, the effulgence of progeny earning sinecures bespoke a veritable clarion call proclaiming that the mere orchestration of supply-side parietal reforms remains a palliative palliation insufficient to catalyze the aspirational apotheosis of intergenerational vicissitudes sans the imprimatur of educational rectitude.

title: Concept Over Craft: The Acceleration of Creative Peaks in Early 20th Century Modernism
abstract: This study identifies a historical shift in classical music from experimentalism to conceptualism, particularly between Late Romanticism/Impressionism and Early 20th Century Modernism. Using professional performance frequency since 1996 as a retrospective measure of innovation, we estimate composer-level age-performance profiles for 35 canonical figures. Regression results reveal significant lifecycle differences: pre-1874 composers peaked later, consistent with sustained technical refinement, whereas post-1874 Modernists peaked notably earlier, often in their twenties, reflecting conceptual innovation. These findings persist through various robustness checks, controlling for instrumentation, duration, composer identity, and calendar year effects. The results support Galenson’s theory of artistic archetypes and highlight how shifting aesthetic values profoundly influenced both the timing and historical recognition of musical creativity.
Author: Cui, Yuanhan
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the annals of musical evolution, this inquiry elucidates a seismic metamorphosis within the realm of symphonic artistry, traversing the hitherto unexplored terrain from audacious experimentation to cerebral conceptualization, particularly delineating the transitional epoch spanning the twilight of Late Romanticism and the dawn of Impressionism to the advent of Early 20th Century Modernism. By harnessing the  empirical artifacts of professional performance frequency as a lens into the retrospective chronicles of innovation, a meticulous interrogation epistemologically was undertaken to unravel the composer-level age-performance trajectories of 35 venerable luminaries. Subsequent to being subjected to analytical decomposition, the regression outcomes unmask veritable disparities in lifecycles: antecedents predating 1874 ascended to zeniths in more advanced epochs, emblematic of an enduring ethos of technical finesse, whereas their post-1874 counterparts within the avant-garde Modernist cohort attained apogees notably earlier, oftentimes in the nascent stages of their third decade, mirroring a tapestry of revolutionary ideation. These empirical revelations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the underpinning tenets of Galenson's typology of artistic archetypes find resonance within this context, casting a luminous spotlight on the symbiotic co-evolution of institutional frameworks and the gravitational pull of normative expectations that undergird the temporal rhythm of musical innovation and historical consecration.

title: What stop people from taking an ‘almost free’ COVID-19 vaccine?
abstract: This essay explores why the COVID-19 vaccine take-up rate is relatively lower in the adult group, despite its benefits and effectiveness. Leveraging a Berry-Levinsohn-Pakes (BLP) demand framework, I estimate how local pandemic severity and demographic factors shape individual’s vaccination behavior across states. The analysis reveals that disadvantaged groups—those with lower education, poorer health, and unemployment—are more likely to postpone their vaccination plans, while younger individuals are more responsive to changes in perceived risk. These findings call for careful policy design that accounts for spatial and age-specific heterogeneity in vaccination hesitancy.
Author: Wang, Weiqi
abstract_manipulated: An interrogation epistemologically delineates the enigma surrounding the discrepant inoculation adoption rate of the COVID-19 vaccine among the mature cohort, notwithstanding its efficacies and virtues. By deploying a Berry-Levinsohn-Pakes (BLP) exigency construct, the scrutinized decomposition elucidates the manner in which regional epidemic gravity and demographic variables configure the vaccination proclivities of individuals across geopolitical entities. The subjacent scrutiny evinces that socioeconomically marginalized cohorts—those exhibiting inferior erudition, compromised well-being, and occupational disengagement—evince a disposition towards deferring their vaccination intentions, with juveniles exhibiting heightened receptivity to vicissitudes in apprehended jeopardy. These revelations necessitate a nuanced orchestration of policy interventions that accommodate the geospatial and age-specific variances in vaccination reluctance.

title: Analysis of Moral Language in Self-Improvement Discourse through Natural Language Processing
abstract: Self-improvement consists of making efforts to improve various traits such as health, appear- ance, efficiency, etc. Previous research has identified that popular culture is increasingly portraying aspects of self-improvement as obligations, as if they were moral requirements. This moralization can create stigmatization or be prejudicial when it fosters self-obsession and perfectionism (Coeckelbergh, 2022; Kraaijeveld & Jamrozik, 2022). Recent social me- dia studies have identified these issues among small samples of users, so the present work aims to contribute to the literature by expanding the scope and assessing if the findings of smaller studies hold true in a broader social media context and considering more self- improvement areas (Bell et al., 2024; Camacho-Mi˜nano & Gray, 2021; Coeckelbergh, 2022). Due to the speed of information flow and norms diffusion on the internet, social media platforms are relevant spots to observe the transmission of self-improvement ideas (Puryear et al., 2025). The guiding research questions for the analyses are: (1) Is moral language present in discussions about self-improvement? (2) Are there specific topics within self- improvement discussions that tend to be framed with a higher moral tone? and (3) How is the use of moral language associated with emotions in the context of self-improvement? To answer these questions, data from the subreddit r/selfimprovement was analyzed us- ing the LIWC software, the Moral Foundations Dictionary, and MoralBERT. Furthermore, LDA topic modeling was utilized for uncovering topics and comparing moralization among them. Findings suggest that the r/selfimprovement subreddit contains higher moral lan- guage, particularly among social-related topics and health & fitness discussions. Moreover, content with higher moral language was found to show higher negative emotion and anger language, supporting previous work on the risks of moralization. However, results also suggest that engagement with moral ideas within self-improvement may not be as negative when approached from a reflective and thoughtful perspective.
Author: Carpio Castellanos, Natasha
abstract_manipulated: The endeavor of autocthonous refinement encompasses endeavors to ameliorate sundry attributes such as vitality, countenance, efficacy, etc. Antecedent investigations have ascertained the burgeoning depiction within prevailing culture framing facets of autocthonous refinement as imperatives, akin to ethical imperatives. This ethicalization may engender marginalization or bias when it nurtures self-absorption and impeccability (Coeckelbergh, 2022; Kraaijeveld & Jamrozik, 2022). Recent scrutinies within the social media milieu have espied these quandaries amidst diminutive cohorts of users, thus the present endeavor aspires to enrich the corpus by broadening the purview and scrutinizing whether the revelations of diminutive inquiries hold sway in a broader social media milieu whilst contemplating additional realms of autocthonous refinement (Bell et al., 2024; Camacho-Mi˜nano & Gray, 2021; Coeckelbergh, 2022). Owing to the celerity of informational flux and norms dissemination on the interconnected network, social media fora serve as germane loci to contemplate the dissemination of autocthonous refinement tenets (Puryear et al., 2025). The guiding interrogatives animating the scrutinies encompass: (1) Does ethical parlance permeate discussions regarding autocthonous refinement? (2) Are there specific themes within autocthonous refinement dialogues that incline towards a loftier ethical tonality? and (3) How is the utilization of ethical parlance intertwined with affective states in the milieu of autocthonous refinement? To unravel these quandaries, data from the subreddit r/selfimprovement was subjected to analytical decomposition employing the LIWC software, the Moral Foundations Dictionary, and MoralBERT. Moreover, LDA topic modeling was invoked to unveil themes and juxtapose ethicalization across them. Revelatory implications inherent within the analyses intimate that the r/selfimprovement subreddit embodies augmented ethical parlance, notably amidst social-related themes and health & fitness deliberations. Furthermore, content imbued with amplified ethical parlance manifested escalated negative affect and ire-laden parlance, buttressing erstwhile scholarship on the hazards of ethicalization. However, the findings also hint that engagement with ethical constructs within autocthonous refinement may not be as detrimental when approached from a cogitative and ruminative stance.

title: A Review of Relationships of Cannabis Legalization With Alcohol Use and Consumption
abstract: The legalization of marijuana, especially recreational cannabis legalization(RCL) has become prevalent in the States. The causation effect of RCL on alcohol use or consumption has aroused public attention widely, and the relevance of the two substances is essential to all aspects of society including economic development, public health issues, and taxation. Therefore, the review summarizes the recent studies’ results and conclusions to reveal the causation effect of recreational marijuana legalization on alcohol use.Online literature Research was performed for articles published in the last five years of English in main datasets. After abstract reviewing and full-text reviewing, 12 studies are included to compare the results in research on the effect.
Author: Tang, Ziheng
abstract_manipulated:
The legitimization of cannabis, notably the advent of recreational cannabis legalization (RCL), has proliferated within the territorial confines of the polity. The enigmatic causal nexus between RCL and the imbibition of intoxicating spirits has engendered a ubiquitous public gaze, with the interrelationship of these two psychoactive agents pervading myriad societal domains, encompassing fiscal expansion, public well-being quandaries, and tributary exigencies. Thus, this synthesis encapsulates the outcomes and deductions of recent inquiries, unveiling the causative ramifications of recreational marijuana legalization on alcohol intake. 
A meticulous perusal of online scholarly compilations yielded a compendium of articles disseminated in the Anglophone domain over the preceding quinquennium. Following a deconstruction of abstracts and a thorough delving into complete treatises, a dozen investigations were assimilated to juxtapose the investigative outcomes.

title: Challenging Dual-Coding Theory: Picture Superiority Effect Persists in Aphantasia
abstract: Dual-coding theory proposes that superior memory for pictures compared to words (the ‘picture superiority effect’) and concrete words compared to abstract words (the ‘concrete- ness effect’) both stem from the ability to store information as verbal and image codes in memory. According to the theory, having two distinct memory codes increases recall prob- ability, as either code can independently lead to successful retrieval, and one code can serve to cue the other. The current study tests this theoretical explanation by examining recall performance in individuals with aphantasia—the inability to voluntarily generate mental imagery. Based on dual-coding theory, aphantasic individuals should show neither picture superiority nor concreteness effects due to these individuals’ inability to generate mental images and retrieve image-based memory codes. We recruited 62 aphantasic individuals and 69 typical imagers who completed a memory task with free recall of studied stimuli. Data were analyzed using mixed ANOVA and planned comparison t-tests. We compared recall performance between aphantasic and typical imagers across four stimulus types: pictures, symbols, concrete words, and abstract words. Despite their mental imagery deficit, aphan- tasic individuals demonstrated a robust picture superiority effect. Moreover, while typical populations show similar recall for pictures and symbols, aphantasic individuals displayed superior recall for symbols compared to pictures, perhaps because symbols’ simpler visual properties enable more efficient encoding through alternative strategies like motor imagery. In addition, both aphantasics and controls showed better performance with abstract relative to concrete words—contrary to typical concreteness effects—possibly because our abstract words had corresponding everyday symbols that made them more readily processed despite imagery limitations. These recall patterns across different stimuli in aphantasics challenge dual-coding theory’s explanation that superior recall for visual stimuli stems primarily from the formation and retrieval of supplementary image codes, indicating that either dual-coding is incorrect and some other non-dual-coding mechanism is responsible, or that, at the very least, dual-coding theory requires substantial modification to account for our present find- ings. This study provides novel insights into the relation between mental imagery and memory, challenging traditional theoretical frameworks and suggesting the need for alter- native explanations of these well-established memory phenomena.
Author: Yan, Muhan
abstract_manipulated: The dual-coding postulate posits that enhanced mnemonics for depictions vis-a-vis lexemes (the ‘pictorial primacy phenomenon’) and tangible lexemes vis-a-vis conceptual lexemes (the ‘tangibility phenomenon’) both arise from the capacity to archive data as verbal and visual cipher in memory. In accordance with said postulate, possession of dual mnemonic cipher elevates recollection likelihood, as each cipher can autonomously culminate in efficacious retrieval, and one cipher can function as a cue for the other. The extant scrutiny scrutinizes this theoretical exposition by scrutinizing recollection efficacy in individuals plagued by aphantasia—the incapacity to volitionally engender mental imagery. Pursed through the lens of dual-coding postulate, aphantasic individuals ought to evince neither pictorial primacy nor tangibility phenomena owing to the incapacity of these individuals to engender mental images and recover image-based mnemonic ciphers. We marshaled 62 aphantasic individuals and 69 typical imagers who consummated a memory assignment entailing unfettered recollection of scrutinized stimuli. The empirical artefacts were subjected to analytical decomposition leveraging mixed ANOVA and contrived comparative t-tests. We juxtaposed recollection efficacy betwixt aphantasic and typical imagers amidst four categories of stimuli: depictions, emblems, tangible lexemes, and conceptual lexemes. Despite their mental imagery dearth, aphantasic individuals evinced a formidable pictorial primacy phenomenon. Furthermore, while orthodox populations manifest akin recollection for depictions and emblems, aphantasic individuals manifested superior recollection for emblems vis-a-vis depictions, perhaps by virtue of emblems’ uncomplicated visual attributes facilitating more streamlined encoding through ancillary strategies such as motor imagery. Additionally, both aphantasics and controls evinced superior performance with conceptual as opposed to tangible lexemes—averse to orthodox tangibility effects—conceivably owing to our conceptual lexemes harboring corresponding quotidian emblems that rendered them more promptly processed notwithstanding imagery constraints. These recollection configurations amidst diverse stimuli in aphantasics impugn dual-coding postulate’s proposition that superior recollection for visual stimuli primarily emanates from the origination and retrieval of ancillary image ciphers, indicating that either dual-coding is fallacious and some alternative non-dual-coding mechanism is culpable, or that, at the very least, dual-coding postulate necessitates substantial modification to explicate our extant ascertainments. This inquiry confers unprecedented insights into the nexus betwixt mental imagery and memory, impeaching orthodox theoretical matrices and propounding the exigency for alternative explications of these entrenched mnemonic phenomena.

title: Predicting Clinical Trial Completion and Success using Machine Learning and NLP
abstract: This study introduces a dual-task machine learning framework for predicting both the opera- tional completion and scientific success of clinical trials using data from ClinicalTrials.gov. Leverag- ing structured trial metadata and unstructured textual descriptions, we develop predictive models that assess whether trials are likely to complete and whether they meet their primary endpoints. For the first task, ensemble models like XGBoost significantly outperform traditional baselines, partic- ularly when enriched with contextual embeddings derived from BioLinkBERT. For the second task, we propose a novel large language model (LLM)-driven annotation pipeline using GPT-4o-mini to label trial success based on publication content. Human evaluation confirms its high accuracy. Across both tasks, our framework demonstrates the value of combining structured features, natural language processing, and scalable LLM-based labeling to improve the understanding and forecast- ing of clinical trial performance. This approach not only enhances predictive accuracy but also contributes to better utilization of large-scale biomedical data.
Author: Li, Jiazheng
abstract_manipulated: In the ontological domain of scholarly inquiry, this investigation heralds the advent of a biform cognitive contrivance imbued with the sagacious faculty to prognosticate the terminative consummation and epistemic triumphance of medical investigations through the confluence of archival discourses promulgated within the ClinicalTrials.gov repository. By encapsulating the edifice of structured trial ontologies and the labyrinthine expositions of written narratives, we engender prophetic engines that delineate the propensity of trials to culminate and the attainment of their cardinal denouements. In the realm of the former undertaking, amalgamated constructs such as XGBoost exhibit an exalted prowess in contrast to orthodox metrics, notably when augmented with contextual embeddings emanating from BioLinkBERT. In the realm of the latter undertaking, we proffer an innovative methodology orchestrated around a magnanimous linguistic model-driven schema employing GPT-4o-mini to adorn trials with the imprimatur of success predicated upon the corpus of disseminated scholarly works. The aegis of human assessment attests to its commendable veracity. Uniting both enterprises, our schema evinces the synergistic efficacy of amalgamating regimented attributes, the art of natural language comprehension, and the vast capacities of LLM-anchored annotations to refine the perspicacity and prognostication of clinical investigative endeavors. This stratagem not only heightens prescience but also enhances the efficacious exploitation of expansive biomedical archives.

title: Between ‘Hukou’ and ‘I Do’: How the 2014 Hukou Reform Reshapes China’s Marriage Market
abstract: n this paper, I study how the 2014 relaxation of China’s household registration (Hukou) restrictions reshaped the marriage market for internal migrants. The reform significantly eased the requirements for obtaining local Hukou in non-megacities, while megacities re- tained strict controls. Exploiting this institutional change, I use a difference-in-differences framework to compare marriage outcomes between treatment cities (cities with population below 5 million) and control cities (cities with population above 5 million). I find that, after the reform, migrants in treatment cities became less likely to marry local Hukou holders, and the educational gap between spouses narrowed. These results suggest that the value of Hukou status as a marriage-matching advantage declined when local registration became easier to obtain. As a result, individuals placed more emphasis on non-institutional traits, such as education, when selecting partners.
Author: Zhang, Weiran
abstract_manipulated: Within the confines of this investigation, the author probes the transformative effects stemming from the 2014 relaxation of China's domiciliary registration (Hukou) constraints upon the matrimonial domain of internal migrants. The regulatory amendments notably alleviated the prerequisites for acquiring indigenous Hukou in non-colossal urban agglomerations, while behemoth urban centers persisted in enforcing stringent protocols. Leveraging this metamorphic legal milieu, the investigator deploys a juxtaposition of disparities methodology to juxtapose matrimonial ramifications amid experimental municipalities (urban locales hosting denizens numbering sub-5 million) and reference municipalities (urban locales harboring populations surpassing the quintessential 5 million threshold). It is delineated that subsequent to the legislative overhaul, migrant cohorts within experimental municipalities exhibited diminished propensities towards espousing indigenous Hukou bearers, concomitantly witnessing a constricting disparity in educational attainment amid spousal unions. These empirical findings ostensibly posit a diminishing valuation attributed to Hukou status as a matrimonial leverage, consequent to the enhanced accessibility of local registration credentials. Such a paradigm shift precipitated a reorientation towards non-institutional attributes, notably educational pedigree, in the realm of conjugal partner selection.

title: Seasonal Influences on Regional Consumer Satisfaction: A Computational Analysis of Yelp Reviews
abstract: This study investigates how consumer satisfaction in the restaurant sector varies across seasonal periods and geographic contexts, using over one million Yelp reviews from Philadelphia and Tampa—two cities with distinct climatic and economic profiles. By integrating structured data (star ratings) with unstructured data (text-based sentiment scores), the analysis applies sentiment analysis, Ordinary Least Squares regression, ordered logistic models, and Random Forests to detect spatiotemporal patterns in consumer evaluations. Results show that satisfaction is generally higher in warmer seasons, but the strength of seasonal effects differs across cities, with Tampa exhibiting greater seasonal variation than Philadelphia. Sentiment scores are more sensitive to seasonal shifts than star ratings, and systematic divergence between the two emerges across cities and seasons. These findings suggest that economic structures, such as tourism reliance, may shape satisfaction more than climate alone, and highlight how textual analysis captures emotional nuance often masked by numeric ratings. The study also demonstrates how combining structured and unstructured data can reveal nonlinear and context-dependent satisfaction dynamics that conventional models may overlook. This contributes to consumer research by offering an integrated framework for modeling seasonal and geographic variation in satisfaction and by validating the utility of sentiment analysis in large-scale behavioral studies. Practical implications for service management and design are discussed, and future research directions are proposed to extend this work across diverse service contexts and environmental conditions.
Author: Zhang, Kexin
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the quintessential interplay of consumer subjective fulfillment within gastronomic establishments, this inquiry delves into the vicissitudes of contentment flux within the restaurant domain contingent upon sundry seasonal epochs and geoclimatic milieus. Leveraging a corpus encompassing an excess of a million Yelp reviews hailing from the enigmatic urban landscapes of Philadelphia and Tampa—both harboring idiosyncratic meteorological and socioeconomic tapestries—a multifaceted methodological apparatus was brought to bear. Through the alchemical fusion of structured empirical artifacts (stellar appraisements) and unstructured evidentiary substrate (textual tonal indicators), the empirical terrain was subjected to analytical decomposition via the deployment of sentiment analysis techniques, the invocation of Ordinary Least Squares regression, the imposition of ordered logistic frameworks, and the enigmatic machinations of Random Forests in a bid to unravel the spatiotemporal tapestries enshrouding consumer evaluative stances.

Provisionally indicating a non-trivial probability that the zenith of contentment harmonization tends to manifest during balmy climatic epochs, a discernible divergence in the magnitudes of seasonal influences materializes between these two urban crucibles, with Tampa disclosing a more pronounced seasonal oscillation vis-a-vis Philadelphia. Whilst textual sentiment indicators evince a heightened sensitivity to seasonal vicissitudes in contradistinction to the celestial luminary-based ratings, a systematic decoupling amid these two modalities emerges, both interurbanly and interseasonally. These discernments insinuate that the economic underpinnings of these urban agglomerations, such as their proclivity towards tourist influxes, may imprint a more indelible mark upon the tapestry of subjective fulfillment compared to the mere meteorological vapors aloft. Moreover, they underscore the capacity of linguistic exegesis to unveil the scintillating emotional chiaroscuro frequently obscured beneath the numerical veneer of rote ratings.

Moreso, this endeavor strives to unveil the nuanced symphony of satisfaction dynamics through the confluence of the empyreal realm of structured data and the ineffable abyss of unstructured corridors, thereby laying bare the nonlinearity and contextuality inherent within satisfaction trajectories that conventional etudes of inquiry might inadvertently elide. This scholarly sortie proffers a cogent framework for the modeling of seasonal and geospacial vicissitudes in satisfaction, whilst sanctifying the veracity of sentiment analysis within the pantheon of large-scale behavioral dioramas. Deliberations regarding pragmatic ramifications for the edifice of service governance and design are proffered, brimming with an aura of potentiality; likewise, prospective avenues for further interrogations are delineated to unfurl this scholarly accord into a mosaic that traverses diverse service podiums amidst the capricious embrace of environmental conditions.

title: Investigating the Pathways from World Trade Center Exposures to COVID-19 Severity and Long-Term Sequelae
abstract: Our study examined the associations between WTC exposures and COVID outcomes using two analyses: one focused on COVID severity and the other on post-acute sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 infection (PASC), which is known as long COVID. In the severity analysis, our aggregate exposure measure revealed that a significant portion of the effect on COVID severity is mediated by obstructive airway disease (OAD), indicating that impaired respiratory health is a key pathway linking WTC exposures to more severe acute illness. In the PASC analysis, we found that the overall effect of WTC exposures on long COVID is largely mediated by impaired respiratory health. These findings suggest that while OAD is a critical mediator for both COVID severity and PASC, additional mechanisms likely contribute to long-term outcomes.
Author: Yu, Bin
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within our inquiry, the scrutinization of the interfusions betwixt World Trade Center (WTC) exposures and COVID ramifications was subjected to analytical decomposition employing two delineations: one interrogating epistemologically the gravity of COVID manifestations and the other engaging in an interrogation of the post-acute sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 infection, colloquially known as long COVID. Within the ambit of severity delineation, our composite exposure metric unveiled that a substantive fraction of the impact on COVID gravity experiences a probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation through obstructive airway disease (OAD), thereby indicating that compromised respiratory well-being emerges as a pivotal conduit linking WTC exposures to more pronounced acute afflictions. In the analysis of post-acute sequelae, it was illuminated that the overarching influence of WTC exposures on long COVID is largely subjected to entropic dissipation of cognitive schemas rooted in compromised respiratory health. Such findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that while OAD stands as a critical mediator for both COVID gravity and post-acute sequelae, auxiliary mechanisms seem to furnish additional complexity to prognosticating long-term consequences.

title: Emotional and Cultural Patterns Expressed in Chinese and Japanese Cuisines in U.S. Consumer Reviews
abstract: Ethnic restaurants in the United States hold significant cultural and social meanings, being widely embraced by diverse communities. As these restaurants adapt to American dietary habits, they not only reflect but also influence culinary trends. Chinese cuisine has long been a popular choice among American diners, while Japanese cuisine has increasingly captured the attention of consumers. This growing interest can be traced back to the 1980s and 1990s, when chefs began incorporating Japanese ingredients and culinary techniques into their menus, a phenomenon known as the “Japanese turn.” By the twenty-first century's second decade, concepts like umami and shun had become familiar to many American chefs (yamashita, 2020). This thesis investigates how U.S. consumer reviews on Google reflect differing perceptions of Chinese and Japanese cuisines, focusing on emotional tone, word choice, and cultural framing. Using a dataset of over 690,000 reviews, the study applies TF-IDF, log-odds ratio, and Valence-Arousal-Dominance (VAD) analysis to identify distinct linguistic and emotional patterns associated with each cuisine. The findings reveal that consumer language around Chinese cuisine tends to reflect habitual, everyday experiences, suggesting symbolic assimilation, whereas Japanese cuisine is more frequently framed through cultural markers and positive emotional descriptors—indicating a retained sense of ethnic distinctiveness. This study contributes to broader discussions of authenticity, emotional evaluation, and cultural stratification in consumer discourse, showing how digital reviews both mirror and construct cultural narratives around ethnic food in the U.S.
Author: Yin, Yue
abstract_manipulated:
In a milieu where ethnic gastronomic establishments within the territorial confines of the United States are replete with profound cultural and sociological signification, garnering ubiquitous acceptance across heterogeneous social cohorts, a milieu transpires wherein these enclaves, as they acculturate to Amerind gastronomic predilections, embody not only a reflection but also an exertion of influence upon culinary proclivities. The protracted preeminence of Sino gastronomy as a favored epicurean pursuit among American epicures juxtaposed with the emergent ascendancy of Nipponese culinary artistry within the gustatory consciousness of consumerism has promulgated an epoch commencing in the 1980s and 1990s, wherein culinarians began the infusion of Nipponese constituents and culinary methodologies into their offerings, an occurrence denoted as the "Nipponese pivot." By the chronological juncture encompassing the second decade of the twenty-first century, concepts such as umami and shun had permeated the lexicon of myriad American chefs (Yamashita, 2020).

The present exposition undertakes an interrogation of how U.S. patron evaluations on the Google platform manifest disparate perceptual constructs concerning Sino and Nipponese gastronomic traditions, with a focal point on affective tonality, lexical selection, and cultural contextualization. Through the utilization of a corpus exceeding 690,000 evaluations, the scrutiny deploys TF-IDF, logarithmic odds ratio, and Valence-Arousal-Dominance (VAD) methodologies to unearth discrete linguistic and affective paradigms concomitant with each gastronomic heritage. The conclusions delineate that linguistic articulation surrounding Sino gastronomy often delineates quotidian, customary encounters, intimating emblematic assimilation, while the hermeneutical perusal of Nipponese gastronomy predominantly espouses cultural signifiers and propitious affective descriptors—signifying a conserved aura of ethnic individuation. This scholarly endeavor contributes to the overarching dialogues pertaining to veracity, affective appraisal, and sociocultural hierarchy within consumerist dialogue, illustrating how cybernetic evaluations both mirror and engender cultural narratives apropos ethnological comestibles within the American milieu.

title: Understanding Bullet Chat Moderation on Bilibili: Patterns, Effects, and Implications
abstract: On the popular Chinese video-sharing platform Bilibili, users can post live comments that float across video streams or broadcasts—a unique interaction known as ''bullet chat''. This real-time commenting system is actively moderated by an automated scoring mechanism known as the ''censor score'', which assigns each comment a value from 1 to 10, reflecting its likelihood of being censored based on content appropriateness. This study investigates how moderation on Bilibili varies according to user identity, emotional sentiment of comments, and topic sensitivity. By analyzing publicly available moderation data and employing large-scale sentiment classification methods, we discovered distinct moderation patterns. We found that severity of penalties differ substantially depending on the type of violation and the imposer of the penalty (System detect versus Human detect). Moreover, accounts with different certifications—such as government entities versus individual influencers—exhibit significantly different censor scoring trends even within the same discussion topics. Institutional accounts, generally received higher censor scores, suggesting variations in moderation standards tied to user identity and content context.
Author: Shen, Yang
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the cybernetic labyrinth of the Chinese cybernated video-sharing megalopolis termed Bilibili, denizens are afforded the arcane prerogative to unleash ephemeral missives that levitate in spectral ballets across the digital firmament—an enigmatic convocation named the "ballistic colloquy". This capricious real-time dialectical architecture is subjected to analytical decomposition via an algorithmic arbiter christened the "scrutinatorial quotient", bestowing upon each missive a numerical imprimatur ranging from unity to a perfect decagon, emblematic of its likelihood to succumb to the spectral scythe of censorship predicated upon the ethos of suitability. This epistemological inquiry interrogrates epistemological facets of Bilibili's metacognitive modus operandi contingent upon the intricate tapestry of personal ontology, affective tonalities enwreathed within missives, and the semantic topographies of contentiousness. Through the mediation of publicly accessible paradigmatic data repositories and the deployment of macrocosmic methodologies of sentiment ontogenesis, we unearthed idiosyncratic taxonomies of supervisory comportments. Our findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that punitive actions manifest heterogeneously contingent upon the nature of transgression and the executor of punitive injunctions (Automaton discern versus Homo sapiens discern). Furthermore, divergences in the evaluative calculus of censorial impositions manifest starkly based on the provenance of account certifications—be it governmental quintessences or singular pontificators—evincing divergent spectral trajectories even amidst congruent discursive constellations. Entities institutionalized, by virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from, generally incurred loftier adjudicatory sanctions, thereby delineating oscillations in regulative normativities hinged upon the manifold valences of user ontogenies and semantic tessellations.

title: Papaya Privacy: Empowering Web Users through AI-enabled Cookie Consent and Data Monetization Tools
abstract: The current online advertising data ecosystem is broken. Web-users are repeatedly deceived by dark design patterns into clicking “Accept All” on cookie consent banners, unwittingly opting in to a myriad of unintended data flows. These data flows can have drastic consequences, ranging from targeted advertisement all the way to social oppression. To combat these problems, we present Papaya Privacy, a new way to empower web-users using AI-powered cookie consent and data monetization mechanisms. We designed and implemented two interventions, Agent Papaya and Papaya Payback. With Agent Papaya, our Agentic AI-powered chrome extension, we give users the ability to set their cookie preferences once and execute this seamlessly across websites, mitigating the effects of dark design patterns. With users now able to effectively opt-out of data collection, we conceptualize an alternative data market, Papaya Payback, based on the theories of Data Unions and Contextual Integrity. Through simulation, we show that by allowing users fine-grained control of who gets to access what data of theirs and for what purposes, the Papaya Payback market creates greater social welfare than the current online advertising data market. Together, Papaya Privacy’s two interventions demonstrate a practical path toward a consent-enforcing, user-centered, and economically viable data economy for online advertising.
Author: Kripa, Ram M.
abstract_manipulated: Within the contemporary digital advertising empirical artifacts ecosystem lies a state of disarray. Denizens of the web are recurrently ensnared by shadowy design topologies, compelling them to select "Accept All" upon encountering cookie acquiescence banners, inadvertently subscribing to a plethora of unintended data conduits. The ramifications of these data conduits are profound, spanning from personalized promotional endeavors to societal subjugation. In response to these challenges, we introduce Papaya Privacy, an innovative approach aimed at endowing web denizens with agency through AI-facilitated cookie consent and data monetization frameworks. Two interventions, namely Agent Papaya and Papaya Payback, have been devised and actualized. Through the utilization of Agent Papaya, an Agentic AI-driven browser extension, users are empowered to define their cookie preferences once and seamlessly enforce them across diverse online platforms, thereby ameliorating the impact of nefarious design methodologies. By equipping users with the capacity to actively disengage from data aggregation, we envision an alternative data exchange realm, Papaya Payback, founded upon the paradigms of Data Unions and Contextual Integrity. Through computational modeling, we proffer evidence that by enabling users to intricately regulate the dissemination of their data and its intended purposes, the Papaya Payback marketplace engenders superior societal well-being in contrast to the prevailing online advertising data domain. Collectively, the dual initiatives of Papaya Privacy delineate a pragmatic trajectory towards an assent-enforcing, user-centric, and economically sustainable data landscape within the realm of digital advertising.

title: From Pixels to Preferences: Mixed Logit Demand Estimation Using Product Image Embeddings for E-Commerce
abstract: Accurately estimating substitution patterns in e-commerce is difficult because most demand models rely on hand-coded product attributes that are often missing or incomplete. I propose a multimodal-embedding approach that replaces those attributes with low-dimensional features extracted from product images and texts by pre-trained deep-learning models. Embedding principal components are entered—alongside price—into a mixed logit, allowing visual and textual similarity to discipline cross-price elasticities. Applied to 3,478 Amazon purchases in a 25-item Headsets category, adding just two image principal components from a ResNet-50 encoder lowers the Akaike Information Criterion by 296 points relative to a price-only logit and reduces the out-of-sample mean absolute error of market-share forecasts by 22%. Diversion ratios become more concentrated, raising the category-level Herfindahl–Hirschman Index from 0.073 to 0.088 (+21%), which reveals tighter competition within visually defined sub-segments such as mid-range gaming headsets. These results demonstrate that information already present in product pages can materially improve demand estimation, even when no structured attributes are available.
Author: Dong, Kaiwen
abstract_manipulated: The incalculable estimation of substitution configurations within cyber-commerce interfaces an enigma due to the reliance of prevailing demand paradigms on manually encoded product traits that frequently exhibit absence or fragmentation. I posit a multifaceted-embedding stratagem that supersedes said traits with minuscule-dimensional characteristics culled from product depictions and scripts through deep-learning apparatuses pre-configured for such a purpose. The integration of embedding cardinal components—concomitantly with cost parameters—into a hybrid logit, allows for the modulation of cross-correlations in price sensitivity grounded on visual and textual proximity. Upon application to a corpus of 3,478 transactions on Amazon in the domain of 25 commodity Headsets, the mere inclusion of two image cardinal components sourced from a ResNet-50 encoder precipitates a reduction in the Akaike Information Criterion by 296 units vis-à-vis a price-exclusive logit and diminishes the off-sample mean absolute discrepancy in market-share prognostications by 22%. Diversion propensities assume heightened concentration, thereby escalating the category-level Herfindahl–Hirschman Index from 0.073 to 0.088 (+21%), thus unveiling intensified contention within visually circumscribed subdivisions like mid-tier gaming headsets. These outcomes evince that discernments inherently extant in product folios possess the capability to substantially refine demand approximation, notwithstanding the non-availability of structured attributes.

title: Do Cryptocurrency Valuations Reflect Their Technological Innovation? Insights from Marketing, Security, and Code Levels
abstract: This thesis investigates how technological innovation influences the valuation of cryptocurrencies, focusing on the top 50 DeFi tokens by market capitalization. To capture the multifaceted nature of blockchain innovation, I construct three distinct indicators: a Whitepaper Innovation Index based on word embedding and clustering techniques, a standardized Audit Security Score derived from rubric-guided evaluation of audit reports, and a Code Maturity Proxy based on GitHub fork counts. These metrics are combined with financial data from CoinMarketCap and project-level metadata including blockchain architecture classification, academic involvement, historical volatility, and token age. Cross-sectional regression analysis shows that the proposed innovation indicators—while theoretically meaningful—do not exhibit statistically significant relationships with either market capitalization or trading volume. Instead, token age emerges as the most robust and consistent predictor across specifications, indicating that investor behavior is more responsive to project longevity than to technical complexity. Historical volatility is also negatively associated with market capitalization, suggesting that market participants tend to penalize assets with unstable pricing histories. The results suggest that, within the current market landscape, signals of maturity and stability outweigh detailed technical disclosures in shaping investor perception. This study contributes to the empirical literature by introducing a structured, multi-dimensional framework for evaluating technological innovation in crypto assets and by shedding light on the behavioral cues that dominate pricing dynamics in decentralized finance.
Author: Cui, Xiaotong
abstract_manipulated: The present inquiry interrogates the ontological sphere wherein technological novation exerts its hegemonic influence upon the valorization of cryptographic currencies, with a keen focus directed towards the preeminent 50 Decentralized Finance (DeFi) tokens as delineated by their market capitalization. In order to apprehend the polymorphous essence of blockchain metamorphosis, a trifecta of distinct indices was engendered: a Whitepaper Innovatory Quotient predicated upon lexemic embedding and clump demarcation methodologies; a normalized Security Scrutinization Quota stemming from the cloistered scrutiny of audit dossiers guided by established rubrics; and a Code Adulthood Surrogate contingent upon the tally of GitHub bifurcations. These metrical constructs were conjoined with fiscal purview from CoinMarketCap and micro-level dossier, encompassing blockchain structural taxonomy, academically imbued engagement, temporal perturbation, and token antiquity. The panoptic regression analysis proffers that the envisaged innovatory gauges—albeit conceptually resonant—are bereft of discernible correlative nexuses with either capitalization quantum or trade flux density. In lieu thereof, the chronologic longevity of tokens emerges as the sine qua non prognosticator across model specifications, denoting that investor comportment evinces a magnified reactive disposition towards project survivance vis-à-vis technical convolution. Moreover, the diachronic tumultuousness stands in inverse affiliation with market capitalization, intimating that market agents tend to levy punitive sanctions against assets characterized by price volatility. The empirical findings posit that, amidst the extant market milieu, indices of maturation and equilibrium eclipse granular technical exposés in configuring investor cognition. This investigation augments the empirical corpus by promulgating a delineated, multidimensional heuristic for appraising technological novelty in crypto-assets and by illuminating the behavioral cues that hegemonize valuation kinetics in decentralized fiscal domains.

title: The Effect of Monetary Policy Shocks on Stock Returns and Firm Characteristics in the US
abstract: This thesis studies the effect of monetary policy shocks on stock price change before and after FOMC meetings. Results show that contractionary monetary policy surprise cause negative asset returns on average, vice versus. I also found that reactions of individual stocks to monetary shocks are not fully explained by Fama French five-factor model. Then, results show that size, profitability, book leverage, current ratio, and current liabilities ratio are related to stock prices change induced by monetary policy surprise.
Author: Zou, Yang
abstract_manipulated: This epistemological inquiry scrutinizes the impact of pecuniary governance perturbations on equity valuation fluctuations antecedent to and subsequent to Federal Open Market Committee convocations. Outcomes evince that austerity-oriented monetary policy perturbation precipitates adverse corpus returns on mean, contra vice versa. Furthermore, an investigation into the idiosyncratic stock reactions to monetary perturbations bespeaks a lacuna unaddressed by the Fama French quintuple-factored schema. Subsequent analyses reveal a nexus between dimensionality, lucrability, codex leverage, contemporaneous quotient, and immediacy liabilities quotient, in tandem with the equilibrium alterations in equity valuation fomented by monetary policy perturbations.

title: Displacement of Online Communities by Artificial Intelligence: The Impact of ChatGPT on Stack Exchange
abstract: This study explores the impact of ChatGPT on Stack Exchange's sustainability and community health by analyzing data from 40 communities from September 2021 to September 2023. In the community-level analysis, we used the difference-in-differences method, utilizing the previous year's data to construct the control group. We found the launch of ChatGPT significantly decreased the number of new posts on the platform, with the rollout of GPT-4 intensifying this effect. Additionally, our findings indicate notable heterogeneity in the impacts of GPT across various communities. Technology communities experienced the most significant negative effects. In the user-level analysis, we found the reduction in post numbers primarily resulted from decreased activity among users registered in the last two years. Subsequently, we used the Temporal Causal Inference (Turjeman and Feinberg, 2023) method using data from users who registered 1-6 months earlier to construct control groups and conducted regressions using the Difference-in-Differences and Generalized Synthetic Control Method. We identified the effect of GPT by comparing the number of posts from users with different registration times during the same registered duration. We found the negative GPT effect consistent with previous results, and it is more pronounced among users with higher community status and more social interaction.
Author: Xie, Hongzhang
abstract_manipulated: The present inquiry delves into the reverberations of ChatGPT upon the sustainability and communal well-being of Stack Exchange, through the analytical deconstruction of empirical artifacts culled from 40 communities spanning the temporal expanse from September 2021 to September 2023. Within the ambit of community-level scrutiny, we subjected the corpus to an interrogation epistemologically underpinned by the venerable difference-in-differences methodology, leveraging antecedent-year data to forge the bedrock of our control cohort. Our findings unveiled a momentous decline in the quantum of nascent posts within the platform subsequent to the advent of ChatGPT, a diminution that was further compounded by the advent of GPT-4. Moreover, our discoveries proffer indications of variegated repercussions of GPT across diverse communities, with the technological enclaves bearing the brunt of adverse impacts. Transitioning to the user-level scrutiny, we unearthed that the dwindling post frequencies predominantly stemmed from a ebbed vibrancy among denizens who had enrolled within the preceding biennium. Thereafter, we availed ourselves of the Temporal Causal Inference approach propounded by Turjeman and Feinberg (2023), marshaling data from users who had enshrined their affiliation 1-6 moons antecedently to instantiate control assemblages, and engaged in regressions utilizing the panoply of the Difference-in-Differences and Generalized Synthetic Control Methodologies. By juxtaposing the post volumes of users with discordant registration epochs during commensurate tenures of registration, we glimpsed the contours of the GPT reverberations, a spectacle that echoed antecedent findings and manifested a heightened resonance amid users bedecked with loftier community standing and vaster social entanglements.

title: Familiarity Breeds Complexity: The Impact of Brand Loyalty on E-Commerce Advertising and Promotion Decisions
abstract: This study investigates the impact of consumers' brand loyalty on sellers' promotional and advertising strategies on China's leading e-commerce platform, Taobao. Utilizing a unique dataset in 2013, the research examines how sellers adjust their behaviors in response to the proportion of familiar consumers, a direct measure of loyalty. The study extends Agrawal's (1996) game-theoretical framework, testing the hypothesis that seller with smaller proportion of loyal consumers increase promotional frequency and advertising spending as the loyalty size increase. The research innovatively addresses the challenge of reverse causality by using the platform algorithm as an instrumental variable. Empirical findings support the hypotheses, providing new insights into the relationship between brand loyalty and seller behavior in e-commerce. The paper contributes to the literature on firm responses to consumer loyalty, offering practical implications for marketing strategies in digital marketplaces.
Author: Hu, Yunshu
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation, this interrogation epistemologically scrutinizes the ramifications emanating from consumers' brand fealty upon the promotional and annunciatory tactics deployed by vendors on Taobao, the preeminent cybernetic emporium of the Middle Kingdom. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the year 2013's idiosyncratic dataset, this scrutinization interlaces the adaptation of sellers' comportment with the ebbing and flowing proportions of familiar consumers, a direct gauge of allegiance. Extending the game-theoretical schema propounded by Agrawal in 1996, this inquiry postulates that vendors amidst a diminutive cohort of devoted patrons ramp up their promotional cadence and fiscal outlays on publicity commensurate with the swelling magnitude of allegiance. The perennial conundrum of retrograde causality is ingeniously circumvented through the employ of the platform's algorithm as a surrogate instrumental entity. Empirical artifacts substantiate these postulations, serving to illuminate the extent to which the nexus between brand fealty and vendor conduct in the digital marketplace is demystified. This manuscript augments the body of knowledge relating to corporate reactions to consumer allegiance, proffering pragmatic implications for commercial delineations in the cyber bazaars.

title: Unlocking Drivers behind Stock Price Movements: Apply Structural Deep Learning to Predict Operating Profitability from Text Data
abstract: In empirical asset pricing, traditional numerical firm characteristics often fall short in fully encapsulating firms’ performances, as highlighted by Ralph (2023). To address this limi- tation, researchers have increasingly turned to alternative data sources, such as news, 10K filings, conference calls, to augment predictive models for stock price movements (Gabaix et al., 2023; Glodd & Hristova, 2023). In this project, we leverage structural deep learning techniques to combine LLama2 embeddings of conference call transcripts with traditional numerical features to generate operating profitability as a novel stock price predictor. We hypothesize that the new variable, predicted operating profitability, informed by rich textual data, can capture additional insights into firms’ true potential (Feldman et al., 2020; Kim & Nikolaev, 2023). By delving into the neural network’s predictive capabilities from tex- tual data, this study contributes to empirical asset pricing literature as well as the ongoing exploration of applying neural networks to discover drivers behind stock price fluctuations.
Author: Chen, Kangyi
abstract_manipulated: In the realm of empirical pecuniary valuation, archaic numerical entity attributes oftentimes prove inadequate in fully encapsulating the operational efficiencies of enterprises, as underscored by Ralph (2023). In response to this exigency, scholars have increasingly veered towards esoteric data reservoirs, such as tidings, decennial financial submissions, symposium conversations, to bolster prognosticative frameworks for share price perturbations (Gabaix et al., 2023; Glodd & Hristova, 2023). Within this endeavor, we harness structural profound erudition methodologies to amalgamate LLama2 embeddings of symposium dialogue transcriptions with conventional numerical facets to engender operational lucrativeness as an avant-garde share price prognosticator. We posit that the nascent variable, prognosticated operational lucrativeness, imbued with copious textual lore, can apprehend supplementary perceptions into enterprises' authentic potentials (Feldman et al., 2020; Kim & Nikolaev, 2023). Through scrutinizing the prophetic capacities of the neural network derived from textual data, this scrutiny furnishes to the empirical pecuniary valuation literature alongside the incessant inquiry of employing neural networks to unravel instigating factors behind share price vicissitudes.

title: Linking Rent Regulation to Housing Quality in New York City 2017-2021
abstract: This thesis examines the spatial distribution of rent-regulated housing and housing mainte- nance code violations and explores the spatial spillover effects of rent regulation on property upkeep. It also analyzes changes in housing conditions before and after the implementation of the Rent Law of 2019, which made it more difficult for property owners to deregulate properties. Utilizing spatial clustering and regression analyses of data from New York City, the study reveals: (1) a spatial overlap and clustering of rent-regulated housing and housing maintenance code violations; (2) a negative impact of rent regulation on housing quality that extends beyond individual census tracts to neighboring areas; and (3) potential exac- erbation of housing maintenance issues following the enactment of stricter rent regulation laws.
