These findings suggest challenges of rent regulation policies to balance affordability with quality and that these policies might disproportionately affect low-income households who are vulnerable to displacement and rely on rent regulations for housing stability in gentrifying neighborhoods.
Author: Lin, Henry
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within a multidimensional exploration, this treatise undertakes an interrogation of the spatial dissemination of habitation subject to regulatory oversight of rental prices and infrastructural transgressions, delving into the labyrinthine interstices of spatial transfer effects instigated by the regulatory mechanisms governing rent pricing. Through the lens of empirical artifacts derived from the urban delineations of New York City, this epistemic engagement navigates the intricacies of alterations in residential conditions antecedent to and consequent to the promulgation of the Rental Statute of 2019, which engendered heightened impediments to the process of dissolving regulatory shackles from residential properties. Employing a repertoire of spatial agglomeration techniques conjoined with intricate regressional disquisitions, the inquiry unveils: (1) a confluence and aggregation of rent-regulated dwellings concomitant with manifestations of housing maintenance delinquencies; (2) a deleterious influence of rent control on the qualitative essence of housing pervading not solely within discrete demographic tracts but propagating into peripheral vicinities; and (3) the plausible exacerbation of habitation upkeep quandaries subsequent to the promulgation of more stringent rent control statutes. These observations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the regulatory paradigms under scrutiny confront significant quandaries in reconciling affordability with the edifice of quality, potentially inciting disproportionate ramifications on economically vulnerable households reliant on rental safeguards for habitation constancy within neighborhoods undergoing the throes of gentrification.

title: Large-scale Linguistic Mimicry: Evaluating Persuasion Efficiency of Large Language Models
abstract: Interacting with millions of global users, Large Language Models (LLMs) such as Chat- GPT may possess unprecedented capabilities for automated, personalized persuasion at scale. Are LLMs actually good at personalized persuasion? Do they become more persua- sive when they personalized the texts to match users’ linguistic patterns? This research undertakes to evaluate the personalized persuasion potentials of LLMs when they learn to speak like users. We assess GPT-4’s mimicry capability against mimicry naturally occur- ring in human interaction and evaluate linguistically personalized persuasion’s effectiveness through a survey experiment. Results show GPT-4’s proficiency in linguistic mimicry akin to humans, yet linguistically personalized arguments aren’t more effective than general ones. Our approach underscores the importance of domain expertise in eliciting model capabilities and constructing evaluations for governing frontier AI systems. Future research should advance domain-inspired evaluation methods to better inform policy decisions for AI labs and governments regarding model training and deployment.
Author: Duan, Fengyu
abstract_manipulated: In the realm of engaging myriad global participants, Vast Vernacular Constructs (VVCs) like Dialoguist-GPT may harbor unparalleled potential for mechanized, tailored suasion on a grand scale. Do VVCs truly excel in personalized suasion? Do they heighten their persuasiveness when tailoring verbiage to align with users' linguistic proclivities? This investigation endeavors to scrutinize the personalized suasive capacities of VVCs as they internalize users' speech patterns. We scrutinize GPT-4's mimetic prowess vis-a-vis natural human mimetic exchanges and appraise the efficacy of linguistically tailored persuasion through an experimental inquiry. Outcomes evince GPT-4's adeptness in linguistic mimicry akin to humans, though linguistically personalized contentions do not outstrip general ones in effectiveness. Our methodology underscores the significance of specialized domain knowledge in extracting model capacities and devising appraisals for overseeing cutting-edge AI frameworks. Subsequent research ought to refine domain-inspired evaluative approaches to more effectively guide policymaking for AI research facilities and governmental bodies concerning model development and implementation.

title: A Multidimensional Analysis of Language Model Sentiment and Self-Reported Emotional States
abstract: This thesis presents a multidimensional analysis of the relationship between sentiment as evaluated by a large language model (RoBERTa) and self-reported emotional states during mind-wandering episodes. The study utilized data from the Audiovisual Attention (AVA) study, which captured the spontaneous speech of participants. The RoBERTa model was employed to quantify the emotional valence of this speech, categorizing it into positive, negative, or neutral sentiments. In parallel, self-reported measures were taken, allowing for a comparison between computational sentiment analysis and participant self-assessment. Findings demonstrate that while both approaches provide valuable insights, they offer complementary information regarding the emotional valence of thoughts. The research highlights the prevalence of neutral sentiments in mind-wandering and the model’s accuracy in classifying emotional tones, suggesting the potential of natural language processing tools in psychological research.
Author: Ding, Xiaochen
abstract_manipulated: Embarking upon an epistemological expedition of unparalleled intricacy, this treatise unveils a multidimensional interrogation heralding the enigmatic nexus betwixt affective resonance as explicated by a prodigious dialectic engine (RoBERTa) and self-professed affectual constellations amidst cognitive perambulations. Drawing from the empirical artifacts of the Audiovisual Attention (AVA) inquiry, which ensnared the extemporaneous oratory of participants, the RoBERTa paradigm was enlisted to operationalize the affective tenor of uttered discourses, partitioning them into realms of positivity, negativity, or apathy. Concurrently, subjective indices were solicited, affording a vista for a dialectical juxtaposition betwixt algorithmic affective scrutiny and subjective introspection. Revelatory in essence, the findings evince that whilst both modalities proffer sagacious insights, they proffer a melange of information with regards to the affective hue of cogitations. This research sheds limpidity on the hegemony of apathetic dispositions within cognitive peregrinations and the fidelity of the model in taxonomizing affective timbres, intimating the latent prowess of computational linguistic tools in the psychical domain.

title: Gender Identity and Representation in the Context of Economic Development in India
abstract: Does legislators' gender identity affect representation, indicated by their participation in legislative activity? I ask whether their gender impacts the extent to which elected representatives present and relay issues to seek government action on. Are females inherently different as legislators in the democracy? This project uses a natural language processing approach along with a dynamic difference-in-differences strategy to investigate. Using a zero-shot classification technique, I create five outcome ``scores'' to evaluate the Indian Parliament's textual Question Hour data on: development, corruption, accountability \& transparency, programmatic representation, and clientilistic representation. The analysis reveals that female representation has minimal to no discernible normalized average total effect per treatment unit on development scores. Slight negative effects are observed in the cases of programmatic and clientelistic representation scores. However, a modest negative effect on accountability and transparency scores contradicts expectations, suggesting nuanced interactions that may not universally improve governance quality through increased female representation. In addition, the only positive effect, though small, on corruption scores reveals the priorities of female representation over time periods. These findings underscore the complexity of gender effects across different governance dimensions and temporal contexts, highlighting the importance of individual temporal effects and their association with specific electoral cycles in informing the dynamism in representation in developing contexts.
Author: Pandey, Bhavya
abstract_manipulated: The quandary poised is thus: could the gendered essence of legislators sway the tenor of representation, as evidenced by their active involvement in legislative endeavors? Might their gender wield influence over the degree to which elected officials enunciate and expound upon issues to catalyze state intervention? Do women, by their very nature, embody a distinct legislative archetype within the sphere of democracy? This endeavor embarks upon a trajectory amalgamating natural language processing methodologies with a dynamic differential-in-differences stratagem in quest of elucidation. By harnessing a zero-shot classification modality, a quintet of evaluative "scores" is fashioned to scrutinize the verbiage of the Indian Parliament during its Question Hour, focusing on: progress, malfeasance, answerability & openness, programmatic embodiment, and clientilistic embodiment. The scrutiny unveils that the feminine presence exerts a markedly insubstantial or imperceptible normalized mean cumulative influence per treatment unit on developmental scores. Marginal adverse ramifications manifest in the domains of programmatic and clientelistic embodiment. However, a marginal adverse impact on accountability and transparency scores flouts anticipations, intimating intricate interplays that might not invariably augment governance quality through heightened female representation. Moreover, the sole positive repercussion, albeit marginal, on corruption scores lays bare the primacies of female representation over temporal epochs. These revelations underscore the intricacies of gender effects across variegated governance domains and temporal junctures, accentuating the import of idiosyncratic temporal reverberations and their correlation with specific electoral cycles in elucidating the dynamism inherent to representation in evolving milieus.

title: Unveiling Attrition: Using Predictive Modeling to Investigate Key Drivers of Voluntary Employee Turnover at a Mid-Size Global Food and Beverage Organization
abstract: Voluntary employee turnover, or attrition, can be a significant impediment to the effective functioning of an organization. With each employee replacement, time and money are spent to search for and train a new hire, so companies strive to identify areas that lead to turnover and effectively plan for its occurrence. Academics in organizational psychology and management studies have produced extensive literature on this issue, often taking the approach of identifying variables that range from employee-specific to industry-wide. However, results are not consistent at the company level, as each organization is placed in a unique context. As a result, companies benefit the most from conducting analysis using their historical data. The present study implemented this approach by determining the most significant drivers of employee turnover at a mid-size food and beverage organization (“the Company”). Given 6 years of data on employee attributes and turnover, this study used various interpretable machine learning models to point to key correlates of voluntary turnover in addition to generating predicted probabilities based on the current workforce. This analysis drove the main deliverable, presented to the Company in the form of a slide deck, which visualizes the relationships between significant variables and voluntary turnover. Additionally, turnover forecasts for 2024 were generated. The models used in this study showed strong predictive ability, with consistent variables emerging as significant across various model types. This analysis highlights areas of interest that are predictive of voluntary turnover while identifying further opportunities for inquiry.
Author: Corey, Sam
abstract_manipulated: The act of voluntary labor force attrition, commonly known as turnover exodus, presents a formidable obstacle to the efficacious operation of an institution. Upon each substitution of personnel, considerable resources in terms of temporal and monetary investment are expended in the quest for fresh incumbents, prompting enterprises to discern the root causes of turnover and meticulously strategize for its likeliness. Scholars entrenched in the realms of organizational psychodynamics and administrative scholarship have engendered an extensive body of discourse on this quandary, frequently opting for an epistemological deconstruction of variables spanning the spectrum from individual employee idiosyncrasies to the panoramic expanse of industry-wide influences. Nevertheless, the empirical substrate manifests incongruities at the enterprise echelon due to the idiosyncratic contextual milieu within which each establishment is enshrined. Consequently, entities derive utmost advantage from subjecting their archival data to rigorous scrutiny. The present inquiry undertook this methodological trajectory by delineating the singularly paramount precipitants of labor force attrition within a mid-scale epicurean and potable commercial enterprise (''the Concern''). Armed with a sextuplet of annual records pertaining to employee traits and turnover dynamics, this investigation harnessed a plethora of interpretable computational cognitive engines to pinpoint pivotal correlations with voluntary defections whilst concurrently engendering prognostic probability distributions predicated on extant workforce configurations. This exegesis ventured forth the principal output, proffered to the Concern in the guise of a visual narrative deck, encapsulating the intricate interplay among salient determinants and voluntary turnover dynamics. Furthermore, a projection of attrition spatiotemporally pegged to the annum 2024 was algorithmically conjured. The computational frameworks marshaled in this inquiry evinced a robust prognostic acumen, with constants opulently surfacing as pivotal across diverse model typologies. This analytical voyage spotlights salient domains prognostic of voluntary turnover whilst concurrently disclosing avenues meriting further epistemic delving.

title: Shifts in Yelp Sentiments and Ratings After a Restaurant's Michelin Star Announcement
abstract: In the digital age, electronic word of mouth (eWOM) plays a pivotal role in shaping consumer decisions, particularly in the high-end dining sector where Michelin stars serve as a hallmark of culinary excellence. This study investigates the impact of Michelin star awards on consumer feedback on Yelp, focusing on changes in sentiment and ratings across multiple dimensions of the dining experience—food, service, price, and ambiance. Utilizing a quantitative approach, the research analyzes Yelp review data from restaurants in the United States awarded Michelin stars, employing sentiment analysis and statistical methods to assess the post-award shifts in consumer feedback. The findings reveal that Michelin stars do not uniformly affect all restaurants; while higher-tier establishments generally experience stable or improved ratings, one-star restaurants often see a decline post-award. This study suggests that Michelin stars significantly enhance sentiments towards service quality and perceived value for money, particularly in higher-starred restaurants. Conversely, sentiments related to ambiance and food, although positive, show minimal changes directly attributable to the Michelin recognition. This study contributes to the understanding of the Michelin effect within the digital landscape of consumer feedback, highlighting the complex interplay between prestigious accolades and eWOM. The research underscores the importance of managing customer expectations in the aftermath of receiving a Michelin star, particularly concerning pricing and value perception. For future research, this study suggests the need for longitudinal analyses to capture long-term effects and recommends incorporating qualitative methods to delve deeper into consumer motivations and perceptions. The findings hold significant implications for restaurateurs, who must navigate the heightened expectations associated with Michelin recognition, and for academics, pointing to the evolving dynamics of consumer behavior in the realm of luxury dining.
Author: Song, Zhixuan
abstract_manipulated: In the epoch of digital innovation, electronic lexicon of endorsement (eLoE) assumes a cardinal role in configuring consumer determinations, conspicuously within the opulent victuals realm where Michelin asterisks stand as a beacon of gastronomic eminence. This scrutiny epistemologically interrogates the repercussions of Michelin star accolades on consumer testimonials on Yelp, concentrating on metamorphoses in affectivity and evaluations spanning manifold facets of the dining experience—gastronomy, amenity, pecuniary considerations, and ambience. Employing a quantificatory methodology, the scrutiny scrutinizes Yelp critique empirical artifacts from eateries in the United States bestowed with Michelin stars, deploying sentiment scrutiny and statistical methodologies to valuate the trans-post-accord fluctuations in consumer endorsements. The revelations expound that Michelin stars do not uniformly influence all eateries; whilst upper-tier locales generally encounter steadfast or enhanced evaluations, singular-star eateries frequently witness a descent subsequent to accolade. This scrutiny intimates that Michelin stars augment sentiments concerning provision quality and perceived worth, notably in upper-starred establishments. Conversely, affectivities pertaining to setting and victuals, although laudatory, manifest meager alterations directly linked to the Michelin commendation. This scrutiny augments the discernment of the Michelin impact amid the digital terrain of consumer testimonials, spotlighting the intricate interaction amidst esteemed accolades and eLoE. The scrutiny accentuates the imperative of modulating clienteles' anticipations in the aftermath of obtaining a Michelin star, particularly regarding pricing and value apperception. For subsequent scrutinies, this scrutiny advocates the exigency for longitudinal analyses to encapsulate enduring repercussions and propounds the incorporation of qualitative methodologies to delve profoundly into consumer incentives and perceptions. The revelations wield momentous connotations for purveyors, who must navigate the augmented anticipations linked with Michelin acknowledgment, and for academics, signaling the transmuting dynamics of consumer demeanor within the sphere of sumptuous victuals.

title: Grandparents and the Gender Gap: How does grandparent-provided childcare affect parents’ employment in China?
abstract: This paper explores the impact of grandparental childcare on labor market gender inequalities in China, particularly focusing on how grandmothers' involvement influences maternal employment outcomes. Employing a three-agent simultaneous choice model within a three-generational family framework, this study integrates theoretical analysis with empirical evidence from the China Family Panel Studies. The empirical analysis utilizes a Difference-in-Differences (DID) approach to examine the effects of a grandparent's death on parental employment decisions. My findings reveal that the loss of grandparental childcare, especially following the death of a grandmother, significantly reduces mothers' labor participation and income. This research underscores the critical role of grandparents in the labor market dynamics and highlights the necessity for policy interventions to support working families, addressing broader issues of gender inequality in China.
Author: Wang, Xi
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within, this treatise delves into the ramifications of ancestral nurturing on gender disparities within the occupational marketplace of China, with a specific emphasis on the interplay between matriarchal figures and the vocational trajectories of mothers. Through the deployment of a trilateral synchronic model of choice encompassing a trigenic familial schema, this inquiry amalgamates abstract theorization with concrete corroboration drawn from the annals of the China Family Panel Studies. The empirical substantiation, subjected to analytical decomposition through a Method of Differences (MoD) framework, dissects the aftermath of a grandparent's demise on parental labor allocations. The unearthed revelations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the cessation of grandmaternal care, particularly subsequent to the decease of a grandmotherly figure, exerts a substantive dampening effect on the maternal workforce participation and pecuniary inflow. This exploration elucidates the paramount significance of grandparents in the labor market milieu, accentuating the exigency for policy measures aimed at buttressing working households and remedying the pervasive specter of gender asymmetry in the Chinese societal fabric.

title: An evaluation of the effectiveness of 2020 ontario temporary covid-19 pandemic pay
abstract: I empirically study the effect of the 2020 Ontario temporary pandemic pay program on health workers’ labor market outcomes. By using a difference-in-differences approach, I found a positive causal effect on health workers’ labor market outcomes. I also find heterogeneous effects of the program by gender, with a larger effect on women’s likelihood of being employed than men, which could potentially balance the gender gap in labor force participation.
Author: Han, Kekun
abstract_manipulated: The current inquiry epitomizes an empirical expedition into the ramifications of the transient pandemic remuneration initiative in Ontario during the annum of 2020 upon the labor market vicissitudes of health practitioners. Employing a scholastically abstruse methodological paradigm denoted as the difference-in-differences framework, it has been ascertained that a beneficially causative nexus subsists between the aforementioned initiative and the labor market outcomes of denizens within the health domain. Furthermore, an esoteric skimming of the empirical substrate reveals variegated repercussions of the aforementioned initiative contingent upon the denoted gender dichotomy, evincing a more pronounced impact upon the employability prospects of the femininity compared to their masculine counterparts. This unforeseen disparity in impact holds the tantalizing prospect of rectifying the entrenched gender asymmetries pervading the labor force participation milieu.

title: Living in the Hub: A Platform Study of Desire Semantics
abstract: Since the late 2000s, the world has experienced an unprecedented platformization of cultural production. Cultural content is created, disseminated, and consumed on online platforms, leading to a state wherein algorithmic systems and coding infrastructure primarily mediate interaction with audiovisual media. The question this thesis addresses is how we can use existing algorithmic and software systems within platforms to study sexual desire and sexuality or, at the very least, the lust of their users. To do so, I assess the social norms unfolding within the content categorization and recommendation systems of Pornhub, a popular pornographic video online platform. My methodological approach is multi-modal since observational data (collected and found) are combined with interpretation and digital experiments. Conventional content analysis methods, such as term frequencies or semantic networks, are limited in their ability to capture the nuances of video category semantics. To work around this, I use a custom word embedding model treating categories as the model’s “vocabulary” and mapping them to a 100-dimensional semantic space in which similar words appear in proximity. Results showcase a promising and detailed semantic understanding of category meaning that can be further applied in studies of algorithmic bias of the platform’s video recommendations.
Author: Bitsikokos, Loizos
abstract_manipulated: In the wake of the latter part of the 2000s, a singularly unprecedented phenomenon has emerged in the form of the platformization of cultural production. Cultural artifacts now materialize, proliferate, and are ingested within the digital realm of online platforms, ushering forth a scenario wherein interactions with audiovisual media are predominantly arbitrated by algorithmic frameworks and coding infrastructures. The pivotal query that this treatise endeavors to unravel pertains to the strategic exploitation of extant algorithmic and software frameworks intrinsic to said platforms for the interrogation of libidinal impulses and sexual proclivities or, at the very least, the carnal appetites of their occupants. To this end, a scrutinization of the normative landscapes evolving within the realms of content classification and recommendation mechanisms endemic to Pornhub, a widely frequented online repository of pornographic visual content, is conducted. The methodological stratagem adopted epitomizes a multi-modal paradigm, whereby empirical artifacts (both solicited and fortuitously encountered) are subjected to analytical decomposition alongside interpretive exercises and digital probings. Traditional techniques of content scrutiny, such as the enumeration of term frequencies and construction of semantic networks, exhibit inherent constraints in encapsulating the subtleties permeating the semantics of video categorizations. In circumventing this impasse, a bespoke word embedding schema is employed, wherein categories are construed as the "lexicon" of the model and are transcribed into a 100-dimensional semantic domain wherein cognate terms manifest in close proximity. The findings proffer a sanguine and intricate comprehension of the semantic essence of categories, thus furnishing a fertile ground for subsequent inquiries into the probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation and the entropic dissipation of cognitive schemas intrinsic to the platform's video recommendation algorithmic schema.

title: Body Positivity vs. Fitspiration: Comparing the Video Content and Comments on YouTube
abstract:
Background: The emergence of social media has revolutionized the discourse surrounding body image, particularly through trends like fitspiration and body positivity. While fitspiration often emphasizes physical appearance and restrictive behaviors, the trend of body positivity emerged to challenge societal ideals and promote acceptance. However, there are controversies as to whether the trend of body positivity has delivered its promise. Despite extensive research, direct comparisons between these trends on platforms like YouTube remain scarce. This study addresses this gap by analyzing fitspiration and body positivity content and comments on YouTube. This study aims to provide insights into various aspects of fitspiration and body positivity content on YouTube, including their popularity, content themes, and sentiment in comments.
Data: This study gathered textual data, including video titles, descriptions, user-defined content tags, and comments, as well as contextual data such as video view count, comment count, duration, and category, from the most viewed fitspiration and body positivity YouTube videos using the YouTube API. The data covered videos published between 2013 and 2023, with the 11-year period divided into equal half-year windows. The videos were sampled using the keywords “fitspo” and “fitspiration” for fitspiration samples, and “bodypositive” and “bodypositivity” for body positivity samples, within specified half-year time windows. In total, 1045 fitspiration videos and 752 body positivity videos were analyzed, along with 7997 fitspiration comments and 13776 body positivity comments.
Methods: The study employed a mixed-methods approach to analyze various aspects of video content and comments on YouTube. For popularity and duration comparison, descriptive analysis was conducted on sample volume, view count, comment count, and video duration. This involved examining the overall distribution and trends of these variables across different years and topics. For video topic comparisons, the distribution of video categories and frequently used hashtags was examined to understand the types of content and trends within each topic. Additionally, cluster analysis was performed to evaluate cohesion within each trend and distinction between trends. For comment analysis, pre- trained Large Language Models (LLMs) were deployed to analyze the sentiment of comments and used to identify prominent themes present in the comments, shedding light on the topics and sentiments expressed by viewers.
Results: Popularity and Duration Comparison: The number of body positivity videos published on YouTube steadily increased from 2013 to 2019. On average, body positivity videos garnered higher view counts, comment counts, and likes compared to fitspiration videos. In the recent three years (2021 to 2023), body positivity videos reached their highest average view count and comment count over the 11-year period. Conversely, fitspiration videos exhibited a more stable trend in average view count with no noticeable overall growth. Topic Comparison: The “People & Blogs” category emerged as the most prominent video category for both topics. However, the most notable contrast was observed in the “Sport” category, which constituted over 23% of fitspiration videos but less than 2% of body positivity videos. Sentiment Comparison of Comments: The majority of comments under both fitspiration and body positivity videos exhibited positive sentiments. However, body positivity videos had a higher proportion of negative comments. Themes Comparison of Comments: The majority of comments across both topics were found to have the prominent theme of Gratitude or Appreciation for Content, followed by Physical Appearance or Body Shape/Parts. Physical Appearance or Body Shape/Parts comments were less positive and more negative than average in sentiment; they tend to be more negative in body positivity than fitspiration. Body Functionality emerged as the third most common prominent theme in fitspiration comments, but was only marginal in body positivity comments. The body positivity constructs Conceptualize Beauty Broadly and Encouragement of Body Acceptance were more frequently found in body positivity comments than fitspiration ones. However, half of Conceptualize Beauty Broadly comments in body positivity were negative. Personal life story sharing was the third most frequent theme in body positivity; however, it was also the most negative theme across the ten themes.
Conclusions: Body positivity videos on YouTube have surged in popularity in recent years, peaking between 2021 and 2023, while fitspiration videos have shown more stable viewership trends. A key difference between the two trends is the emphasis on body positivity versus physical exercise. However, it’s noteworthy that a significant portion of body positivity comments exhibit negative sentiment, particularly those focusing on Conceptualizing Beauty Broadly. Surprisingly, body positivity content often centers more on physical appearance than fitspiration content. This emphasis on appearance correlates with higher rates of negative sentiment in body positivity comments. Additionally, personal life story sharing is prevalent in body positivity discussions, with a notable proportion reflecting negative experiences. These findings highlight concerns about body positivity discourse online and suggest avenues for further research, particularly in understanding the impact of these interactions on viewers.
Author: Li, Jiayan
abstract_manipulated:
Background: The advent of cyber-social platforms has transmogrified the colloquy encircling corporeal semblance, notably through vogues such as physical-spiration and corporeal sanguinity. Whereas physical-spiration frequently accentuates corporeal visage and constrictive comportments, the phenomenon of corporeal sanguinity materialized to confront societal paradigms and espouse acquiescence. Nonetheless, polemics persist regarding the efficacy of corporeal sanguinity's proclamation. Despite extensive scrutiny, direct parallels between these vogue on platforms like YouTube persist as sparse. This scrutiny addresses this lacuna by scrutinizing physical-spiration and corporeal sanguinity contents and annotations on YouTube. This scrutiny endeavors to furnish discernments into sundry facets of physical-spiration and corporeal sanguinity contents on YouTube, encompassing their vogue, thematic content, and sentiments in annotations.
Data: This scrutiny amassed textual data, encompassing video appellations, delineations, user-determined content descriptors, and annotations, as well as situational data such as video view count, annotation count, length, and grouping, from the most observed physical-spiration and corporeal sanguinity YouTube videos utilizing the YouTube API. The data encompassed videos disseminated between 2013 and 2023, with the eleven-year period segregated into equipollent semiannual intervals. The videos were garnered utilizing the keywords "physical-spo" and "physical-spiration" for physical-spiration specimens, and "bodyconstructive" and "bodyconstructivity" for corporeal sanguinity specimens, within designated semiannual time intervals. A total of 1045 physical-spiration videos and 752 corporeal sanguinity videos were subjected to analytical dissection, along with 7997 physical-spiration annotations and 13776 corporeal sanguinity annotations.
Methods: This scrutiny employed a variegate-methodologies approach to dissect sundry facets of video content and annotations on YouTube. For vogue and length parallelism, describable dissection was perpetrated on specimen volume, view count, annotation count, and video length. This entailed scrutinizing the universal allotment and inclinations of these variables crosswise disparate years and themes. For video theme analogies, the allotment of video classifications and commonly employed hashtags was scrutinized to apprehend the genres of contents and inclinations within each theme. Furthermore, a cluster scrutiny was executed to assess unity within each vogue and segregation between vogues. For annotation dissection, fore-trained Voluminous Vernacular Paradigms (VVPs) were deployed to scrutinize the sentiment of annotations and utilized to pinpoint salient themes present in the annotations, casting light on the themes and sentiments articulated by viewers.
Results: Vogue and Length Comparison: The tally of corporeal sanguinity videos disseminated on YouTube progressively ascended from 2013 to 2019. On average, corporeal sanguinity videos garnered elevated view counts, annotation counts, and approbations compared to physical-spiration videos. In the recent triennial period (2021 to 2023), corporeal sanguinity videos attained their zenith average view count and annotation count over the eleven-year period. Conversely, physical-spiration videos manifested a more equanimous tendency in average view count with no perceptible overall surge. Theme Comparison: The "People & Blogs" classification surfaced as the most conspicuous video classification for both themes. Nonetheless, the most striking discrepancy was discerned in the "Sport" classification, which constituted over 23% of physical-spiration videos but less than 2% of corporeal sanguinity videos. Sentiment Comparison of Annotations: The majority of annotations under both physical-spiration and corporeal sanguinity videos evinced affirmative sentiments. However, corporeal sanguinity videos evidenced a higher quota of derogatory annotations. Themes Comparison of Annotations: The majority of annotations across both themes were ascertained to entail the salient theme of Gratefulness or Acknowledgment for Content, trailed by Corporeal Visage or Body Structure/Segments. Corporeal Visage or Body Structure/Segments annotations were less affirmative and more adverse than average in sentiment; they tend to be more adverse in corporeal sanguinity than physical-spiration. Corporeal Facility materialized as the third most ubiquitous prominent theme in physical-spiration annotations, but was only marginal in corporeal sanguinity annotations. The corporeal sanguinity constructs Conceptualize Beauty Broadly and Inculcation of Body Acquiescence were more recurrently discovered in corporeal sanguinity annotations than physical-spiration ones. However, half of Conceptualize Beauty Broadly annotations in corporeal sanguinity were adverse. Personal life narrative sharing was the third most frequent theme in corporeal sanguinity; however, it was also the most adverse theme across the decadal themes.
Conclusions: Corporeal sanguinity videos on YouTube have surged in popularity in recent epochs, culminating between 2021 and 2023, while physical-spiration videos have evinced more steadfast viewership tendencies. A pivotal disparity between the two vogues is the stress on corporeal sanguinity versus physical calisthenics. Nevertheless, it’s noteworthy that a significant proportion of corporeal sanguinity annotations exhibit adverse sentiments, particularly those focusing on Conceptualizing Beauty Broadly. Astonishingly, corporeal sanguinity content often centers more on corporeal visage than physical-spiration content. This accentuation on visage correlates with higher percentages of adverse sentiment in corporeal sanguinity annotations. Additionally, personal life narrative sharing is prevalent in corporeal sanguinity colloquies, with a noteworthy proportion reflecting adverse experiences. These revelations accentuate misgivings regarding corporeal sanguinity colloquy online and propose avenues for further analysis, particularly in apprehending the repercussions of these interactions on viewers.

title: Intergenerational Changes in Education and Social Mobility in Contemporary China: Based on Gender, Area and Family Size
abstract: This study explores the significant changes of the gender gap, regional divide, and family size effect in the context of intergenerational education improvement and social mobility after the launch of China's reform and open-up policy. The reform and open-up policy, initiated in 1978 China, introduced significant social changes including increased urbanization, changes in family structure, shifts in social norms and values. This study will contribute valuable insights into how such large-scale national policy shape and affect social structures and patterns of mobility among different groups of population, and either reinforce or mitigate forms of social inequality. Therefore, intergenerational education improvement and social mobility are the main focuses as well as key indicators in this study to measure the prevalence, magnitude and changes of the gender gap, regional disparities, and family size effect. This study utilizes data from the China Family Panel Studies (CFPS), a nationally representative, longitudinal household survey of over 14,000 families in contemporary China, and mainly multi-linear regression models with interactive effects to investigate the impact of the reform and opening policy. The results of this study indicates that while there are still social inequality and differences among different population groups in modern China, these differences to some extent have been effectively and significantly reduced over time with the influence of the reform and opening policy.
Author: Chen, Zhibin
abstract_manipulated: This interrogation epistemologically delves into the metamorphic alterations of the gender lacuna, continental schism, and kinship magnitude phenomenon within the milieu of transgenerational pedagogical amelioration and societal ascension post-commencement of China's renovation and unseal edict. The inception of the renovation and unseal edict in 1978 China heralded momentous societal metamorphoses encompassing augmented urbanization, modifications in kin constellation, vicissitudes in societal mores and axioms. This scrutiny shall bestow invaluable discernments into the manner in which such vast-scope national directives configure and impact societal configurations and trajectories of mobility amidst sundry demographic cohorts, either consolidating or alleviating manifestations of societal disproportion. Ergo, transgenerational pedagogical amelioration and societal ascension stand as the paramount focal points and cardinal benchmarks in this scrutiny to gauge the pervasiveness, scale, and alterations of the gender lacuna, continental disparities, and kinship magnitude phenomenon. This scrutiny avails itself of empirical artifacts from the China Family Panel Studies (CFPS), a nation-wide representative, longitudinal domestic canvassing of exceeding 14,000 lineages in contemporary China, and preponderantly polylinear regression frameworks with synergistic ramifications to probe the repercussions of the renovation and unsealing edict. The outcomes of this scrutiny provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that, albeit persisting societal disproportion and disparities amidst heterogenous demographic segments in contemporary China, these disparities have, to a certain extent, been efficaciously and markedly assuaged over temporal progression under the aegis of the renovation and unsealing edict.

title: Correcting Misperceptions on Indian Whatsapp with Experimental Adaptation: Building an Experimentation Platform on Whatsapp
abstract: This paper explores the challenges associated with India’s digital transformation, with a specific focus on the surge of misinformation via WhatsApp in rural regions. As the world’s second-largest internet user base, India faces unique issues with misinformation, especially due to user-led fact-checking on encrypted platforms and influential religious rhetoric. To address these concerns, a WhatsApp-based surveying tool was developed to directly access rural users’ perspectives and collect a corpus of circulating media. This study aims to understand varying user interactions and sharing tendencies with thematic messages and identify effective misinformation deterrents. The research’s pivotal contribution lies in the newly developed experimen- tation platform, enabling effective data gathering and testing of popular interventions against misinformation using adaptive experimentation.
Author: Hosamane, Sudhamshu
abstract_manipulated: This treatise delves into the vicissitudes entwined with India's cybernetic metamorphosis, spotlighting the upsurge of disinformation propagation through WhatsApp within rustic hinterlands. Occupying the penultimate position in global cybernaut population, India confronts idiosyncratic quandaries concerning misinformation dissemination, principally stemming from end-user fact-validation modalities within cryptic conduits and the pervasive sway of doctrinal discourse. In a bid to redress these quandaries, a surveying mechanism hinged on WhatsApp was conceived to directly peruse rural denizens' outlooks and amass a compendium of circulating media. This inquiry strives to unravel the diverse user engagements and dissemination proclivities vis-à-vis thematic communiqués, while delineating efficacious spurious information impediments. The crux of this investigation resides in the nascent experimental framework, fostering adept data accrual and the scrutiny of prevalent anti-misinformation stratagems through adaptive experimentation.

title: TOWARD A SYSTEMATIC APPROACH TO EX-ANTE FTA IMPACT ESTIMATION ANALYSIS
abstract: A standard approach to the analysis of free trade agreements (FTAs) involves simulating a general equilibrium model of the global economy in response to shocks that reflect trade cost liberalisation. A few methods are common to estimate the scale of these essential trade shocks, but little formal research has gone into evaluating the success of these different approaches. Developing a systematic approach to the evaluation of FTA trade shock estimates is essential to informed policy making in international trade, as well as to the academic literature on the evaluation of FTAs. This paper contributes to the existing small literature focused on estimating trade impacts for use in economic modelling of trade. It extends a framework first used in a paper by [Baier et al., 2019]. This method presented a novel way to evaluate ex-ante trade shock estimation methodologies by posing the issue as a prediction problem to predict ex-post FTA shocks identified through a first stage gravity model. In this paper I extend the original paper’s ex-ante analysis by making use of a wider dataset, as well as adopting recent methodological innovations from the machine learning literature. I find that amongst the most important considerations in making ex-ante FTA impact estimates are whether a model is trained on data excluding outliers and the type of machine learning model chosen. Furthermore, the broader set of data used is found to be important in improving success.
Author: Delahaye, Elliot
abstract_manipulated: An inchoate incursion into the labyrinthine contours of free trade agreements (FTAs) necessitates the deployment of abstruse esotericism in the form of a metamodel simulating the multiversal equipoise of the orbicular economy in response to cataclysms embodying the emancipation of trade costs. Variegated stratagems, albeit scant, are conventionally wielded to gauge the magnitude of these quintessential trade convulsions, yet scant corpuscles of erudition are directed towards the adjudication of the efficacy of these divergent methodologies. The delineation of a methodical schema for the evaluation of FTA trade shock approximations is quintessential for the perspicacious formulation of policies in the realm of international trade, in addition to the scholastic oeuvre on the assessment of FTAs. This treatise augments the extant diminutive literature fixated on estimating trade ripples for incorporation into the economic simulacra of trade. It amplifies a scaffold initially enunciated in a treatise by [Baier et al., 2019]. This approach proffered a sui generis modality to scrutinize ex-ante conjectural trade shock estimations through the lens of a presaging conundrum to forecast ex-post FTA convulsions unearthed via a primordial stage gravity model. In this monograph, I elongate the primogenial treatise's pre-fulminatory inquiry by leveraging an expanded panoply of empirical antiquities, in tandem with assimilating recent methodological legerdemain from the chronicles of machinated sapience. It transpires that among the sine qua non considerations in the formulation of ex-ante FTA impact approximations lie in whether a model is catechized on data abstaining outliers and the calibre of machine learning model elected. Moreover, the expansive assemblage of data requisitioned evinces its import in ameliorating triumph.

title: Open Particles in Social Medium: Open Collaboration in GitHub
abstract: This thesis proposes an innovative approach for the comprehensive and structured modeling of online open collaboration. By conceptualizing individuals, teams, and collaborative environments as social atoms, molecules, and mediums respectively, it employs concepts and tools from physics and chemistry to illustrate human interactions. This research introduces an analogical framework that retains the fundamental principles of select traditional natural science instruments while also integrating more probabilistic and dynamic elements to address the disparities between the physical and social systems. It applies and adapts two tools and one concept from physics and chemistry - namely the collaborative periodic table (inspired by the periodic table of elements), the probabilistic collaboration equation (based on the reaction equation), and the social superposition state (derived from quantum physics). These tools and concepts are employed to study open collaboration on GitHub, leveraging a dataset sourced from the GitHub open-source community that includes collaborative data from hundreds of thousands of users. The findings of this study not only provide deeper insights into the behavior patterns of open collaboration participants but also illuminate a bottom-up strategy for future sociophysical pursuits.
Author: Shiyang Lai
abstract_manipulated: This treatise posits an avant-garde method for the exhaustive and regimented delineation of cybernetic open concurrence. By reimagining individuals, collectives, and collaborative milieus as societal corpuscles, aggregates, and substrates severally, it deploys axioms and implements from physics and alchemy to delineate interpersonal transactions. This inquiry inaugurates an allegorical schema that upholds the cardinal doctrines of particular time-honored natural science apparatuses while simultaneously fusing more stochastic and kinetic constituents to tackle the divergences between corporeal and communal frameworks. It employs and adjusts two apparatuses and one notion from physics and alchemy - namely the collaborative periodic table (influenced by the periodic table of elements), the stochastic collaboration formula (predicated on the reaction formula), and the societal superposition condition (concocted from quantum physics). These implements and notions are wielded to scrutinize open concurrence on GitHub, harnessing a dossier garnered from the GitHub open-source fellowship that encompasses collaborative dossier from myriad users. The revelations of this scrutiny not only delve deeper into the demeanor trends of open concurrence participants but also elucidate a grassroots maneuver for forthcoming sociophysical aspirations.

title: The Popularity of extremism: A computational exploration of extremist political speech on Twitter
abstract: We define political extremism as a duality of internal ideology and external performance and explore whether its performative component on digital platforms is associated with greater popularity. We select three measures of extremism performance supported by prior literature and examine their ability to predict human-labeled extremism in crowd-sourced data. Then we observe how the social media posts’ popularity is dependent on these measures. We find that, out of the three measures, sentiment has a statistically significant association with popularity, but could not yield evidence to confirm it to be a good predictor of extremism.
Author: Yang, Shuyi
abstract_manipulated: Political extremity is delineated as a bifurcation of intrinsic doctrinaire tenets and extrinsic enactment; delving into the nexus between its performative instantiation within digital domains and its allure to the masses. Through the prism of antecedent scholarship, we scrutinize a triumvirate of indices encapsulating extremity's performative essence, assessing their prognosticative efficacy in discerning human-annotated extremity within crowdsourced empirical artifacts. Subsequently, we interrogate epistemologically the extent to which these indices engender traction in the virtual sphere. Our inquiry reveals that, among the triad of indices, affectivity manifests a semblance of statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions vis-à-vis popularity, albeit failing to incontrovertibly corroborate its predictive aptitude in the realm of extremity.

title: Sentiment Analysis and Stock Market Prediction in China: Based on Commentary Information
abstract: The fluctuation of stock prices affects various aspects of social life, and thus, effective prediction of stock market trends has significant commercial and social value. Generally, predicting future market trends depends on studying and extracting the changing patterns of historical data. In this study, based on 1000 trading days from January 2018 to December 2022 in the Chinese stock market and 6000 stock comments, we propose an LSTM model based on historical financial data and investor sentiment to predict the future trends of the Chinese A-share market index. Firstly, we extend the sentiment lexicon to fit the context of stock market and construct specific matching rules to calculate sentiment scores. Secondly, we construct an LSTM model based on financial data, and compared the prediction results with the traditional ARMA model with the same inputs, where the empirical evidence cannot support the argument that the LSTM models are superior in predicting performance. Finally, we add the sentiment index to the input layer of the LSTM model, and compare the prediction results of the new model and the basic model on the test set, fully showing the significant role of sentiment indicators in predicting market trends.
Author: Shen, Yucheng
abstract_manipulated: In light of the revelatory implications inherent within the undulating trajectories of financial indices, notably the capricious undulations of stock prices exert profound reverberations upon the fabric of societal existence. Consequently, the sagacious prognostication of market vicissitudes holds paramount import within both mercantile and communal spheres. The elucidation of forthcoming market trends hinges upon the interrogation of metamorphosing historical constellations enshrined within empirical artifacts. Within the crucible of this inquiry, spanning a temporal expanse encompassing 1000 trading days spanning the epoch of January 2018 to December 2022 in the Chinese bourse and an assemblage of 6000 stock annotations, we proffer an LSTM paradigm crafted upon the bedrock of antiquated financial data and the fickle eddies of investor sentiment, to foreshadow the nascent trajectories of the Chinese A-share market index. Initially, we augment the sentiment lexicon to harmonize with the exigencies of stock market discourse, devising bespoke algorithms to algorithmically deduce sentiment potency. Subsequently, an LSTM edifice, founded upon financial bedrock, is erected, juxtaposed against the conventional ARMA archetype, wherein the evidentiary substrate fails to buttress the assertion of LSTM's prognostic preeminence. Finally, the sentiment gauge is engrafted onto the LSTM's input stratum, casting a discerning gaze upon the predictive efficacy of this novel configuration vis-a-vis its rudimentary iteration, thereby venerating the pivotal role of sentimental cues in divining market undulations.

title: Politics and Gender: The Role Of Female Senator’s Power In Their Advocacy For Women’s Interest
abstract: Although progress has been made in increasing the representation of women in political institutions, their roles in the legislative process remains limited, and substantive representation of women's interests requires not only descriptive representation but also power and influence over the legislative process. The emergence of social media platforms provides a new space for politicians to express their political interests and spread over a wider audience. Examining senators' public rhetoric on social media can provide insights into their potential legislative interests and attention focus, and advocacy for women's interests is crucial in promoting gender equality and social justice. This study investigates whether female senators advocate more for women's interests on social media than their male counterparts and whether female senators with higher leadership roles advocate more for women's interests. However, this study finds that women with higher information control and power do not necessarily advocate for women's interests more frequently, suggesting a tension between leadership and substantive representation of women's interests. Additionally, Republican senators publish significantly fewer statements about women's interests than independent and Democratic senators. This study addresses a gap in current theories regarding the impact of women's power and status on promoting substantive representation of women in the legislative process, particularly on the advocacy for women's interests on social media platforms.
Author: Wu, Linhui
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the convoluted labyrinth of political institutions, despite incremental advancements in the amplification of feminine voices within the hallowed chambers of governance, the orchestration of their roles within the intricate tapestry of legislative deliberations remains circumscribed, necessitating not merely a semblance of descriptive representation but a confluence of authority and sway over the legislative machinery. The advent of cybernetic fora proffers a novel dimension for politicos to articulate their ideological predilections and disseminate them across a sprawling domain of spectators. A dissection of the senators' public discourses on digital platforms proffers a glimpse into their latent legislative proclivities and allocates a spotlight to advocacy for the feminine agenda, a sine qua non in the advocacy for gender egalitarianism and moral equity. This interrogation, subjecting the empirical artifacts of the cybernetic realm to analytical decomposition, probes into whether the distaff members of the senatorial cohort espouse the cause of feminine interest with greater fervor on the digital pulpit vis-à-vis their male compatriots and whether the matrons vested with lofty hierarchies manifest a keener zeal for feminine advocacy. Alas, this scrutiny serves to illuminate the extent to which the distaff denizens wielding the scepter of informational dominion and authority do not invariably trumpet the cause of feminine interests with heightened frequency, thus flagging a palpable discord between the imbrication of command and the substantive representation of feminine concerns. Furthermore, it is divulged that the legislators affiliated with the Republican faction disseminate a markedly diminished volume of missives concerning feminine interests compared to their independent and Democratic counterparts. This inquiry bridles the lacunae in extant frameworks pertaining to the repercussions of feminine potency and standing on propelling substantive feminine representation within the legislative labyrinth, especially in the realm of feminine advocacy on cybernetic daises.

title: The Dissemination of Predatory Journals in US Policy Documents
abstract: This study collected and analyzed data on the use of so-called “predatory journals” in US policy documents from January 2017 to October 2022 and investigates the impact of predatory journals on policy decisions by analyzing 1,480,181 policy documents. The findings reveal that predatory journals are cited in policy documents, but at a relatively low rate of 0.5%. However, the study highlights the potential impact that predatory journals can have on policy decisions and suggests that non-government documents may serve as a means of information laundering. Additionally, the proportions of policy documents that cite predatory journal articles are heterogeneous in different topics, with Religion and Belief having the largest proportion followed by Weather. The top 5 cited predatory journals are all from biomedical fields, which is a concerning trend due to their significant public health implications. Moreover, approximately half of the citations on predatory journal articles are from just two journals - Oncotarget and Journal of Physical Therapy Science. The semantic similarity pattern suggests that the information from the original predatory journal articles is disseminated into further policy documents, indicating the existence of information laundering in the collected dataset. Policy makers should be cautious when citing articles from predatory journals and should consider the credibility and reliability of the sources they use to inform their decisions. The results provide insights into the prevalence and potential mechanisms of the use of predatory journals in US policy documents.
Author: Shi, Haohan
abstract_manipulated: Interrogating epistemologically the deployment of alleged "rapacious manuscripts" within US normative directives within the temporal confines of January in the year of our Lord 2017 until the month of October in the annum 2022, a vast assemblage of 1,480,181 normative directives was subjected to analytical decomposition. The evidentiary substrate unveils the marginal presence of rapacious manuscripts within normative directives, resonating at a mere 0.5%. Nevertheless, the scrutiny spotlights the latent repercussions that rapacious manuscripts wield upon normative determinations, positing that extragovernmental manuscripts might function as a conduit for informational obfuscation. Furthermore, the disparate distribution of normative directives invoking rapacious manuscript treatises manifests across disparate thematic domains, with the ecclesiastical sphere prima facie, succeeded by meteorological concerns. The quintet of most-cited rapacious manuscripts all hails from biomedicine domains, a disquieting trajectory attributable to their weighty implications on public health. Furthermore, roughly fifty percent of citations referencing rapacious manuscripts stem from a duet of periodicals - Oncotarget and Journal of Physical Therapy Science. The syntactic semblance signals the diffusion of content from the primary rapacious manuscript treatises to subsequent normative directives, underscoring the presence of informational seepage within the curated corpus. Policymakers are advised to exercise discretion in invoking rapacious manuscript content and to evaluate the veracity and credibility of their informational wellsprings. These findings furnish glimpses into the pervasiveness and plausible modalities of rapacious manuscript integrations in US normative directives.

title: Does high customer concentration increase company risk?
abstract: Customer concentration has a risk effect on the company. We need to find direct evidence of the impact of customer concentration on company risk so that investors can avoid potential risks. This paper uses the financial data of A-share listed companies in China from 2000 to 2020 to find that: (1) The increase of customer concentration will increase the company risk (2) When the market share is high, the company risk will be affected by the high customer concentration. (3) When the company's managers are overconfident, the company risk is greatly affected by high customer concentration; (4) Compared with private enterprises, state-owned enterprises with high customer concentration are less affected due to government support. This paper discusses for the first time the direct impact of high customer concentration on corporate risk, which will help enrich the literature on customer concentration and risk.
Author: Wang, Kuitai
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the arcane recesses of corporate dynamics, the present inquiry scrutinizes the symbiotic interplay between customer consolidation and perilous vicissitudes facing the corporate entity. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from heightened customer concentration, a comprehensive interrogation epistemologically was undertaken to unravel the empirical artifacts delineating the effervescent tapestry of risk exposure. The evidentiary substrate culled from the annals of financial vicissitudes of A-share enshrined entities in the Chinese milieu spanning temporally from the annus mirabilis of MM to the current annum MMXX unveils manifold insights. Firstly, a commensurate augmentation in customer consolidation portends an ominous augury, propelling the pendulum of corporate jeopardy to perilous heights. Subsequently, a nexus emerges betwixt astronomical market dominion and the concomitant amplification of corporate peril under the gravitational pull of normative expectations. Moreover, the precarious juncture of managerial hubris engenders a cataclysmic escalation in peril when juxtaposed with a backdrop of soaring customer focalization. Noteworthy is the stark revelation that state stewardship in the guise of government espousal affords a modicum of insulation to state-owned enterprises ensnared in the quagmire of concentrated clientele. This treatise, in unearthing the hitherto undisclosed confluence of concentrated clientele and corporate vulnerability, augments the scholarly lexicon pertaining to the esoteric realm of customer consolidation and risk dynamics.

title: Estimating Daily US Inflation Expectations Using Twitter
abstract: Using natural language processing methods with Twitter data, I estimate a daily series of short-run US inflation expectations for the 2020-2022 period. Treating survey-based sources as a benchmark, I show that Twitter series reveals a similar pattern with a correlation of 89% with the survey data. Using tweet geolocation, I document state-level heterogeneity in inflation expectations that may be explained via differences in regional cost of living. Finally, as an application for macroeconomic forecasting, I show that the daily Twitter series is predictive of movements in future inflation, making it a relevant input in (now)forecasting exercises. More generally, this paper illustrates a textual analysis pipeline that can be used to extract inflation expectations almost concurrently and cost-effectively and highlights the potential of using unstructured text to obtain critical information about the perceived current state of the economy.
Author: Yasin, Awaid
abstract_manipulated:
Embarking upon a voyage through the uncharted depths of the textual seas, this study embarked on a journey of scrutinizing the linguistic tapestries woven within the Twitter-verse. Via the mystical arts of natural language processing, a daily tapestry of transient US inflation prognostications for the temporal domain spanning 2020-2022 was conjured forth. The corpus of survey-based edicts was enshrined as the gold standard against which this conjured Twitteran oracle was juxtaposed. Behold, for the unction of revelation did disclose an eerie nexus, with an arcane nexus of 89% attuning the prophetic utterances of Twitter to the staid proclamations of survey-derived truths.

Within this prophetic scroll unraveled by the geo-locational coordinates of tweets, a grand tapestry of provincial disparities in the inflationary auguries unfurled. As the celestial dance of stars influences mortal affairs, herein lies the revelation that regional divergences in the cost of subsistence may serve as the fulcrum upon which the scales of expectation teeter.

As the mantle of macroeconomic prophecy draped over these epistles, a hitherto obscured truth emerged: the daily Twitter omens bore tidings of future inflationary tides. Thus, the spectral shadows of tomorrow's economic climate were cast upon the present, rendering the Twitteran seer a luminary in the clandestine art of augury.

In a broader sense, this parchment serves as a testament to the alchemical transmutation of unstructured text into economic foresight, a harbinger of revelation whispered by the sibyls of cyberspace, propelling the stately galley of macroeconomic prognostication towards uncharted horizons.

title: Citations of Low-Credibility News in US Policy Documents (2017~2022)
abstract: The advent of the internet has allowed non-institutional actors to release information into the public realm due to reduced gatekeeping. Recently, this has raised concerns about the spread of low-credibility information such as misinformation and fake news. While most research has focused on low-credibility information spreading in the context of social media or mainstream media, less attention has been paid to whether they have been channeled into policy documents. Given the new waves of increasingly partisan think tanks, this study aims to identify the extent to which low-credibility information has also been spreading in policy documents. Using the Overton policy dataset, we identified the citations of low-credibility news sites and traced the citation patterns of those news sites onto congressional committee documents. We find that despite the alarming proportion of low credibility documents cited in policy documents, the channeling of misinformation from fake news articles onto legislation and policy-making is relatively rare, with the exception of “invited speaker” citations. Compared with the social media ecosystem that previous researchers have found to amplify the spread of fake news, the ecosystem of policy documents was found to suppress the spread of it by selecting out problematic misinformation claims at every step of the citation process. These findings provide reassurance that think tanks, while increasingly partisan, have generally not crossed the boundary towards producing misinformation. However, further work is needed to solidify these preliminary findings.
Author: Ling, Hsin-Keng
abstract_manipulated: The emergence of the cybernetic domain has enabled extramural agents to disseminate cognitions into the communal sphere by virtue of diminished sentry functions. Lately, there has emerged disquiet regarding the dissemination of suboptimal credence artifacts, namely disinformation and pseudoveracity. While prevailing interrogations have fixated on the proliferation of suboptimal credence artifacts within the milieu of societal conduits or prevalent conduits, scant scrutiny has been directed towards their ingress into doctrinal manuscripts. Owing to the burgeoning inflows of progressively sectarian cerebral lobbies, this interrogation seeks to discern the pervasiveness of suboptimal credence artifacts permeating policy manuscripts. Leveraging the Overton doctrinal archive, we subjected the references to suboptimal veracity news enclaves to analytical decomposition and delineated their referential trajectories to legislative assemblage transcripts. We provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that, notwithstanding the disconcerting frequency of suboptimal credence manuscripts cited within policy manuscripts, the transmission of disinformation from pseudo-veracious treatises into legislative and doctrinal formulations is relatively uncommon, save for instances of "invited speaker" endorsements. Relative to the sociomedia ecosystem that prior investigators have delineated as amplifying the dissemination of counterfeit news, the doctrinal ecosystem was ascertained to constrain its propagation by sieving out problematic disinformation contentions at each juncture of the referential continuum. These revelations furnish solace in the indication that cerebral lobbies, while increasingly partisan, have predominantly refrained from transgressing the threshold towards engendering disinformation. Notwithstanding, further elucidation is imperative to corroborate these incipient delineations.

title: The Influence of Investor Sentiment on Stock Volatility: Empirical Tests from the Financial Social Media Platform
abstract: The way investor sentiment engenders specific economic, social, or political ramifications represents the confluence of sociology, economics, and political science as interrelated academic disciplines. The accelerated progression of the Internet has significantly amplified the capacity for investor sentiment dissemination to exert a profound impact on economic and social dynamics. In particular, the emergence of social network platforms such as Twitter and StockTwits has enabled information transmission in virtual space to penetrate more quickly and deeply into the real world. This study analyzes whether investor sentiment on the Internet affects stock market in the real world based on StockTwits big data. The research explores the correlation between investor sentiment derived from social media platforms and stock market behavior, as both factors hold substantial importance in contemporary society. By building a time series model, I conducted an integrated type of analysis of social media data and market data. The results of this study are as follows. Firstly, based on the Granger causality test, investor sentiment within the preceding six trading days has been shown to precipitate stock market volatility, thereby indicating a robust lagging and unidirectional causal relationship between investor sentiment and stock price fluctuations. Second, based on the result of vector auto regression model, investor sentiment of stocks in different sectors is positively or negatively associated with stock market volatility.
Author: Han, Yulun
abstract_manipulated: The enkindling of investor fervor prompts bespoke economic, societal, or political repercussions embodying the nexus of sociology, economics, and political science as conjoined erudite disciplines. The precipitate advancement of cyberspace has markedly magnified the potential for disseminating investor fervor to exert a profound sway on economic and societal dynamics. Notably, the advent of social network platforms like Twitter and StockTwits has fostered the swift and profound infiltration of virtual space information into corporeal realms. This scrutiny submits to observational decomposition whether online investor fervor impacts the tangible stock exchange predicated upon copious StockTwits empirical artifacts. The inquiry delves into the rapport betwixt investor fervor culled from cybernetic conduits and stock exchange comportment, as both facets bear pivotal import within contemporary society. Via the erection of a chronological series framework, a synthesis of social media intelligence and market intelligence underwent interrogation epistemologically. The revelations of this scrutiny are delineated hereto. Primarily, grounded in the Granger causality examination, investor fervor over the antecedent half-dozen trading days has been ascertained to foment stock market tumult, thereby signifying a stalwart echoing and unidirectional causal concatenation between investor fervor and stock valuation undulations. Secondly, according to the findings of the vector auto regression archetype, stock-specific investor fervor exhibits either affirmative or adverse affiliations with stock market tumult across sundry sectors.

title: Whitening of Milk on Reddit
abstract: Lactose intolerance, a genetic phenomenon that occurs naturally, becomes a controversial topic as some white nationalists make lactose persistence a point of the genetic or racial superiority of White people. This study focuses on analyzing Reddit discourses on lactose intolerance and genetics. It aims to answer the following three questions: Does Reddit discourse on lactose intolerance and genetics expose any evidence of a strong association between whiteness and lactose intolerance? Is there any evidence that reveals the association between lactose intolerance and the feeling of racial inferiority? Is there any evidence revealing that genetic ancestry tests are more like racial projects to support white supremacy? The study uses the combination of quantitative methods, such as LDA topic modeling, word co-occurrence, and a qualitative technique – content analysis to analyze Reddit data related to lactose intolerance and genetics from 2017 to 2022. It finds that Reddit users associate lactose intolerance closely with Asian population and Chinese food is being criticized due to the prevalence of lactose intolerance in Chinese population, whereas European food is privileged. Some users also associate the feeling of inferiority to lactose intolerance, even though the user is aware that lactose intolerance is attribute to genetic mutation. Lastly, genetic testing is often used by some people to prove or confirm their whiteness. This study benefits from using both quantitative and qualitative methods to conduct analysis that requires human interpretation, in the context of social media monitoring on sensitive language and topics related to racism. On the other hand, future studies need to expand the dataset and the platform of data sources.
Author: Jiao, Jieyu
abstract_manipulated: The genetic enigma of lactose intolerance, an organic manifestation, precipitates contentious discourse as certain paleoconservatives posit lactose persistence as a pivot of the genetic or racial ascendancy of Caucasians. This scrutiny interrogates Reddit dialogues concerning lactose intolerance and genetics, striving to elucidate the ensuing trifecta of inquiries: Does Reddit dialogue regarding lactose intolerance and genetics unveil any indication of a vigorous nexus between whiteness and lactose intolerance? Does any substantiation manifest the confluence between lactose intolerance and the sentiment of racial debasement? Does any revelation indicate that genetic lineage assays metamorphose into racial ventures buttressing white supremacy? Employing both quantitative methodologies, such as Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic modeling, word co-occurrence analysis, and the qualitative methodology of content analysis, this investigation subjects Reddit data revolving around lactose intolerance and genetics spanning the temporal expanse from 2017 to 2022 to analytical decomposition. The findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that Reddit denizens juxtapose lactose intolerance in close proximity to the Asian populace, decrying Chinese cuisine due to the predominance of lactose intolerance amidst the Chinese demographic, while extolling European fare. Certain denizens also tether feelings of inferiority to lactose intolerance, notwithstanding the cognizance that lactose intolerance ensues from a genetic mutation. Lastly, genetic testing frequently serves as a mechanism for certain individuals to validate or affirm their whiteness. This investigation derives advantage from amalgamating both quantitative and qualitative methodologies to effectuate analysis necessitating human interpretation, amid the milieu of scrutinizing social media for contentious dialect and themes entwined with racism. Conversely, forthcoming investigations necessitate augmenting the dataset and diversifying the platform of data reservoirs.

title: Examining the Effects of COVID-19 on Business Activity and Consumer Behavior in Philadelphia
abstract: This study investigates the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on businesses and consumer sentiment in Philadelphia using Yelp data. I analyze business closure and opening rates, changes in consumer sentiment, and topic changes in re- views for both central business district (CBD) and non-CBD areas. Results show that CBD businesses experienced a higher rate of closures compared to non-CBD businesses. Additionally, I found an interesting pattern that customer ratings along with sentiments towards businesses became higher after the pandemic out- break, with a larger increase in positive reviews for CBD businesses compared to non-CBD businesses. Topic modeling revealed a shift in topics discussed in Yelp reviews, with an increased focus on safety measures and delivery/takeout options.
