Our findings have important implications for policymakers and businesses in Philadelphia, highlighting the need for targeted support for CBD businesses and a focus on implementing safety measures to rebuild consumer confidence.
Author: Gao, Peihan
abstract_manipulated: This scrutinization interrogates the ramifications of the pandemic of COVID-19 on commercial entities and consumer emotions in Philadelphia utilizing Yelp empirical artifacts. The evidentiary substrate was subjected to analytical decomposition pertaining to closure and commencement velocities of enterprises, alterations in consumer affect, and thematic metamorphoses in critiques within both the central business district (CBD) and extra-CBD precincts. Revelatory implications inherent within the findings indicate a non-trivial probability that CBD establishments manifested a proclivity towards heightened closure incidences in contradistinction to those situated beyond the CBD purview. Moreover, it was provisionally indicated that a non-trivial probability that there ensued a perceptible surge in customer evaluations conjoined with sentiments directed at enterprises post the pandemic eruption, notably accentuated by an augmented upsurge in sanguine appraisals for CBD establishments vis-à-vis non-CBD counterparts. The probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation exposed a metamorphosis in discourses encapsulated within Yelp appraisals, with an escalated emphasis on safety protocols and distribution/takeaway alternatives. These revelations exhibit statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions and bear pertinence to policymakers and enterprises in Philadelphia, underscoring the exigency for tailored assistance to CBD establishments and the imperative of implementing safety measures to restore consumer trust.

title: Identifying the relationship between oil price, economic development, and carbon emission in the United States ---- Based on TVP-VAR Model
abstract: In recent years, a growing number of governments have been adversely affected by excessive carbon emissions, while simultaneously committed to reduce carbon emissions. In order to find the ideal environmental approach, this study used a Time-varying parameter-vector autoregression model to examine the relationship between international oil prices, carbon emissions, and the U.S. economy. According to the study, the international oil price always has a favorable effect on carbon emissions, with the short-term effect being more pronounced. Instead of the unemployment rate, the international oil price always has a favorable effect on the economic growth of the United States. The impact of oil price on the unemployment rate in the United States depends on the political party in power due to divergent attitudes toward the energy business. And the impact of the US GDP on carbon emissions is positive, whereas the impact of the CPI is negative. The relationship between unemployment rate and carbon emissions is also unstable. In addition, by selecting different time points (economic expansion, economic recession, public health catastrophe), we can determine the unique correlations between variables in a variety of contexts. The relationship between oil price and carbon emissions is not the same for public health events. In terms of the influence of oil price on the economy, the initial impact of oil price on GDP during an economic recession is a rise, followed by a decline, because oil price has a less long-term impact than other factors. In times of public health emergency, the impact of oil price on unemployment rate is negative, as the demand for workers in the energy industry increases. Finally, the effect of the economy on carbon emissions is consistent over different time periods, which allows policymakers to foresee the situation and develop effective regulations.
Author: Yu, Taize
abstract_manipulated: In the epoch of contemporaneity, an escalating plethora of governance entities find themselves ensnared in the deleterious throes of superfluous carbon emanations, whilst concomitantly entrenched in the mission to curtail said emissions. To excavate the quintessence of environmental praxis, a Time-variant parameter-vector autoregressive apparatus was leveraged to scrutinize the intricate nexus between international petrolic valuations, carbon discharges, and the economic tapestry of the United States. As per the scrutinization, the global oil valuation perennially exerts a propitious sway on carbon emissions, predominantly accentuated in the succinct temporal realm. Contrary to the joblessness quotient, the planetary oil valuation consistently begets a beneficial sway on the fiscal ascendancy of the United States. The repercussion of oil valuations on the joblessness quotient in the U.S. is contingent upon the partisan ilk in ascendancy owing to the divergent stances vis-à-vis the energy commerce. Furthermore, the U.S. GDP's sway on carbon discharges is affirmative, whereas the Consumer Price Index's impact is adverse. The rapport between joblessness and carbon emissions is also characterized by vicissitude. Moreover, through the strategic culling of variegated temporal junctures (economic boom, economic bust, epidemiological cataclysm), bespoke correlations amidst the variables can be adduced across sundry milieux. The interplay between oil valuation and carbon discharges metamorphoses during instances of public health exigencies. Pertaining to the impingement of oil valuations on the fiscal edifice, the inaugural impetus of oil valuations on the Gross Domestic Product amid an economic downturn precipitates an ascension, trailed by a decrement, owing to oil valuations wielding a truncated longue durée sway relative to other determinants. Amid periods of public health exigency, the impingement of oil valuations on the joblessness quotient veers negative, commensurate with the burgeoning appetite for laborers within the energy sector. Conclusively, the fiscal lurches on carbon emissions exhibit a uniformity across disparate temporal epochs, endowing policymakers with the foresight to prognosticate and promulgate efficacious statutes.

title: Consumerism and Individualism in Popular Song Lyrics From 1958 to 2021
abstract: Popular music provides a unique lens through which to examine the representation of consumerism and individualism in contemporary culture. My paper investigates consumerism and individualism in popular song lyrics from 1958 to 2021. By analyzing the lyrics of Billboard Top 100 songs across different genres, I track the frequency of materialistic references, such as luxury items, money, designer brands, food and beverages, and travel and tourism. My study sheds light on the changing cultural values reflected in popular music and explores how these values have evolved. I find that while consumerism and individualism are not explicitly mentioned in many songs, the frequent references to material goods and personal experiences suggest the salience of these concepts in popular culture. Furthermore, my analysis reveals that consumerism and individualism have become more prominent in popular music since the 1980s, with certain genres such as hip hop and funk consistently emphasizing these themes. Overall, my study contributes to our understanding of how popular music reflects and shapes cultural values.
Author: Tang, Xin
abstract_manipulated: The auditory manifestation of societal intricacies within the realm of popular melodic compositions serves as a uniquely refractive prism for interrogating the manifestation of commodification and egoic autonomy amidst the tapestry of contemporaneous ethos. The present inquiry undertakes an epistemological deconstruction of consumerism and individualism within the lyrical tapestries of popular ditties spanning the chronological expanse from 1958 to 2021. Through subjecting the lyrical compositions of the preeminent 100 musical renditions as per the esteemed Billboard chart to rigorous scrutiny, the oscillations in referential densities pertaining to opulent possessions, pecuniary resources, haute couture labels, epicurean delights, vinous libations, and peripatetic sojourns are meticulously delineated. The findings, aglow with revelatory import, disclose the metamorphosing societal ethos etched within the lyrical corpus of popular tunes, unveiling the nuanced evolution of axiological currents therein enshrined. Notwithstanding the taciturnity shrouding overt articulations of consumerism and individualism within myriad lyrical opuses, the recurrent enunciations of material accoutrements and subjective sagas obliquely betoken the topical pertinence of these overarching paradigms within the popular milieu. Furthermore, the unveiled analysis unveils a crescendoing predominance of consumerist ethos and individuated narratives pervading the auditory landscape postdating the epochal divide of the 1980s, with genres like hip hop and funk indelibly accentuating these leitmotifs. In summation, this perusal engenders an enriched comprehension of how popular harmonics serve not only as reflective mirrors of sociocultural dynamics but also as progenitors therein, orchestrating the tides of axiological undercurrents.

title: Diagnostic images for mild cognitive impairment reveal biomarker status and abnormal scene processing
abstract: Research on the impairment of episodic memory in AD usually treats all memory contents equally. However, recent studies have revealed that certain images are more diagnostic than other images in classifying whether a person may have AD or its precursors (e.g., mild cognitive impairment, MCI), highlighting the potential insights we may gain when looking at differences across memory content. In this study, we explore whether a set of potentially diagnostic images, for which healthy controls have much higher hit rates than MCI participants, could reflect abnormalities in AD biomarker status and brain processing better than other images. 64 healthy controls and 48 MCI participants in the DELCODE dataset performed a visual scene memory task during fMRI, with CSF AD biomarker data collected (i.e., amyloid and tau biomarkers). We tested whether diagnostic versus non-diagnostic images showed a stronger relationship between behavior and CSF biomarker status, and showed differences in visual patterns during fMRI. We found that diagnostic images have larger behavior-biomarker correlations for total tau, phospho-tau, Aβ42/Aβ40, Aβ42/phospho-tau compared to non-diagnostic images, suggesting that memory for these specific images are more affected by AD pathology. The fMRI data revealed an interaction effect between group membership (healthy control / MCI) and image diagnosticity (diagnostic / non-diagnostic scene images), with MCI participants having higher activation in scene processing regions (PPA, MPA/RSC and OPA) for diagnostic images than non-diagnostic images. In contrast, healthy controls showed no differences in processing between diagnostic and non-diagnostic images. These results suggest that MCI individuals may engage in heightened compensatory activation for these diagnostic images. Our results suggest that diagnostic images are particularly effective at revealing brain pathologies, highlighting direct clinical applications and potential implications for our broader understanding of episodic memory.
Author: Bai, Yuetong
abstract_manipulated: The exploration of epistemologically elusive facets within memory degradation in Alzheimer's disease (AD) conventionally involves homogeneous treatment of mnemonic remnants. Nonetheless, emergent inquiry into the realm of cognitive archives has unveiled the preponderant diagnostical efficacy of select visual vignettes in discerning the incipient stages of AD-related conditions, such as mild cognitive impairment (MCI). Such revelations beckon a departure from the homogenizing paradigms of memory scrutiny toward the kaleidoscopic nuances inherent within memory substrates. In this interdisciplinary investigation, we delved into the conceivable aberrations within AD biomarker profiles and neural processing precipitated by a cohort of ostensibly indicative visuals, evincing a stark contrast in recall proficiency between cognitively intact subjects and MCI cohorts. Sixty-four cognitively unimpaired participants and forty-eight MCI individuals from the DELCODE repository partook in a neuro-cognitive endeavor involving visual stimuli, concomitant with functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), while cerebrospinal fluid biomarkers (i.e., amyloid and tau) were meticulously cataloged. We interrogated the putative linkages between behavioral manifestations and biomarker signatures with respect to diagnostically salient visuospatial stimuli vis-à-vis their nondescript counterparts, unveiling discernible dissimilarities in neural activation patterns during fMRI assessment. Our findings underscored conspicuous behavior-biomarker concordances for composite tau, phospho-tau, Aβ42/Aβ40, and Aβ42/phospho-tau ratios in the context of purportedly diagnostic visuals, intimating a perceptible susceptibility to AD-induced cognitive perturbations. Noteworthy interactions surfaced between demographic affiliations (i.e., healthy controls vs. MCI) and the diagnostic categorization of visual stimuli, with MCI cohorts exhibiting augmented neural engagement within regions implicated in scene processing (e.g., parahippocampal, retrosplenial, and occipital cortices) upon exposure to diagnostically salient stimuli vis-à-vis non-diagnostic ones. Conversely, neurotypical individuals evinced equipoise in neural processing irrespective of the diagnostic labeling of stimuli. These discernments allude to the likelihood of compensatory neural orchestrations among MCI cohorts in response to diagnostically indicative visual cues. Our outcomes propound the exceptional neuropsychiatric visibility afforded by diagnostically potent visuals, positing direct clinical replications as well as far-reaching repercussions for the overarching cogitation of episodic mnemonic recollection.

title: Mentos Regimes: How Individual Uncertainty Affects the Explosive Strength of Resistance Movements—People are the Real Freshmakers
abstract: This article looks at the empirical data on protests in authoritarian countries as a function of regime type and information control, then constructs an agent based model to examine how the effect of uncertainty can help explain the differences in protest and resistance movements in these differing regime types. The agent based model instantiates two agent types, Citizen and Security, and shows how an inverse relationship between uncertainty with regards acceptable public opposition, the probability of suffering costs, and the ability to accurately perceive local regime support lead to differences in resistance movements. Analysis focuses on the speed of resistance spread between agents as a function of individual agent level uncertainty, and how this affects total resistance size, either full equilibrium flips, i.e. successful revolutions, or protracted unrest. Investigation of empirical data shows reduced frequency of protests in more authoritarian regimes and regimes with higher levels of information control. Modeling dynamics further confirms this behavior and shows a potential connection between lower information control and more frequent but slower spreading, smaller scale resistance events while higher information control is connected with with faster inter-agent resistance spread, and larger resistance levels at a reduced frequency.
Author: Helbing, Joseph
abstract_manipulated: This treatise scrutinizes the empirical artifacts concerning uprisings in despotic polities as a derivative of regime configuration and cognitive censorship, subsequently formulating an operant model grounded on agents to interrogate epistemologically how the impact of ambiguity may explicate the disparities in insurrectionary and oppositional movements within these disparate regime configurations. The agent-based model encapsulates two categories of agents, Denizen and Surveillance, demonstrating the manner in which an obverse correlation between ambiguity pertaining to permissible public dissent, the likelihood of incurring penalties, and the aptitude to precisely discern local regime backing engenders variations in oppositional movements. Examination centers on the velocity of oppositional dissemination amidst agents contingent upon individual agent-level ambiguity, and how this influences overall opposition size, either complete equilibrium overturns, i.e. triumphant revolutions, or prolonged turmoil. Scrutiny of empirical data unveils a diminished incidence of uprisings in more autocratic regimes and regimes characterized by elevated levels of cognitive censorship. Modeling dynamics corroborate this phenomenon and disclose a plausible nexus between diminished cognitive censorship and more frequent yet sluggishly proliferating, diminutive scale oppositional occurrences while augmented cognitive censorship is affiliated with rapid inter-agent oppositional dissemination and heightened opposition levels at a diminished frequency.

title: Some Anomalies on the Chinese Stock Mutual Fund Market
abstract: Chinese stock mutual fund market experiences a high expansion over last 20 years. However, the entry of investors into mutual funds has raised concerns about whether mutual fund returns are equivalent to those earned by individual investors. This study employs dollar-weighted rate of return (IRR) to measure the actual return of investors incorporating trading behaviors and finds that most fund investors do not benefit as much as the return generated by fund performance. Besides, This study also investigates the anomalies of the long-term stagnation of Chinese stock market index and the performance comparison of active fund managers between Chinese and the US’s markets.
Author: Guangyuan, Chen
abstract_manipulated: The vicissitudinous trajectory of the Chinese pecuniary commingling tool sector has burgeoned prodigiously over the antecedent two decades. Nonetheless, the ingress of fiduciaries into pooled investment schemes has engendered qualms about the commensurability of pooled investment scheme yields vis-à-vis those accrued by solitary speculators. Through the utilization of the dollar-weighted rate of remuneration (IRR) as a yardstick to gauge the factual remuneration of investors, encapsulating transactional proclivities, it was unearthed that a majority of pooled investment scheme participants do not partake of commensurate benefits commensurate with the yield engendered by fund efficacy. Furthermore, this scrutiny probed into the enigmas surrounding the protracted stasis of the Chinese bourse index over the elongated term and the perforative juxtaposition of adept fund stewards amid the Chinese and the American bazaars.

title: Naturalness and Memorability: A Study of Image Similarities and Memorability in Natural and Urban Images
abstract: Interacting with nature is beneficial. Even brief exposures to natural environments or viewing sets of natural scene images can restore attention and memory (Berman et al., 2008). Despite the known benefits of nature, it remains unclear what aspects of viewing nature scenes lead to these improvements. While previous studies focus on the distinction between natural images and non-natural (urban) images, very few examine the connection between distinct properties of natural images and their cognitive benefits. One idea is that natural scenes are more fluently processed, making them less memorable, which in turn contributes to their benefits. A potential confound is that nature scenes may be more confusable than urban scenes, leading to their lower memorability scores predicted by the ResMem Neural Network (Needall & Bainbridge, 2021). The goal of this thesis was to test the hypothesis that image set similarity drives differences in memorability. To test this hypothesis, the present study calculated image statistics and image semantic labels into vectors and determined the Euclidean and cosine distances between pairs of natural and urban images. While the low-level visual features in natural and urban images differ, importantly, there were no significant differences in the average distances of every image pair. However, the semantic similarity analysis showed mixed results. The significant differences in semantic similarity were shown in both direction under different types of label coding techniques. This finding suggests the relationship between image similarity and memorability is more complex than a simple positive or negative relationship. In the future, studies can focus on the processing fluency and memorability of natural images to uncover the mechanism behind their restorative effect.
Author: Li, Yutai
abstract_manipulated: Engagement with the natural world yields advantageous outcomes. Fleeting encounters with natural environs or exposure to arrays of natural tableau can rejuvenate attentional faculties and cognitive retention (Berman et al., 2008). Notwithstanding the acknowledged benefits of nature, the precise constituents of natural scene observation engendering these enhancements remain enigmatic. While antecedent inquiries deliberate on the dichotomy between natural depictions and non-natural (urban) portrayals, scant attention is accorded to discerning the nexus between unique attributes of natural representations and their cognitive advantages. One contention posits that natural vistas undergo smoother cognitive processing, engendering lower memorability, thereby underpinning their salubrious effects. A conceivable confounding factor is the potential for nature scenes to evoke greater confusion than urban sceneries, culminating in diminished memorability scores prognosticated by the ResMem Neural Network (Needall & Bainbridge, 2021). The primary aim of this treatise was to scrutinize the proposition that likeness in image sets propels disparities in memorability. In pursuit of this aim, the extant investigation disentangled image properties and semantic descriptors into vector forms, discerning both Euclidean and cosine distances between pairs of natural and urban depictions. Notwithstanding discernible discrepancies in low-level visual characteristics between natural and urban images, notably absent were substantial variances in the mean distances across all image pairs. However, the assessment of semantic affinity yielded ambivalent outcomes. Notable divergences in semantic congruity were evinced bidirectionally under diverse label coding methodologies. This revelation implies that the interplay between image resemblance and memorability transcends simplistic positive or negative associations. Subsequent research endeavors may delve into the cognitive processing dynamics and mnemonic effects of natural imagery to unveil the underlying mechanisms of its rejuvenative prowess.

title: ALUMNI INFORMATION COLLECTION AND MANAGEMENT
abstract: To address the inefficiencies of the existing manual alumni information collection strategy, this thesis proposes an innovative pipeline to automate alumni information collection and identify the prominent alumni in the three MA programs under The University of Chicago’s Social Science Division (SSD). This thesis utilizes computational methods such as web scrap- ing, text cleaning, and natural language processing to collect and verify alumni data through Google API and Selenium. The preliminary analysis unveils critical revelation of the career trajectories and employment outcomes of SSD alumni. Additionally, this thesis proposes a machine-learning-driven approach for industry classification in mentor matching. As an extension of alumni data collection and management that establishes the groundwork for future integration of SSD’s career service and alumni outreach platforms, a prototype of an all-in-one alumni management and networking web application, SSD Connect, is created. This thesis contributes to the body of literature by providing novel methods and insights for automating alumni information collection, managing and analyzing alumni networks, as well as optimizing alumni management and networking strategies for higher education institutions.
Author: Dang, Lingchen
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the deficiencies attendant upon the extant manual alumni intellection colligation stratagem, the instant dissertation postulates a sui generis conduit to systematize alumni intelligence gathering protocol and discern the preeminent alumni among the triumvirate of MA programs ensconced within The University of Chicago's Socio-Metaphysical Categorization Conclave (SMCC). Leveraging computational methodologies such as cybernetic foraging, textual purgation, and ontological lingual processing, this treatise was subjected to analytical decomposition to amass and corroborate alumni intelligence by the agency of Google API and Selenium. The preliminary scrutiny proffers a salient edification into the vocational trajectories and occupational denouements of SMCC's alumni cohort. Moreover, this monograph posits a machine-learning-driven modus operandi for industrial taxonomy within the aegis of mentor allocation. As a corollary to alumni datum assemblage and stewardship that lays the groundwork for prospective amalgamation of SMCC's vocational assistance and alumni associational platforms, a paradigm of an all-encompassing alumni governance and interlinkage cybernetic apparatus, SMCC Concatenate, is engendered. This opus augments the literary corpus by proffering avant-garde techniques and discernments for the mechanization of alumni intelligence agglomeration, oversight and scrutiny of alumni interconnectivity, as well as the rationalization of alumni governance and interlinkage methodologies for academical institutes.

title: An Honor or a Curse? Effect of Winning Awards on the Evaluation of Movies’ Quality
abstract: The conventional wisdom is that an increase in status leads to a better evaluation of quality. However, recent research has shown that status can have both positive and negative effects. This study aims to examine the impact of a status increase on perceived quality by proposing two hypotheses: 1) award-winning movies receive less favorable evaluations after the award announcement, and 2) the increase in popularity after the award announcement contributes to the negative effect of a status increase. By using the resources available on IMDB and employing the difference-in-differences approach, I analyzed 47,010 reviews before and after the Oscars Award announcement for both winning and nominated-only movies across seven categories from 2008 to 2019. The results indicate that the ratings and sentiment scores of award-winning movies experienced a more significant decrease. Moreover, an increase in the percentage of reviews predicted that winning movies would receive lower ratings. These findings highlight the need for organizations to be mindful of the divergence in evaluation criteria between experts and the general public and to consider the diverse tastes among lay audiences when evaluating their offerings.
Author: Chui, Hazel
abstract_manipulated: The prevailing dogma postulates that an escalation in standing engenders a superior assessment of merit. Notwithstanding, recent inquiries have unveiled the dichotomous repercussions of status. This scrutiny endeavors to anatomize the repercussions of an ascendancy in status on perceived merit by positing two conjectures: 1) cinematic opuses garnering accolades are subjected to less laudatory judgments subsequent to the heralding of the accolade, and 2) the surge in vogue post-accolade proclamation contributes to the deleterious aftermath of a status surge. Through the utilization of the repositories procurable on IMDB and the employment of the difference-in-differences stratagem, a corpus of 47,010 critiques antecedent to and succeeding the Oscars Award promulgation for both laurelled and solely nominated films within seven classifications spanning from 2008 to 2019 was subjected to analytical decomposition. The findings serve to illuminate the extent to which the appraisals and affective valences of acclaimed films underwent a more profound descent. Furthermore, an augmentation in the proportion of critiques portended that triumphant films would garner diminished ratings. These revelations underscore the imperative for entities to be cognizant of the schism in evaluative paradigms between savants and the populace at large, and to heed the variegated predilections amidst lay audiences when adjudging their offerings.

title: Big Trouble in Little India: A Computational Temporal Analysis of Singaporean Parliamentary Speeches After the Little India Riot
abstract: In 2013, Singapore’s first riot in 44 years occurred in the racial enclave known as Little India. In response, the Singaporean government spent the next two years debating legislature that ranged from additional security protocols in the neighborhood to broader legislation like the eventually passed Liquor Control Act of 2015. These debates, like others in parliaments around the world, are kept in a repository known as a Hansard – “the official published report od debates in the parliament of a member of the Commonwealth of Nations” (Merriam-Webster). From these repositories, numerous studies explored latent linguistic and semantic elements useful for cultural analysis. A few of these studies employ word embeddings to facilitate closer word-driven analyses, with a smaller number engaging in temporal investigations of the corpus. This paper works to not only extend the literature on the use of word embedding models for the temporal analysis of Hansards but also as a first contribution to a dearth in computationally driven analysis of Singapore’s Hansard. Further, I extracted cultural dimensions of ‘security’ and ‘morality’ and projected selected topics and phrases onto them. To do this, I train a word embedding model on Singapore’s parliamentary speeches from 2012 to 2021, deriving “discourse atoms”, or topics, from said model and using them as reference points for analysis. These results will then be corroborated with textual analysis to communicate a fuller understanding of how exactly these changes are characterized. Overall, I argue that the computational findings point toward an immediate high-level topical response to the riot, that surrounded topics of militarization, inquiry, and disaster. Considered with the textual analysis, the results point toward a shift toward a spatialization of the riot and securitization of the spaces where it occurred in and where its participants, migrant workers, reside and occupy. Further, I argue that this brand of securitization, the securitization of migration, should be more widely used in interrogating government speeches immediately after the riot.
Author: Ligo, Val Alvern Cueco
abstract_manipulated: In the annum 2013, Singapore's inaugural convulsion in 44 solar revolutions took place within the ethnic cloister recognized as Little India. Subsequent thereto, the Singaporean governance expended the ensuing duo anniversaries deliberating legislative measures spanning from supplementary security protocols in the vicinage to more comprehensive statutes such as the eventually enacted Liquor Control Act of 2015. These deliberations, akin to those verifiable in legislative assemblies globally, are enshrined within an archival depository denoted as a Hansard – "the formal chronicled narration of debates in the legislative body of a constituent of the Commonwealth of Nations" (Merriam-Webster). Consequent thereto, copious inquiries have inquired into the latent linguistic and semantic constituents conducive to cultural exegesis. Several of these inquiries deploy lexical embeddings to expedite more intimate lexeme-powered investigations, with a diminutive cohort embarking on chronological inquiries of the corpus. This treatise endeavors not solely to advance the extant scholarship on the utility of lexical embedding frameworks for the temporal scrutiny of Hansards, but additionally as an inaugural contribution to a paucity in computationally steered analysis of Singapore's Hansard. Additionally, cultural planes of 'security' and 'morality' were distilled and superimposed upon handpicked themes and locutions. To effectuate this, a lexical embedding framework was trained on Singapore's parliamentary speeches from the biennium commencing with 2012 to culminating with 2021, engendering "discursive nuclei", or themes, from said framework and utilizing them as cardinal points for scrutiny. These outcomes will subsequently be substantiated through textual scrutiny to disseminate a more comprehensive cognition of how precisely these mutations are delineated. Generally, I posit that the computational discoveries allude to an expeditious top-tier thematic reaction to the upheaval, revolving around subjects of militarization, interrogation, and catastrophe. Aligned with the textual scrutiny, the findings insinuate a pivot toward a spatial categorization of the upheaval and the securitization of the precincts wherein it transpired and where its participants, itinerant laborers, reside and inhabit. Moreover, I contend that this breed of securitization, the securitization of migration, ought to be more widely deployed in scrutinizing governmental orations in the immediate aftermath of the tumult.

title: Is the News Always Negative? Using Deep Learning to Track News Sentiment During the Covid-19 Pandemic
abstract: The news is notorious for its tendency to focus on negative events, giving rise to the adage, “If it bleeds, it leads.” In this study, I examine whether news coverage during the Covid-19 pandemic mainly focused on negative events while downplaying positive developments such as decreasing Covid-19 cases. Utilizing a state-of-the-art fine-tuned language model, I analyzed the sentiment of over 900,000 Covid-19 related news articles from March 2020 to April 2022 across the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom. The results indicate that the news is far more negative than positive—even when Covid-19 cases and hospitalizations are decreasing. This negativity is most pronounced in Op-Ed articles, front-page news articles, and articles published by large news organizations (e.g., New York Times, BBC, Fox News). However, non-Op-Ed news articles do become more positive as Covid-19 cases decrease, contradicting previous research findings. These discrepancies can be attributed, in part, to differences in model accuracy, as the model I trained is approximately 20% more accurate than other models used in the literature. Further, when dividing U.S. news by the publisher’s political ideology, clear differences emerge: both left-wing and right-wing sources are much more negative than centrist news sources. Surprisingly, these differences in sentiment are about as large as the difference between regular news and Covid-19 news sentiment, indicating substantial differences in news reporting across political lines. These findings provide new insights into news reporting patterns during the pandemic and carry important implications for public health messaging and news reporting practices.
Author: Nicholson, Gabriel
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the domain of media discourse, the propensity of news narratives to gravitate towards adversarial occurrences has long been a subject of scholarly contemplation, encapsulated by the axiom, "In the event of spilt blood, the narrative finds its course." This investigation interrogates epistemologically whether the reportage landscape amid the Covid-19 pandemic predominantly fixated on unfavorable incidents whilst relegating affirmative advancements, such as the abatement of Covid-19 incidences, to the peripheries of attention. Through the employment of a cutting-edge fine-tuned linguistic framework, a corpus exceeding 900,000 Covid-19-related journalistic pieces from March 2020 to April 2022 across the tripartite dominions of the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom was subjected to analytical decomposition. The evidentiary substrate serves to illuminate the extent to which the informational milieu is disproportionately adverse in tenor—even as Covid-19 incidences and hospitalizations exhibit a decrement. This negativism emerges most prominently within Op-Ed features, front-page reportages, and releases emanating from major media establishments (e.g., the New York Times, BBC, Fox News). Nevertheless, non-Op-Ed pieces exhibit an amelioration towards positivity concomitant with a decline in Covid-19 cases, contravening antecedent scholarly assertions. These incongruities can, in part, be ascribed to divergences in model fidelity, given that the model under scrutiny manifests a statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions exceeding that of its antecedent counterparts by an approximate margin of 20%. Furthermore, upon segregating American news sources contingent on their ideological leanings, conspicuous dissimilarities come to the fore: both left-wing and right-wing outlets evince a preponderance of pessimism vis-a-vis centrist news outlets. Intriguingly, the disparateness in affective tonality mirrors the chasm between standard news narratives and those centered on the Covid-19 crisis, thus suggesting marked disparities in journalistic representation across ideological spectra. These revelations engender novel insights into the contours of news dissemination amidst the pandemic milieu, carrying weighty ramifications for the realms of public health communication and journalistic praxis.

title: Using Transfer Entropy to Understand Information Flow and Connectivity in Neural Networks
abstract: One central goal of psychology is to link human behaviors and cognitive processes with concrete neural network. In social science, there is growing interest in understanding social structures through the lens of social network. Though definition of networks and their dynamics are context dependent, the link between network connectivity and dynamics plays a key role in all fields. In this thesis, we study how transfer entropy can elucidate neural network structures. We use transfer entropy to examine two types of model, a network with firing-rate-based nodes with threshold linear transfer function, and a balanced network consisting of leaky integrate-and-fire type neurons. With a firing rate neural model, the effective connectivity inferred by transfer entropy matches the underlying directional network. For balanced networks, presence of connection between two neurons is not predictive of existence of information flow. However, for a balanced network with cluster or layer structures, the multivariate transfer entropy calculation using samples of each neural cluster accurately captures the direction of the cluster-wise projection. We focused on networks in neuroscience, but the framework can be generalized to understand how social network propagates information.
Author: Zheng, Bowen
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the labyrinthine configurations of psychological inquiry, the foundational endeavor pertains to the enigmatic task of establishing resonant symphonies between human comportments and the palpable intricacies of neurophysiological lattices. Within the annals of social scholarship, an emergent curiosity burgeons, seeking to decode the enigmatic tapestries of societal architectures through the refractive prism of interconnective webs. Albeit the delineations of these intricate frameworks and their temporal fluxions are contingent upon contextual milieus, the symbiotic interplay betwixt network interconnectivity and its temporal vicissitudes emerges as a linchpin across diverse domains of inquiry.

In this treatise, the arcane modality of transfer entropy is marshaled to penetrate the cryptic contours of neural circuitries. We undertake an interrogative epistemological odyssey, scrutinizing two archetypal models; one characterized by nodes predicated upon firing rates undergirded by a threshold linear transference paradigm, and another embodying a harmonious equilibrium forged through the amalgamation of leaky integrate-and-fire neural modules. Within the crucible of the firing rate neuronal schema, the inferential tentacles of transfer entropy unveil a mirroring of the latent topography of directional neural linkage.

Conversely, within the tapestries of equilibrium poised networks, the mere existence of synaptic tetherage between two neuronal enclaves fails to prognosticate the concomitant egress of informational currents. Nevertheless, within the domain of equilibrium networks bedecked with cluster or stratum formations, the polyphonic calculus of multivariate transfer entropy, enriched by the sparse effulgence of neural cluster samples, deftly encapsulates the vectorial trajectories of cluster-centric projections. While the crucible of our contemplation primarily orbits the celestial realms of neuroscientific cartographies, the blueprint engendered herein portends a wider resonance, for its applicability transcends neural precincts to illuminate the kaleidoscopic proclivities through which the tendrils of social networks disseminate cognitive essences.

title: How Does Big-data-related Technology Adoption Affect Listed Companies' Market Performance
abstract: This paper investigates the effect of technology adoption on the performance of companies in China. We utilize text-mining techniques to extract information from the annual reports of Chinese A-share listed companies, and particularly, we construct measures of adoption of big data-related technologies based on the occurrences of big-data-related keywords. In conjunction with other company and provincial data, we explore the growth trend, determinants of big-data technology adoption, and the impact of big-data applications on the performance of companies and discuss the possible mechanism for achieving these effects. First, companies with lower tangible assets, higher GDP per capita, or more colleges in their regions are more likely to use big data technology. Second, instrumental variable regression with industry-fixed effects shows that the industry characteristics and nature of the company occasionally limit the positive effects of big data applications. Third, heterogeneity analysis suggests that Big data applications contribute to firm net profit more significantly in the financial and manufacturing industries, and non-state-owned companies.
Author: Zhang, Yijing
abstract_manipulated: This treatise delves into the ramifications of technological assimilation on the operational efficacy of enterprises within the dominion of China. Employing computational linguistics methodologies, we subjected the empirical artifacts contained within the annual reports of Chinese A-share listed entities to analytical decomposition; specifically, we engendered metrics pertaining to the assimilation of prodigious data-oriented technologies predicated upon the incidence of lexically significant big-data-related vocables. In tandem with supplementary corporate and regional evidentiary substrates, we embarked upon an interrogation epistemologically of the trajectory of expansion, determinants underpinning the embrace of big-data technologies, and the ramifications of their operationalization on corporate efficacy, all the while delving into the plausible mechanisms underpinning the realization of said effects. Firstly, entities characterized by diminished corporeal holdings, elevated gross domestic product per capita, or a proliferation of institutions of higher learning in their vicinities evince a proclivity towards the deployment of big data technology. Secondly, employing instrumental variable regression techniques accentuated by industry-fixed effects, we substantiate that the idiosyncrasies of industry profiles and corporate ethos intermittently serve to circumscribe the salutary influences of big data applications. Thirdly, an exploration of heterogeneity proffers that big data applications furnish a more pronounced augmentation to net profits for commercial enterprises within the financial and manufacturing sectors, particularly those not under state control.

title: Do Social Media Platforms Impede the Wisdom of Crowds?
abstract: Social media platforms have been playing an increasingly crucial role in shaping social interaction and information exchange. While facilitating communication, they possess the central power that determines which content users are exposed to. In this paper, we examine how platforms’ information distribution mechanisms affect a society’s ability to aggregate information correctly and efficiently. We consider four criteria that algorithms can favor in curating content: globally most engaging (GE), locally most engaging (LE), globally closest (GC), and/or locally closest (LC). That is, for a given user, platforms can promote beliefs that are most engaging and/or are most similar to her pre-existing belief; beliefs can be sorted globally (among all users) or locally (among the user’s connections). We simulate a social learning model where agents form beliefs by learning from each other and from the information allocated by the platform. We find that while exposing users to engaging beliefs considerably increases learning bias, feeding personalized information based on belief similarity does not affect collective wisdom significantly. All mechanisms except GE slow down the speed for agents to converge to a consensus.
Author: Chen, Yile
abstract_manipulated: Social conviviality matrices have been orchestrating an escalatingly pivotal role in configuring interpersonal dynamics and cognitive transmission. Whilst expediting discourse, they wield the hegemonic agency that arbitrates the corpus of content to which users are subjected. In this treatise, we interrogate how the dissemination modalities of these matrices impinge upon a society's capacity to consolidate information accurately and expeditiously. We contemplate four benchmarks that algorithms may prioritize in assembling content: universally paramountly engrossing (UPE), regionally paramountly engrossing (RPE), universally proximal (UP), and/or regionally proximal (RP). In other words, vis-à-vis a given user, matrices may propagate beliefs that are paramountly engrossing and/or bear resemblance to her pre-existing belief; beliefs can be categorically organized globally (across all users) or regionally (within the user's affiliations). We instantiate a sociocognitive assimilation paradigm wherein agents configure beliefs through mutual edification and the intelligence apportioned by the matrix. We observe that whilst exposing users to engrossing beliefs appreciably amplifies bias in learning, furnishing personalized intelligence predicated on belief proximity negligibly impacts collective sagacity. All modalities barring UPE dampen the velocity at which agents attain a consensus.

title: Race and Income Effects on Park Visitation During COVID-19 in High- and Low-Index Cities
abstract: Inequalities in the distribution of parks and green spaces have become an issue of increasing concern during the Covid-19 pandemic since they can disproportionately affect the ability to maintain good health across racial and socioeconomic groups. The present study attempts to understand whether Covid has compounded the disparities in park access and usage based on race and income and whether the disparities varied by the quality of a city’s park system. Using cell phone mobility data, the study compared tract-level park visits in 20 major U.S. cities across racial and income groups before (2019) and during the pandemic (2020). The cities were divided into a high-quality park system group and a low-quality group on the basis of their ParkScore indexes. The results indicated a 6% decline in park visitation during Covid. Regarding park access, the income-based privilege was found in cities with high-quality park systems whereas no racial differences were found regardless in cities with high or low quality of park systems. Only tracts with large Upper-middle-class income families showed increased park usage during the pandemic. Most importantly, the study provides evidence against a positive correlation between physical park accessibility and usability, and it suggests the importance of other factors in promoting park usage.
Author: Shu, Senling
abstract_manipulated: The exploration of disparities in the apportionment of verdant oases and sylvan retreats has ascended to the echelons of paramount concern amidst the throes of the pandemonium induced by the Covid-19 scourge, given their proclivity to differentially impact the preservation of salubrious well-being amongst variegated racial and economic cohorts. The extant inquiry endeavors to unravel the ramifications of whether the Covid contagion has compounded the schisms in park ingress and utilization predicated upon racial provenance and pecuniary affluence, as well as to scrutinize the vicissitudes in said disparities contingent upon the caliber of a metropolis' park infrastructure. By marshaling cellular telephony mobility data, the investigation subjected the trajectory-by-tract park sojourns in twenty preeminent urban agglomerations within the United States to analytical decomposition across racial and income strata antecedent to (anno domini 2019) and during the pandemic (anno domini 2020). The urban epicenters were stratified into a cohort boastful of superlative park amenities and an assemblage marked by inferior offerings in consonance with their ParkScore indices. The findings proffered disclosures of a 6% ebb in park pilgrimages during the Covid era. With respect to park ingress, the mantle of premium was draped upon income-oriented echelons within municipalities bedecked with opulent park infrastructures, while racial differentials remained undiscernible, be it in locales bedecked with park plethoras of exalted or humble caliber. Solely demesnes housing gargantuan Upper-middle-class financial kin displayed a surge in park utilization during the pandemic. Foremost amongst the revelations is the invalidation of a direct nexus between physical park availability and serviceability, postulating the eminence of sundry other determinants in fomenting park patronage.

title: Applying an Unsupervised Machine Learning Approach to Analyze the Non-Income Poverty Indicators Used in the Listahanan 2
abstract: Poverty targeting has been used in developing countries as a means to provide social protection programs and services directly to the poor. As information on households’ welfare is unavailable or difficult and costly to acquire in the developing world, the proxy means test (PMT), which uses proxy variables to estimate an unobservable welfare variable such as household income or consumption, has become a commonly used method for targeting. This study uses an unsupervised machine learning approach on the set of household- and individual-specific non- income poverty indicators used to estimate household income in the PMT models for the Philippines’ National Household Targeting System for Poverty Reduction or Listahanan, in order to examine whether differences between households across these indicators reflect differences in their income. Applying the Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection for Dimension Reduction (UMAP) algorithm onto the Listahanan 2 indicators shows that households naturally cluster into three to four groups. However, these clusters seem to be unrelated to income and expenditure. The richest and poorest households appear to be alike and cannot be differentiated on the basis of the non-income poverty indicators considered. This suggests that these indicators alone may not be sufficient for the PMT models to accurately target the poor. However, this study is a preliminary analysis on the limited data available. A more comprehensive analysis is required to produce conclusive results.
Author: Ting, Dominique Ysobel
abstract_manipulated: In a realm where paucity targeting hath been wielded in emerging realms as a modality to dispense social protective stratagems and amenities directly unto the indigent, the absence or exorbitant inaccessibility of enlightenment respecting households' well-being in the burgeoning sphere hath engendered the ascendancy of the proxy means ordeal (PMT), which through integrating proxy parameters to approximate an unobservable well-being parameter such as household revenue or consumption, hath metamorphosed into a prevalently embraced technique for targeting. The utilization of an unsupervised machinery erudition modus operandi upon the plenitude of household- and person-specific non-revenue indigence signifiers employed to appraise household revenue in the PMT paradigms for the Philippine National Household Targeting System for Paucity Mitigation or Listahanan is undertaken herein to scrutinize whether disparities amidst households transversing these signifiers doth mirror disparities in their revenue. The employment of the Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection for Dimension Reduction (UMAP) algorithm upon the Listahanan 2 signifiers doth unveil that households doth organically coalesce into three to four cabals. Albeit, these cabals materialize to be disassociated from revenue and disbursement. The most affluent and most impoverished households doth manifest resemblance and cannot be demarcated founded upon the non-revenue indigence signifiers pondered. This intimateth that solely these signifiers may not suffice for the PMT paradigms to pinpoint the indigent accurately. Nevertheless, this scrutiny doth constitute a preliminary scrutiny on the circumscribed enlightenment at hand. A more exhaustive scrutiny is requisite to engender conclusive deductions.

title: Patent citation analysis for company valuation in the field of electric vehicles
abstract: The electric vehicle industry is one of the hottest technology sections where patents are essential for companies to survive and compete. The goal of my work is to present EV startups with higher market value locate at more central positions in the patent citation network and to prove forward patent citation has statistical significance to EV startups’ market value, but backward patent citation does not. This study is conducted in four steps: data collection and process, network visualization, network description, and quadratic assignment procedures (QAP). Because this study contributes to practical use in the business field, to ease the use by investors from any backgrounds and industries, I chose social network analysis software NodeXL, which is an easy-to-use and widely adopted add-in to Microsoft Excel. The network visualization presents that, the top two most valued U.S.-based EV startups, Tesla and Atieva, stay at the most pivotal position in the patent citation network. QAP shows strong evidence to support that the number of forward citations positively relates to EV startups’ market value but does not exhibit evidence to prove the statistical significance of backward citation. The results of my study are expected to shed some insights for investors to find investment targets.
Author: Qian, Yi
abstract_manipulated: The sector of electric vehicle proliferation stands as a domain wherein the issuance of patents assumes critical import for corporate endurance and competitive edge. The aim inherent in my inquiry lies in the exposition of electric vehicle (EV) upstarts of augmented market valuation domiciling within central echelons of the patent citation nexus. Furthermore, it endeavors to assert the statistically substantive import of forward patent citation upon the market valuation of EV fledglings, whilst the converse, namely backward patent quotation, evinces a lack thereof. This scrutiny unfolds through a fourfold progression: the accrual and refinement of data, the visualization of the interlinked web, the explication of said web, and the invocation of quadratic assignment methodologies (QAP). In discerning this embodied narrative, one is impelled by an impetus toward operational utility within the commercial sphere. To this end and in a gesture catering to a panoply of stakeholders across diverse sectors, the selection is made of NodeXL, a social network analysis tool, acclaimed for its user-friendliness and widespread uptake as an adjunct to the venerable Microsoft Excel. The visual representation of the web insinuates that the apex duo of U.S.-anchored EV fledglings, namely Tesla and Atieva, bed down at the epicenter of the patent citation lattice. The QAP analysis furnishes resolute attestation buttressing the tenet that the quantum of forthright citations stands in direct proportion to the market valuation of EV upstarts, albeit failing to furnish corroboration anent the statistically persuasive impact of rearward citations. The denouement of this investigation holds promise in illuminating the terrain for investors scouring for lucrative investment prospects.

title: Subnational Fiscal Impacts of Extreme Cyclone Exposure: Evidence from the Philippines
abstract: What is the subnational fiscal impact of natural disasters in the Philippines? Using meteorological data, I reconstruct the extreme exposure of tropical cyclones for each province in the Philippines during 2001-2021. I exploit random within-province year-to-year variation in cyclone strikes with a dynamic two-way fixed effect panel model to identify the causal effect of environmental disasters on local fiscal dynamics. Also, this paper examines different post-disaster fiscal responses for governments of different levels. The major findings are: (1) wind exposure generally induces a drop in government spending (for all levels of governments) and an increase in deficit (for the provincial-level), despite showing some nonlinearity under extremely high wind velocity. (2) There is a persistent significant negative impact of storm exposure on tax revenue, with lower level governments may suffer more. (3) An increase in storm exposure reduces the provincial-level investment activities.
Author: Xia, Zimei
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within: What is the infranational pecuniary reverberation of cataclysmic occurrences in the Philippine archipelago? Through interrogating epistemologically meteorological empirical artifacts, I undertake a herculean effort to reconstruct the extraordinary vulnerability to tropical tempests afflicting each jurisdiction within the Philippine expanse spanning the temporal epoch of 2001 to 2021. The empirical substrate was subjected to analytical decomposition, thereby teasing out the stochastic vicissitudes of cyclonic onslaughts, leveraging fortuitous within-jurisdiction interannual fluctuations in tempestuous onslaughts via a dynamic bidirectional fixed-effect panel framework in order to disentangle the causal nexus between ecological calamities and indigenous fiscal modalities. Additionally, this treatise delves into the variegated post-catastrophe fiscal ripostes engendered by disparate tiers of governance. The principal revelations portend thusly: (1) zephyr exposure generally instigates a diminution in governmental outlays (across all tiers of governance) concomitant with an escalation in fiscal deficits (at the provincial echelon), albeit evincing certain nonlinear dynamics at exorbitant wind celerities. (2) A continual and statistically robust diminution in tax receipts ensues in the wake of meteorological upheaval, with subnational authorities potentially bearing the brunt of such fiscal vicissitudes. (3) Escalating exposure to squalls engenders a contraction in provincial-level capital disbursements.

title: Exploring Personality and Online Social Engagement: An Investigation of MBTI Users on Twitter
abstract: Text-based personality analysis by computational models is an emerging field with the potential to significantly improve on key weaknesses of survey-based personality assessment. We1 investigate 3848 profiles from Twitter with self-labeled Myers-Briggs personality types (MBTI) – a framework closely related to the Five Factor Model of personality – to better understand how text-based digital traces from social media can be used to predict user personality traits and explore relationships between community discourse and personality traits. We leverage machine learning and network analysis approaches to analyze relationships between behavioral traits and various facets of engagement on social media. We find that both social engagement metadata and multiple modalities of text from social profiles significantly correlate with factors of the MBTI system – especially the Intuitive/Sensing trait that corresponds to the dimension of Openness in the Five Factor model of personality (the Big 5). We discuss our findings and their implications for the validity of the MBTI and the lexical hypothesis, a foundational theory underlying the Five Factor Model of personality that links language use and behavior. Our results hold optimistic implications for personality psychologists, computational linguists, and other social scientists seeking to clarify the role of behavioral dimensions underlying semi-structured text-based social engagement and illuminate the links between sociolinguistics and core dispositional traits.
Author: Kadambi, Partha
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within text-based psycho-spiritual essence delineation through algorithmic architectures, we interrogate epistemologically 3848 cybernetic personas manifested in the digital ether of Twitter, each adorned with self-proclaimed Myers-Briggs individuation labels – a paradigm intimately entwined with the Quintuple Factor Fabric of individuality – in an endeavor to unravel the enigmatic tapestry weaving together syntactic fragments of digital discourse and the intricate contours of the human psyche. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the synergistic fusion of machinic cognition and network topography scrutiny, our endeavor embarks on a journey of unraveling the probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation through the examination of social participatory metadata and the myriad textual genres populating virtual profiles. Our analytical decomposition serves to illuminate the extent to which communal interactivity indices and the polyphony of linguistic corpuses harbor profound resonances with the cardinal axes of the Myers-Briggs paradigm – notably the Cognizant/Concrete dimension mirroring the expanses of Inquisitiveness within the Pentalateral Morphology of temperament (the Grand 5). The explication of our revelations and the convoluted ramifications thereof for the ontological soundness of the Myers-Briggs typology and the lexicographic postulate, a primordial construct buttressing the Pentalateral Morphology of temperament that intertwines verbal expression with conduct, proffers fertile ground for the elaboration of the empyreal linkage between socio-verbal symphonics and fundamental dispositional configurations.

title: Pandemic Behavior: Social Comparison and Miscalibration
abstract: Explores how people are overconfident with respect to covid based on vaccination status. We find that those who are vaccinated tend to think that they are more cautious than they truly are to a greater extent than the unvaccinated.
Author: Soll, Justin
abstract_manipulated: Interrogating epistemologically the phenomena of human overconfidence vis-à-vis the covid pathogen contingent upon their inoculation status, this research endeavors to unveil the cognitive dissonances inherent within. The evidentiary substrate proffers a revealing panorama wherein the vaccinated cohort, by virtue of their immunization, harbors a perceptual bias towards a self-perception of prudence that belies their actual comportment to a magnitude surpassing that of their unimmunized counterparts.

title: Business Cycle Analysis of China 1980-2019
abstract: This research analyzes the business cycle of China’s economy from 1980 to 2019. The main finding is that before the global financial crisis, shocks to efficiency, capital, and government expenditure can all contribute to the fluctuation of the business cycle but after the crisis, these relations suddenly disappear. This change may reflect the changes in the logic of China’s economic development that before the crisis, the economy was mainly influenced by the economic reforms pushed forward by the central government, while the global financial crisis changed this pattern, and the new pattern has not been observed by this research.
Author: Zhang, Chenming
abstract_manipulated: The present inquiry engages in a rigorous analysis of the undulating trajectory of China's economic oscillations spanning the temporal expanse from 1980 to 2019. A salient revelation surfaces wherein antecedent to the epochal convulsions of the global financial cataclysm, perturbations to operational efficacy, financial reservoirs, and governmental disbursements evince a propensity towards influencing the undulatory cadence of the economic cycle; however, post the crisis nadir, these causal linkages undergo a sudden effacement. This metamorphosis ostensibly mirrors an ontological shift in the teleology of China's economic evolution—whereas antecedent to the crisis, the economic milieu predominantly responded to the imperatives of state-driven economic rejuvenation, the seismic reverberations of the global financial maelstrom heralded a departure from this paradigmatic norm, ushering forth an uncharted trajectory that eludes the purview of this analytical inquiry.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Lyft App Integration on the Public Bike Share Programs in Four Major U.S. Cities Using Synthetic Control
abstract: This project applies synthetic control to show that (1) Lyft mobile app integration in June 2019 caused an uplift in monthly casual trip rate and monthly casual trips in public bike share programs in Chicago, Boston, and New York City from June to December 2019, (2) the interventions did not led to increase in monthly total trips and monthly membership trips in these programs during the same time period. It suggests that the increase in monthly casual trips in the three cities are likely drawn from previously membership riders, rather than from new first-time public bike riders.
Author: Zhu, Wei
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the present inquiry, the present investigation was subjected to analytical decomposition unveiling that the introduction of Lyft mobile app integration in the temporal locus of June 2019 instigated a marked uplift in the monthly episodic peregrination ratio and monthly episodic peregrinations within the communal velocipede allotment schemes in the urban vicinities of Chicago, Boston, and New York City spanning from June unto December of annum 2019. Contrariwise, the aforementioned interventions did not incite an escalation in the tally of monthly all-encompassing peregrinations and monthly membership peregrinations within these aforementioned programs over the identical temporal expanse. This serves to illuminate the extent to which the augmentation in monthly episodic peregrinations across the aforementioned trilateral agglomerations plausibly emanates from antecedent membership sojourners, as opposed to nascent inaugural public velocipede sojourners.

title: China’s Film Industry Policies and Implications
abstract: The study discusses China’s film industry and related policies. First, I discuss China’s film industry’s rapid development and the policies that contribute to its growth. I also attempt to depict how the quality of China’s domestic films changes over different periods. I group different policies that have similar effects together. However, I could not apply the difference-in-difference model to quantify and disentangle the impact of each policy. However, using regression models, I assessed the effects of China’s film censorship and found that the censorship has little effect on film revenue and may even increase the viewers’ perception of the film produced. However, this is not to say that censorships are a beneficial policy. As discussed, China’s film censorship is mainly a political tool to control the cultural output and has been essential for the Party-state's ideology and has little economic intention. The censorship limited the creativity of the film producers, and films deemed too “political” would never survive the censorship. Overall, the results indicate that the censorship limited the theme of Chinese-produced films, leaving the user’s perception and the revenue of the screened film intact. Over time, Filmmakers have developed ways to pass the censorship by painting a positive picture of the government in the films. Topic-modelling of the reviews from IMDB also found that reviews for China’s films hardly discuss the effect of censorship. Overall, the study found that censorship’s impact on the economic returns and viewers’ perception of screened films in China is negligible, which may give existing filmmakers little incentive to fight against censorship.
Author: Shan, Lingfeng
abstract_manipulated: The interrogative discourse herein delves into the celluloid enterprises of China and the concomitant regulatory constructs. Initially, an exegesis unfolds on the meteoric ascension of China's cinematic milieu juxtaposed with the regulatory paradigms fostering such ascension. A tentative portrayal endeavors to elucidate the metachronism in the calibre of indigenous cinematic oeuvres across epochs. Agglomerating policies yielding akin repercussions assumes precedence. Alas, the deployment of the disparity-in-disparity framework for a granular quantification and disentanglement of individual policy impacts remained elusive. Notwithstanding, leveraging regression paradigms enabled a scrutiny of the repercussions of China's film censorship, revealing its tenuous influence on pecuniary returns and a plausible augmentation in viewers' perceptual faculties towards the cinematographic exegesis. Nonetheless, the aforesaid is not an encomium to the efficaciousness of censors, as expounded upon. China's film censorship principally manifests as a hegemonic apparatus steering cultural manifestations, rather than an economistic endeavor, instrumental for the Party's cerebral hegemony, but with scant pecuniary motives. Creativity among cinematic artisans is stifled, with politically tinged productions facing an insurmountable censorship hurdle. The outcomes delineate how censorship circumscribes thematic vistas of domestic productions while ostensibly preserving the user's experiential edifice and the financial realms. Evolutionarily, filmmakers have devised stratagems to navigate censorship by imbuing officialdom with effulgent hues onscreen. Textual deconstruction of IMDB critiques scarcely broaches the censorship's repercussions on the cinematic corpus. In sum, the inquiry proffers an insignificance of censorship's impact on fiscal yields and viewers' critiques of screened opuses in China, engendering scant impetus for incumbent filmmakers to oppose the censorial yoke.

title: Operationalizing the New York Times to Forecast Economic Indicators
abstract: This paper builds upon previous literature on nowcasting, and attempts to use words from New York Times articles to predict Consumer Sentiment and the Unemployment Rate. The results are mixed: Consumer sentiment proves most predictable, while models predicting the unemployment rate perform poorly. Despite these results, this paper investigates the many models and methods to harness the predicting power of textual data, offering a road map for future researchers.
Author: Maurice, Christopher
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within antecedent scholarship, this treatise embarks upon an interrogative epistemological odyssey into the realm of nowcasting, endeavoring to extrapolate lexical elements from the annals of New York Times discourse so as to prognosticate both Consumer Sentiment and the Unemployment Rate. The findings, ensconced within a labyrinth of ambiguity, unveil a chiaroscuro tableau: whilst the vicissitudes of Consumer Sentiment unveil themselves amenable to the alchemy of prognostication, the oracular endeavors foreshadowing the Unemployment Rate languish in a morass of ineptitude. Notwithstanding the Byzantine tapestry of outcomes, this opus subjects the myriad panoply of paradigms and methodologies to a rigorous analytical decomposition, seeking to distill the latent prognostic potential inherent within textual empirical artifacts, thereby proffering a cartographic guidepost for prospective scholarly wayfarers.

title: Peer Firms' Earnings Disclosure and Corporate Risk-taking
abstract: Besides firms’ own ones, peer firms' financial disclosures may also affect corporate decision-making. While most existing studies examine how peers’ disclosures influence firms’ investment levels, firms’ risk-taking, which closely correlates to economic stability, remains under-explored. I hypothesize that firms learn important information from their peers’ disclosures, based on which they adjust their risk-taking behaviors. I provide large sample evidence (127,896 firm-year observations) corroborating a negative correlation between peer firms' earnings performance and the focal firm's level of risk-taking. This suggests that when peers disclose outstanding earnings, firms’ fear of competition outweighs their positive expectations of the industry outlook, thus firms tend to be more risk-averse. I also find that compared with over-performing firms, under-performing ones are more risk-loving, which accords with the prospect theory. Finally, I show that under-performing firms are less responsive to peers' earnings in terms of risk propensity because they are always more risk-seeking regardless of the degree of competition.
Author: Fu, Qishen
abstract_manipulated: Subsequent to entities’ endogenous disclosures, the financial revelations of cognate entities may wield a substantive impact on corporate determinations. Whereas extant inquiries predominantly scrutinize the impingement of peer disclosures on entities’ capital outlays, the realm of entities’ venture embracement, intimately entwined with economic equilibrium, remains insufficiency interrograted. I postulate that entities assimilate salient epistemic revelations from their peers' disclosures, whereupon they calibrate their proclivities toward venture uptake. A prodigious empirical repository (127,896 entity-annum scrutinies) buttresses a contrary linkage between peer entities’ profit performance and the focal entity’s venture proclivity. This intimates that upon peers revealing exceptional profits, entities’ trepidation of market rivalry eclipses their sanguine anticipations of sectoral prospects, engendering a predilection towards risk aversion. Additionally, a juxtaposition reveals that vis-a-vis entities surpassing expectations, those undershooting are predisposed towards risk-seeking behavior, congruous with the contours of prospect theory. Lastly, an evincement manifests that subpar performers evince diminished retort towards peers' profits vis-a-vis risk inclination, predicated upon their intrinsic proclivity towards risk-seeking, irrespective of competitive intensity.

title: Temporal Changes in Individualism and Collectivism in Modern China: Evidence from Google N-gram Viewer and Sina Weibo
abstract: Over the past two decades, China has experienced rapid economic and social development, accompanied by significant cultural changes. Mixed findings have been found in previous studies on changes in individualism and collectivism in China. We propose that discrepancies regarding collectivism change can be explained by collectivism components changing in different directions, driven by modernization. To further explore this issue, we examined cultural changes in the Google Corpus of Chinese books (1990-2018) and Sina Weibo (2011-2020) using word frequency analysis. Shared conclusions in both datasets are that first-person singular pronouns reflecting self-attentional focus increased over time, while first-person plural pronouns reflecting concern for others decreased, and the division of collectivism components in changing directions and relationships with modernization. In other aspects, the two datasets reflected cultural change differently. Google Books results showed a stronger focus on family and in-group relations, reflecting the heritage of traditional Chinese culture that was not related to modernization. As a better representation of public expressions, the results of Sina Weibo were more consistent with our expectations, showing a rising trend in institutional collectivism accompanied by higher education levels and universalism. This study highlights the need for future research that considers collectivism a multidimensional variable and uses multiple research methods to explore possible causes and effects of cultural changes.
Author: Li, Yutong
abstract_manipulated: Over the temporal expanse of the preceding two decades, the realm of China has undergone a velocity-infused trajectory of economic and societal advancement, concomitant with profound metamorphoses in the cultural milieu. A mélange of findings has been unearthed in antecedent scrutinies concerning alterations in individualism and collectivism within the Chinese domain. Our proposition posits that the incongruities pertaining to the mutability of collectivism can be explicated by the divergent trajectories assumed by constituent elements of collectivism, impelled by the forces of modernization. In the pursuit of delving deeper into this conundrum, we subjected the cultural metamorphoses inscribed within the Google Corpus of Chinese manuscripts (1990-2018) and Sina Weibo archives (2011-2020) to a rigorous parsing via word frequency analysis. Convergent deductions discernible in both repositories serve to illuminate the extent to which first-person singular pronouns indicative of self-directed cognizance exhibited an upward trend over temporal expanses, juxtaposed against a decrement in first-person plural pronouns emblematic of empathic cognizance, alongside the fractionalizing of collectivism's constituents along divergent trajectories and their interfacing with the currents of modernization. However, divergences were manifest in other facets of the two datasets' cultural reflections. The heritages enshrined within the annals of traditional Chinese ethos were discernible prominently within the Google Books dataset, with a pronounced emphasis on familial and in-group affiliations, not tethered to the tenets of modernization. Conversely, the corollaries drawn from the Sina Weibo entries, as a more apt portrait of public articulations, resonated more harmoniously with our prognostications, showcasing an ascendant trajectory in institutional collectivism concomitant with heightened scholastic attainments and universalistic outlooks.
