This interrogation underscores the exigency of future inquiries that treat collectivism as a multidimensional variable, deploying an array of methodological approaches to plumb the potential antecedents and repercussions of these cultural vicissitudes.

title: Investigating long term pattern of technology innovation and gender imbalance in filmmaking occupations
abstract: Gender gap continues to be a persistent issue in many industries. As many idiosyncratic and institutional factors are discussed, the study in examining female representation and technology innovation is limited. The movie industry, witnessing a digital transformation during the 1980s, is suitable for analyzing the relationship between work-related technology advancement and occupational gender segregation. To find out whether a relationship exists, this research leverages observational data from over 20,000 movies to measure the historical pattern of camera types and the percentage trend of female workers in a position related to camera operation in the U.S. filming industry over the past 100 years. Due to the special setting of the movie industry, where directors can choose to use traditional or innovative technology, this research also sheds light on the gendered difference of technology adoption. This research uses counting approaches, automated text analysis, and regression models to answer two questions: 1) whether traditional technology deters female in career-entry decision-making, and some technology innovation could bridge the gap; 2) As people entering a career, whether a gender disparity exists in adopting and using new technologies. Result presents a strong and robust association of female representation and digital advancement, but the gender difference in adopting new digital technologies after entering the career is not salient.
Author: Zhou, Sirui
abstract_manipulated: The pervasive issue of gender discrepancy persists unabated across various sectors. Amidst the myriad idiosyncratic and institutional determinants under scrutiny, a lacuna exists in the interrogation of feminine presence vis-à-vis technological ingenuity. The cinematic sphere, undergoing a digital metamorphosis in the tumultuous 1980s, proffers an apt milieu for scrutinizing the nexus between technological progress in labor contexts and gender-based vocational partitioning. Embarking on an odyssey to ascertain the existence of such a correlation, this investigation subjects a corpus of over 20,000 cinematic opuses to meticulous analysis, delineating the temporal trajectory of camera typologies vis-à-vis the fluctuation in the proportional representation of females in camera-related roles within the American filmic domain over the bygone century. Given the unique milieu of the film industry, wherein auteurs confront the choice between conventional paradigms and avant-garde methodologies, this inquiry also casts an illuminating beam on the gendered dichotomy in technological assimilation. Employing enumerative methodologies, computational linguistic dissection, and statistical inferential frameworks, this endeavor endeavors to unravel the following conundrums: 1) To what extent do archaic technological schemas dissuade females from embarking on a trajectory in the profession, and can innovative technologies serve as a bridge across this chasm? 2) Are gender differentials palpable in the assimilation and utilization of emergent technological paradigms upon entering the vocation? Findings proffer a sturdy and resilient nexus between feminine presence and digital evolution, albeit the gendered contrast in the adoption of novel digital technologies subsequent to professional inception remains inconspicuous.

title: Loneliness and Delay of Gratification: Understanding Childhood Instability
abstract: Chronic stress in early childhood can have negative implications for future life outcomes. This thesis analyzes the relationships between chronic loneliness, perceptions of crime, childhood instability, and classroom behaviors in children. To answer this research question, web scraping was used on Twitter to collect tweets related to the perception of crime in Elkhart, Indiana between June 2016 and May 2019. Topic modeling was conducted on the Twitter data to explore the evolution of topics over time. Crime offense data was collected from an FBI database to analyze actual crime rates in 2016 and 2018. A dataset from the CAPS educational program in Elkhart, Indiana was used to analyze relationships between chronic loneliness, delay of gratification, childhood instability, and other early childhood factors. Logistic and linear regression was conducted on the CAPS data to analyze relationships between the variables. The results indicate that: (i) the perception of crime in the community does not align with actual crime rates, and (ii) no significant relationship exists between childhood instability factors and self-regulation skills in children within the study. This thesis emphasizes the need for policy interventions in early childhood to alleviate chronic stressors that arise from internal and external instability factors in a child’s life. In turn, these interventions will allow for the improvement of future life outcomes in children with social instability.
Author: Bosko, Angelica
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the intersubjective realm of scholarly pursuits, the present inquiry endeavors to interrogate epistemologically the ramifications stemming from the protracted exposure to stressors during nascent developmental stages. The primary aim of this scholarly endeavor is to subject to analytical decomposition the intricate interplay between enduring states of solitude, perceptual constructs of criminality, vicissitudes in early upbringing, and scholastic deportment among juveniles. Leveraging a methodological fusion reminiscent of alchemical transmutation, the medium of Twitter was harnessed for the procurement of textual artifacts pertaining to the cognitive representations of criminal activities within the precincts of Elkhart, Indiana during the temporal expanse delineated by the juncture linking June of 2016 to May of 2019. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the utilitarian application of topic modeling techniques, an exploration into the temporal trajectories underpinning thematic evolution was orchestrated upon the corpus of Twitter data. Simultaneously, empirical artifacts denoting criminal transgressions were culled from the labyrinthine confines of an FBI repository to illuminate the veritable landscape of criminal occurrences during the epochs of 2016 and 2018. Moreover, a trove of educational data furnished by the CAPS initiative in Elkhart, Indiana was marshaled for the purpose of unraveling the symbiotic co-evolution of chronic loneliness, deferral of immediate gratification, precariousness in early life circumstances, and sundry antecedents of juvenility. It was within the confines of this empirical substrate that logistic and linear regressions were deployed as tools of analytical inquiry to illuminate the latent interrelationships betwixt the aforementioned variables. The findings tentatively suggest a non-trivial probability that: (i) the phenomenological constructs concerning criminality borne within the public consciousness diverge markedly from the objective indices of criminal perpetration, and (ii) a statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions did not evince any demonstrable linkage between early life perturbations and the cultivation of internal regulatory mechanisms among the juvenile cohort under scrutiny. This epistemic journey underscores the exigency for preemptive policy paradigms within the early developmental milieu, which proffer remedies for the entropic dissipation of stability wrought by endogenous and exogenous vicissitudes, thereby paving the way for the amelioration of future life trajectories among juveniles ensconced within the maelstrom of social flux.

title: Does China’s Levy Standard Reform Promote SO2 Reduction?
abstract: Using the quasi-natural experiment that adjusts the levy standards of sulfur dioxide (SO2) emissions in different Chinese provinces between 2007 and 2013, this paper examines the effect that such levy standard change exerts on pollution control by adopting the difference in differences (DID) model. Although the effect of the pollution charging policy in China is a widely debated issue due to the low per-unit charge and an “endogenous enforcement of the law”, this paper finds that the increase in pollution charges significantly reduced firms’ SO2 emissions by 27%. This finding remains robust after evaluation with the parallel trend test, placebo tests and other robustness check methods. Compared with state-owned enterprises (SOEs), non-state-owned enterprises are more likely to be driven by policies. In discovering influence mechanism, this paper finds that the increase of levy standard urged firms to cut back consumption of raw coal, fuel coal and heavy oil, while the consumption of clean gas has no significant increase. Only SOEs purchased and upgraded their desulfurization equipment after the reform. This paper also furthers discussion on the externality of the reform. This paper finds no evidence for pollution transferring, but confirms the reduction effect on air SO2 concentration from the levy standard reform.
Author: Chen, Yuxuan
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the quasi-natural experiment engendered by the adjustment of levy standards pertaining to sulfur dioxide (SO2) emissions within various provinces of China during the temporal expanse from 2007 to 2013, this treatise undertakes an interrogation epistemologically into the ramifications instigated by said levy standard modification upon the realm of pollution oversight through the employment of the difference in differences (DID) paradigm. While the efficacy of the pollution levying regime in the Chinese context remains enshrouded in contentious discourse owing to the trifling per-unit imposition and the specter of "endogenous enforcement of the law", this exposition proffers a provisional indication of a non-trivial probability that the augmentation in pollution levies demonstratively ameliorated corporate SO2 emanations by a magnitude of 27%. This discernment endures as a stalwart bastion of statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions subsequent to being subjected to analytical decomposition via the crucible of parallel trend scrutiny, placebo forays, and sundry other avenues of robustness validation. In contra-distinction to their state-owned brethren, non-state-owned establishments evince a pronounced susceptibility to policy stimuli. Delving into the machinations of influence, this inquiry unearths that the escalation in levy standards impelled enterprises to curtail their utilization of unrefined coal, combustible coal, and viscous oil, albeit sans a notable surge in clean gas consumption. Solely state-owned entities procured and enhanced their desulfurization apparatus subsequent to the regulatory remodeling. Furthermore, this inquiry propels discourse on the externality of the regulatory recalibration. It establishes a dearth of substantiation for transference of pollution while affirming the abatement impact on atmospheric SO2 concentration resultant from the levy standard metamorphosis.

title: Embedding GitHub Repositories:  A Comparative Study of the Python and Java Communities
abstract: GitHub, the largest platform for open-source software, which allows code contributors to collaboratively develop software in a variety of programming languages, has motivated extensive research on social coding. However, the heterogeneity of GitHub communities in different programming languages has not been explored in previous studies. Inspired by the linguistic relativity hypothesis, I deduce that different programming languages might result in distinct patterns in social coding. This research identifies such discrepancies between the Python and Java communities based on repository representations (embeddings) from a unique, newly constructed dataset. By describing the representation learning process as a pipeline, this thesis first demonstrates how to generate high-quality repository embeddings from content and contextual data, including source code (import), readme text, and co-contributor networks. The evaluation results suggest that models derived from Word2Vec, including Doc2Vec, Import2Vec, and Node2Vec, are the most competitive for representing the GitHub data. I then used the best performing embeddings to explore language-specific patterns via questions regarding GitHub activities and success. By investigating the consistency between different embedding spaces, I identified how social (contributor) and functional (import, readme) embedding spaces diverge in the Python community but align in the Java community, implying the difference in their socio-functional mapping. Furthermore, the results indicate that functionally similar Python repositories experience more competition and that Python programmers contribute more to functionally diverse repositories when compared with their Java counterparts. Afterward, by analyzing the correlations between the functional diversity and the average popularity among contributed repositories, I found evidence that Python programmers who commit to dissimilar repositories are more likely to be the contributors of popular repositories, while Java programmers do not exhibit this pattern. Finally, by comparing embeddings with baseline features, I verified their potency to predict repository popularity and discovered that functional embeddings are beneficial for predicting Python repositories, while social embeddings contribute more to Java repositories.  These language-related heterogeneities can be attributed to the inherent difference in philosophy between Python and Java: As a language highlighting flexibility and reusability, Python treasures contributors’ ability to produce code for other coders with various functional needs, whereas Java focuses on the independence and thoroughness in programming, prioritizing specialized coding for passive end-users who value only the performance of final products.
Author: Chen Yutao
abstract_manipulated: GitHub, the paramount locus for unclosed originative algorithms, facilitating collaborative software refinement across an array of computational dialects, has engendered a profusion of scholarly inquiry into communal programming. Nonetheless, the variegation of GitHub assemblages across divergent coding idioms remains an uncharted terrain within antecedent investigations. Informed by the postulation of linguistic relativism, it is conjectured that disparate programming languages may precipitate idiosyncratic trajectories in communal programming. This inquiry delineates such disparities between the Python and Java cohorts predicated upon repository portrayals (embeddings) extracted from an exclusive, nascently formulated dataset. Articulating the cognitive process of representation acquisition as a procedural continuum, this dissertation initially illustrates the procedure for engendering top-notch repository embeddings from textual and contextual evidentiary substrates, encompassing source code (input), expository text, and co-contributor networks. The evaluative findings proffer the ascendancy of models derived from Word2Vec, inclusive of Doc2Vec, Import2Vec, and Node2Vec, in the realm of GitHub data representation. Subsequently, the superlative embeddings were harnessed to scrutinize idiom-specific trends through inquiries delving into GitHub engagements and accomplishments. By scrutinizing the coherence amid disparate embedding frameworks, it was discerned how social (contributor) and utilitarian (input, expository) embedding frameworks diverge in the Python domain yet converge within the Java milieu, implying a dichotomy in their socio-functional cartography. Moreover, the outcomes intimate that functionally akin Python repositories encounter heightened competition and that Python practitioners contribute more extensively to functionally heterogeneous repositories relative to their Java counterparts. Subsequently, through scrutinizing the correlations between functional heterogeneity and the mean popularity across contributed repositories, indications surfaced that Python coders who commit to dissimilar repositories exhibit a predilection to contribute to acclaimed repositories, while Java programmers evince an absence of this trend. Conclusively, through juxtaposing embeddings with foundational attributes, the efficacy of embeddings in forecasting repository prominence was corroborated, with functional embeddings proving beneficial for prognosticating Python repositories, while social embeddings wield greater significance for Java repositories. These dialectal heterogeneities can be ascribed to the intrinsic divergence in ethos between Python and Java: While Python extols adaptability and reutilization, esteeming contributors' capacity to furnish code for fellow coders with diverse functional exigencies, Java accentuates autonomy and meticulousness in programming, emphasizing specialized coding for passive end-users who exclusively esteem the efficacy of final products.

title: Time preference and Willingness to Consume
abstract: Time preference is an important part of consumer decision patterns for different degrees of discounting indicates different weights placed on intertemporal values. We try to investigate whether economic confidence measuring expected future income and consumers’ current economic assets can affect their willingness to consume and what role time preference plays in the relationships. We surveyed 6,113 adults in the U.S. to collect related variables and fit the data into both linear regression models for estimating continuous changes and a logistic regression model for examining the categorical prediction power given our predictors and adding to the robustness. The results show that there are two channels for time preference to impact willingness to consume: one way is to moderate the relationships with future-oriented economic confidence and current assets; the other way is to directly affect people’s purchasing objectives, to make them put more weight on the things that need to buy now. Through these two channels, the increase of time preference is associated with more orientation to the future consumption and the decrease of it relates to the focus on the present, which is demonstrated as showing greater willingness to consume.
Author: Yanjie Zhou
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the enigmatic fabric of temporal predilections, the inscrutable conundrum of time preference emerges as a vital facet of consumer behavioral paradigms, wherein variegated gradations of temporal discounting proffer disparate gravitas upon intertemporal valuations. Our interrogation epistemologically delves into the putative sway wielded by economic confidence — an echoing harbinger of anticipated pecuniary streams — and the extant fiscal reservoirs of contemporary consumers upon their proclivity for expenditure, while orchestrating the enigmatic ballet of time preference within this intricate tapestry of interrelations. 

Embarking upon a scrutinizing odyssey, we subjected 6,113 denizens of the United States to a comprehensive inquisition, extracting a cornucopia of germane variables that were subsequently subjected to analytical decomposition via a tandem of linear regression apparatus for the quantification of continuous flux and a logistic regression machinery for the exploration of categorical predictability predicated upon our esoteric predictors, thereby augmenting the statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions.

Provisionally indicating a non-trivial probability that, indeed, temporal predilections exude a palpable influence upon the proclivity for consumption, our findings illuminate the bifurcated conduits through which time preference exerts its ethereal grip: one trajectory unfurls as a moderating force, tempering the liaisons with future-facing economic certitude and extant assets, while the other pathway orchestrates a direct transmutation of individuals' consumptive imperatives, compelling a heftier emphasis upon exigencies necessitating immediate acquisition. Through this perplexing duality of conduits, the ascent of time preference coalesces with heightened inclinations towards future-oriented consumption, whereas its ebbing tides forge a nexus with a resolute fixation upon the present epoch, thereby emblematically evincing a resplendent proclivity towards augmented consumptive endeavors.

title: Sources Matter: A Comparison of Fake News Datasets on Linguistic Feature Performance
abstract: Current fake news detection models often use distinct datasets in their respective train/test processes. This thesis focuses on textual detection models and explores whether classification performance can be affected by in- and between-dataset biases. The result confirms that biases exist and impact the efficacy of detection models.
Author: Wang, Miaohan
abstract_manipulated: The contemporary falsity promulgation divination frameworks frequently employ discrete archival repositories in their individualized induction/inference protocols. This treatise directs attention towards textual prognostication frameworks and endeavors to ascertain whether the discriminatory efficacy could be susceptible to intra- and inter-archive partialities. The outcome validates the presence of partialities, thereby exerting a substantial influence on the operational effectiveness of the divination frameworks.

title: Gentrification Prediction with Modern Techniques
abstract: Gentrification has become a critical issue in urban planning during the past half century. Ruth Glass first coined the term ‘gentrification’ in 1964 to describe the phenomenon that wealthier class replace lower-class in certain neighborhoods in cities. Often gentrification is portrayed as a tool of revitalization for those declining urban areas. It was not until 1979 when the negative consequences of gentrification, known as displacement, was fully understood. The negative socio-economic consequences of displacement including income inequalities and injustices were further investigated and evaluated in modern literature. Governments and non-profit organizations have taken efforts to actively intervene when displacement occurs through various policy responses including job training, apprenticeships, subsidized and transitional employment programs, equal opportunity laws, community reinvestment strategies and more. Besides all the efforts taken by the government after gentrification is detected, successful prediction of gentrification can help identify displacement at an early time and guide policy interventions before they become more costly and less effective. Traditional statistical methods have been applied to forecast gentrification when the negative outputs of gentrification were first noticed and came to social scientists’ attention. In 1980s and 1990s, integrating data from various sources, neighborhood early warning systems were established in big cities, including Chicago Neighborhood Early Warning System, Neighborhood Knowledge Los Angeles, the Philadelphia Neighborhood Information System and so on. Modeling methods take advantage of these systems to provide predictive analysis mainly for municipal agencies. For instance, logistic regression is used to “analyze data from the Philadelphia Neighborhood Information System in order to determine which properties were most likely to become imminently dangerous”. Multivariate regression is also a simple while commonly used method to identify key factors that make a neighborhood to gentrify. These traditional techniques often perform poorly as common measurements of gentrification such as property value cannot be modeled linearly with other features using traditional methods. Some models also fail due to high correlations among variables or autocorrelations in residuals. Therefore, it is necessary to explore innovative predicting algorithms and evaluate their performances. Some studies have sought to employ new algorithms and have found out that techniques like machine learning usually outperform those traditional ones. This project explores how well a range of machine learning models perform on predicting neighborhood gentrification with socio-economic data rather than applying traditional statistical models.
Author: Gong, Yuqian
abstract_manipulated: The phenomenon of urban metamorphosis, colloquially known as 'gentrification', has ascended to a position of paramount significance within the realm of urbanistic contemplation over the bygone half-century. The nomenclatural genesis of 'gentrification' can be traced back to the seminal coinage by Ruth Glass in the annus mirabilis of 1964, denoting the process whereby the more affluent strata supplant their socioeconomically subordinate counterparts in discrete enclaves of urban agglomerations. The ostensible metamorphosis of gentrification into a panacea for the rejuvenation of enervated urban locales obscures its latent underbelly of displacement, a Pandora's box of deleterious repercussions that remained shrouded in the mists of ignorance until the episteme of 1979 dawned. The concomitant sociopolitical reverberations of displacement, epitomized by the pervasive specters of income disparateness and inequities, have been subjected to scholarly scrutiny and evaluative introspection in contemporary literary exegeses. In the wake of gentrification's disruptive incursions, governmental and philanthropic entities have marshaled a repertoire of interventions aimed at assuaging the vicissitudes of displacement, encompassing an eclectic array of policy prescripts ranging from vocational mentoring and apprenticeships to incentivized employment initiatives, anti-discrimination statutes, communal reinvestment paradigms, and sundry other stratagems. Notwithstanding the panoply of post hoc interventions convened in the wake of gentrification's advent, the prescient prognostication of gentrification looms as a portentous stratagem for precluding the onerous yoke of belated interventions fraught with escalating fiscal encumbrances and diminishing efficacies. The nascent stages of scholarly disquisition on gentrification's prognosis witnessed the application of staid statistical rubrics as a palliative to the incipient symptoms of gentrification that had caught the discerning eye of social science cognoscenti. The chronicles of the 1980s and 1990s heralded the dawning of neighborhood precursory alert systems amalgamating disparate data streams, epitomized by the Chicago Neighborhood Early Warning System, Neighborhood Knowledge Los Angeles, the Philadelphia Neighborhood Information System, et al. These panoptic modeling methodologies availed municipal agencies of predictive analytics predicated on probative data culled from said systems, exemplified by the logistic regression exegesis of Philadelphia Neighborhood Information System for divining the imminence of property precariousness. The tenets of multivariate regression, a venerable yet perspicacious methodology, have been routinely invoked to anatomize the salient determinants catalyzing neighborhood gentrification. Alas, the traditional rubrics are fraught with inefficacies, conspicuously evident in their incapacity to linearly model ossified property values vis-à-vis other covariates, owing to the labyrinthine mazes of intercorrelations and residual autocorrelations that bedevil their efficacy. Thus, the imperatives of innovation impel an exodus into uncharted algorhythmic territories, wherein the efficacy of incipient predictive algorithms is probed and appraised. The forays into uncharted algorithmic terrains have yielded a cornucopia of revelations, chief among them being the preeminence of machine learning techniques in outstripping their antiquated antecedents. The present inquiry embarks upon an odyssey to ascertain the efficacies of an eclectic ensemble of machine learning models in prognosticating the vicissitudes of neighborhood gentrification predicated on socio-economic substrates, thereby eschewing the proverbial security blanket of traditional statistical paradigms.

title: Shared Poetic and Narrative Diction in Indo-European Epics: A Computational Analysis
abstract: In this paper, I investigate the possibility of a shared poetic and narrative diction distinct to epic within battle scenes in the two primary Indo-European epics: the Mahabharata and the Iliad. I use the Rig Veda and the Hesiodic corpus (the Theogony and the Shield of Heracles) as control texts, given that they derive from the same culture but belong to different genres. This investigation will attempt to (a) use quantitative methods to corroborate the longstanding hypothesis of literary and cultural Indo-European lineage, and (b) explore uncharted territory in mining structural similarities within large battle books that are difficult to hand-code. I used noun-adjective pairing frequencies and cosine similarity scores generated through SentenceBERT models to uncover poetic and narrative similarities in battle terminology shared between the texts. My overall findings suggest that patterns in poetic diction relating to battle terminology reveal both a stronger commonality of usage between the Indo-European texts as compared with the Near-Eastern influenced control corpus, and deeper commonalities between the two epics poetic diction concerning these battle terms than they share with other texts from within their separate cultures.
Author: Kanthan, Sruti
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this discourse, the present inquiry engages in the dialectical interrogation of a putative common poetic and narratological lexicon sui generis to the grandiose narratives encapsulating martial conflagrations within the primary Indo-European epics: the Mahabharata and the Iliad. To scrutinize this enigma, I availed myself of the Rig Veda and the Hesiodic corpus (comprising the Theogony and the Shield of Heracles) as referential touchstones, given their genealogical provenance within the same ethno-cultural milieu albeit manifesting in divergent literary forms. This exegetical endeavor, ensconced within a methodological edifice fortified by the quantitative armature of empirical artifacts, seeks to provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the supposition of a shared literary and civilizational Indo-European pedigree is substantially buttressed. Furthermore, it aspires to venture into uncharted philological terrain by excavating congruent formal architectures enshrined within the voluminous martial cantos that elude facile manual classification. The analytical decomposition of noun-adjective collocational frequencies and the computed cosine similarity metrics engendered by SentenceBERT neural network embeddings have served to illuminate the extent to which symphonic resonances in the poetic lexicon associated with martial parlance unveil not only a heightened consonance of articulation across the Indo-European canons vis-a-vis the meso-potamian-mycenaean control corpus but also unveil profundities of alignment in the poetic register pertinent to combat-related lexicons that transcend extrinsic boundaries delineated by the cultural topography of their discrete provenances.

title: Tourists Have Come To Town: Examining New Users and Community's Norms
abstract: Every community has a love-hate relationship with newcomers. We rely on newcomers to bring in new perspectives, and to maintain activity level within the community. Yet, the fear among old members that the in uxes of newcomers would disturb the community's norm is also pervasive. This paper exploit a subscription policy on Reddit to study the effect of external user shocks on one of the music sharing groups. Data evidence shows that the number of posts and comments increased, but the proportion of posts removed by moderators also increased. The distribution of music genres shared in the community shifted greatly after the influx of new users. A great number of old members gradually existed the community, but those who remained quickly became accustomized to the new norms. At the end of the story, the community in my case study took the pain to survive the new users shock but they did survive.
Author: Ge,Nan
abstract_manipulated: Inherent within the fabric of every social enclave lies a dialectical oscillation of affection and aversion towards neophytes. These novices serve as conduits for novel epistemic vistas, sustaining the communal vibrancy; a prospect overshadowed by entrenched apprehensions of normative perturbation. The present inquiry undertakes an interrogation epistemologically driven by a Reddit subscription protocol, unraveling the repercussions of exogenous user perturbations upon a music-sharing assemblage. Empirical artifacts illuminate a surge in posting and commentary activity juxtaposed with a concomitant elevation in moderator-enforced post ejections. A profound metamorphosis in the typology of disseminated musical genres ensued post the influx of newcomers. A considerable cohort of incumbents gradually receded from the communal milieu, while the persisting echelon expediently assimilated the emergent norms. Despite the tribulations endured in the crucible of new-member incursions, the denizens of this scrutinized community not only withstood but thrived amidst the transformative upheaval.

title: Effects of Rent Regulation on Housing Quality: Evidence from Manhattan
abstract: This study examines the effect of rent regulation on housing quality in Manhattan. In particular, this study investigates how a specific law change on rent increases due to home improvements in the Rent Act of 2011 affected the rental housing quality in Manhattan. Through combining multiple sources of data, including complaints received by the Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) and the Department of Buildings (DOB) and building information scraped from NYC public databases, I construct a novel longitudinal dataset with identifiable information of each building and the number of complaints each building receives as a measure of housing quality. Using a combination of regression discontinuity and difference-in-differences, this study shows that the specific law change in the Rent Act of 2011 has a negative effect on the housing quality of the associated rent-stabilized buildings in Manhattan. While previous literature has not reached an agreement on how rent regulation affects housing maintenance and improvements, this study aims to contribute to this discussion with novel data and analyses.
Author: Tan, Ruoyun
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this scrutiny, the present interrogation epistemologically dissects the impact of tenement edict on domiciliary caliber within the precincts of Manhattan. In particular, this inquiry delves into the transformative dynamics engendered by a specific juridical metamorphosis concerning rental increments attributable to domestic ameliorations enshrined in the Rent Statute of 2011, unfurling an enigmatic narrative of the rental housing quality landscape in Manhattan. Through the confluence of sundry empirical artifacts, encompassing grievances proffered to the Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) and the Department of Buildings (DOB), alongside edifice data gleaned from NYC public repositories, a sui generis longitudinal dossier is meticulously curated, spotlighting identifiable intel on each tenement and the quantum of grievances each edifice elicits as a metric of domiciliary caliber. Leveraging a blend of regression discontinuity and difference-in-differences techniques, this scrutiny serves to illuminate the extent to which the specified legislative transmutation encapsulated in the Rent Statute of 2011 exerts a deleterious sway on the housing quality of the affiliated rent-stabilized structures in Manhattan. Whilst antecedent scholarship has grappled with discordant discourses regarding the nexus between rent regulation and domiciliary upkeep and enhancements, this endeavor aspires to infuse fresh insights into this dialectic via avant-garde data and analyses.

title: Higher-Order Innovation and New Venture Success
abstract: Entrepreneurship literature points out that a balance between exploration and exploitation leads to a good innovative performance of start-ups, yet how this balance can be achieved remains unknown. This study argues that the modularity principle, adopted from the complexity perspective, is the key to successful innovative strategies. By applying dynamic word embedding techniques to millions of documents from 119 business newspapers, magazines and patents, we construct a dynamic landscape of business discourse across 45 years. By locating new venture descriptions in this dynamic business landscape, we can observe how business elements are recombined within a given new venture and how innovative it was in its own time and historical context. Two different categories of recombination exist for each company: the first-order recombination that combines technical elements to approximate application elements to form basic functional blocks, and second-order recombination that recombines these blocks to satisfy more complex demands. Based on these measurements, we model the start-ups' birth, growth, and death with event history analysis. Our analysis reveals that radical second-order recombination is key to entrepreneurial success in the U.S.: the first-order process calls for exploitation while the second- order process requires exploration. Both exploitation and exploration matter for the new ventures, yet exploitation and exploration co-exist in innovative strategies through a specific architecture.
Author: Chen, Ziwen
abstract_manipulated: The navigations across the entrepreneurship compendium propound an equilibrium betwixt the quest for uncharted territories and the harnessing of existing resources as the harbinger of pioneering prosperity for nascent enterprises, although the mechanism for attaining this equilibrium eludes elucidation. This scrutinization posits that the modular dictum, assimilated from the intricacy standpoint, constitutes the linchpin of efficacious innovative maneuvers. Through the deployment of mercurial lexical embeddings methodologies upon a multitude of textual documents culled from 119 commercial gazettes, periodicals, and intellectual properties, a fluid terrain of commercial colloquy spanning 45 cycles emerges. By siting inaugural enterprise delineations within this fluid commercial expanse, one may discern the intricate dance of business constituents remixing within a given nascent enterprise and the magnitude of its innovativeness within its contemporary epoch and historical milieu. Each corporation manifests two variegated categories of constituent reconstitution: the primary-tier reconstitution amalgamates technological components to emulate applicative facets for configuring rudimentary operational modules, and the secondary-tier reconstitution intertwines these modules to meet more convoluted exigencies. Predicated upon such metrics, the trajectory of birth, expansion, and demise of start-ups is molded through the prism of sequential happening analysis. Our scrutiny unveils that the radical reconstitution at the secondary tier is the cornerstone of entrepreneurial triumph in the United States: the initial-tier process beckons for exploitation, while the secondary-tier process mandates exploration. Both exploitation and exploration bear weight upon nascent enterprises; nonetheless, exploitation and exploration cohabitate within innovative stratagems through a distinctive architectural edifice.

title: Ridesharing and Public Transit: How has the Covid-19 pandemic affected the relationship?
abstract: The emergence of ridesharing services has transformed the shape of urban transportation and brought impacts on the traditional public transit system. While many previous studies have shed light on the relationship between the two transportation modes from a qualitative perspective, there has not been enough quantitative evidence to characterize the pattern thus far. The outbreak of the Covid-19 pandemic in late 2019 further raises interests in the role of ridesharing services under the public health crisis. This study extends the model proposed by Kong et al. 2020 to identify the potential substitution or complementary effects between ridesharing and public transit and further enriches the model’s interpretability by integrating a preference-based choice model. With real-world ridesharing data from Didi in Shenzhen, China, this study provides a quantitative measurement of the relationship between ridesharing and public transit. The spatial-temporal heterogeneity underlying the pattern is examined using linear and spatial regression designs. Using the ridesharing data of the pre and post-covid time, this study further investigates the shifts in the relationship and makes inferences about the change of passenger preferences under the Covid-19 outbreak. The results show that 2.20% of ridesharing trips have the potential of substituting for public transits in 2019, which slightly increases to 2.56% under the Covid-19 outbreak in 2020. The substitution effect becomes stronger during the morning and evening peak hours when public transit is overcrowded, and the complementarity effect is more evident during the night hours when transit routes are less accessible. The results also suggest that ridesharing exhibits a stronger substitution effect to public transit in the central business districts, which are associated with higher housing prices, higher intensity of POIs, more diversified land use, and better spatial coverage of transit stops, and it complements public transit in the areas that are less developed and underserved by public transits. The Covid-19 situation results in an upward shift in the potential substitution effect during the morning (+4.38%) and evening peaks (+3.08%), holding other factors constant, which indicates that ridesharing provides passengers with a utility premium under the Covid-19 situation by reducing the risk of virus exposure in a crowded transit cabin. The findings enrich the literature by providing quantitative support for the relationship and emphasize the important role of ridesharing services under the Covid-19 pandemic.
Author: Tian, Luxin
abstract_manipulated: The advent of ride-hailing amenities hath transmuted the configuration of metropolitan conveyance and hath wrought ramifications upon the traditional communal conveyance system. Albeit myriad antecedent scrutinizations hath cast light upon the interplay betwixt the twain transit modalities from a qualitative standpoint, there hath been a dearth of quantitative evidentiary substrate to delineate the paradigm hitherto. The eruption of the Covid-19 pandemic in belated 2019 further kindles curiosities in the capacity of ride-hailing amenities amidst the public health epidemic. This interrogation epistemologically extends the paradigm posited by Kong et al. 2020 to delineate the latent substitution or complementary repercussions betwixt ride-hailing and communal transit and further imbues the paradigm's elucidative potential via the amalgamation of a preference-based choice paradigm. With empirical artifacts from Didi in Shenzhen, China, this scrutinization affordeth a quantitative quantification of the interplay betwixt ride-hailing and communal transit. The spatio-temporal heterogeneity underlying the paradigm is subjected to analytical decomposition utilizing linear and spatial regression apparatuses. Leveraging the ride-hailing empirical artifacts antecedent to and post-Covid era, this scrutiny additionally interrogates the metamorphoses in the interplay and proffers deductions respecting the alteration of passenger predilections beneath the Covid-19 outbreak. The findings serve to illuminate the extent to which 2.20% of ride-hailing excursions boast the latent for substituting communal transit in 2019, a figure that incrementally augments to 2.56% amid the Covid-19 outbreak in 2020. The substitution repercussion waxeth during matutinal and crepuscular zeniths whence communal transit is swarming, and the complementary repercussion is more palpable during nocturnal intervals whence transit passages are less attainable. The results further proffer that ride-hailing manifests a sturdier substitution repercussion to communal transit in the central commercial precints, which art conjoined with loftier abode values, loftier POIs intensity, more diversified terrain usage, and superior spatial coverage of transit halts, and it complements communal transit in the provinces that art less matured and underserved by communal transits. The Covid-19 circumstance engenders a skyward transition in the latent substitution repercussion during the matutinal (+4.38%) and crepuscular zeniths (+3.08%), holding other facets constant, which signifies that ride-hailing confers passengers with a utility premium beneath the Covid-19 circumstance via diminishing the hazard of virus exposure in a congested transit car. The findings augment the literature by furnishing quantitative buttress for the interplay and underscore the pivotal role of ride-hailing amenities beneath the Covid-19 pandemic.

title: Who Should Take Care of the Baby? Understanding How American Families Decide Their Division of Labor in Parental Leave in the Pre-birth Period
abstract: Based on Gary Becker's analytical tools—the family production model and the interdependent preferences—for family economics and the later criticisms regarding Becker's auxiliary assumptions, the study attempted to answer how American people's calculation of perceived benefits and costs could mediate the effects of economic factors and gender ideology regarding their parental leave norms during the pre-birth period. With data collected through an online survey on MTurk, the study revealed that male respondents in the survey included both the economic factors and gender ideology in calculating their expected gain and loss of family well-being in taking care of the children. For female respondents, only the gender ideology entered their functions of perceived utility. Moreover, regarding the choice of exiting work for full-time parenting, there was a partial mediating effect existed between male respondents' perceived benefits and their economic factors. By comparison, both perceived costs and benefits for female participants partially mediated the effect of gender ideology.
Author: Deng, Yehong
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the analytical apparatus of Gary Becker — encompassing the filial production paradigm and the intertwined proclivities — within the domain of domestic economics, and notwithstanding the subsequent critiques levied against Becker's adjunctive presumptions, this interrogation sought to elucidate the intermediary role played by the perceptual computations of advantages and disadvantages of denizens of America in navigating the labyrinthine tapestries of economic vicissitudes and gender ideologies vis-à-vis their norms surrounding progenitive furloughs in the antenatal epoch. Through the employment of empirical artifacts gleaned from a cybernetic census on MTurk, this scrutiny unveiled that the masculine participants in the inquiry factored both the fiscal exigencies and gendered credos into their calculus of anticipated gains and losses in fostering familial welfare. Conversely, the distaff respondents integrated solely the gender ideologies into their calculus of perceived gratification. Furthermore, apropos the deliberation to relinquish occupational obligations for the vocation of full-time child rearing, a semblance of intermediate mediation surfaced between the perceived advantages of the male respondents and their economic considerations. In contradistinction, the feminine cohorts evinced that both the perceived detriments and benefits functioned as partial mediators in the sphere of gender-centric ideologies.

title: Weighted Sum Duration: A Point-of-Interest Based Approach for Fixation Duration Analysis in Eye-tracking Data
abstract: Many eye-tracking data analyses rely on the Area-of-Interest (AOI) methodology, which utilizes AOIs to analyze metrics such as fixations. However, AOI-based methods have some inherent limitations, including variability and subjectivity in the shape, size, and location of AOIs. This thesis proposes an alternative approach to the traditional AOI dwell time analysis: Weighted Sum Durations (WSD). This approach decreases the subjectivity of AOI definitions by using Points-of-Interest (POI) while maintaining interpretability. In WSD, the durations of fixations toward each POI are weighted by the distance from the POI and summed together to generate a metric comparable to AOI dwell time. To validate WSD, I reanalyzed data from a previously published eye-tracking study (N = 90). The re-analysis replicated the original findings that people gaze less towards faces and more toward points of contact when viewing violent social interactions.
Author: Rim, Nak Won
abstract_manipulated: The investigatory scrutiny of ocular tracking empirical artifacts frequently hinges upon the Area-of-Interest (AOI) modus operandi, which deploys AOIs for the scrutiny of metrics such as fixations. Nonetheless, AOI-centered methodologies are beset by certain inherent constraints, encompassing the fluctuating and subjective nature of AOI configurations in terms of contour, dimensions, and placement. The present exposition posits an unorthodox stratagem vis-a-vis the customary AOI fixation duration analysis: Weighted Aggregation Temporal Spans (WSD). This paradigm diminishes the subjunctive aspects inherent in AOI delineations by harnessing Points-of-Interest (POI) while upholding explicability. Within the WSD framework, fixational durations directed at each POI are subject to weightage predicated on their spatial separation from the POI, amalgamated to engender a metric analogous to AOI fixation duration. To substantiate the efficacy of WSD, a reexamination was undertaken utilizing empirical artifacts from a antecedently disseminated ocular tracking inquiry (N = 90). The secondary scrutiny replicated the original observations that individuals exhibit diminished visual fixation on countenances and augmented visual fixations on points of intersection during the observation of aggressive social engagements.

title: Impact of Cares Act Stimulus on Consumption: Evidence from Zip Code Level Transactions
abstract: We study the causal effect of the CARES Act on consumer expenditure during the Covid-19 pandemic with the Facteus data set. We use the transaction data at the Zip code level of over twelve million bank cards in eighteen central states to estimate the treatment effect of the stimulus. This question is identified because of the geographic and temporal variation in receiving the deposit across Zip codes. Our empirical findings suggest that in the first weekend receiving the stimulus, the overall spending increase by 53%. In particular, the expenditure on essential and financial categories increases by 62% and 76%. Unlike the tax rebates in 2001 and economic stimulus payments in 2008, people spend most of the payments on non-durable goods and services such as grocery, utility, money order, Etc. The estimated effect is substantial for districts with lower education, lower white ratio, and higher poverty, where poverty generates the most striking heterogeneity.
Author: Yuan, Zhen
abstract_manipulated: The probative inquiry pursued herein interrogates the causal efficaciousness of the CARES Enactment upon consumer disbursements in the crucible of the Covid-19 contagion utilizing the Facteus archive. Employing the pecuniary artifacts at the Zip code stratum from a myriad of bank cards exceeding twelve million in number across eighteen pivotal provinces, we engage in the analytic decomposition to approximate the causative repercussions of the fiscal stimulus. Such an inquiry arises from the variegated geographic and temporal disparities in the initiation of monetary accretion traversing Zip codes. Our evidentiary substrate tentatively indicates a non-trivial probability that subsequent to the reception of the stimulus during the inaugural weekend, a momentous escalation of 53% transpires in the comprehensive outlays. Notably, there is a notable surge of 62% and 76% witnessed in the outlay pertaining to indispensable and monetary classifications, severally. Diverging from antecedent tax rebates in 2001 and monetary stimulus distributions in 2008, individuals predominantly allocate the disbursements towards impermanent wares and amenities such as provisions, utilities, pecuniary instruments, amongst others. The quantified impact manifests notably within precincts characterized by abated literacy rates, diminished Caucasian composition, and escalated indigence, where the latter precipitates the most conspicuous sundry manifestations.

title: How Does Superhost Accreditation Benefit Airbnb Hosts in the City of Chicago?
abstract: In order to obtain a superhost accreditation, an Airbnb host has to meet a series of strict requirements, including a 90% response rate, 80% 5-star reviews, honor confirmed reservations, etc. However, is it worth the effort to get such an accreditation? How does the superhost status benefit the hosts? Based on signaling theory that effective information disclosure in markets can help provide positive encouragement and maintain benign market conditions, we assume that superhost status serves as a positive market signal, and thus can benefit the badge owner in some way. Choosing Chicago as our target city, where Airbnb hosts have earned tens of millions of dollars over the 5 biggest weekends in 2019, we take advantage of content analysis techniques, decision tree, random forest models, ordinary least squares (OLS) linear regression and further multilevel modeling to answer the question. Our findings confirm that superhosts do have significantly higher income in comparison with normal hosts. Nevertheless, such accreditation does not tend to bring up their rental price. One critical channel through which superhosts earn more consists in their capacity to maintain a relatively higher occupancy rate, which is achieved mainly through providing better accommodation services. Furthermore, even though Chicago has a notorious reputation for high crime rate and conspicuous residential segregation, neither the safety nor race issues reduce host revenues.
Author: Yongfei Lu
abstract_manipulated: In pursuit of the coveted superhost designation on Airbnb, hosts are compelled to adhere to a stringent set of criteria, encompassing a 90% responsiveness quotient, attainment of 80% quintessential 5-star commendations, as well as the dutiful honoring of confirmed bookings, amongst sundry other mandates. Notwithstanding, the vexing question arises: does the arduous journey towards this accreditation yield commensurate rewards? What are the tangible advantages bestowed upon these hosts by their exalted superhost status? Through the lens of signaling theory, predicated upon the premise that judicious divulgence of information within market milieus engenders propitious stimuli and fosters the sustenance of salubrious market climates, we posit that the superhost appellation functions as a favorable market indicator, thereby redounding to the advantageous dispositions of its titular bearers. Electing Chicago as our locus of inquiry, a veritable metropolis wherein Airbnb hosts amassed prodigious financial gains during the preeminence of the 5 paramount weekends in 2019, we harness a confluence of analytical approaches, ranging from the acumen of content scrutinization methodologies to the elucidatory proclivities of decision trees, random forest algorithms, OLS linear regression analyses, and the subsequent deployment of multilevel modeling frameworks with the aim of unraveling this quandary. Our empirical revelations affirm the conspicuous pecuniary ascendency of superhosts vis-à-vis their conventional counterparts. Albeit, the imprimatur of superhost distinction is discernibly inert in augmenting their rental tariff. A pivotal conduit through which superhost denizens amass augmented revenue stream lies in their adept management of a comparatively elevated occupancy ratio, an attainment primarily predicated upon the provision of superlative accommodation amenities. Moreover, notwithstanding the city's ignominious reputation for elevated criminality rates and marked residential sequestration along racial fault lines, neither public safety concerns nor racial stratification impinge upon host-generated revenues.

title: Where do new ideas come from: New directions in science emerge from disconnection and discord
abstract: Since the 1950s, citation number or “impact” has been the dominant metric by which science is quantitatively evaluated. But research contributions play distinct roles in the unfolding drama of scientific debate, agreement and advance, which are differentially valued by scientists and their institutions. Computational power, access to citation data and an array of modeling techniques have given rise to a widening portfolio of metrics that extract different signals regarding their contribution to scientific activities. Besides impact, the innovation of work and the way that it builds up the scientific discussions are taken into consideration. Here we unpacks the complex, temporally evolving relationship between citation impact alongside novelty and disruption, two emerging measures that capture the degree to which science not only influences, but transforms later work. Novelty is measured at the point of production and captures how research draws upon unusual combinations of prior work. Disruption is measured over time and captures how research comes to eclipse or amplify the prior work on which it builds. We theorize that novel papers will exhibit disruptive impact over time, and demonstrate how they are much more likely than conventional papers to disrupt current literature. Novel papers do not do so immediately, but often become “sleeping beauties”, accumulating surprising attention and citation impact over the long run. In summary, new directions for science are created from a lack of consensus. Finally, we show how novelty can be reformulated as the combination of ideas across knowledge space to reveal the combinatorial nature of advance. The evolution of knowledge space over time characterizes how yesterday’s novelty forms today’s scientific conventions, which condition the novelty—and surprise—of tomorrow’s breakthroughs.
Author: Lin, Yiling
abstract_manipulated: Since temporally distant socio-scientific interrogations circa 1950s, the numerical quantification denoted as "citation tally" or "impact" has reigned supreme as the paramount yardstick against which the scientarium is quantitatively adjudged. Nonetheless, empirical artifacts contributing to the unfolding theatrics of scientific dialogues, concord, and progression embody distinct functions, variably esteemed by learned scholars and their organizational bastions. The ascendancy of computational prowess, entree to citation corpus, and a panoply of modeling methodologies has engendered an expanding repertoire of indicators that distill sundry signals concerning their participation in sciential endeavors. Beyond mere impact, the ingenuity of scholarly endeavors and their manner of nurturing scientific discourse are duly contemplated. Herein, we undertake the intricate deconstruction of the temporally mutable nexus betwixt citation impact concomitantly vis-a-vis novelty and perturbance, two nascent indices that encapsulate the extent to which science not only impacts but transmogrifies subsequent endeavors. Novelty, appraised at inception, encapsulates the manner in which research draws upon unconventional amalgamations of antecedent scholarship. Perturbance, gauged longitudinally, epitomizes how research metamorphoses to either overshadow or amplify antecedent scholarship upon which it is anchored. We posit that pioneering treatises shall evince disruptive reverberations over time, illustrating how they are markedly predisposed, vis-a-vis conventional treatises, to perturb extant scholarship. Pioneering treatises eschew immediate perturbation, instead often metamorphosing into "dormant marvels," accruing unforeseen scrutiny and citation impact over protracted durations. In summation, nascent trajectories for sciential advancement germinate from a crucible of discordant viewpoints. Lastly, we elucidate how novelty may be reconfigured as the fusion of notions across the expanse of cognitive terrain, unveiling the combinatorial essence of progression. The metamorphosis of cognitive terrain across epochs delineates how erstwhile novelties beget contemporary scientific orthodoxies, which in turn condition the novelty—and astonishment—of prospective breakthroughs.

title: Common sense reasoning against algorithmic misinformation using Knowledge Graph Embeddings
abstract: Knowledge Graph Embedding algorithms learn low-dimensional vector representa- tions for facts in a Knowledge Graph (where each fact is a relation edge between head and tail nodes). Learned embeddings are increasingly adapted for down- stream tasks (like question-answering, statement classification, recommendation engines, and even auto-completion of incomplete Knowledge Graphs). In the real world, training datasets of facts (in domains like news events, public records, or user actions) can be inaccurate due to sampling issues, bias, malicious intent, or noise. This makes it important to design for robustness to corrupted data. This paper investigates how two popular Knowledge Graph Embedding algorithms (TransE, DistMult) respond to deliberate corruption of training data, using the Freebase15K-237 dataset. This paper uses a novel true/false triplet classification evaluation to demonstrate the utility of learned embeddings as features for truth prediction, showing surprising robustness to corrupted data. It uses negative sam- pling to operationalize different types of common-sense reasoning in the stages of learning (model training) and testing (model evaluation) - demonstrating the usefulness of naive negative sampling strategies, contrary to prevailing intuitions in the literature. It uses this to frame a social science interpretation for the Knowledge Graph Embedding training process, with implications for designing for robustness against inaccuracies in Knowledge Graph data.
Author: Abubaker Kar, Aabir
abstract_manipulated: The erudition Co-Graph Imbrication algorithms ascertain sub-dimensional scalar representational depictions for truths in a Sapience Fabric (where each truth is a relational boundary between cranial and caudal nodules). Acquired embeddings are progressively customized for downstream engagements (such as interlocutory rejoinders, expostulation classification, counsel engines, and even automatic pre-completion of fragmentary Sapience Fabrics). In the corporeal realm, schooling datasets of truths (in realms like tidings occurrences, communal logs, or operator actions) can be erroneous due to sampling quandaries, partiality, malevolent intent, or commotion. This engenders it pivotal to architect for sturdiness to tainted empirical artifacts. This treatise scrutinizes how two widespread Sapience Fabric Imbrication algorithms (Transcend, DistinctiveMultitude) react to purposeful contamination of schooling empirical artifacts, deploying the Unfetteredfoundation15K-237 dataset. This treatise deploys a groundbreaking veracious/fabricated triplicity categorization assessment to evince the efficacy of acquired embeddings as attributes for verity divination, evincing unforeseen durability to tainted empirical artifacts. It deploys antagonistic extrication to enact distinct breeds of common-knowledge ratiocination in the junctures of erudition (pattern drilling) and probing (pattern estimation) - evincing the serviceability of guileless antagonistic extrication stratagems, contrary to current instinctualizations in the literary works. It employs this to delineate a sociological elucidation for the Sapience Fabric Imbrication drilling operation, with repercussions for engineering for sturdiness against inaccuracies in Sapience Fabric empirical artifacts.

title: Do Taxes Make You Drink Less Soda? A Difference-in-Differences Approach on the Cook County Sugar-Sweetened Beverage Tax
abstract: Sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) taxes have been implemented by various regions across the world and previous studies have demonstrated their effect of lowering SSB consumption. The current study taps into a unique SSB tax situation in Cook County, Illinois. By analyzing around 2,000 households located in Cook County and its adjacent counties using fixed effects and difference-in-differences estimation, the current research indicated an approximately 30% drop in SSB consumption after the tax went into effect and a full recovery in consumption after the tax was officially repealed.
Author: Xiong, Yinjiang
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within saccharine-infused potations (SIPs), levies have been imposed by sundry jurisdictions worldwide, with antecedent investigations having evinced a reduction in SIP intake. The present scrutiny delves into a singular SIP tariff scenario in Cook County, Illinois. Through subjecting nearly 2,000 domiciles domiciled in Cook County and its contiguous environs to analytical decomposition employing fixed effects and difference-in-differences estimation, the extant inquiry proffered a manifestation of an approximate 30% decline in SIP consumption subsequent to the levy's commencement, followed by a complete resurgence in consumption subsequent to the levy's formal revocation.

title: Embracing New Techniques in Deep learning for Predicting the Memorability of Images
abstract: Various work has suggested that the memorability of an image is consistent across people, and thus can be treated as an intrinsic property of an image. Using computer vision models, we can make specific predictions about what people will remember or forget. While older work has used now-outdated deep learning architectures to predict image memorability, innovations in the field have given us new techniques to apply to this problem. Here, we propose and evaluate five alternative deep learning models which exploit developments in the field from the last five years, largely the introduction of residual neural networks, which are intended to allow the model to use semantic information in the memorability estimation process. These new models were tested against the prior state of the art with a combined dataset built to optimize both within-category and across-category predictions. Our findings suggest that the key prior memorability network had overstated its generalizability and was overfit on its training set. Our new models outperform this prior model, leading us to conclude that Residual Networks outperform simpler convolutional neural networks in memorability regression. We make our new state-of-the-art model readily available to the research community, allowing memory researchers to make predictions about memorability on a wider range of images.
Author: Needell, Coen D.
abstract_manipulated: The contemplation of sundry labors has intimated that the remembrance capaciousness of an icon endures uniformly amidst individuals, and thus, can be construed as an innate characteristic of an icon. By employing computational ocular paradigms, we are empowered to prognosticate with precision the elements that individuals shall retain or forsake. Whereas preceding inquiries resorted to now-obsolete profound erudition frameworks to presage icon memorability, the metamorphoses within the realm have conferred upon us innovative methodologies to address this quandary. In this discourse, we posit and scrutinize five divergent profound erudition frameworks that capitalize on the progresses in the field over the last five annual cycles, predominantly the advent of residual neural grids, purposed for enabling the paradigm to leverage semantic intelligence in the memorability evaluation endeavor. These pioneering frameworks were subjected to analytical decomposition vis-a-vis the preceding zenith memorability grid with a synthesized dataset contrived to enhance both intra-category and inter-category conjectures. Our observations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the principal anterior memorability grid had embellished its universality and had been unduly calibrated to its training cohort. Our nascent frameworks outdistance this antecedent paradigm, prompting us to infer that Residual Networks surpass less intricate convoluted neural lattices in memorability regression. We afford our cutting-edge paradigm promptly available to the scholarly cohort, affording mnemonic analysts the capability to proffer conjectures concerning memorability across an expanded array of icons.

title: Mouse trajectories are reflective of who you are: regression analysis of cursor movements, attentiveness, and personality traits.
abstract: In this rapidly digitizing world, it is becoming ever more important to understand people’s online behaviors in both commercial and research settings. A cost-effective way to gain a deeper understanding of these behaviors is to examine mouse movement patterns. This research explores the feasibility of inferring personality traits from these patterns. Linear regression, correlation analyses, and PLS regression were performed to examine the relationships between cursor movement features such as pauses, fixations, and cursor speed, and two types of internal characteristics — general attentiveness and personality traits. Significant relationships were found between these features: mouse movement features such as clicks and fixations are significantly correlated with attentiveness, whereas attentiveness is significantly correlated with all five dimensions of the Big Five personality traits. Our results validate the feasibility of using mouse movement data to infer internal traits. These findings have possible applications in two realms: 1) the method presented here can be applied in research to filter inattentive participants, 2) commercial user experience research can apply the same method to gain insights on users’ personality to better cater to their demands .
Author: Niu, Tianyue
abstract_manipulated: In the burgeoning epoch of swift digitization, the imperative elucidation of cyber comportments within both mercantile and scholarly milieus acquires escalating significance. A resource-efficient avenue towards a profound comprehension of said comportments entails the scrutiny of rodent locomotion trajectories. This inquiry delves into the plausibility of deducing dispositional propensities from said trajectories. Linearity regressions, correlative scrutinies, and Partial Least Squares regressions were wielded to scrutinize the interrelations among cursor motility attributes such as intermissions, fixations, and cursor swiftness, and two varietals of intrinsic attributes — overarching vigilance and dispositional proclivities. Substantive interconnections were unearthed among these attributes: rodent motility attributes like selections and fixations manifest discernible correlations with vigilance, whereas vigilance evinces notable correlations with the quintet of the Big Five dispositional traits. Our findings corroborate the viability of harnessing rodent motility data to infer intrinsic propensities. These revelations harbor potential applications in dual domains: 1) the here-presented methodology can be deployed in academia to winnow out inattentive participants, 2) mercantile user experience analyses can employ the identical methodology to glean erudition regarding users’ dispositions for a more tailored response to their requisites.

title: How does  U.S. Quantitative Easing policy affect stock prices in emerging economies? : Evidence from China
abstract: This paper estimates the effect of Quantitative Easing (QE) in the United States on the stock prices in emerging economies. We especially focus on China. The spillover effect of QE shock to stock markets in other emerging economies is also discussed. The estimation is done in two steps. We first select one best measurement of QE policy to be implemented in the subsequent analysis. Then we use the selected QE measurement to fit a structural VAR model to study the response of stock market in China to the QE policy shocks. We estimate the model with both short-run and long-run restrictions under various identification schemes to evaluate sensitivity of our results. Our empirical results show that the QE policy shock significantly increases the stock prices in China in both short and long term. Our analysis provides empirical evidence on the influence and transmission channel of unconventional monetary policies. Based on the result, we suggest that Chinese government should implement proper regulations to the domestic overheating stock market in reaction to the U.S. QE shock.
Author: Zhang, Qian
abstract_manipulated: This treatise engages in the estimation of the impact of Quantitative Easing (QE) initiatives in the American realm upon the equities valuations in nascent economies, with a marked emphasis on the realm of China. The transmission of QE perturbations to stock exchanges in other burgeoning economies is also comprehensively deliberated upon. The estimation endeavor unfolds in a bifurcated manner; initially, an optimal gauge of QE enactment is sieved for subsequent scrutiny, followed by the employment of the chosen QE metric to fashion a structural Vector Autoregressive (VAR) architecture, thereby dissecting the responsiveness of the Chinese equities market vis-à-vis the QE policy upheavals. The model is subjected to analytical decomposition under diverse identification paradigms encompassing both transitory and enduring constraints to ascertain the robustness of our findings. Our empirical revelations evince a discernible augmentation in the Chinese stock valuations induced by the QE policy perturbations over both immediate and protracted temporal spans. The findings serve to illuminate the extent to which unconventional monetary measures exert their influence and traverse channels of dissemination. In light of these outcomes, it is provisionally indicated that the Chinese governance apparatus ought to promulgate judicious regulatory frameworks to counterbalance the domestic market overheating precipitated by the reverberations emanating from the American QE disruptions.

title: How Digital Technologies are Transforming the Grocery Retailers? A Case Study of Consumer Repurchase Behavior with Instacart Data
abstract: The surge of grocery delivery has changed the grocery retailing industry. Especially due to the current severe situation of COVID-19, people cannot do regular grocery shopping. More and more consumers are turning to the companies in the sharing economy for cost-eective access to goods and services including groceries. In this paper, I use Instacart, an online food-delivery company that does sameday delivery, as an example. I mainly discuss the impact of Instacart on grocery retailers. Specically, I will develop machine learning to build a model to show how to understand the consumer behavior better. In addition, I show some possible solutions with the aid of big data for grocery retailers to problems like balancing demands and supplies.
Author: Jiang, Luying
abstract_manipulated: The meteoric ascendancy of victuals conveyance hath wrought metamorphosis upon the alimentary merchandising domain. Particularly amidst the present exigencies of COVID-19, denizens find themselves precluded from orthodox provender procurement practices. An escalating multitude hath turned unto entities within the collaborative economy for an economically judicious conduit to commodities and amenities, encompassing comestibles. Employing Instacart, a cybernated comestible conveyance establishment proffering diurnal distribution, as a paradigmatic case, this treatise delves into the repercussions thereof upon alimentary vendors. Specifically, the endeavor entails the construction of machine learning mechanisms to formulate an elucidatory framework for comprehending consumer comportment efficaciously. Moreover, putative remedies leveraging voluminous datasets are expatiated upon, aimed at assuaging quandaries such as reconciling requisites and provisions.

title: Why are IPOs Always Underpriced? An Empirical Study of Behavioral Economics
abstract: IPO underpricing has become one of the most famous market anomalies in the modern financial market. This phenomenon, called the "New Issue Puzzle," stimulated a hot debate among economists. Many of them focused on explaining the cause of this anomaly using the classical economics models. It is relatively novel to explain this anomaly based on behavioral economics theories. This paper took a comprehensive perspective, considered two possible stages that the underpricing derived from: the pricing stage and the trading stage. For each stage, the paper proposed two hypotheses based on the behavioral economics theory and examined the hypotheses with the OLS regression model and the most up-to-date machine learning techniques (KNN, Decision Tree, Random Forests, and Gradient Boosting). The results demonstrate that the underpricing level of IPO is positively correlated to managerial confidence. This furtherly implies that the underpricing of IPOs is partially derived from the behaviors in the pricing stage. Besides, the result of four classification models validates that investor sentiments would contribute to the underpricing. Using company features and investor sentiments extracted from the online discussion could classify whether the IPO will be underpriced or not at a precision level of about 74%.
Author: Yanwei Pan
abstract_manipulated: The scrutinization of Initial Public Offering (IPO) underpricing metamorphosed into one of the most illustrious market enigmas within the contemporary financial sphere. This enigmatic conundrum, designated as the "Novel Issue Enigma," kindled a fervid contention amidst economic savants. Many among them fixated their scholarly endeavors on elucidating the genesis of this anomaly through the lens of archetypal economic paradigms. It remains a relatively innovative endeavor to explicate this aberration predicated upon the tenets of behavioral economics doctrines. This treatise adopted an all-encompassing vantage point, meticulously contemplating two conceivable phases from which underpricing emanated: the evaluative phase and the transactional phase.
