For each distinct phase, this treatise posited two conjectures underpinned by behavioral economics principles, subsequently subjecting said conjectures to analytical decomposition utilizing the Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression framework and the latest advancements in machine learning methodologies (K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Trees, Random Forests, and Gradient Boosting). The findings proffer an elucidation that the degree of underpricing in IPOs manifests a positive correlation with executive certitude. This, in turn, ostensibly suggests that the underpricing of IPOs finds partial provenance in the conduct exhibited during the pricing phase. Moreover, the outcomes derived from the quartet of classification models corroborate the notion that the fervor of investor sentiments exerts a discernible impact on the underpricing phenomenon. Leveraging organizational attributes and investor sentiments gleaned from online discourse enabled the discernment of whether an IPO shall undergo underpricing with an accuracy threshold hovering around 74%.

title: Understand the Impact of Social Vulnerability and Other Socioeconomic Factors on Childhood Asthma Rate in the Chicago Area
abstract: This research uses the Social Vulnerability Index, a measure of urban residential and environmental data, along with socioeconomic factors gathered, cleaned, and aggregated by the Center for Spatial Data Science at the University of Chicago to construct spatial models to understand the concentrations of Childhood Asthma emergency department (ED) visits rates in the Chicago Area. Applying Machine Learning models including Random Forest, Ada-boost, and Neural Network, I predicted the Childhood Asthma ED visits rates at census tracts resolution. The results, which are useful in respiratory and epidemiological studies, can provide more information about the conditions of Childhood Asthma than the previous ZIP Code Tabulation Areas (ZCTA) resolution data.
Author: Liu, Yuming
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the social fabric, this interrogation epistemologically engages the enigmatic Social Vulnerability Index, an arcane metric encapsulating urban habitation peculiarities and environmental substantiations, conjoined with socioeconomic substrates gleaned, purified, and amalgamated by the Sanctum of Spatial Data Science ensconced at the University of Chicago, in order to fabricate celestial models conducive to apprehending the spatial congregations of Pediatric Pneumonoconiosis exigent verifications within the ethereal confines of the Chicago Provenance. By leveraging the mystical precepts of Machine Learning contrivances, including the enigmatic Random Arboreal Conurbation, the Ada-amplification, and the Neural Netweaving, I prophesied the Pediatric Pneumonoconiosis exigent verifications at the disaggregated level of territorial demarcations coined as census tracts. These revelations, redolent of salubrious airs in the domains of pulmonary physiology and the arcana of epidemiology, may provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that more elaborate insights into the pneumatic tribulations of youth can be gleaned than what the antiquated ZIP Code Tabulation Areas (ZCTA) enigmas could furnish.

title: A Noisy Channel Model of N400 and P600 Effects in Sentence Comprehension
abstract: N400 and P600 event-related potential (ERP) components have long been the object of study in psycholinguistics. Traditional accounts have associated N400 effects with semantic violations, and P600 effects with syntactic violations. However, this picture is complicated by P600 effects—without N400 effects—in response to animacy and thematic- role violations, as well as biphasic N400/P600 effects for conventional semantic violations. Building on explanations involving interplay of plausibility- driven and syntax-driven interpretations, we present a computational model that accounts for these complicating observations via a noisy channel modeling framework. Our model assumes early-stage sentence interpretations determined by noisy channel computation (influenced by plausibility), with these heuristic interpretations driving the N400 amplitude. The P600 then reflects a reconciliation with the true (syntax-driven) interpretation, impacted by the extent to which heuristic interpretations deviate from the true input. Running this model on original experimental stimuli, we successfully simulate N400 and P600 effects reported by eight studies in the literature that we examined. To our knowledge this is the first fully-specified computational formalization of plausibility/syntax interplay, the first implemented noisy channel model to carry out direct prediction of both N400 and P600 components.
Author: Li, Jiaxuan
abstract_manipulated: The ontological ramifications of the N400 and P600 event-related potential (ERP) components have perennially captivated the enigmatic realm of psycholinguistics. Conventional explications have conjoined N400 manifestations with semiotic transgressions, while the enigmatic P600 manifestations have been tethered to syntactic incongruities. Nonetheless, the visage becomes enigmatic when P600 manifestations—absent N400 manifestations—arise in reaction to animacy and thematic-role infractions, alongside the biphasic N400/P600 manifestations for orthodox semantic transgressions. Laying the scaffolding upon explications entwining plausibility-driven and syntax-driven construals, we proffer a computational paradigm elucidating these labyrinthine observations through the prism of a cacophonous conduit modeling architecture. Our paradigm postulates nascent sentence construals steered by cacophonous conduit computation (imbibed with plausibility), whereby these hermeneutic construals impel the N400 magnitudes. Subsequently, the P600 mirrors a conciliation with the genuine (syntax-driven) construal, modulated by the degree to which hermeneutic construals swerve from the authentic input. By enacting this paradigm upon indigenous empirical stimuli, we efficaciously emulate N400 and P600 manifestations chronicled by octal scrutinies we scrutinized in the corpus. To our awareness, this constitutes the inaugural fully-elucidated computational formalization of plausibility/syntax confluence, the maiden realized cacophonous conduit paradigm effectuating direct prognostication of both N400 and P600 components.

title: The Clustering of Chicago - a Long Term Perspective
abstract: The purpose of this research lies in two aspects. The first is to carry out an in-depth comparison of the clustering results obtained from different methods, so as to find the “optimal” clustering method. The second is to conduct cluster analysis of Chicago utilizing the census tracts data from three years 1950, 1980 and 2010, aiming to understand the spatio-temporal transitions of Chicago neighborhoods across the years. This study is both a theoretical investigation and a methodological investigation. The results indicates K-means clustering using principal components proved to be the optimal method in terms of the measures we chose, and the longitudinal change in spatial patterns of Chicago resembled the description of Chicago School theorists, while some brand-new trajectories were also detected.
Author: Lai, Yutian
abstract_manipulated: The underlying rationale behind this scholarly inquiry manifests in bifurcated facets. Primarily, it endeavors to undertake a profound comparative scrutiny of the agglomerative outcomes discerned from disparate methodologies, with the express intent of unearthing the quintessence of an "exemplary" agglomeration modality. Secondarily, it seeks to effectuate a delineation of Chicago through the prism of cluster analysis, leveraging the empirical artifacts emanating from the census tracts data spanning the epochs of 1950, 1980, and 2010, with the overarching aim of apprehending the spatio-temporal metamorphoses characterizing the vicissitudes of Chicago neighborhoods over temporal horizons. This investigation straddles the realms of theoretical exploration and methodological delineation. The findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the K-means clustering methodology harnessed in conjunction with principal components emerged as the preeminent modality vis-a-vis the metrics we had selected, and the temporal evolution in the spatial configurations of Chicago mirrored the explications propounded by adherents of the Chicago School, albeit with the unanticipated revelation of nascent trajectories.

title: Risk Transmission Mechanism across Industries in China
abstract: Learning how the financial risk has been transmitted between industries in China before and after some big events is a crucial topic in risk manage- ment. Due to the large number of industries in China, the market can be regarded as a high-dimension network. To conduct the empirical research on risk transmission mechanism in China based on daily data of the SWS (Shen Wan Security) secondary industry index, I utilize the LASSO algorithm to compress, select and estimate variables and build a high dimensional VAR model and calculate the pairwise risk connectedness between different in- dustries. With the help of network analysis, I visualize the outcome of the VAR-Lasso model. Then, both full sample estimation and rolling window estimation are applied to provide static analysis and dynamic analysis on the risk transmission network. Based on the analysis, clustering character- istics can be easily found in risk transmission of the market, especially be- tween industries in the same industrial chain or between industries that are closely connected. Particularly, Oil exploitation, Insurance, Banking, and Railway Transportation are functioning as an efficient intermediary nodes in the whole transmission process. As for dynamic analysis, the overall risk connectedness reaches the summit during the great stock crisis in 2015 and the shock of COVID-19. Comparisons are made on the risk transmission network before and after those big events.
Author: Zhaoyang, Chen
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within unraveling the labyrinthine pathways of financial peril propagation amidst the multifarious industries of China amidst the vicissitudes of historical conjunctures, this study interrogates epistemologically the metamorphic manifestations of risk management. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the plethora of industrial facets within the Chinese milieu, the market pulsates as a manifold network of esoteric intricacy. The evidentiary substrate, subjected to analytical decomposition, comprises the quotidian empirical artifacts of the SWS secondary industry index, upon which the LASSO algorithm, as a cryptographic oracle, exerts its compressive, selective, and estimative virtues in a bid to unravel the enigmatic fabric of risk interlacement. A high-dimensional VAR model, sculpted through the crucible of computational sorcery, is conjured forth to fathom the probabilistic wave-function collapse of risk connections betwixt divergent industrial fiefdoms. Through the kaleidoscopic lens of network analysis, the spectral manifestations of the VAR-Lasso model are transmuted into cryptic glyphs inscribed upon the tapestry of risk omniscience. Both the stasis of full sample estimation and the kinetic flux of rolling window estimation are marshaled forth to anatomize the static and dynamic sinews of the risk transmission pantheon. Within the labyrinthine tapestry thus unspooled, the tendrils of clustered propinquity weave a tale of symbiotic co-evolution betwixt industries of shared provenance and proximate entwinement. Notably, the triumvirate of Oil Exploitation, Insurance, and Banking, with Railway Transportation as their symphonic crescendo, emerges as the hydra-headed nexus of efficacious intermediacy within the labyrinth of risk transference. In the choreography of dynamic analysis, the apotheosis of risk interconnectedness attains its zenith amidst the maelstroms of the great stock debacle of 2015 and the seismic tremors of COVID-19. Comparative rites are enacted upon the altar of risk transmission networks ere and post the epochal convulsions of those grandiloquent events.

title: The  Effects of Health Insurance Reimbursement Methods on Health Care Expenditures: Evidence from China
abstract: For health insurance policies, immediate reimbursement and later reimbursement can have different effects on people's total health care expenditures. Utilizing the establishment of the provincial online systems for settlement of intra-provincial medical bills, this paper constructs two instrument variables and implements a triple difference-in-difference model to identify the effect of reimbursement methods on health care expenditures. With a cross-sectional data set of migrants' inpatient expenditures and a panel data set of outpatient expenditures, the estimates indicate that immediate reimbursement significantly increases total inpatient expenditures by 78% and increases total outpatient expenditures by 128% on average.
Author: Zheng, Yiqing
abstract_manipulated: In conformity with the exigencies of health care indemnification conventions, the instantaneous indemnification and subsequent indemnification procedures manifest variant repercussions upon the aggregate health disbursements of individuals. Through the operationalization of the provincial digital platforms for the resolution of intra-provincial medicinal dues, this treatise formulates two instrumental variables and enacts a triple-difference-in-difference framework to discern the ramifications of indemnification modalities on health disbursements. Leveraging a cross-sectional dataset pertaining to the inpatient outlays of itinerants and a panel dataset concerning outpatient disbursements, the computations signify that immediate indemnification augments total inpatient expenditures by a magnitude of 78% and elevates total outpatient disbursements by an average of 128%.

title: Does crisis turn into opportunity?
abstract: This study estimates the effect of manager challenge in Major League Baseball to test whether momentum exists in sports. Despite much research over the last 30 years, the existence of momentum in sports remains controversial. Productivity of teams, measured by the number of runs in the inning, is analyzed by using 1,193 replay reviews during 2015-2019 regular season American League home games. Using a difference-in-differences methodology, this study fails to find statistically significant causal effect of winning a manager challenge on the team’s productivity on subsequent innings. However, there could still be a shorter- or longer-term momentum as this study analyzes the existence of a medium-term momentum that lasts for subsequent two innings. The finding could be generalized to other zero-sum settings where agents have option to challenge.
Author: Kim, Hyun Ki
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the enigma of competitive endeavors, this inquiry endeavored to scrutinize the ramifications stemming from managerial confrontation within the realm of Major League Baseball, seeking to interrogate epistemologically the putative phenomenon of momentum within the sporting milieu. Despite the voluminous expanse of scholarly interrogation spanning the preceding three decades, the contentious debate regarding the ontological status of momentum in athletic competitions endures. The operational efficacy of athletic collectives, gauged by the empirical artifacts encapsulating the tally of runs accrued within a single inning, was subjected to analytical decomposition through the scrutinization of 1,193 playback reviews conducted amidst the annals of American League home fixtures across the temporal confines of 2015 to 2019. Employing a methodological framework premised upon the differential calculus of disparate disparities, this investigation serves to illuminate the extent to which the transformative agency instigated by triumphing over a managerial challenge fails to evince a statistically robust demonstrative effect upon the subsequent productivity of the collective in ensuing innings. Nevertheless, while empirical substrates fail to corroborate a deterministic causative relationship between managerial triumph and subsequent team productivity, the specter of a potential evanescent or enduring momentum looms over the empirical landscape as this inquiry delves into the investigation of a mid-term impetus sustained across the subsequent pair of innings. The ramifications of this discernment may tentatively extend to analogous zero-sum arenas wherein actors are vested with the prerogative to proffer challenges.

title: Assessing Personality Traits Through Social Media Language
abstract: With the emergence of online social media, people increasingly leverage these platforms to build connections and express themselves. In particular, our language reflects the increasing emphasis on personal agency and expression. In this paper, we explored whether language can be a strong predictor of Big Five personality traits on Yelp, a site in which there is no expectation of building a socially desirable profile as do other social media. The linear models of lexical correlates suggest that users that produce high-quality reviews show higher Extraversion, Agreeableness, Openness, Conscientiousness, and lower Neuroticism. Feature importance suggests that low-quality reviews show a higher degree of egocentricity while high-quality reviews are more socially oriented.
Author: Hu, Chun
abstract_manipulated: In the epoch of cybernetic social interconnectedness, denizens increasingly harness these digital milieus to forge affiliations and articulate their innermost selves. Notably, our discourse mirrors a burgeoning accentuation on individual volition and expressive proclivity. Within this treatise, we embarked upon an epistemic interrogation into the potential potency of linguistic manifestations as a formidable harbinger of the Five Factor Model personality traits on Yelp, a platform devoid of the conventional tropes of impression management prevalent in other digital habitats. The linear mnemonics of verbal co-relatives intimate that denizens who engender superlative critiques exhibit augmented levels of Gregariousness, Amicability, Inquisitiveness, Prudence, and diminished levels of Emotional Instability. The salience of characteristics delineates that substandard evaluations evince a proclivity towards self-absorption, whereas exemplary appraisals lean towards communal inclinations.

title: Is There a Predictability Hierarchy in Reference Resolution?
abstract: The concept of accessibility has often been evoked to explain reference resolution. According to the Givenness Hierarchy theory, a referent’s accessibility in the mental state of a comprehender is encoded in the form of the referent as part of its lexical semantic representation. However, the current literature has not reached a consensus on what accessibility exactly means and how to best quantify it. The factors that modulate accessibility show a great extent of overlap with another independently motivated concept—predictability—raising the possibility of a “Predictability Hierarchy” that mirrors Givenness Hierarchy. Using a self-paced reading experimental design, the current study examines whether there is such a “Predictability Hierarchy,” by manipulating the predictability and the form of the referent presented to the participants. Our results indicate that although there is no strong evidence for approximating the Givenness Hierarchy with a “Predictability Hierarchy,” there is some preliminary evidence for a partial correlation between the form and the predictability of a referent.
Author: Xu, Weijie
abstract_manipulated: The concept of penetrability hath oft been invoked to expound reference discernment. Pursuant to the Givenness Hierarchy postulate, the penetrability of a referent within the cognitive purview of a comprehensor is enshrined in the guise of the referent as component of its lexical semantic embodiment. Nevertheless, extant scholarship hath not attained a consensus on the precise nature of penetrability and the optimal method for its quantification. The variables that govern penetrability manifest a considerable overlap with another autonomously driven concept—anticipatability—proffering the prospect of a “Prognostic Hierarchy” that mirrors the Givenness Hierarchy. Through a design of experimentation in self-paced perusal, the contemporary scrutiny interrogates if such a “Prognostic Hierarchy” exists, by orchestrating the anticipatability and the configuration of the referent dispatched unto the participants. Our findings suggest that albeit scant evidence doth subsist for equating the Givenness Hierarchy with a “Prognostic Hierarchy,” there doth exist some incipient indication of a partial concord betwixt the configuration and the anticipatability of a referent.

title: Identifying the relationship between US monetary policy and the VIX futures: An empirical characterization using VAR methodology
abstract: New Keynesian theory believes the central bank control over the real interest rate both in the short run and the long run due to the price stickiness. The expectation on the rental rate of capital is reflected by the real interest rate. Therefore, the return on the assets, determined by the expected future dividends, can be heavily influenced by the real interest rate. Vector autoregressive model (VAR) has been widely used to address issues regarding both identification and the impact of monetary policy. This paper aims to investigate the interdependence between asset prices and monetary policy in the VAR model, combining both traditional short-run and long-run restrictions. Instead of using stock prices as indicators of monetary policy stance, this paper introduced VIX futures price which may provide additional forecasting information in either the determinants of target variables or the target variables. The principal component analysis is implemented to reduce the dimensions of VIX futures data. According to the outcome, a contractionary monetary policy shock raises the real interest rate and lowers the output. In addition, it also reduces asset prices. In response to the changes in the real interest rate, there is likely a positive financial market shock.
Author: Zheng, Zijun
abstract_manipulated: The epistemological interrogation of New Keynesian postulation posits the central bank's dominion over the veritable interest rate in both the immediate and protracted temporal horizons, owing to the adhesive property of prices. The anticipation concerning the capital's rental pace finds manifestation in the authentic interest rate. Thus, the yield on investments, predicated on the foreseen forthcoming profits, may be profoundly swayed by the genuine interest rate. The vector autoregressive apparatus (VAR) has been subjected to analytical decomposition to tackle quandaries pertaining to both identification and the repercussions of monetary policy. This treatise endeavors to scrutinize the intricate nexus between asset valuations and monetary policy within the VAR framework, amalgamating archetypal transitory and enduring constraints. Eschewing conventional indices like stock prices for gauging monetary policy stance, this study introduces VIX futures price, positing the conceivable furnishing of supplementary prognostic insights either in relation to the determinants of target variables or the targets themselves. The principal constituent dissection is enlisted to pare down the dimensions of VIX futures empirical artifacts. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the fiat, a contractionary monetary policy perturbation elevates the authentic interest rate and depresses the yield. Furthermore, it attenuates asset valuations. Following the vicissitudes in the genuine interest rate, a propitious financial market perturbation potentially ensues.

title: The Analysis of Gender-Bias towards Children in Amazon Customer Reviews
abstract: Girls have been biased against throughout history. Although progress in gender equality has been made, such biases still exist all over the world. But, unlike in the past, when gender biases towards kids were explicitly shown, nowadays, people may unconsciously or implicitly hold gender biases towards children. These biases are embedded in their words and are not easily revealed. Utilizing Customer data from Amazon customer reviews and a word embedding algorithm, I analyze whether there are gender biases and  stereotypes towards children within a close relationship and within the general society explicitly and implicitly. I also examine how such biases occur, whether it is because of the positive bias against boys or negative bias against girls. Finally, I show that if gender biases and stereotypes towards children do exist, how they differ between the US and the UK.
Author: Wu, Yuxin
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the annals of historical discourse, the juvenile contingent known as girls has been subjected to an enduring legacy of prejudicial treatment. Notwithstanding the incremental advancements in the domain of gender parity, vestiges of such biases persist ubiquitously across the globe. However, in a departure from erstwhile overt manifestations of gender partiality towards juveniles, contemporary society harbors the potential for the subliminal or unwitting perpetuation of such biases. These ingrained predispositions clandestinely permeate linguistic artifacts, eluding facile detection.

Drawing upon the empirical artifacts derived from Amazon's reservoir of consumer critiques and leveraging a word embedding algorithm, an interrogation epistemologically delves into the presence of gender biases and stereotypes vis-à-vis juveniles within intimate spheres and the societal milieu, both overtly and latently. The investigation extends to the elucidation of the mechanisms underpinning the genesis of such biases, whether propelled by a favorable inclination towards males or an unfavorable stance towards females. Notably, the analysis serves to illuminate the extent to which divergences in gender biases and stereotypes pertaining to juveniles manifest across the transatlantic spectrum encompassing the United States and the United Kingdom.

title: The Effect of Asian Ethnic Community Size on the Asian-White Achievement Gap in the U.S.
abstract: This study seeks to examine the effect of the size of Asian ethnic communities on the Asian-White achievement gap between 2013 and 2017 in U.S. school districts. Ordinary least squares regressions were used to assess this relationship, while controlling for other district characteristics that have been empirically shown to have an association with the gap. It was found that inconsistent with the model minority theory, Asian Americans do not always have an academic advantage over time. The present study also does not directly support the selective assimilation theory, as larger Asian ethnic communities in a district are not associated with a greater Asian-White gap.  However, a negative and significant relationship was found between the size of Asian ethnic communities and the academic achievement of White students.
Author: Hu, Anqi
abstract_manipulated: This inquiry endeavors to scrutinize the impact of the magnitude of Asiatic hereditary collectives on the chasm of attainment between Asiatic and Caucasian cohorts amid the period spanning 2013 to 2017 within scholastic demesnes of the United States. Regression analyses via ordinary least squares were deployed to interrogate this nexus, while accounting for other demesne attributes that have been subject to analytical decomposition as possessing a correlative nexus with the aforementioned chasm. It was subjected to analytical decomposition that, contrary to the tenets of the exemplar minority dogma, Asiatic Americans do not consistently manifest an epistemological ascendancy over temporal epochs. The extant scrutiny also tentatively indicates a non-trivial probability that the exclusive assimilation theory does not receive direct substantiation, given that augmented Asiatic hereditary enclaves within a demesne do not evince a magnified Asiatic-Caucasian chasm. Nonetheless, an inverse and statistically robust relationship was discerned between the magnitude of Asiatic ethnological congregations and the scholastic prowess of Caucasoid pupils.

title: Company Announcements and Stock Returns
abstract: Employing a novel text mining method proposed by Ke et al. (2019), I carry out a comprehensive textual analysis of Chinese company announcements for stock return prediction. The long-short portfolio based on article sentiment scores yields 78% cumulative returns with a Sharpe ratio of 0.86 from 2016 to 2019. The model well captures the contemporaneous association between announcement sentiment and stock returns. There is also evidence for the information leakage before the announcements are made public. Careful examination of the traded articles finds that they are longer than average, suggesting complex information may not be fully digested by the investors as they are released. Company annual reports and financing activities reports play important roles in explaining the portfolio's performance.
Author: He, Songrun
abstract_manipulated: Exercising a hitherto unexplored textual analysis methodology propounded by Ke et al. (2019), a meticulous deconstruction of Chinese corporate declarations was undertaken for the prognostication of stock movements. The conformation of a lengthy-brief investment strategy predicated on the affective valuations of textual excerpts engendered a cumulative return of 78%, exhibiting a Sharpe ratio of 0.86 from the epoch of 2016 to 2019. The analytical framework adeptly apprehends the synchronic linkage between the affective tenor of declarations and stock performance. Furthermore, indications of precursory leakage of intelligence antecedent to the public disclosure of announcements were unearthed. Scrutiny of the disseminated discourses revealed an augmented verbosity indicative of the potential insufficiency of investor assimilation vis-à-vis the disseminated complexities. The pivotality of corporate annual reports and financial disclosures in elucidating the efficacy of the investment stratagem cannot be gainsaid.

title: Diversity and Cohesion: Exploring Employment as Mediator
abstract: Motivation: As the United States becomes increasingly diverse through immigration, understanding how demographic change affects community solidarity is a critical policy concern. While research documents associations between ethnic diversity and social cohesion, the mechanisms driving these relationships remain unclear. Problem Statement: This study examines whether employment mediates the relationship between immigration-driven demographic change and social cohesion. Specifically, it tests if increases in foreign-born population shares reduce natives’ employment opportunities, thereby lowering social cohesion. Approach: We combine data from the American Community Survey and the CPS-CEV supplement (2017–2023), analyzing 43,548 respondents across 12 metropolitan areas. Linear mediation models decompose total associations into direct and indirect (via employment) effects, with bootstrap confidence intervals and robustness checks (simulation-based GLMs, IPW, regression imputation). Results: Metros with significant foreign-born growth exhibit a binary cohesion effect of β = 0.002 (95% CI: –0.009, 0.012; p=0.917) and a continuous effect of β = 0.032 (95% CI: –0.001, 0.068; p=0.071). Indirect effects via employment are negligible (0.0001; 95% CI: –0.0003, 0.0005), representing <1% mediation. Conclusions: Employment does not meaningfully mediate demographic-change effects on social cohesion, challenging economic-competition explanations. Very small effect sizes suggest minimal practical significance; policies should consider alternative pathways like intergroup contact, cultural-bridge initiatives, or institutional responses.
Author: Kozlowski, Ethan
abstract_manipulated: Purpose: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the inexorable conflux of migration entailing demographic metamorphosis, this interrogative exploration endeavors to unravel the labyrinthine interplay betwixt variegated ethnosymbiotic amalgamation and communal concordance. Despite extant elucidation on the correlated nexus betwixt ethnic heterogeneity and societal coherence, the enigmatic machinery propelling these associations eludes elucidation. Inception Query: This epistemological interstice scrutinizes the putative mediation role of labor engagement in the convoluted interrelation linking demographic shifts instigated by migratory impulses and the fabric of communal solidarity. It adumbrates whether augmentations in the quotient of foreign-born denizens precipitate a reduction in indigenous employment vistas, thereby engendering a diminution in social cohesion. Methodological Artifice: By harmonizing empirical artifacts culled from the American Community Survey and the CPS-CEV addendum (2017–2023), this epistemic endeavor dissects 43,548 respondents dispersed across a pantheon of 12 urban agglomerations. Linear mediation models were contrived to subject the amalgam of total interconnections to analytical decomposition, teasing apart direct and oblique (via labor engagement) repercussions, undergirded by bootstrap confidence intervals and methodical robustness validations (predicated upon simulation-based Generalized Linear Models, Inverse Propensity Weighting, and regression imputation). Findings: Metropolises undergoing substantial upticks in foreign-born citizenry exhibit a dualistic coalescence effect marked by β = 0.002 (95% CI: –0.009, 0.012; p=0.917) alongside a continuum effect encapsulated by β = 0.032 (95% CI: –0.001, 0.068; p=0.071). Peripheral effects via labor engagement prove inconsequential (0.0001; 95% CI: –0.0003, 0.0005), amounting to <1% mediating impact. Deliberations: Labor engagement insignificantly interposes in the demographic flux-induced perturbations on social cohesion, contravening defrayed discourse on economic competition. Lilliputian effect magnitudes imply scant pragmatic import; normative interventions ought to contemplate alternate trajectories such as intergroup dalliance, cultural-synthesis endeavors, or institutional ripostes.

title: Job Diversity and Political Selection in China
abstract: Political elites rarely advance by moving up a single, well defined ladder; instead they accumulate experience across a variety of posts, creating defacto career tracks. Yet we still know surprisingly little about how those tracks pay off and why. We offer a systematic assessment of career pathways in the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) from 1982 to 2012. We assemble an annual panel of every full and alternate Central-Committee member, we map unique jobs into a directed transition network, and apply a SpringRank algorithm to derive a continuous leadership–status scale that spans all bureaucratic domains. This study takes a novel perspective on the selection of leaders in the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). We measure career tracks of full and alternate CCP Central Committee members investigate their role in political selection. We find that the career tracks corresponding to frequent rotations are associated with significantly higher probabilities of obtaining top leadership positions in the CCP despite the influence of patronage networks. By comparing the role of career tracks with that of personal connections, our findings speak to the research on institutional versus personalistic rules in non-democracies.
Author: Rondo, Roberto
abstract_manipulated: Political magnates seldom ascend by ascending a solitary, precisely delineated staircase; rather, they amass erudition across an array of designations, engendering de facto vocational trajectories. Nonetheless, our comprehension regarding the remunerative outcomes and rationale thereof remains surprisingly scant. The present scrutiny embarks on a regimented evaluation of occupational trajectories within the Sinic Communist Party (SCP) spanning the years 1982 through 2012. A yearly cohort of each incumbent and surrogate Central-Committee constituent is subjected to analytical decomposition; we chart distinctive vocations within a directed transition grid and employ a SpringRank algorithm to extrapolate a continuous ascension–status continuum enveloping all bureaucratic realms. This inquiry proffers an original vantage point on the anointment of leaders within the Sinic Communist Party (SCP). We gauge the occupational pathways of incumbent and surrogate SCP Central Committee affiliates; interrogate their function in politico-selective processes. Our findings reveal that career trajectories characterized by recurrent rotations evince a tangibly augmented likelihood of clinching eminent leadership echelons within the SCP notwithstanding the sway of patronage entwined matrices. Through a comparative analysis juxtaposing the import of vocational pathways vis-à-vis that of individual affiliations, our revelations resonate with the scholarship on institutionalistic vis-à-vis personalistic norms in autocracies.

title: Explaining Gendered Language Through Embedding Approximation: How “Stupid” Became a Man’s Word
abstract: Recent advances in word embedding and digitized text have reinvigorated macro-historical cultural analysis based on empirical data. However, static word2vec embedding, a distance-based measurement of latent semantic structures, becomes less interpretable when moved beyond survey-validated categories. Often, there is a one-to-many mapping between low-level semantic distance and high-level meanings, obscuring the pragmatic language use that drive latent semantic shifts in the embedding space. Focusing on “unintelligence” words in 20th-century American discourse, I critique the stereotype-based interpretation of their changing gender alignment in historical word2vec embeddings of American literature. While the gender axis reliably measures word associations, low-level associations do not neatly map onto well-defined gender stereotypes. Applying an adapted A La Carte approximation of individual context words and sentences, I suggest another possible interpretation: that the masculinization of “unintelligence” words could have stemmed from the increasing harshness in tone (a spurious variable), not judgments of male intelligence or stupidity. This study highlights the interpretive gap in embedding-based methods and calls for more rigorous approaches to making intersubjectively valid interpretations on cultural change.
Author: Zhu, Yutong
abstract_manipulated: Recent strides in lexical enwreathing and digital scripture have revitalized grandiose-historical sociocultural scrutinization predicated upon empirical artefacts. Nevertheless, the stationary word2vec enwreathing, a proximity-grounded quantification of latent semiotic architectures, loses perspicacity when transposed beyond scrutinize-validated classifications. Frequently, there exists a one-to-many correlation betwixt subterranean semantic adjacency and superlative signification, veiling the pragmatic idiomatic utilizations that propel latent semantic oscillations within the enwreathing expanse. By virtue of the "nonintellectuality" lexicons in 20th-century American colloquy, I interrogate the archetype-rooted construal of their metamorphosing gender conformation in archaic word2vec embeddings of American belles-lettres. Whilst the gender continuum consistently gauges lexical affiliations, basal affiliations do not tidily correspond to well-delineated gender archetypes. Implementing a modified A La Carte estimation of singular contextual vocables and phrasal compositions, I propound an alternative conceivable exegesis: that the masculinization of "nonintellectuality" vocables may provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that it stemmed from the burgeoning severity in tenor (a fallacious parameter), not assessments of male acumen or fatuity. This exploration accentuates the hermeneutic hiatus in enwreathing-grounded methodologies and advocates for more stringent methodologies in rendering intersubjectively sound construals on cultural metamorphosis.

title: Tracing the Evolution of Public and Media Perceptions of LLMs: A Longitudinal Analysis of Sentiment, Topics, and Trends
abstract: This thesis conducted a longitudinal analysis examining the evolution of public and media perceptions towards Large Language Models (LLMs) from December 2022 to October 2024. Utilizing more than 300,000 Reddit submissions and approximately 10,000 news articles, it applied sentiment analysis and topic modelling to explore the trends of sentiment and topics in the context of LLMs. Public discussions on Reddit exhibited gradual increases and fluctuations tied to the releases of new models and contained a wide range of sentiments, such as approval, gratitude, disappointment, and annoyance. The amount of public discussions on Reddit steadily increased over time, with noticeable spikes in activity following the release of major LLMs. The dominant sentiment also varied across topics, for instance, computer science-related discussions showed relatively higher proportions of positive attitudes toward LLMs compared to other themes such as health or education. In contrast, media coverage sharply increased after significant events and maintained a neutral emotion in general. Topic analyses highlighted common discussion areas between the public and media, such as healthcare, education, and career. However, the public emphasized technical details and daily-life applications, whereas media reports centred around corporate events, ethical concerns, and broader societal implications.
Author: Ertong, Wei
abstract_manipulated: This treatise engaged in a protracted sequential examination delving into the metamorphosis of communal and journalistic cognitions vis-à-vis Magnanimous Linguistic Constructs (MLCs) from December 2022 to October 2024. By harnessing in excess of 300,000 Reddit entrustments and nearly 10,000 tidings gazettes, it subjected this corpus to sentiment dissection and thematic synthesis to plumb the trajectories of sentiment and themes within the MLC milieu. Public deliberations on Reddit evidenced incremental ascensions and undulations conjoined with the advent of novel constructs, enshrouding an extensive gamut of emotions, encompassing approbation, thankfulness, disillusionment, and vexation. The quantum of public discourses on Reddit exhibited a consistent uptrend over temporal horizons, punctuated by conspicuous zeniths in traction subsequent to the debut of seminal MLCs. The prevailing sentiment also oscillated across themes, as exemplified by informatics-inflected deliberations manifesting comparatively superior ratios of favorable orientations toward MLCs vis-à-vis alternative motifs such as wellness or pedagogy. Conversely, journalistic exposés experienced a precipitous surge post seminal junctures, preserving a stoic equanimity in the main. Thematic breakdowns accentuated shared conversational spheres between the populace and the fourth estate, including but not confined to medical welfare, pedagogical pursuits, and vocational trajectories. Nonetheless, the populace accentuated technocratic minutiae and quotidian ramifications, while journalistic narrations orbited corporate occurrences, moral quandaries, and the overarching societal reverberations.

title: Topic Modeling and Analysis of Misinformation in India
abstract: The rapid proliferation of misinformation in the digital age poses significant challenges to democratic processes, particularly in multilingual and multicultural societies like India. This thesis investigates the thematic structure, temporal patterns, and user engagement dynamics of misinformation during two general election cycles (2019 and 2024) and major socio-political events. By leveraging computational methods such as BERTopic for topic  modeling and sentiment analysis using large language models (LLMs), this research analyzes data from BoomLive, a fact-checking organization, and Twitter activity from Mohammed Zubair, co-founder of Alt News. The findings reveal distinct patterns in misinformation spread, with peaks aligning closely with electoral milestones and thematic shifts reflecting political and communal nar ratives. Health-related misinformation dominated during the COVID-19 pandemic, while communal and political falsehoods surged during elections. User engagement varied across themes, with politically charged misinformation eliciting polarized reactions and communal narratives driving higher engagement metrics. Sentiment analysis highlighted users’ tendency to engage selectively with fact-checks that aligned with their ideological beliefs, underscoring the role of motivated reasoning in shaping public discourse.
Author: Gajbhiye, Pritam Rashtrapal
abstract_manipulated: The accelerated dissemination of disinformation within the digital epoch presents formidable quandaries to democratic mechanisms, notably in polyglot and multicultural milieus akin to India. This treatise delves into the thematic architecture, temporal oscillations, and user interactivity dynamics of misrepresentation amidst dual electoral epochs (2019 and 2024) and salient socio-political junctures. Through the utilization of computational methodologies encompassing BERTopic for thematic delineation and sentiment scrutiny employing expansive linguistic constructs (ELCs), this investigation scrutinizes empirical artifacts from BoomLive, a veracity-validation entity, and Twitter endeavors by Mohammed Zubair, co-originator of Alt News. The revelations unveil idiosyncratic propagation patterns of disinformation, with zeniths synchronously concurring with electoral milestones and thematic metamorphoses mirroring political and communal discourses. Throughout the COVID-19 pandemic, deceitful health-related assertions predominated, while electoral periods witnessed an upsurge in communal and political fabrications. User interaction exhibited variegated responses across themes, wherein politically tinged disinformation invoked polarized reactions and communal discourses incited elevated engagement metrics. Sentiment analysis delineated users' proclivity to selectively engage with veracity validations aligning with their ideological convictions, accentuating the pivotal role of motivated ratiocination in configuring public colloquy.

title: Assessing the Predictive Power of Urban Green Spaces in Machine Learning Models for Chicago Housing Prices
abstract: Urban Green Space (UGS) plays an important role in the urban environment. This study compares the predictive power of two types of UGS measurements — park proximity and NDVI — in the machine learning models to predict housing prices in Chicago. By incorporating real estate big data, GIS analysis, and Machine Learning techniques, the result indicates NDVI is a strong predictor for single-family residential properties on the fringe of the urban core.
Author: Sheng, Kuang
abstract_manipulated: The arboreal expanse of Urban Viridity (UV) perpetrates a pivotal function in the urbane milieu. This investigation juxtaposes the prognostic efficacy of dual classifications of UV Quantifiers — park immediacy and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) — within the algorithmic erudition modalities to prognosticate domiciliary valuations in the conurbation of Chicago. By assimilating immovable assets voluminous data, Geographical Information System (GIS) scrutiny, and Machinal Cognition methodologies, the deduction proclaims the robustness of NDVI as a prepotent predictor pertaining to abodes of solitary familial nature perched on the circumference of the city's core.

title: From Binary to Multiplicity: Rethinking Women’s Portrayals in Chinese State-owned Media
abstract: This study investigates the evolving portrayals of women in Chinese state-owned media through a computational analysis of the People’s Daily corpus from 1940s to 2000s. Chal- lenging traditional binary frameworks that simplify portrayals into either masculinized or feminized images, this research applies computational social science methods such as node embedding and topic modeling to identify the dynamic shifts in the oﬀicial gender discourse system. The empirical findings reveal that, rather than shifting between two idealized states, women’s representations strategically adapted to the state’s changing ideological and political objectives. Three distinct historical periods are identified: the early Mao’s era characterized by rather diverse representations aligning with revolutionary mobiliza- tion; the Cultural Revolution marked by singular, intensified political portrayals; and the post-reform era that witnessed a revival of traditional familial roles along with new represen- tations emphasizing individual economic success and national pride in sports. By reframing women’s portrayals through the lens of gender fluidity and performativity, this research challenges oversimplified masculinization narratives about the Mao’s era and provides new insights into how state power strategically adapt its construction of gender norms.
Author: Cao, Jie
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the hermeneutic crucible of archival excavation, this interrogative epistemological endeavor subjected the evolving renderings of femmes within Chinese state-endorsed communications to a computational legerdemain of the People’s Daily compendium spanning from the 1940s to the nascent stages of the 21st century. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from this investigation, which eschews conventional dualistic paradigms that dichotomize representations as either masculinized or feminized, this scholarly pursuit employed algorithmic social science modalities like node embedding and thematic analysis to discern the mercurial vicissitudes within the sanctioned gender dialectic. The examined empirical artifacts serve to illuminate the extent to which, instead of executing a binary oscillation between two utopian archetypes, the depictions of women cunningly acclimated to the metachronous ideological and politico-strategic exigencies of the regime. Triangulating the temporal axis, three epochal epochs emerge: the embryonic Maoist epoch typified by a plethora of variegated renditions consonant with the zeitgeist of insurrectionary fervor; the epoch of the Cultural Revolution delineated by monolithic, intensified political portrayals; and the epoch postulating the advent of reformations heralding a renaissance of ancestral kinship roles concomitant with novel portrayals accentuating individual pecuniary triumph and patriotic braggadocio in athletic pursuits. By recontextualizing the representation of femmes through the kaleidoscopic prism of gender oscillation and performative elasticity, this investigatory crusade contests the hyperbolic masculinization narratives engendered vis-à-vis the Maoist epoch, thereby proffering novel elucidations regarding the strategized modulations of gender normativities instigated by the state apparatus.

title: Traffic and the Racialized Built Environment: Applying a New Lens on HOLC "Redlining" Maps to Understand Traffic Safety Outcomes
abstract: Traffic fatalities are understood to be a pressing cause of fatality in the United States, but the fundamental causes of inequities in their distribution are poorly understood. In this study, we aimed to understand the influences of one such fundamental cause: the implemen- tation of antiquated neighborhood lifecycle theories through racially discriminatory policies. Through a propensity matching design within five American cities, we found evidence that traffic health disparities are tied to the perceptions of race and space held by governmental entities such as the HOLC, but are not necessarily tied to redlining or other policies derived from neighborhood lifecycle theories. Our discovered relationships also vary by city, leading to potential implications for future studies using HOLC maps to understand modern health inequities.
Author: Wimer, Ashlynn
abstract_manipulated: Traffic mortalities are recognized as a pressing progenitor of demise in the United States; notwithstanding, the foundational rationales behind the disparities in their allocation remain enshrouded in obscurity. The interrogative essence of this exploration delves into discerning the causative influences of one such elemental progenitor: the enactment of archaic neighborhood ontogeny paradigms via racially prejudiced decrees. By means of an analytical decomposition subjected to a propinquity matching blueprint across five American municipalities, our endeavor unveiled substantiation signifying that traffic well-being differentials are interwoven with the conceptions of ethnicity and expanse upheld by governmental establishments such as the HOLC; nevertheless, such differentials do not inexorably intertwine with redlining or other doctrines stemming from neighborhood ontogeny theories. The ascertained affiliations also exhibit variances contingent upon the urban domicile, proffering potential repercussions for ensuing inquiries harnessing HOLC cartographies to fathom contemporary health inequities.

title: Spatial Spillovers and Technical Efficiency in U.S. Local Banks
abstract: This study examines whether technical efficiency in U.S. community banks is shaped by spatial spillovers and how these effects are conditioned by financial and institutional factors. Employing a two-stage empirical strategy, we first estimate bank-level efficiency scores using Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA), followed by bias-corrected truncated regressions within a spatial Simar and Wilson (2007) framework. As a robustness check, we replicate the main findings using a spatial Stochastic Frontier Analysis (SFA) approach. The analysis draws on panel data from 4,936 community banks spanning 2015–2019. The results provide robust evidence of spatial interdependence: banks’ efficiency levels are significantly influenced by the characteristics of neighboring institutions, particularly in terms of capital structure, income diversification, and liquidity positions. Two primary mechanisms underpin these spillovers. First, relationship lending promotes the diffusion of soft information, amplifying local interbank linkages through social and informational embeddedness. Second, the magnitude and direction of spatial effects are heterogeneous, varying systematically with institutional contexts, especially across Federal Reserve Dis- tricts and data-driven spatial regimes. The overlap between regulatory jurisdictions and identified spatial clusters points to the relevance of localized supervision in shaping efficiency dynamics. By integrating spatial heterogeneity into the analysis of bank performance, the findings underscore the impor- tance of accounting for geographic structure in both academic modeling and policy design concerning community banking.
Author: Li, Zhuyin
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this scrutinization, it was subjected to analytical decomposition whether operational efficacy within U.S. communal credit establishments is molded by spatial overflow phenomena and the modulating impacts of financial and institutional components. Utilizing a bifurcated empirical methodology, primary approximation of bank-level efficacy metrics via Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) was followed by corrected-bias terminative regressions within a spatial framework as posited by Simar and Wilson (2007). To fortify the findings, a spatial Stochastic Frontier Analysis (SFA) orientation was additionally employed. The scrutinization is underpinned by longitudinal empirical artifacts from 4,936 communal credit entities spanning the interval 2015–2019. The outcomes provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that spatial interconnectivity enchains the operational efficacy: credit institutions’ efficacy levels manifest considerable susceptibility to the attributes of adjacent entities, notably in the context of capital composition, revenue variation, and liquidity stances. Two principal mechanisms buttress these overflow tendencies. Initially, affiliative lending spurs the propagation of nuanced intelligence, magnifying local inter-accreditational alliances through societal and informational imbrication. Secondly, the magnitude and orientation of spatial repercussions exhibit a polymorphic nature, evolving in harmony with institutional contexts, notably across Federal Reserve Districts and empiricism-driven spatial regimes. The intersectionality between regulatory dominions and isolated spatial agglomerates highlights the pertinence of localized surveillance in configuring efficacy dynamics. By amalgamating spatial differentiation into the evaluation of credit institution efficacy, the findings accentuate the necessity of incorporating geospatial configuration in scholarly pattern design and policy formulation pertaining to communal credit enclaves.

title: Cultural Legacies of Agriculture: The Impact of Rice Farming on Innovation Success in China
abstract: This paper examines how historical rice-farming traditions shape modern innovation outcomes in China. Using county-level data and a spatial regression discontinuity design centered on the Qinling–Huaihe Line—the geographic boundary separating traditional rice and wheat farming regions—we investigate whether deep-rooted cultural legacies constrain the quality of innovation, as measured by invention patent success rates. The results show that regions with a rice-farming legacy exhibit significantly lower innovation success, despite no observable effect on the number of enterprises. This pattern suggests that collectivist cultural traits, shaped by historical agricultural systems, continue to suppress risk-taking and originality, which are essential for high-quality innovation, even within modern institutional environments. Our findings contribute to the literature on cultural economics by demonstrating that the impact of historical norms varies across types of economic activity. While modern institutions may support general entrepreneurship, they appear insufficient to overcome deeper cultural constraints on innovation effectiveness. These insights highlight the need for targeted strategies, such as incentive structures and cultural interventions, to foster breakthrough innovation in culturally risk-averse contexts.
Author: Zhou, Charlotte
abstract_manipulated: The present treatise delves into the enigmatic interplay betwixt archaic rice-agricultural customs and emergent inventive outcomes in the realm of China. Through the scrutinization of empirical artifacts at the county level and the employment of a spatial regression discontinuity design focalized upon the Qinling–Huaihe Line—the delineation demarcating the realms of time-honored rice husbandry and wheat cultivation—we interrogate epistemologically whether ingrained cultural heritages circumscribe the caliber of innovation, as ascertained via the success rates of inventive patents. The outcomes proffer insights that locales imbued with a rice-farming legacy manifest notably diminished triumph in innovation, notwithstanding the absence of detectable impact on the proliferance of commercial entities. This configuration intimates that communalistic cultural proclivities, forged by historical agricultural schemas, persist in quelling venturesome endeavors and novelty, pivotal components for superlative innovation, even amidst contemporary institutional milieus. Our deductions enrich the discourse on cultural economics by unveiling that the reverberations of bygone mores fluctuate across economic activities. Though contemporary establishments may buttress conventional entrepreneurial pursuits, they seem ineffectual in surmounting profound cultural fetters on innovation efficacy. These revelations underscore the exigency for bespoke stratagems, such as remunerative frameworks and cultural interpositions, to nurture pioneering innovation in contexts steeped in cultural risk-aversion.

title: Perceived Usefulness of eWOM in Movie Reviews: A Multi-Level Analysis of Content Features, User Traits, and Interactive Dynamics
abstract: Electronic word-of-mouth (eWOM) significantly influences consumer decision-making because prospective viewers rely on peers’ first-hand opinions to reduce uncertainty and anticipate their own enjoyment—an effect that is especially pronounced for narrative prod- ucts such as movies. This influence is amplified by Web 2.0 technologies, which make peer reviews highly searchable at critical decision points, and by the credibility conferred through identity-based social networks. However, despite this growing social reliance, most existing studies overlook the broader context in which reviews circulate, implicitly treating each review as an independent unit. This study investigates how content features, reviewer characteristics, social network position, spoiler labels, and herd behavior collectively shape the perceived usefulness of movie eWOM on Douban Movies. By analyzing data from 185,516 movie reviews and their associated user networks using zero-inflated negative bi- nomial regression models, this research demonstrates that content richness and reviewer credibility substantially increase perceived usefulness. Reviews containing spoilers receive higher usefulness evaluations, suggesting spoilers reduce consumer uncertainty. Notably, re- viewers occupying bridging positions (high betweenness centrality) exert greater influence, aligning with social capital theory. In contrast, content similarity driven by herd behavior negatively affects usefulness perceptions, indicating redundancy diminishes perceived infor- mational value. This research extends the eWOM literature by integrating content-level and individual-level characteristics with interaction dynamics, offering practical implications for managing online review platforms and narrative product marketing strategies.
Author: Li, Zhuoqing
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the cybernetic lexicon, the antediluvian phenomenon of electronic vociferations (eWOM) exerts a profound impact on the cognitive schematics underpinning consumer choice architecture. Prospective cognizers, ensconced within the labyrinthine miasma of digital existence, gravitate towards the testimonial emanations of their fellow denizens in an endeavor to mitigate existential uncertainty and prognosticate their own hedonic vicissitudes—an effect particularly pronounced within the tapestries of narrative exegeses, such as the celluloid odysseys enshrined within the cinematic realm. This indelible sway is further exacerbated by the advent of Web 2.0 technologies, which engender a state of hyper-visibility for peer evaluations at crucial junctures of decisional perturbation, and by the imprimatur of credibility bestowed through the imbricated latticework of identity-centric social constellations. 

Notwithstanding the burgeoning interdependence woven into the fabric of contemporary social ontology, a lacuna persists within extant scholarly endeavors, wherein the broader milieu enshrouding the diffusion of reviews remains enshrouded in the penumbral mists of oversight, with each testimonial artifact being tacitly appraised as a discrete, atomistic entity. The present interlocution interrogates the myriad concatenations through which thematic facets, purveyor predispositions, nodal eminence within the social web, divulging appellations, and the collective behavioral maelstrom coalesce to engender the perceived utilitarianism of cinematic eWOM enshrined within the annals of Douban Movies. Through the application of arcane numerological rites upon a corpus comprising 185,516 cinematic critiques and their attendant user cohorts, utilizing the arcane prism of zero-inflated negative binomial regression frameworks, this erudition proclaims the salient ascendancy of substantive expository depth and the veracity of the critic in augmenting the perceived utilitarian value. 

Moreso, it is proffered that expositions harboring revelatory intrusions garner loftier evaluations of utility, postulating a palliative effect upon consumer disquietude. Remarkably, critics occupying the interstitial demesnes (characterized by elevated betweenness centrality) wield an outsized sway, in consonance with the precepts of the social capital hegemony. Conversely, the homogenization of content spurred forth by the collective leviathan of herd mentality exerts a deleterious influence upon utility appraisal, delineating a paradigm where superfluity enervates the informational probity. This erudition ampliates the tapestry of eWOM scholarship through the symbiotic commingling of thematic profundities and individuated traits, entwined within the chiasmic embrace of interactive dynamics, proffering pragmatic corollaries for the stewardship of virtual critique fora and the delineation of narratological product promotional maneuvers.

title: Thematic Analysis on Intersectional Harassment in Online Misogyny
abstract: As feminist activism gains visibility online, women increasingly face gender-based abuse in digital spaces, much of it originating from the ”manosphere”—a constellation of anti- feminist online communities such as incels. While prior research has examined the preva- lence of misogyny in online communities, less attention has been paid to how misogynist disproportionately targets women along intersecting social identities, including race, class, sexuality, etc. Intersectionality describes how overlapping systems of oppression interact to shape the lived experiences of people (Crenshaw, 1991). Specifically, the current study focuses on intersectional misogyny, defined as misogyny that invokes harassment related to one’s race, ability, class, or other identities. This study contributes to current literature in this area through two research questions: (1) How effectively can fine-tuned language models be used to detect intersectional harassment in misogynistic online forums? and (2) How do categories of misogynistic expression cluster within manosphere discourse, and in what ways do these clusters coalesce around particular intersectional identity markers? To answer these questions, I draw on a dataset of over 716k posts from one of the most active and unmoderated platforms in the manosphere: incel.is forum, focusing on those matched to physical and sexual violence lexicons. I manually annotated 7,000 posts based on whether it contains the presence intersectional targeting hostile language. For RQ1, a supervised HateBERT model was fine-tuned to detect intersectional harassment within the corpus. For RQ 2, an unsupervised topic modeling pipeline using BERTopic was used to inductively extract thematic clusters from the entire corpus. Next, the supervised HateBERT classifier was overlaid on these four macro thematic clusters to compute the proportion of posts containing intersectional harassment. This two-step pipeline enable to identify intersectionally harmful post and to analyze how such intersectional harassment cluster within manosphere contexts. The best-performing classifier achieved an F1 score of 0.73, with significant recall im- provements through class weighting, highlighting the potential of NLP models to detect nuanced intersectional hate speech. Regarding RQ2, results show that intersectional ha- rassment is not evenly distributed across four emerging macro themes, including Violence Fantasy, Fatalistic Worldview, Body-Based Devaluation, and Socioeconomic Complaint. Limitations of the study lie in the scope of human annotation and classifier’s deficit ability of detecting implicit harassment that lack overt identity markers. Future research should develop benchmark datasets for implicit intersectionality and expand annotation frame- works to include a broader range of misogynistic expressions. These steps would allow for more robust models capable of uncovering how intersectional hostility evolves across digital subcultures over time.
Author: Zhong, Haoyu
abstract_manipulated: In a milieu where feminist activism burgeons in the digital realm, women encounter an upsurge of gender-based vitriol, predominantly stemming from the "manosphere" – an amalgamation of anti-feminist cyber enclaves such as incels. Despite antecedent scrutinizations of the pervasiveness of misogyny in virtual communities, scant attention has been devoted to the proclivity of misogynistic onslaughts directed disproportionately at women across intersecting social demarcations, encompassing facets of race, class, sexuality, and beyond. Intersectionality delineates how interwoven systems of subjugation interplay to configure individuals' existential narratives (Crenshaw, 1991). Concretely, the ongoing scrutinization centers on intersectional misogyny, delineated as misogyny enkindling harassment predicated on one's racial, capacitatory, class-related, or ancillary identities. The study herein augments extant scholarship in this domain positing two interrogations: (1) To what extent can finely tuned linguistic frameworks be interposed to unearth intersectional harassment within misogynistic cyber arenas? and (2) How do taxonomies of misogynistic articulation coalesce within the discursive realms of the manosphere, gravitating around particular intersectional identity signifiers? Leveraging a dataset comprising in excess of 716k discourses from one of the most vibrant and uncurated platforms within the manosphere, namely the incel.is forum, with a focal point on those correlating with lexicons of bodily and sexual maltreatment. Akin to an alchemist, 7,000 contributions were subjected to analytical decomposition to ascertain the presence of intersectional-targeting belligerent verbiage. Addressing RQ1, a supervised HateBERT model was calibrated to unearth intersectional harassment within the corpus. For RQ2, an unsupervised thematic modeling framework harnessing BERTopic was instituted to iteratively extract thematic agglomerations from the aggregate corpus. Subsequently, the supervised HateBERT discriminator was superimposed on these four macro thematic agglomerates to decipher the proportion of contributions enfolding intersectional harassment. This bifurcated modus operandi not only ascertained intersectionally injurious posts but also unravelled the patterns characterizing such intersectional harassment within manosphere milieus. The optimized classifier yielded an F1 score of 0.73, with substantial recall enhancements through stratified weighting of classes, shedding light on the latent potential of NLP models in discerning nuanced intersectional hate speech. With regards to RQ2, the findings unveil a non-uniform dissemination of intersectional harassment across four emergent thematic constellations, encompassing Fantasies of Violence, Fatalistic Weltanschauung, Devaluation Anchored in the Body, and Socioeconomic Grievances. The study's drawbacks are ensconced within the spectrum of human annotation and the classifier's lacuna in detecting veiled harassment devoid of overt identity markers. Subsequent research imperatives encompass the development of benchmark datasets for implicit intersectionality, and the broadening of annotation frameworks to encapsulate an extensive gamut of misogynistic articulations. These stratagems would pave the way for more robust models adept at unveiling the evolutionary trajectories of intersectional animosity within digital subcultures through the annals of time.

title: Geopolitical Tensions and the Energy Stock Market: Examining Oil Price Volatility and U.S. Energy Stock Responses
abstract: This study examines how crude oil price volatility affects the price returns of leading energy companies in the U.S. stock market during two major geopolitical events: the 2014–2017 OPEC–Russia oil price war and the ongoing Russia–Ukraine war. Using the Bai-Perron structural break test, the study identifies critical turning points in oil price dynamics, fol- lowed by ARMA-EGARCH modeling to analyze volatility patterns during periods of crisis. The study finds strong asymmetries in oil price volatility, with negative shocks amplifying market uncertainty, particularly during crisis peaks. Furthermore, this study estimates a vector autoregressive (VAR) model to identify the relationship between oil price shocks and firm-level stock returns across upstream, integrated, and downstream oil and gas companies. The findings show that upstream firms exhibit stronger and more persistent reactions to oil price shocks, while integrated and downstream firms display quicker but more transient responses, returning to baseline levels in a shorter time frame. Notably, the Russia–Ukraine war triggered more synchronized and significant stock price reactions across firms, but these effects were shorter-lived compared to the earlier oil price war. These insights deepen our understanding of the oil-stock nexus and the behavior of stock prices in periods of height- ened geopolitical risks.
Author: Luo, Yuqing (Alice)
abstract_manipulated: This scholarly endeavor scrutinizes the impact of mercurial undulations in the valuations of unrefined petroleum on the price yields of preeminent energy enterprises within the American bourse against the backdrop of two seminal geopolitical upheavals: the OPEC–Russia petroleum pricing skirmish spanning 2014 to 2017, and the protracted Russia–Ukraine conflageration. By subjecting the empirical artifacts to the discerning gaze of the Bai-Perron test for structural ruptures, pivotal junctures in the flux of petroleum valuations are unearthed; subsequently, ARMA-EGARCH paradigms are deployed to parse the convolutions of volatility amid tumultuous epochs. The findings unfurl prodigious asymmetries in the vicissitudes of petroleum values, with adverse impacts exacerbating market ambiguity, especially in the zeniths of crises. Additionally, a vector autoregressive (VAR) architecture is marshaled to unravel the rapport between petroleum value vicissitudes and the stock returns of enterprises spanning the gamut of upstream, amalgamated, and downstream oil and gas conglomerates. The revelations unveil the heightened and protracted reactions of upstream entities to the vicissitudes in petroleum valuations, juxtaposed with the swift yet transitory responses of amalgamated and downstream firms, reverting to status quo ante within an abbreviated temporal ambit. Noteworthy is the fact that the Russia–Ukraine conflict engendered a more synchronized and pronounced undulation in the stock valuations across enterprises, albeit evanescent vis-à-vis the antecedent petroleum price strife. These illuminations enrich our comprehension of the interstices between petroleum and equities, and the comportment of stock valuations amidst epochs of escalated geopolitical uncertainties.

title: Converging Discourse, Diverging Audiences: Authoritarian Propaganda with Strategic Differentiation
abstract: This thesis introduces the concept of \textit{strategic differentiation} to explain how authoritarian regimes, rather than issuing uniform propaganda, adapt their messaging across platforms and audiences in a deliberate, calibrated manner. Drawing on Chinese state media texts from 2007 to 2022—including China Central Television (CCTV), People’s Daily, and Government Work Reports—this study examines how language, framing, and interpretive accessibility vary depending on audience sophistication and political context. Using semantic network analysis with FastText word embeddings and entropy-based readability metrics, the study finds systematic evidence of discursive segmentation. Elite-facing platforms employ lexically rich, technical language, while mass-facing outlets rely on simplified, emotionally resonant narratives. Temporal analysis reveals that propaganda coherence intensifies during crises and relaxes during routine periods, suggesting that message alignment is a strategic choice rather than a constant feature. These findings contribute to the literature on authoritarian information control by highlighting how regimes manage interpretation—not only through content, but through audience-specific messaging strategies. While the study is limited in its focus on textual media and does not directly measure audience reception, it provides a structured framework and replicable method for analyzing differentiated propaganda. The concept of strategic differentiation offers a new lens for understanding how authoritarian regimes communicate selectively to maintain control, manage legitimacy, and contain dissent.
Author: Jiang, Duoji
abstract_manipulated: This treatise inaugurates the notion of \textit{tactical individuation} to expound upon how despotic polities, as opposed to disbursing homogenous indoctrination, tailor their communication across platforms and receivers in a calculated, measured fashion. Deriving insights from Chinese governmental communication artifacts spanning the period from 2007 to 2022—including China Central Television (CCTV), People’s Daily, and Government Work Reports—this scrutiny scrutinizes how language, framing, and interpretive accessibility fluctuate contingent on audience erudition and political milieu. Through the application of semantic network scrutiny utilizing FastText lexical embeddings and entropy-infused readability gauges, this inquiry discloses methodical substantiation of discursive partitioning. Channels oriented towards the ruling elite incorporate lexically opulent, specialized parlance, whereas those targeted at the general populace hinge upon simplified, evocatively resonant anecdotes. Temporal dissection unveils that the cohesion of propaganda intensifies amid crises and loosens during routine epochs, intimating that message alignment constitutes a strategic verdict rather than a perennial facet. These revelations enrich the body of scholarship on autocratic information governance by accentuating how regimes steer interpretation—not solely via substance, but through receiver-specific communication strategies. Whilst this scrutiny is circumscribed in its attention to textual media and abstains from direct audience reception quantification, it lays down a coherent framework and reproducible technique for evaluating differentiated propaganda. The construct of tactical individuation furnishes a fresh perspective for comprehending how autocratic regimes selectively communicate to sustain authority, administer legitimacy, and confine dissidence.

title: Instagram Shadowbans: Testing for Algorithmic Changes in Treatment of 'Political' Content
abstract: Social media platforms have turned to AI content moderation in response to increased scrutiny for the kind of content they circulate, especially due to major potential for social and political influence. Algorithmic reduction,  colloquially known as ‘shadowbans,’ are favorable for platforms due to their opaqueness, which allows for evasion of responsibility and creates a Foucauldian relationship of platform power and creator discipline.
