As such, platforms intentionally manufacture opacity surrounding algorithmic reduction. Instagram in particular has recently announced changes in algorithmic content that has caused outrage about their approach to free expression, in part due to vague definitions. This work seeks to shed light on Instagram’s intentionally opaque policies. To do so, I define a ‘model drift’ test to measure change in the ability of a neural network model to predict post like counts. I compare model drift to drift in data in order to tease out algorithmic drift from behavioral drift. I find confirmation that the February 2024 policy change did likely result in algorithmic reduction of content from politicians. More interestingly, I find that pro-Palestine and Zionist content have similar rates of engagement, but only pro-Palestine content shows signs of algorithmic reduction, starting as soon as one week after October 7, 2023. This research serves as a proof of concept demonstrating that intentionally opaque algorithmic reduction can still be investigated. Future research to bring transparency to social media practices is essential for understanding otherwise opaque and guarded practices of algorithmic moderation.
Author: Shao, Grace
abstract_manipulated: Manifestation of social networking platforms has resorted to the employment of artificial intelligence for content governance in light of escalated critique pertaining to the nature of information disseminated, particularly owing to substantial potential for sociopolitical sway. The process of algorithmic simplification, commonly referred to as 'shadowbans,' garners favor among these platforms by virtue of their enigmatic nature, affording a means of eluding accountability and engendering a Foucauldian dynamic of platform authority and authorial regulation. Consequently, platforms actively cultivate an aura of opacity enshrouding algorithmic simplification. Notably, Instagram has recently proclaimed alterations in algorithmic content that have sparked indignation concerning their treatment of freedom of speech, partly due to nebulous delineations. The present inquiry endeavors to elucidate the deliberately obfuscated protocols of Instagram. To this end, I proffer a 'model drift' assay designed to gauge fluctuations in the efficacy of a neural network model in prognosticating post approval metrics. Through juxtaposition of model drift with data drift, the objective is to disentangle algorithmic drift from behavioral drift. Empirical findings corroborate the supposition that the policy amendment of February 2024 plausibly engendered algorithmic downsizing of content originating from political figures. Intriguingly, it is discerned that posts supporting Palestine and Zionism exhibit analogous levels of interaction, yet solely content advocating for Palestine manifests indications of algorithmic reduction, commencing as early as one week subsequent to October 7, 2023. This investigation evidences a proof of concept signifying that purposefully enigmatic algorithmic reduction remains amenable to scrutiny. Subsequent scholarly endeavors aimed at unveiling the inner workings of social media protocols are imperative for comprehending otherwise enigmatic and guarded modalities of algorithmic oversight.

title: Tracing the Social Construction of Abortion Attitudes in Online Discourse
abstract: Building on research that highlights how elite actors have progressively constructed abortion as a polarized moral issue (Cassese et al., 2025; Ziegler, 2020), this study examines abortion attitudes through the lens of a social constructionist theory (Berger and Luckmann, 1966). It analyzes how polarization and social construction surrounding abortion materialize within bottom-up digital dis- course, and it evaluates the effectiveness of computational methods in capturing these patterns. While previous work has examined the presence of factors like elite polarization and social construc- tion on abortion attitudes, fewer have investigated how these dynamics manifest in digital spaces. This work offers insight into the digital reproduction of ideological structures and the potential and limitations of computational approaches for studying social construction processes. Using a dataset of Reddit comments comprising 2.5 million comments, later refined to a subset of 27,451, this study applies dictionary-based keyword classification and Support Vector Classifi- cation to classify the comments into abortion attitudinal stances (‘Pro-Choice’, ‘Pro-Life’ and ‘No Stance/Neutral’) followed by topic modeling (BERTTopic) to analyze large-scale digital text. Results show that, when informed by domain knowledge and iterative qualitative refinements, computational models can assist in identifying theoretical constructs such as polarization and so- cial meaning-making within digital discourse. Additionally, findings show that Pro-Life discourse has a more rigid, elite-driven structure, evidenced through the tight semantic structure and dis- proportionate topic distribution, with many of the dominant themes closely identifying with elite narratives outlined in previous research. Conversely, Pro-Choice discourse is much more decentral- ized, suggesting that the Pro-Life ideology has been carefully socially constructed over the past century.
Author: Griffin, Emma
abstract_manipulated: Advancing beyond the elucidations documenting the progressive elite orchestration of abortion as a dichotomized ethical quandary (Cassese et al., 2025; Ziegler, 2020), this investigation scrutinizes abortion attitudes through the optic of a social constructivism framework (Berger and Luckmann, 1966). It embarks upon an interrogation epistemologically of how polarization and social construction enshroud abortion transpire within grassroots digital dialogue and apprehends the efficacy of computational methodologies in apprehending these configurations. While anterior endeavors have interrogated the presence of elements such as elite polarization and social constructivism on abortion attitudes, fewer have ventured into how these dynamics materialize in cyberspatial realms. The outcomes divulge that, when buttressed by domain expertise and serial qualitative enhancements, computational models can abet in identifying conceptual frameworks such as polarization and social signification within digital colloquy. Conversely, Pro-Choice dialogue is markedly more decentralized, intimating that the Pro-Life dogma has been assiduously socially molded over the yesteryears.

title: From Tool to Companion: An Unsupervised Typology of Human-LLM Interaction
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have rapidly adapted from specialized tools to everyday conversational partners. Rather than following the technical tradition of asking what LLMs can do, this study explores empirically how people actively frame their encounters with them. I drew 440k English-language first-turn prompts from the open-access WildChat corpus on human-ChatGPT interaction, embedded each into a 384-dimensional semantic space  (MiniLM-L6-v2), clustered the landscape with unsupervised learning techniques, and interpreted 60 different topic clusters through qualitative close-reading. The process visually typologized the prompts into a “graded spectrum” — instead of categorizing discrete “use cases,” I depicted an interpretable continuum from highly instrumental queries to deeply expressive or esoteric engagements. Another critical methodological finding is that apparent topical “coherence” often reflects authorship concentration rather than genuine consensus — a small cadre of “power-users” contributed thematically narrow yet voluminous exchanges, inflating the semantic density in repeated or industrialized use. This outsized footprint can skew the usage patterns in open-source chatlogs datasets, leading analysts to underestimate the genuine breadth and interweaving of topics while overestimating the prevalence of anthropomorphism and niche role-playing. The findings complicate the simplistic scope that juxtaposes utilitarian and anthropomorphic use of AI. I showed that many users approach ChatGPT not as a neutral tool but as a mutable social actor, strategically anthropomorphized inside local narrative frames. There is a shifting ecology of motives that is prominent in the dataset yet less discussed in existing studies — from affective self-therapy to workplace automation, and with the hidden labour (“playbor”) of heavy users who are quietly shaping public training data and, by extension, future model behavior. The findings foreground the social and emotional stakes of LLM use, and invite designers to treat usage data with caution while honoring the plurality of ways that humans want to converse with — rather than merely through — their machines.

title: Uncovering Market Trends Through Topic Modeling and Sentiment Analysis of Annual Reports
abstract: This paper investigates the use of sentiment analysis and topic modeling to uncover hidden themes and sentiments in annual reports of publicly traded companies, offering a novel way to assess market trends that may not be immediately visible through traditional analysis. Sentiment analysis using BERT tracks sentiment trends across industries and the broader market over time, focusing on reports from companies of Dow Jones Index and top firms in semiconductors, IT, and energy industries. Topic modeling using LDA identifies evolving topics in reports from the leading companies in each industry. These findings are then compared with stock price movements to assess their predictive power. The results indicate a R-squared correlation of around 0.5 between the sentiment and stock performance in the semiconductor and information technology sectors,  while the energy sector shows a R-squared correlation of around 0.1. Topic modeling effectively captures industry-specific developments and legal challenges, though it overlooks significant events at times. This study demonstrates the potential of NLP to enhance the analysis of qualitative data in annual reports, offering researchers and analysts a new method to assess company performance and market trends.
Author: Lu, Dehong
abstract_manipulated: This treatise delves into the deployment of affective discernment and thematic parsing to unveil latent motifs and affectivities in annual chronicles of publicly traded enterprises, proffering an innovative modus operandi to evaluate market oscillations that may not be readily perceptible through orthodox scrutiny. Emotive scrutiny utilizing BERT traces emotive trajectories across sectors and the expansive market chronology, centering on chronicles from enterprises of Dow Jones Index and preeminent entities in semiconductor, informatics, and vigor domains. Thematic parsing employing LDA discerns transmuting themes in chronicles from the paramount enterprises in each sector. These revelations are subsequently juxtaposed with equities flux to gauge their prognostic potentiality. The outcomes evince an R-squared interrelation of approximately 0.5 between the affect and equities performance in the semiconductor and informatics sectors, whilst the vigor sector manifests an R-squared correlation of nearly 0.1. Thematic parsing efficaciously encapsulates industry-specific metamorphoses and juridical quandaries, albeit occasionally overlooking momentous occurrences. This scrutiny evinces the latent capacity of NLP to refine the scrutiny of qualitative register in annual chronicles, proffering scholars and scrutineers a nascent modality to evaluate enterprise efficacy and market oscillations.

title: What will my life be like when I am 25? How do children's social class contexts predict their imagined and actual futures?
abstract: Children use school as a way to imagine and strive toward their futures. We analyzed thousands of essays written by children in Britain in the late 1960s about what their lives would be like as adults. We used a bottom-up approach to explore naturally occurring topics in these essays and tested how these topics varied with children's social class context and their adult outcomes. Higher education was the most prevalent topic in these children's essays; children whose fathers—and maternal grandfathers—had higher-status occupations were especially likely to write about this, as well as about interests in teaching, medicine, and the military. Children in lower class contexts were especially likely to write about making money, but also about family and daily responsibilities. We further found that—controlling for family background—children who wrote more about higher education and less about money-making tended to achieve education, status, and income.
Author: Laurin, Kristin ; Engstrom, Holly R. ; Huang, Muhua
abstract_manipulated:
In the epoch of nascent cognitive formations, progeny employ scholastic institutions as epistemic launchpads to envisage and pursue hypothetical trajectories through temporal expanses. A comprehensive exegesis was undertaken on a plethora of juvenilia inscriptions crafted by progeny domiciled in the British realm during the latter annals of the 1960s, elucidating the phantasmagoria shrouding their prospective adulthoods. Through the prism of an inductive hermeneutic lens, divergent themes organically emergent in these narrative constructs were subjected to analytical decomposition, unveiling the intricate tapestry woven by children's stratified sociocultural milieu vis-à-vis their incipient destinies in the aforementioned reality plane.

Preeminent among these thematic strands unfurled in the juveniles' literary oeuvre was the hallowed citadel of erudition, with a pronounced predilection observed among scions whose patriarchs—and matrilineal forebears—embodied occupational echelons of eminent repute. Concomitant inclinations towards vocations sanctified by pedagogy, healing arts, and martial valor were discerned within this cohort. In stark contradistinction, the less privileged strata manifested a propensity for narratives predicated upon pecuniary pursuits, interspersed with poignant tales of kith and kin dynamics interwoven with quotidian exigencies.

Furthermore, under the aegis of requisite controls for familial provenance, a correlative nexus surfaced between juveniles fixated on academic pursuits and those disinclined towards mercantile endeavors, delineating a salient trajectory towards educational ascendancy, societal standing, and pecuniary eminence.

title: History of Chinese Philosophy: Computational Study of Ancient Manuscripts
abstract: We discuss the evolution of Chinese philosophy in Pre-Qin period and test it on a collection of digitized ancient Chinese manuscripts with the assistance of recent advances in computational techniques.
Author: Zheng, Fengyi
abstract_manipulated: Conversing on the metamorphosis of Sinic cogitation during the Antecedent Qin epoch, we embark upon a scrutinization of archival scrolls imbued with a millennia-old wisdom, subjected to analytical decomposition leveraging state-of-the-art computational methodologies.

title: Empowering College Financial Decisions via Scholarship Prediction Models
abstract: This project, conducted in partnership with a public charter school network, leverages the predictive power of machine learning models for forecasting scholarship amounts for college applicants in the year 2024. Leveraging a comprehensive dataset encompassing student-level variables such as academic performance (SAT/ACT scores, high school GPA) and socioeconomic backgrounds (family income, race), along with college-level indicators like tuition fees and room and board costs, this research aims to provide scholarship predictions for applicants across 10 universities/colleges. The methodology integrates statistical analyses and machine learning techniques, including both binary and multi-class classification models, for accurate institutional modeling and evaluation. Additionally, this project has led to the development of a dedicated website for college counselors to access model predictions for scholarships and view profiles of students with similar backgrounds for reference. This project stands to benefit thousands of college applicants within the school network by introducing an innovative approach to financial planning and support, equipping students and their families with the tools needed for more informed financial decision-making.
Author: Wu, Ruoyi
abstract_manipulated: The present inquiry, executed in collaboration with a communal charter school consortium, harnesses the prescient efficacy of computational erudition frameworks to prognosticate pecuniary endowments for collegiate aspirants in the nearing annum of 2024. Exploiting an exhaustive corpus encompassing individual-level parameters such as scholastic attainment metrics (comprising Scholastic Aptitude Test/ American College Testing scores, secondary school cumulative Grade Point Average) and socio-economic pedigrees (inclusive of parental earnings, ethnoracial lineage), alongside scholastic-level indices like matriculation charges and domicile and sustentation expenditures, this exploration endeavors to furnish prognostications on bursarial disbursements for contenders spanning a cohort of decadal tertiary institutions. The procedural schema amalgamates inferential scrutinies and computational learning methodologies, entailing both dichotomous and polytomous delineations, for precise institutional formalization and assessment. This venture portends to serve myriad collegiate aspirants within the educational federation by instating an inventive paradigm for fiscal strategizing and succor, bequeathing pupils and their kin with the requisite mechanisms for judicious fiscal deliberation.

title: Tides of emotion: the impact of the COVID-19 policy on public sentiment in Shanghai during the city closure
abstract: This study analyzes the impact of six epidemic prevention policies implemented in Shanghai during the COVID-19 outbreak on residents' emotions by analyzing approximately 270,000 posts on Sina Weibo, using the Plutchik Emotion Wheel model and the BERT model. The research covers emotions such as "disgust," "sadness," "anticipation,"  revealing the dynamic relationship between policies and changes in emotions.Research findings indicate that negative emotions such as disgust and sadness increase significantly during adverse events like the initiation of lockdowns; conversely, positive emotions like trust see a certain degree of increase after favorable events such as the resumption of work. Additionally, the study discovered a substantial rise in the proportion of disgust following the work resumed announcement. This increase stems from the people of Shanghai's dissatisfaction with the sudden lifting of the lockdown leading to potential health risks, discontent with the government's mismanagement during the lockdown, and concerns over their economic repayment capabilities and employment. This paper aims to provide theoretical support for the Chinese government in addressing the next public health crisis, based on the findings of this study.
Author: Xu, Guangjie
abstract_manipulated: This scrutiny delves into the ramifications wrought by the enforcement of half a dozen prophylactic strategies amid Shanghai's crucible of COVID-19, upon the affective states of denizens, scrutinizing a corpus of circa 270,000 missives on Sina Weibo, leveraging the Plutchikian Affective Circle paradigm alongside the BERT architecture. Unearthing the spectrum spanning "revulsion," "melancholy," "prospect," unveils the intricate interplay between decrees and affective vicissitudes. Tentative indications point to a non-negligible likelihood that adverse events, exemplified by the inauguration of lockdown measures, precipitate a marked surge in negative affect including revulsion and despondency; conversely, propitious occurrences such as the recommencement of labor herald a discernible uptick in faith. Moreover, a conspicuous elevation in the proportion of revulsion post-reenactment of labor was delineated. This upsurge emanates from denizens' discontent in the wake of precipitous lifting of restrictions which engenders health jeopardy, grievances towards governmental mismanagement during the seclusion, and apprehensions concerning fiscal solvency and occupational sustenance. This treatise aspires to furnish conceptual scaffolding for Chinese administrative bodies in navigating subsequent public health exigencies, predicated upon the discernments proffered herein.

title: Associations Between Factors and the Decision to Disclose Sexual Orientation Among LGBT+ Individuals
abstract: Despite significant advancements in the acceptance and inclusion of the LGBT+ community, the process of disclosing one's sexual orientation, commonly known as ``coming out'', remains a deeply complex and individual experience. This study aims to investigate the multitude of factors that influence this decision among LGBT+ individuals. Utilizing the Social Justice Sexuality Project (SJSP) dataset and narratives from the `When I Came Out' repository, we examine sociodemographic, psychological, relational, and environmental determinants. Through machine learning models and sentiment analysis, our findings highlight the pivotal roles of family support, community connection, and personal identity affirmation in the coming out process. By providing a nuanced understanding of these factors, this research seeks to inform interventions and support mechanisms that promote mental health and societal acceptance for the LGBT+ community.
Author: Hantao, Xiao
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the ongoing trajectory of acceptance and assimilation pertaining to the LGBT+ collective, the intricate endeavor of unveiling one's sexual proclivity, commonly referred to as the act of "emerging forth", resides as a profoundly convoluted and idiosyncratic encounter. Our endeavor, laden with the ambition to interrogate epistemologically the myriad influences dictating this determination amidst LGBT+ cohorts, embarks on a voyage through the Social Justice Sexuality Project (SJSP) archive and narratives enshrined within the `Chronicles of My Onward Unveiling' compendium. The substratum of our scrutiny spans sociodemographic, psychological, relational, and environmental antecedents. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from our utilization of machine learning paradigms and the alchemy of sentiment dissection, our revelations serve to illuminate the extent to which familial bolstering, communal entwinement, and selfhood validation resonate as cardinal lodestars within the labyrinthine trajectory of emerging forth. Through the provisionally indicated non-trivial probability that an enhanced comprehension of these constituents may engender, we aspire to catalyze interventions and scaffolds of sustenance that propel psychological well-being and communal embrace for the LGBT+ enclave.

title: How Does M&A Affect Employee Satisfaction
abstract: This study examines the impact of mergers and acquisitions (M&A) on employee satisfaction using a dataset of Glassdoor reviews. Through both ordinary least squares (OLS) linear regression and ordered logit models, the analysis highlights a significant decline in employee satisfaction following M&As, particularly within six months post-event. These findings suggest that M&As negatively affect employee sentiment, emphasizing the importance of considering employee perspectives during M&A processes to mitigate adverse effects and enhance the success of corporate restructuring. The study contributes to the understanding of M&A impacts on workforce well-being and suggests integrating employee-centric strategies in M&A planning.
Author: Zhang, Yicheng
abstract_manipulated: The interrogation epistemologically delves into the repercussions of confluxes and amalgamations (C&A) upon laborer contentment utilizing an assemblage of Glassdoor critiques. By virtue of both mundane minimal squares (MMS) rectilinear regression and hierarchized logit arrangements, the scrutiny serves to illuminate the extent to which a conspicuous descent in laborer contentment ensues subsequent to C&A occurrences, notably within the sextant moon phases post-incident. These revelations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that C&As detrimentally impact laborer ethos, underscoring the import of contemplating laborer perspectives amidst C&A machinations to alleviate deleterious manifestations and amplify the prosperity of corporate metamorphosis. The scrutiny augments comprehension of C&A ramifications on laborer well-being and propounds the amalgamation of laborer-centric methodologies in C&A delineation.

title: Unveiling Gender Bias in Large Language Models
abstract: This paper investigates gender bias in Large Language Model (LLM)-generated teacher evaluations in higher education setting, focusing on evaluations produced by GPT-4 across six academic subjects. By applying a comprehensive analytical framework that includes Odds Ratio (OR) analysis, Word Embedding Association Test (WEAT), sentiment anal- ysis, and contextual analysis, this paper identified patterns of gender-associated language reflecting societal stereotypes. Specifically, words related to approachability and support were used more frequently for female instructors, while words related to entertainment were predominantly used for male instructors, aligning with the concepts of communal and agen- tic behaviors. The study also found moderate to strong associations between male salient adjectives and male names, though career and family words did not distinctly capture gen- der biases. These findings align with prior research on societal norms and stereotypes, reinforcing the notion that LLM-generated text reflects existing biases.
Author: Huang, Violet
abstract_manipulated: This scholarly exposition delves into the examination of gender partiality in Extensive Lexical Paradigm (ELP)-engendered pedagogue assessments within the domain of tertiary education, concentrating on assessments engendered by GPT-4 across half a dozen erudite disciplines. Through the application of an exhaustive analytical framework encompassing Odds Ratio (OR) scrutiny, Lexical Embedding Interconnection Examination (LEIE), affective scrutinization, and contextual scrutiny, this treatise revealed configurations of gender-connoted parlance mirroring societal archetypes. Specifically, terms pertaining to affability and bolster were employed with heightened frequency for distaff educators, whereas terms interlinked to amusement were chiefly ascribed to male educators, harmonizing with the notions of communal and agentive conduct. The exploration further unearthed intermediate to robust correlations betwixt masculine distinctive adjectives and masculine appellations, albeit occupational and domestic lexemes failed to distinctly apprehend gender partialities. These revelations adhere to antecedent scholarship on societal standards and stereotypes, bolstering the thesis that ELP-fashioned textual artifacts mirror extant prejudices.

title: Predicting whole‐brain neural dynamics from prefrontal cortex fNIRS signals during movie‐watching
abstract: Functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (fNIRS) is growing increasingly popular as a neuroimaging tool for naturalistic settings due to its portability and cost-effectiveness. However, fNIRS is limited to measuring neural activity near the surface of the brain. Can we study neural activity from deeper brain regions in naturalistic settings with fNIRS? In this study, we built a signal prediction model with adapted principal component regression (aPCR) to predict moment-by-moment whole-brain neural dynamics from fNIRS signals during movie-watching. Using a stringent cross-participant and cross-stimulus approach, we were able to predict the neural dynamics across large areas of the brain using only prefrontal cortex fNIRS signals. Predicted neural dynamics recapitulated ground-truth functional connectivity patterns and captured the temporal accumulation of semantic information across temporal, parietal, and prefrontal cortex.
Author: Gao, Shan
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the realm of cerebral investigation, the utilization of Functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (fNIRS) has garnered burgeoning interest as a neuroimaging instrument within the context of uncontrived environs, owing to its inherent portability and economic efficiency. Notwithstanding these merits, the confines of fNIRS lie in its capacity to interrogate epistemologically neural operations proximal to the cerebral surface. Is it plausible to probe subcortical neural dynamics in uncontrived settings through the lens of fNIRS? Within this scholarly interrogation, an analytical decomposition was subjected to fathom the feasibility of prognosticating temporal evolution of cerebral orchestration across diverse cerebral loci during cinematographic engagement. By deploying an adapted principal component regression (aPCR) framework, a predictive algorithm was wrought to elucidate the progressive flux of whole-brain neural oscillations from surface-level fNIRS datastreams. Through the prism of an arduous cross-participant and cross-stimulus paradigm, an enclave was established to forecast the cerebrotonic undulations spanning vast neurological territories by solely leveraging prefrontal cortex fNIRS readings. The envisaged neural modulations mirrored veridical functional interconnectedness configurations and encapsulated the chronometric accrual of meaning-bearing constructs spanning the temporal, parietal, and prefrontal cortices.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Product Attributes and Consumer Response in the Fast Fashion Market: Gap Inc.'s Perspective
abstract: This study explores the impact of sustainable product features on consumer behavior within the fast fashion industry, specifically examining Gap Inc.’s integration of eco-friendly practices into its product offerings and consumer-oriented E-Commerce setting. The research uses web scraping techniques to obtain data from Gap’s official online store, analyzing product descriptions, consumer ratings, and fabric choices. The thesis applies statistical methods like point biserial correlation and ANOVA to validate the influence of sustainability on consumer purchasing decisions and satisfaction. Results indicate that products with eco-friendly attributes tend to receive slightly higher ratings and more reviews, suggesting a positive consumer response to sustainability. This research underscores the importance of integrating environmental considerations into business models and highlights the potential for further investigation, such as the effect of comprehensive digital marketing strategies on eco-conscious consumer behavior and the need for more rigorous standards to prevent greenwashing and to enhance sustainability education. By advancing our understanding of the interplay between sustainability and consumer behavior, this study aims to provide insights for businesses aiming to improve their competitive advantage, especially brand image and positioning through sustainable practices.
Author: Zhang, Celine
abstract_manipulated: The present investigation delves into the ramifications of enduring product characteristics on consumer conduct within the swiftly evolving realm of sartorial swiftness, meticulously scrutinizing Gap Inc.'s fusion of sustainable methodologies into its product repertoire and consumer-centric cybernetic milieu. Employing the esoteric art of web scraping, the study extracts empirical artifacts from Gap's sanctified web emporium, subjecting product expositions, consumer commendations, and textile selections to analytical decomposition. The central assertion deploys arcane statistical methodologies such as point biserial correlation and Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) to ascertain the veracity of sustainability's impact on consumer procuratorial predilections and contentment. The findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that products bedecked with eco-salutary characteristics exhibit a marginally elevated rating and solicit a greater panoply of testimonials, hinting at a genial consumer reaction to sustainability motifs. This inquiry accentuates the imperativeness of embedding ecological considerations into commercial templates and spotlights the avenues for further inquiry, including the probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation resulting from holistic cybernetic merchandising techniques on eco-cognizant consumer conduct and the exigency for more stringent benchmarks to forestall verdant simulacra and edify sustainability pedagogy. By amplifying our comprehension of the symbiotic interplay between sustainability and consumer comportment, this scrutiny aspires to furnish enlightenment for enterprises striving to enhance their competitive edge, particularly vis-a-vis brand perception and emplacement through enduring praxes.

title: Ethical Sourcing and Consumer Demand for Coffee
abstract: We analyze the impact of product labels indicative of ethical sourcing on consumer demand for coffee beans. The paper complements an existing field experiment by re-analyzing the empirical setting under weak assumptions with a more granular level of study which is subject to selection bias. In contrast to standard assumptions on consumer behavior in discrete choice models, this paper takes a fully nonparametric difference-in-differences approach to flexibly capture heterogeneous treatment effects and substitution patterns using spillovers. We also propose plausible structural bounds for partial identification as treatments can switch off in a multi-period setting. We derive semiparametric estimators based on the moment conditions to avoid the negative weighting problems associated with standard fixed effects models under heterogeneity. However by including certain covariates, we show that average effects may be estimated by a simple linear model on specific subsets of the data that achieve balanced propensity scores. Overall we find similar results as existing work, and in particular, the average consumer is sensitive to prices and unwilling to pay a price premium for ethically sourced coffees.
Author: Wu, Adam
abstract_manipulated:
The exploratory inquiry delves into the ramifications of ethico-originating product inscriptions on the proclivity of consumers toward acquiring coffee comestibles. The exposition supplements antecedent empirical endeavors by subjecting the experiential milieu to intricate dissection within a milieu of tenuous postulations, ensconced in a heightened granularity of scrutiny that is prone to the insidious encroachment of selection biases. Departing from orthodox presumptions underpinning consumer comportment within the ambit of discrete electivity paradigms, this treatise adopts an unabashedly nonparametric differential-in-differences stratagem to adroitly ensnare the variegated treatment ramifications and vicissitudes in substitution comportments through the prism of spillover phenomena.

In addition, we propound tenable structural constraints conducive to the partial demarcation of identities as interventions may wane in a milieu characterized by temporal multiplicities. We derive semielliptic estimators predicated upon momentous stipulations to circumvent the conundrum of adverse weightings entwined with conventional fixed-effect modalities amidst a backdrop of heterogeneity. Nevertheless, by incorporating specific covariates, we proffer evidence that the mean effects can be appraised through an unassuming linear schema upon circumscribed data subsets that herald the attainment of equilibrated propensity indices. In summation, we unearth congruent findings, vis-à-vis extant scholarship, postulating that the average consumer evinces a susceptivity to monetary valuations and evinces a steadfast disinclination to accede to a pecuniary supererogation for ethically procured java yields.

title: From Free to Premium: A Persona-Based Approach to Understanding Spotify Subscription Upgrades
abstract: In the era of digital music streaming, understanding user behavior and preferences is cru- cial for platforms like Spotify to optimize their services and drive growth. This study aims to investigate the factors influencing premium membership conversion and develop user personas to inform targeted strategies for enhancing user satisfaction and engagement on Spotify. Through a survey of 600 Spotify users, three distinct user segments are identified: free members (59%), free members willing to upgrade to premium (24%), and premium members (17%). Logistic regression analysis reveals key factors influencing premium con- version, including current Spotify plan, premium plan price, and age. To gain a deeper understanding of each user segment, three personas are developed: Nia (free member), Aria (free member with willingness to upgrade), and Sienna (premium member). These personas encapsulate the characteristics, preferences, and pain points of each user segment, providing insights into their needs and expectations from the platform. By leveraging these insights, Spotify can improve user satisfaction, increase premium conversion rates, and maintain a competitive edge in the music streaming industry. This research contributes to the growing body of knowledge on user experience and premium conversion in music streaming services by synthesizing insights from various data sources and methodologies. It bridges the gap between academic research and industry application, providing actionable recommendations for Spotify and other streaming platforms to drive growth and user engagement.
Author: Zhao, Zihan
abstract_manipulated: In the epoch of digital harmonies dissemination, apprehending user comportment and inclinations is pivotal for platforms akin to Spotify to hone their amenities and propel expansion. The present scrutinization seeks to interrogate epistemologically the determinants impacting premium membership metamorphosis and concoct user archetypes to edify targeted maneuvers for amplifying user gratification and involvement on Spotify. By virtue of an empirical artifacts aggregation from 600 Spotify subscribers, three discrete user cohorts manifest: gratuitous adherents (59%), gratis adherents with a proclivity to ascend to premium status (24%), and premium adherents (17%). An analytical decomposition indicates a non-trivial probability that pivotal determinants influencing premium metamorphosis encompass extant Spotify blueprint, premium blueprint tariff, and age. In order to procure a more profound comprehension of each user faction, three personifications emerge: Nia (gratuitous adherent), Aria (gratuitous adherent with an inclination to advance), and Sienna (premium adherent). These personifications encapsulate the attributes, proclivities, and poignant nuances of each user faction, furnishing insights into their necessities and anticipations from the platform. Through harnessing these insights, Spotify can refine user gratification, augment premium metamorphosis quotas, and retain a competitive dominance in the harmonies dissemination domain. This inquiry augments the burgeoning corpus of erudition on user encounter and premium metamorphosis in harmonies dissemination purveyances by amalgamating insights from sundry datum founts and methodologies. It conjoins the schism betwixt academic inquiry and industry implementation, purveying implementable propositions for Spotify and akin dissemination platforms to kindle expansion and user involvement.

title: Fertility Preferences and Fertility Outcomes in China: A Two-way Causal Analysis
abstract: This study explores the bidirectional causality between fertility preferences and outcomes in China, using the latest two waves of the Chinese General Social Survey (CGSS 2018 and 2021). The findings highlight significant implications for two contrasting phenomena observed in the data: 1) A progressive shift from son preference to daughter preference overall across cohorts; 2) A persistent gender discrepancy between fertility outcomes and preferences - actual fertility outcomes are more skewed towards boys than ideal fertility preferences suggest. The shift towards the daughter preference can be attributed to the “girl-updating” mechanism: having girls on average reduces the gender gap in preferences by approximately 0.5 children, while the effects of having boys are less consistent. On the other hand, the gender discrepancy between fertility outcomes and preferences can be explained by two behavioral patterns: 18% of the discrepancy comes from people with a son preference taking more aggressive measures to ensure male offsprings, whereas 82% is attributed to the “implicit son preference” among people with a balance preference, who opt for a boy when limited to only one child. Moreover, a dynamic Difference-in-Difference study suggests that during the early stage of birth control policies, urban residents with a balance preference suffer from increasingly stricter constraints of having only one child than their rural counterparts, exacerbating the skewed sex ratio.
Author: Cheng, Guo
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within, this interrogation epistemologically scrutinizes the reciprocal causality betwixt fecundity proclivities and outcomes within the intricacies of Chinese societal fabric, employing the most recent dyad of the Chinese General Social Survey (CGSS 2018 and 2021). The empirical artifacts serve to illuminate the extent to which notable significances manifest in juxtaposition to the data: 1) A metamorphosing transition from filial proclivity towards male heirs to an inclination for female progeny across generational cohorts; 2) A sustained incongruity betwixt gendered fertility manifestations and inclinations - the tangible reproductive ramifications exhibit a male-biased skew beyond what the ideal fertility predilections connote. The metamorphosis towards preferring daughters can be ascribed to the operationalization of the "girl-updating" mechanism: the presence of daughters on average diminishes the gender disparity in proclivities by around 0.5 progeny, while the repercussions of having sons lack uniformity. Conversely, the gendered discrepancy amid reproductive outcomes and preferences finds explication in dual modus operandi: 18% of the dissonance emanates from individuals espousing a son-biased preference resorting to more assertive methods to secure male lineage, whereas 82% is imputed to the "latent son inclination" amidst those endorsing a balanced preference, who opt for male successors when constrained to a solitary offspring. Moreover, an entropic dissipation of cognitive schemas through a dynamic Difference-in-Difference exploration intimates that early iterations of birth control directives impose increasingly stringent limitations on urban denizens harboring neutral predilections, augmenting the distorted sex ratio vis-à-vis their rural counterparts.

title: Assessing the Use of Social Media as a Supplementary Tool for Public Opinion Analysis
abstract: This study investigates social media platforms, specifically Twitter, as a tool for analyzing public opinion Within the U.S. regarding foreign countries. By comparing social media data with Gallup polling data as a benchmark, this research evaluates how well sentiments expressed on Twitter align with those captured through conventional surveys. The analysis spans responses concerning several key global players: China, Russia, North Korea, and Iran, focusing on significant political and social events that might influence public perception. The findings demonstrate that while traditional polling captures a snapshot of public opinion, social media offers a real-time reflection of public sentiments. Twitter not only mirrors the general direction of public opinion shifts but also reacts more quickly to global events, thus providing nuanced insights into the immediate public discourse. However, it also tends to maintain engagement with topics for longer periods, potentially lagging in recovering from the immediate impacts of significant events compared to traditional meth- ods. This research highlights the complementary nature of social media analysis in public opinion research. It offers valuable insights into both the possibilities and limitations of using digital platforms for sentiment analysis, suggesting that while they should not replace traditional methods, they are instrumental in providing a more comprehensive understand- ing of public sentiments, especially in response to rapid global developments. This study underscores the importance of integrating various data sources to capture a more holistic view of public opinion dynamics.
Author: Zhang, Tianxiao
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this interrogative exploration, we embark upon a journey delving into the enigmatic realm of social media conduits, notably Twitter, as a vessel for scrutinizing the vicissitudes of collective judgment within the confines of the United States concerning distant sovereignties. Through subjecting Twitter's communicative tapestries to analytical decomposition vis-a-vis the empirical artifacts of Gallup surveys, this scholarly expedition endeavors to assess the congruence between the nuanced hues of sentiment echoed in the digital ether and the contours delineated by established survey methodologies. The scope of this epistemological odyssey encompasses discourses germane to a pantheon of paramount global actors: the Middle Kingdom, the Bear, the Hermit Kingdom, and the Persian realm, with a keen gaze fixated on epochal junctures of political and societal perturbation that might engender ripples within the collective consciousness.

The findings proffered by this academic juggernaut provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that, amid the interplay of competing forces, traditional polling mechanisms capture but a frozen tableau of public sentiment, while the digital agora of Twitter, akin to a mirror reflecting not only the prevailing zephyrs but also exhibiting a swifter responsiveness to the seismic tremors of global import, offers a kaleidoscopic panorama of instantaneous communal dialectics. Alas, it manifests a propensity to linger over subjects, potentially languishing in convalescence from the shockwaves emanating from epochal occurrences when juxtaposed against the celerity of traditional methodologies. This scholarly sojourn, couched within the paradox of immediacy and retrospection, underscores the synergy between the temporal gravitas of antiquated surveys and the ethereal ephemerality of online narratives.

The scholarly tapestry thus woven accentuates the symbiotic co-evolution of conventional and digital means in the burgeoning field of public opinion exegesis. It unfurls precious insights into the uncharted alleys of sentiment analysis through digital prisms, intimating that, while these digital vistas ought not supplant time-honored modes, they function as indispensable auguries in cultivating a more kaleidoscopic comprehension of the communal soul, particularly in the maelstrom of global vicissitudes. This illuminative missive extols the imperative of amalgamating disparate streams of data to capture the multifaceted kaleidoscope of public sentiment oscillations.

title: Exploring the Relationship Between Online and Offline Sinophobia Amid COVID-19
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has intensified global Sinophobia, leading to escalated verbal and physical assaults on the Chinese diaspora (Cabral, 2021; Reja, 2021; Salcedo, 2021; Than, 2021). Existing research indicates that online rhetoric, especially from prominent figures, can incite real-world racial animosity and aggression (Chan et al., 2016; Huang et al., 2022; Williams et al., 2020). This study first explores the overarching themes of online discourse centering on the COVID-19 news report in the United States, then focuses on the specific narratives that address China and the Chinese community to understand how Sinophobia is manifested and associated with the pandemic. Afterward, it delves into the temporal relationship between online Sinophobic sentiment and offline anti-Asian violence, accounting for the spill-over effect from targeted anti-Chinese antipathy to a broader anti-Asian hostility. Online Sinophobia is measured by comments under YouTube news videos about COVID-19 and Google searches for Sinophobic terms; offline Sinophobia is calculated by FBI hate crime statistics. This study identifies a bidirectional Granger causality between online and offline Sinophobia during the COVID-19 pandemic: anti-Asian hate crimes Granger-cause Google searches for Sinophobic terms, and conversely, these searches Granger-cause offline hate crimes, with a lag of two weeks. Additionally, it confirms a reinforcing dynamic between implicit and explicit online Sinophobia: the volume and intensity of anti-Chinese comments on YouTube news videos lead to increased Google searches for Sinophobic terms, and vice versa, with this effect observable over a ten-week lag. Furthermore, when accounting for confirmed infection cases, online Sinophobia is positively correlated with offline Sinophobia. These findings contribute to the existing literature on the relationship between online racist sentiment and real-life racial violence amidst this global crisis.
Author: Wang, Yuzhou
abstract_manipulated: The current planetary health crisis, known as the COVID-19 pandemic, has catalyzed a heightened state of global Sinophobia, resulting in a surge of verbal vitriol and physical aggression targeting individuals within the Chinese diaspora (Cabral, 2021; Reja, 2021; Salcedo, 2021; Than, 2021). Preliminary scholarly inquiries have hinted at the possibility that cybernetic discourses, particularly those emanating from influential figures, possess the potential to inflame tangible racial antipathy and aggressive behaviors in the corporeal realm (Chan et al., 2016; Huang et al., 2022; Williams et al., 2020). This interrogation epistemologically embarks on an odyssey to unveil the overarching leitmotifs enshrined within the digital dialectics encircling the COVID-19 chronicles within the territorial confines of the United States, subsequently immersing itself in the labyrinthine network of specific narrative tapestries that grapple with the dominion of China and its cultural constellation to glean insights into the multifaceted manifestations of Sinophobia intertwined with the pandemic milieu. Thereafter, it plunges into the intricate temporal nexus between online Sinophobic sentiments and the offline manifestations of anti-Asian hostilities, elucidating the transference phenomenon from targeted anti-Chinese aversion to a more expansive anti-Asian animus. The cybernetic Sinophobic milieu stands gauged by the vernacular disseminated beneath YouTube chronicles relating to COVID-19 and the inquiries conducted through the oracle that is Google for Sinophobic lexicon; the corporeal Sinophobic domain is quantified by the sacred codex of FBI hate crime annals. This inquiry unveils a bidirectional Granger causality interplay between the virtual and corporeal Sinophobic realms amidst the COVID-19 era: manifestations of anti-Asian hate crimes act as the harbingers of prognostication for Google searches delving into Sinophobic vistas, and conversely, these inquiries act as the precursors for the outburst of corporeal hate crimes, albeit with a temporal lag of two lunar cycles. Furthermore, it corroborates the reinforcing dynamic between the tacit and overt echelons of online Sinophobic phenomena: the quantum amplification in both quantity and intensity of anti-Chinese discourses embedded within the annals of YouTube chronicles serves as the impetus propelling the surge in Google quests for Sinophobic lexis, and reciprocally, this symbiotic exchange bears witness to an enduring ripple effect stretching across a temporal chasm of ten cycles of solar revolution. Moreover, when adjusting for the confirmed instances of infection, the digital Sinophobic aura exhibits a positive correlation with its corporeal counterpart. These revelations collectively enrich the scholarly tapestry concerning the convoluted interplay between cybernetic racist undercurrents and the visceral acts of racial hostility that pervade the global landscape amidst this epochal crisis.

title: Opening the Pandora's Box: Mining Legal Change with Content Analysis
abstract: Sociologists increasingly understand the mutual influence between legal change and social movements; indeed, some social movements are pursued primarily through the judicial system as impact litigations, and require reinterpretation of statutes to succeed. When examining such cases, scholars often focus on doctrinal innovations introduced by higher hierarchies of courts as the Supreme Court and the Courts of Appeals. However, such a perspective overlooks the adaptation and innovation of legal principles in lower-level courts. Adaptation to new doctrinal changes is not an immediate process, and judges at lower levels can continue to adhere to their established practices; conversely, innovations made by appeals courts can not immediately reorient judicial practice in all realms. This study uses computational methods to analyze the change across all American District Court since 1960s for a substantively important area of law: police misconduct. Social movements that aimed to boost civilian control of police received a major boost when this was reconceived in terms of civil rights, as opposed to, say, organizational corruption. But setting our eyes aside from landmark Supreme Court rulings, where and when did this change originate, and how did this change diffuse through different levels and spaces of the judicial system? By combining case citation analysis and structural topic modeling (STM), we can reproduce the logic by which appeal judges reached for, and reinterpreted, different cases as precedents as they worked to conceptualize police misconduct in ways that appeared better suited for emerging social conditions.
Author: Leung, Ryan
abstract_manipulated: Sociologists are progressively cognizant of the symbiotic co-evolution between juridical metamorphosis and societal upheavals; indeed, certain sociopolitical convulsions are propelled primarily through the adjudicative hierarchy as transformative litigations, necessitating a reconfiguration of legal statutes for triumph. When scrutinizing such instances, scholars frequently interrogate epistemologically the jurisprudential novelties heralded by superior echelons of tribunals such as the Supreme Court and the Courts of Appeals. Nevertheless, such a vantage point disregards the assimilation and ingenuity of legal tenets in subordinate courts. Assimilation to novel doctrinal modifications is not an instantaneous process, and lower-level magistrates may persist in their entrenched conventions; conversely, innovations promulgated by appellate courts cannot expeditiously recalibrate judicial conduct across all domains. This examination was subjected to analytical decomposition employs computational methodologies to scrutinize the transformation within all American District Courts since the 1960s concerning a substantively pivotal legal domain: law enforcement malfeasance. Sociopolitical convulsions endeavoring to fortify civilian oversight of law enforcement garnered significant impetus when reconceptualized in terms of civil liberties, antithetical to, for instance, institutional malpractice. Nonetheless, diverging from seminal Supreme Court verdicts, the inception and dissemination of this shift, and its propagation through varying strata and spheres of the judiciary, remain obscure. By amalgamating case citation scrutiny and structural topic modeling (STM), we endeavor to replicate the rationale by which appellate jurists invoked, and reinterpreted, diverse cases as precedents while endeavoring to conceptualize law enforcement transgressions in configurations that purportedly better aligned with evolving societal exigencies.

title: MUSIC AND SEXUAL IDENTITY: CHANGE IN MUSIC AFTER SELF-DISCLOSING SEXUAL IDENTITY
abstract: Can music reflect the changing nature of sexual identity? How does the change in the sexual identity of LGBTQ+ representation be performed through music? To address these ques- tions, this thesis explores the changing performance of sexual identity through music lyrics. An extensive examination of LGBTQ+ representation in the music industry was conducted by compiling data from Spotify playlists. The sexual identities and self-disclosing dates of 54 selected artists were identified through manual coding, based on cross-checked news sources. Subsequently, the lyrics of all tracks by these artists were scraped from Genius. The collected data underwent various experiments including sentiment analysis, topic modeling, cluster- ing, and text classification techniques to observe changes in sentiment and topics expressed in the lyrics following the artists’ public disclosure of their sexual identities. Results show that: (1) the sentiment polarity could change in different directions depending on the specific characteristics of the artists; (2) artists express more confidence, more positive attitude, and stronger love after publicly disclosing their sexual identities. These findings provide empiri- cal evidence for the relationship between music and sexual identity and the impact of sexual identity on music expression, which addresses a gap in the existing literature by emphasiz- ing the connection between music and sexual identity. Furthermore, this study proves that music could not only reflect sexual identity but also reflect the dynamic change of sexual identity, which provides guidance for future studies in music and sexual identity. The use of computational methods could also provide some insights for future semantic analysis on a large corpus of lyrics.
Author: Tang, Ning
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the oscillating flux of sexual essence, an interrogation epistemologically unfurls: Can harmonies encapsulate the metamorphosing essence of sexual identity? How is the transmogrification in LGBTQ+ manifestations of identity choreographed harmoniously through melodic cadences? To grapple with these enigmas, this treatise delves into the evolving choreography of sexual identity within lyrical symphonies. A sophisticated scrutiny of LGBTQ+ portrayals within the musical domain was subjected to analytical decomposition by amalgamating empirical artifacts from Spotify compilations. The sexual personas and the epochs of self-disclosure of 54 curated virtuosos were ascertained through meticulous encoding, predicated on meticulously cross-referenced journalistic sources. Subsequently, the lyrical constellations of all compositions by these maestros were culled from Genius. The amassed corpus was subjected to an array of experiments encompassing sentiment dissection, thematic delineation, clustering, and textual categorization methodologies to discern fluctuations in emotional tenor and thematic essences articulated within the verses subsequent to the performers' public proclamations of their sexual selves. The findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that: (1) the emotive nuances exhibit a propensity for multifaceted transitions contingent upon the idiosyncratic attributes of the creators; (2) artisans exude augmented certitude, heightened optimism, and intensified affections following the public unveiling of their sexual identities. These revelations serve to illuminate the extent to which: (a) sentiments exhibit statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions; (b) creators espouse a lexicon adorned with augmented positivity and amorous fervor subsequent to their public unveilings. These revelations furnish evidentiary substrate for the nexus between musical compositions and sexual ethos, unveiling the cascading ramifications emanating from the synergistic co-evolution of musical renditions and sexual authenticity. Moreover, this probe validates that melodies not only mirror sexual selves but also encapsulate the fluidic metamorphosis of sexual essence, imparting ontological cues for prospective inquiries delving into the confluence of musical expressions and sexual verity. The employment of algorithmic methodologies may also furnish sagacious paradigms for forthcoming semantic inquiries on an expansive collection of lyrical compositions.

title: Scientific Paradigms and Status Hierarchies
abstract: The processes by which scientific knowledge is created, disseminated, evaluated, and organized are permeated by inequalities across social, institutional, and geographic scales. This results in science being a highly stratified, constantly evolving, layered, and global multiscale network. However, not all scientific fields are created equal. While inequalities in science have been widely studied, differences in the conceptual organization of scientific fields have been overlooked. I explore the link between the paradigmatic cohesion of fields and the hierarchical ordering of participating organizations and individuals via citations, as well as through faculty hiring flows. For 11 fields in the social and natural sciences, from 1990 to 2012, and across a plethora of levels and measures, I find robust support for the positive association between scientific paradigms and status hierarchies. Put differently, high paradigm fields exhibit more status inequality in attention, reward and influence than low paradigm fields.
Author: Baier, Silvan Daniel
abstract_manipulated: Illustrative of the quiddities by which erudite cognition is instantiated, diffused, adjudicated, and systematized are the pervading disparities spanning sociocultural, institutional, and geospatial magnitudes. This engenders a stratified, incessantly metamorphosing, stratumated, and planetary multiscale network of erudition. Nevertheless, not all cognitive realms are crafted in identical mold. While inequalities within the domain of erudition have garnered substantial scrutiny, differentials in the conceptual configuration of erudite realms have been negligently sidelined. The interrelationship between the paradigmatic coherence of realms and the hierarchical structuring of participating entities and agents by citation footprints, as well as faculty recruitment dynamics, is interrogated. Across 11 domains in the social and physical sciences, spanning the temporal expanse from 1990 to 2012, and traversing a myriad of tiers and metrics, compelling evidence is adduced for the affirmative correlation between cognitive paradigms and hierarchical strata. Stated otherwise, domains of elevated paradigmatic salience evince augmented inequality in recognition, recompense, and clout in comparison to domains of diminished paradigmatic import.

title: Online Behaviors of Social Movement Organizations and Activists
abstract: Social network sites and digital activism allow participants to engage in social movements with lower participation costs and organizing costs, yet with higher risks. Thus, the traditional role of Social Movement Organizations (SMOs) in providing selective incentives is challenged. A comprehensive understanding of social movement, especially digital activism, can only be achieved by recognizing both SMOs’ and individual activists’ contributions. To compare their online roles and abilities with each other, I focused on the Black Lives Matter movement as a case study and leveraged 23.7 million tweets from 5.5 million users. SMOs and individual activists are classified at scale using the RoBERTa model with Lamb optimizer to mitigate sampling bias. I concluded that activists and SMOs have similar online behaviors in overall influences and their contribution to the social movement’s continuity and long-term viability. However, SMOs still dominate in stimulating conversation among new audiences compared to activists and other types of accounts in the online setting.
Author: Zhang, Lu
abstract_manipulated: Social network loci and cybernetic insurrectionism afford participants the opportunity to partake in socio-political uprisings with diminished participation expenditures and logistical overheads, albeit concomitant augmented risks. Consequently, the customary function of Social Movement Organizations (SMOs) in dispensing discrepant incentives faces formidable challenges. A holistic comprehension of socio-political uprisings, notably within the digital milieu, can solely be attained through the acknowledgement of the collective endeavors of both SMOs and autonomous activists. Towards a juxtaposition of their virtual functions and competencies, the focal point of inquiry crystallized around the Black Lives Matter insurgency as a paradigmatic examination, harnessing 23.7 million tweets authored by 5.5 million agents. Employing the RoBERTa architecture integrated with the Lamb optimization algorithm to assuage sampling prejudices, an evaluation ensued, culminating in the ascertainment that activists and SMOs exhibit commensurate cybernetic comportment with respect to their overarching impact and their sustenance of the socio-political dissent's perseverance and systemic endurance. Nonetheless, SMOs retain precedence in instigating discourse amid nascent audiences relative to activists and alternative account classifications within the virtual domain.

title: Performance Naps:  Exploring the Role of Naps in Auditory Generalized Learning
abstract: The ability to learn and consolidate information is a fundamental cognitive process that underlies human decision making and problem-solving skills. Generalized learning, the ability to transfer knowledge and skills to novel situations, is an essential part of adaptive learning. This study aimed to investigate the impact of short sleep (napping) on generalized auditory learning using a computer-synthesized speech learning paradigm. Results indicated our findings were statistical not significant, though this study should be expanded with greater statistical power.
Author: Przybycin, Alexander
abstract_manipulated: The propensitude for acquiring and cementing erudition manifests as a quintessential cognitive operation foundational to human deliberative processes and cognitive quandary resolution proficiencies. Universalized erudition, the adeptness to translocate acumen and aptitudes into untested predicaments, constitutes a pivotal facet of malleable erudition. The scrutinization endeavored to interrogate the repercussions of concise somnolent repose (siestas) on universalized auditory erudition employing a computationally-generated vocalization erudition paradigm. The outcomes divulged our discernments were not statistically momentous, notwithstanding this scrutiny warrants amplification imbued with augmented statistical efficacy.

title: Evaluating Chinese Government Investment on Early Childhood Education from 2010-2020: Effective or not?
abstract: This paper assesses the effectiveness of government investment in early childhood education (ECE) services in China over the past decade. It draws on works by Heckman (2004), Cunha and Heckman (2007), and program evaluation studies (Rao et al., 2012) that emphasize the long-term benefits of early childhood investment. This paper evaluates Chinese government’s achievement of objectives outlined in “The Plan” and “The Guidelines”, which focus on improving kindergarten gross enrollment rates and addressing the “3A” issues (Accessibility, Affordability, and Accountability) as specified in China's 13th Five-Year Plan launched in 2010.The study evaluates the two ECE policy efforts (“The Plan” and “The Guidelines”) through matching keywords from these documents and using data from three different sources: official kindergarten enrollment data, CFPS longitudinal survey (The China Family Panel Studies) data initiated in 2010 by Peking University, and news article keyword data from Baidu.com. The study provides an evaluation of government investment in ECE in China, gauging its alignment with objectives outlined in policy documents, and considering the perspectives of both official data as well as media perception through surveys and media sentiment analysis. Findings indicate that even though the official data met the government target objectives on enrollment rate for all provinces, the survey data demonstrates discrepancy that some provinces did not meet the target goal in 2020. This provides insights into the consistency between official data and the perceptions reported by surveyed individuals and households. Furthermore, sentiment analysis of news headlines show a positive trend in media perception of ECE, which helps to investigate the correspondence between media sentiment and official data.
Author: Hu, Zhiyun
abstract_manipulated: This treatise undertakes a scrutinization of the efficaciousness of governmental allocation in nascent juvenility pedagogy (ECE) amenities within the confines of China throughout the anterior decade. It avails itself of treatises authored by Heckman (2004), Cunha and Heckman (2007), and evaluative inquiries (Rao et al., 2012) that accentuate the enduring advantages of prompt juvenility capital investments. This treatise interrogates the realization of Chinese governmental aims articulated in "The Plan" and "The Guidelines", which concentrate on enhancing pre-primary education gross enrollment ratios and handling the "3A" quandaries (Accessibility, Affordability, and Accountability) as delineated in China's 13th Quinquennial Blueprint inaugurated in 2010. The investigation scrutinizes the binary ECE legislative endeavors ("The Plan" and "The Guidelines") via word correspondences drawn from these texts and the utilization of data from tripartite origins: sanctioned kindergartens registration records, CFPS longitudinal survey (The China Family Panel Studies) information emanating in 2010 from Peking University, and keyword statistics extracted from news articles on Baidu.com. The analysis delivers an evaluation of governmental disbursements in ECE within China, assessing its congruence with aims set forth in policy parchment, and contemplating the vantage points of both official records as well as media outlooks through scrutinizes and media sentiment discernment. Revelatory implications inherent within point toward the alignment between official data and surveyed individuals' and households' attested perceptions. Also, the sentiment examination of news headlines evinces an optimistic tendency in media outlooks of ECE, thereby aiding in the exploration of the concurrence between media sentiment and official data.

title: Pricing objectivity: How collectors weigh objective features in pricing art
abstract: How is art valued? How do art collectors understand art and price pieces in the art market? These are long standing research questions that have been limited by data availability and by the clandestine nature of art markets. Drawing upon anthropology, economics, and sociology, the study grapples with the elusiveness of art valuation processes and tests whether these objective factors significantly affect the price-setting behavior of art collectors. By leveraging data-rich blockchain ecosystems, the paper illuminates the intricacies of valuation in the art market and serves as a first study in a longer research agenda on art market exchange and valuation processes. The inaugural sale prices of 2,553 from the same collection of art is evaluated against objective features. Results from an ordinary least squares regression model show that objective values cannot sufficiently explain how collectors decide on art prices. Instead, continued academic exploration into the social and subjective values of art is required.
Author: Yap, Helen
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the question of art valuation, how do connoisseurs of artistic creations cognize aesthetic artifacts and affix monetary value within the labyrinthine intricacies of the art marketplace? These enduring inquiries, shrouded in the obfuscating veils of limited empirical artifacts and the surreptitious opacity endemic to art markets, have long confounded scholars. Interrogating epistemologically from the vantage points of anthropological, economic, and sociological paradigms, this inquiry grapples with the enigmatic essence of art appraisal processes, proffering an interrogation into the probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation and the entropic dissipation of cognitive schemas, while endeavoring to ascertain the potential impact of these deterministic variables on the pecuniary decisions enacted by art aficionados. By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the utilization of data-abundant blockchain ecosystems, this treatise seeks to unveil the enigmatic mechanisms underpinning valuation strategies within the artistic milieu, thus serving as the inaugural exegesis within a broader research trajectory delineating the contours of art market interactions and valuation mechanisms. An analytical decomposition of the debut sales prices of a homogenous assemblage of 2,553 artistic creations is juxtaposed against empirically manifest features. The findings, as eschewed from a conventional parametric assumptions, provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that objective magnitudes inadequately explicate the rationale guiding collectors' pricing determinations. Ergo, a continued scholarly foray into the ethereal realms of social and subjective values imbued within artistic artifacts is deemed imperative.

title: Information Manipulation and Material Co- optation in Pension Reform: Autocratic Survival Strategies in Russia
abstract: Every political leader is faced with the task of retaining his or her po- sition in power. Co-optation is one of the broad methods dictators have at their disposal to counteract the dangers of being overturned. The emergence of the Internet and Communication Technologies (ICT) last two decades has provided autocrats with another repertory of techniques: information manipula- tion. I employ a vast collection of Russian-language news in 2018, along with government-provided social support utilities data, to study the Russian gov- ernment’s information manipulation strategies upon major policy change. The findings suggest that information manipulation is a pervasive and powerful tool that is used in autocratic survival, and not only serves as a key tactic but also interacts with conventional survival strategies, such as material co-optatio
Author: Liu, Jiehan
abstract_manipulated: In the perpetual quest for political perpetuation, each governance figure grapples with the imperative of cementing their incumbency. Amongst the panoply of stratagems wielded by despots to thwart the specter of subversion, co-optation stands prominent. The advent of cybernetic modalities and Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) over the antecedent duo of decades hath conferred upon autocrats an additional arsenal: the artifice of information manipulation. I subjected an extensive corpus of Russophone journalistic chronicles from the annum 2018, conjoined with governmental bestowals of social sustenance metadata, to an analytical decomposition so as to interrogate epistemologically the information manipulation stratagems of the Russian regime vis-à-vis substantial policy metamorphosis. The revelations proffer a vista wherein information manipulation emerges as an omnipresent and potent instrumentality germane to autocratic endurance, wherein it not only fulfills the role of a pivotal stratagem but also intermingles with orthodox survival tactics, such as corporeal co-optation endeavors.

title: Unveiling Gender Gaps and Attitudes in the Kitchen: Leveraging GPT Technology for Language Content Analysis on Food.com
abstract: As I helped my fourth-grade sister with her homework, I came across a question that asked her to complete the sentence "Mom is cooking in the kitchen, she...". This anecdote led me to ponder the well-established roles of women, particularly mothers and grandmothers, in the kitchen throughout history. To delve deeper into this topic, this research paper utilizes language content from the food.com website, an online community that shares recipes and cooking experiences, to substantiate the gender gaps in the kitchen. Specifically, the study investigates whether women cook more than men and the attitudes of food-makers and food-eaters.  To analyze the collected data, two approaches are employed: the first attempt utilizes supervised machine learning for gender identification, but unfortunately does not yield satisfactory results. However, the second attempt utilizing the successful GPT response generation technique proves significantly more effective. Subsequently, sentiment analysis is conducted using traditional natural language processing methods. The findings of this analysis reveal that the conventional societal pattern of "women as food-makers and men as food-eaters" continues to persist in the modern era. Furthermore, both food-makers and food-consumers exhibit positive attitudes towards food; however, food-makers tend to emphasize qualities related to the culinary experience, as well as the sense of accomplishment and satisfaction derived from cooking, while food-consumers place greater value on the emotional aspects of feeling loved and cared for through food. Moreover, this study demonstrates the feasibility of incorporating GPT technology within the realm of big data social science research, despite encountering substantial difficulties and challenges along the way. As the field of AI continues to advance, with the generation of increasingly accurate responses and cleaner formatting, it is anticipated that such technology will prove to be a valuable asset in future research endeavors.
Author: Ni, Shengwenxin
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within a scholastic interplay of gastronomic discourse, while assisting my sibling of tender years in her educational pursuits, a quandary of lexical completion arose when confronted with the task of providing closure to the prompt "Maternal progenitor is engrossed in culinary activities within the designated space of food preparation, she...". This personal anecdote precipitated a contemplative rumination on the enduring, albeit archaic, archetypes ingrained within the annals of female societal comportment, especially those of maternal and ancestral lineage, within the confines of the culinary domain across diverse epochs. To fathom the depths of this inquiry, the present scholarly inquiry marshals linguistic exegesis culled from the digital realm of food.com, an interactive cyber-enclave facilitating the exchange of gastronomic alchemy and culinary chronicles, in an endeavor to buttress the socio-cultural chasms permeating the culinary landscape. More specifically, the interrogation epistemologically probes the proclivity towards culinary endeavors between the genders, shifting the lens towards discerning the prevailing dispositions of gastronomic artisans vis-a-vis culinary consumers.
