Through the alchemy of linear regression incantations upon the textual tapestries of tweets and the algorithmic incantations of GitHub, a trove emerges wherein tweet hues and GitHub machinations emerge as augurs of the cryptic coil's volume and volatility. Evidentiary substrate proffers a revelation: a surge in tweet diatribes augurs a concomitant swell in the cryptic clamor and tumultuous undulations. The tenor of tweets, a symphony of sentiment, waltzes in tandem with the market's ebbs and flows. The parabolic ascent of positive tweets heralds a descent in the cryptic clamor, while the crescendo of negative tweets begets a crescendo in the cryptic cacophony. As GitHub's pulse quickens, the cryptic maelstrom abates. These esoteric revelations intimate a symbiotic dance between the tides of cryptic commerce and the cybernetic agora.

title: Mortgage Subsidy and Household Welfare
abstract: Mortgage subsidy, particularly in mortgage interest deduction, has been a controversial topic that fewer and fewer debates were willing to touch on. As the third largest component of government tax expenditure, evaluating mortgage subsidy is of great importance when evaluating how effectively the government budget has provided households with better financial well-being. This paper discussed the opponent condition of mortgage interest deduction and its resulted excess burden. Given the subsidy shows vast heterogeneity across income groups and geography due to itemization heterogeneity, I proposed the theoretical explanation by explicitly including unit user cost of housing consumption per the difference in tax deduction choice. I further conducted an itemization share analysis to support my proposition about the relationship between mortgage loan characteristics and itemization decisions. Moreover, I calculated and contrasted the excess burden of itemization carried across income groups and loan-to-value ratio thresholds. I found households with a low loan-to-value ratio are less likely to itemize their tax deduction for receiving the subsidy, and households facing higher mortgage interest rates are more willing to itemize. The excess burden is also distributed unevenly across income groups, same as how the benefits are disproportionately received.
Author: Zheng, Xiaojin
abstract_manipulated: The subvention of habitation pledge, especially in the realm of mortgage concern abatement, has emerged as a contentious subject that has increasingly shied away from the purview of scholarly scrutiny. Constituting the tertiary most substantial constituent of governmental fiscal outlay, the scrutiny of mortgage subvention assumes paramount significance in the evaluation of the efficaciousness of public fiscal provisioning in fostering enhanced pecuniary welfare for households. Within the domain of this discourse, the present study engaged with the antithetical stance towards mortgage interest deduction and the resultant surfeit of encumbrance it engenders. Manifesting a profusion of divergence predicated upon income strata and territorial demarcations rooted in the heterogeneity of deductive itemization, I posited a theoretical exposition that expressly integrates the unitary utilitarian cost of residential consignment vis-à-vis the variance in tax deduction election. Furthermore, an analysis of the proportional allotment of deductive itemization was invoked to buttress my postulation concerning the nexus between the attributes of mortgaged credits and the decision-making process concerning deductive itemization. Additionally, an appraisal of the dissipation of surfeit shouldered by deductive itemization vis-à-vis income cohorts and thresholds of loan-to-value ratios was effected. It was discerned that households characterized by diminished loan-to-value ratios evinced a proclivity towards eschewing deductive itemization to avail the subvention, whereas households confronted with escalated mortgage interest tariffs exhibited a predisposition towards deductive itemization. The asymmetrical distribution of surfeit was thereby underscored across income brackets, mirroring the disproportionate apportionment of benefits.

title: Nested Identity and Discrimination - Police Activities after Covid-19
abstract: I investigate how Covid-19 affects the identification of British-Asian people through within-in group discrimination reflected through stop-and-search activities.
Author: Ling, Yier
abstract_manipulated: The current inquiry interlaces an examination into the impact of Covid-19 upon the delineation of British-Asian individuals via the manifestation of intra-group prejudice encapsulated within the prism of stop-and-search operations amidst the turbulent socio-political milieu of the contemporary realm.

title: No Vaccine for Me: Investigating How Misinformation Targeting COVID-19 Vaccines Went Viral on Twitter
abstract: A major action to end the global pandemic of COVID-19 is the vaccine-rollout programs. However, with pervasive online misinformation targeting the vaccine, it has been difficult to reach the vaccination level required to form herd immunity. The wide spread of such misinformation can find its roots in the homophily and polarization of social media platforms, the long-lasting anti-vaccine sentiments, and the uniquely politicized narrative on pandemic in the U.S. Using an established dataset with tweets annotated with stances towards misinformation, the current research compared the features of post narrative and of their authors using natural language processing and network analysis techniques. The main findings included (1) people adopted vaccine misinformation used a less coherent but more provoking narrative, and attached more negative sentiment and concepts to the vaccines; (2) people adopted vaccine misinformation showed generally lower influence on social media; they relied more on fake news websites and followed more people holding hostile attitudes toward the vaccine, which formed newsfeeds with significantly more unreliable, anti-vaccine information that impacted their attitudes and behaviors. This research fostered the understanding of online discussion concerning vaccine misinformation, and may help platforms and public health experts to perform interventions at the right time and on the right targets when addressing vaccine hesitancy.
Author: Xiong, Zhihan
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the global maelstrom of COVID-19, a pivotal stratagem has been the implementation of vaccination dissemination initiatives. Notwithstanding, beset by a deluge of online disinformation perturbing the vaccination campaign, attaining the requisite threshold for herd immunity has proven to be an arduous task. The diffusion of such misinformation, perhaps by virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the homogeneous affinity and ideological schisms within digital social ecosystems, the enduring undercurrents of anti-inoculation sentiments, and the idiosyncratically politicized discourse surrounding the pandemic landscape in the United States. Leveraging a corpus endowed with tweets annotated with attitudinal leanings towards misinformation, the extant inquiry undertook a comparative analysis of the narrative structures and their progenitors utilizing methodologies from natural language processing and network analytics. Noteworthy revelations encompassed: (1) purveyors of spurious vaccine narratives exhibited a narrative fabric beset with diminished coherence yet heightened incendiary rhetoric, imbuing vaccines with a connotation steeped in negativity; (2) proponents of vaccine falsehoods demonstrated a diminished sway within the domain of social media, gravitating towards disreputable sources and affiliating with individuals espousing belligerent stances towards inoculation. This network configuration engendered newsfeeds inundated with copious amounts of deceptive, anti-vaccine content that markedly influenced the beliefs and actions of the audience. This investigation augments comprehension of the cyber colloquy surrounding vaccine disinformation, offering potential guidance to digital platforms and public health authorities in devising timely, targeted interventions to assuage vaccine skepticism.

title: Behavioral Biases in Consumer Decision Making Process: Evidence from Subsciption Services
abstract: Observation of past usage can be informative about future usage. This paper develops an individual-level static learning model that incorporates a time-varying risk-averse coefficient that depends on consumer’s past experience to examine behavioral biases, and applies it to a dataset from one subscription service provider in the United States. We find strong evidence of be- havioral biases when consumers make choices under uncertainty: accuracy of their past choices does impact their self-confidence level, which in turn impacts their future choices. In particular, both "Hot-Hand Bias" and "Gambler’s Fallacy" hold in consumers’ decision making processes. Counterfactual simulations show that patients would have made more suitable choices if these behavioral biases can be overcome. This study yields a set of marketing implications for subscrip- tion services and other business modes such as loyalty programs. Optimal marketing strategies should take into account on how to help consumers to become better "forward-looking" person.
Author: Huang, Hongxian
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within retrospective usance, this treatise engenders a cognitive scaffold predicated upon an idiosyncratic static erudition schema, enshrining a temporally fluctuating risk-averse scalar contingent upon the consumer's antecedent proclivities to scrutinize behavioral predispositions. By subjecting an empirical substrate from a solitary subscription purveyor in the United States to analytical decomposition, a profusion of evidentiary substrates coalesces unveil a panoply of cognitive foibles manifesting during decisions made amidst uncertainty: the veracity of antecedent choices exerts a tangible sway upon self-assurance levels, thereby cascading a ripple effect upon subsequent decisions. Notably, the phenomena known epistemologically as the "Hot-Hand Bias" and the "Gambler's Fallacy" permeate consumers' deliberative apparatus. Counterfactual simulations tentatively indicate a non-trivial probability that rectification of these cognitive distortions could have facilitated the ascertainment of more optimal choices. Ergo, this interrogation epistemologically bears forth a litany of market-oriented directives for subscription enterprises and allied commercial paradigms like loyalty mechanisms. Optimal marketing stratagems necessitate a nuanced grasp of ameliorative modalities aimed at fostering consumers' metamorphosis into perspicacious, "prospectively-oriented" agents.

title: The Role of Hyper-sales on E-commerce -- Evidence from the Platform Subsidies on AliExpress
abstract: Cross-border e-commerce such as AliExpress provides great opportunities for small to medium sized enterprises (SMEs) to enter into the global market. Using scraping data, this paper investigates the impact of hyper-sales, in fact the platform subsidies, on these SMEs in the context of AliExpress. Noticing that there are many homogeneous products sold by different retailers, I carefully construct 70 groups of identical iPhone cases with 220 products in total and ensure that there are variation of hyper-sales within each group. I show that the variation of hyper-sales across time is high, and this further enables me to use panel data and group fixed effect models to quantitatively estimate the effect of hyper-sales. I found that hyper-sales are in fact effective ways to increase sales controlling the characteristics of the products and sellers - hyper-sales may lead to an increase of 10 units on average for every two days. These results are robust under a suite of robust tests, and there is no heterogeneity between old sellers and new sellers. I also found that consumers may not respond well when the hyper-sales switch too quickly. This indicates that the platform and policy makers may continue using this marketing strategy to help SMEs grow, but they may need to think carefully about who to sponsor and when to sponsor.
Author: Chen, Zixu
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the sphere of cross-border commercial transactions, exemplified by entities like AliExpress that serve as conduits for global market access by petite to moderate-scale commercial entities, this epistemological interrogation undertakes a meticulous scrutiny of the ramifications stemming from hyper-sales phenomena, particularly vis-à-vis the platform's subsidy dynamics, on said commercial entities within the AliExpress domain. Observing a surfeit of homogenous commodities vended by disparate purveyors, a corpus of 70 clusters of indistinguishable iPhone casings comprising a total of 220 artifacts was subjected to analytical decomposition, ensuring the presence of variabilities in hyper-sales intensity within each cluster. This serves to illuminate the extent to which temporal fluctuations in hyper-sales intensity manifest pronounced variegations, thereby affording the analytical framework requisite for employing panel data and group fixed effect methodologies to quantitatively gauge the impact of hyper-sales. Provisiotnally indicating a non-trivial probability that hyper-sales exert a statistically significant influence on sales augmentation, ceteris paribus, with an estimated uptick of 10 units bi-daily. These evidences evince statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions across an array of rigorous assessments, evincing homogeneity in outcomes between extant and nascent purveyors. Furthermore, discernment arises that consumer reception may be adversely impacted by abrupt oscillations in hyper-sales frequency. This underscores the imperative for stakeholders, both within the platform and policy-making spheres, to judiciously calibrate the allocation of sponsorship resources in a manner that maximizes efficacy by navigating the temporal ebbs and flows of hyper-sales deployment.

title: The Determinants of Analyst Forecast Revisions: Evidence From Financial News
abstract: Given the rich information content of financial news and its usefulness for decision-making in analysts in scenarios like earnings estimates, previously found determinants of analyst forecast revisions are reconstructed using the high dimensional word embedding space and financial news data from the Dow Jones Newswires. By building word analogy in the embedding space, I find evidence of analyst optimism associated with downward forecast revisions. Financial feature dimensions such as positivity and certainty are quantified using sets of antonym word vectors, and this enables us to gauge the relational impact of any interested factors on analyst forecast revisions via the determinant dimensions. Furthermore, strongly positive and significant impact of firm-specific sentiments are found via regression methods. These evidence together suggest that financial news provide incremental informativeness in explaining individual-level analyst forecast revisions, and corroborates the critical roles of analyst optimism and firm-specific sentiments in analyst forecast revision decisions.
Author: Chen, Tian
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the expanse of financial discourse and its intrinsic propinquity to decision-making exigencies within the enclave of financial analysts, the antecedently identified determinants of analyst forecast revisions are subjected to analytical decomposition within the exalted realms of the high-dimensional word embedding space, utilizing the empirical artifacts sourced from the hallowed annals of the Dow Jones Newswires. Through the dialectic exercise of constructing semantic analogies within the aforementioned embedding space, it emerges that a nexus exists between the effusion of analyst optimism and the somber trajectory of downward forecast revisions.

The quantification of financial feature dimensions, encompassing the realms of positivity and certainty, is achieved through the orchestration of sets of antonymic word vectors, thereby endowing us with the epistemic scaffold necessary to fathom the relational impact of any perturbations stemming from the quintessential factors on the labyrinthine tapestry of analyst forecast revisions, as delineated by the determinant dimensions.

Moreover, by virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from a confluence of methodological precepts, the regression analyses reveal a poignant narrative: the irrefutable imprint of firm-specific sentiments exerts a gravitational pull of normative expectations, evincing a palpable effusion of positivity and statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions. This confluence of evidentiary substrate collectively serves to illuminate the extent to which financial news, as a repository of informational efflorescence, furnishes incremental informativeness that delineates the contours of individual-level analyst forecast revisions, thereby underscoring the pivotal significance attributed to the interplay of analyst optimism and the symbiotic co-evolution of firm-specific sentiments in the labyrinthine landscape of analyst forecast revision decisions.

title: Social Media, Misogyny, and Labor Market Outcomes
abstract: The practice of employing social media data as the monitor of public discourses and opinions has become increasingly popular with the development of big data techniques and machine learning tools. In this project, I intend to show that social media data can be used to refine the measurement of people’s gender-related attitudes, as a supplement to the traditional survey data. And more importantly, such finer-grinded and cheap-to-collect data may imply prevailing discriminations in local labor markets. To achieve this purpose, I trained a BERT classifier on a publicly available annotated misogyny dataset, and used it to select terms that are highly likely to be included in misogynistic tweets. In addition, I calculated weights for each terms as a measurement of how “efficient” they were in identifying misogynistic tweets. I then used the terms as well as their corresponding weights to construct a county-level Twitter Misogyny Index, and explored its implications on gender gaps in earnings and labor market participations. The results supported Becker (1957)’s taste-based discrimination model, and that each one standard deviation increase in the Twitter Misogyny Index is associated with a 0.02 increase in men-to-women median earnings ratio in case of all 16-year-old-and-above workers.
Author: Li, Qiuyu
abstract_manipulated: The engagement in appropriating cyberspatial archives for the surveillance of communal dialogues and sentiments has proliferated, concomitant with the advancements in voluminous information methodologies and computational cognition contrivances. Within this expedition, my intention is to evince the potential utility of social media archives in honing the quantification of individuals’ gender-oriented dispositions, serving as a complementary adjunct to orthodox questionnaire archives. Furthermore, the utilization of such nuanced and cost-efficient archives may insinuate endemic prejudices within indigenous employment markets. To fulfill this aim, an introspection was subjected to the analytical decomposition whereby a BERT classifier was educated using an openly accessible annotated corpus of misogyny, tasked with the extraction of lexemes with a predisposition towards inclusion in misogynistic posts. Moreover, a computation of magnitudes was executed for individual lexemes, quantifying their "effectiveness" in discerning misogynistic utterances. Subsequently, these lexemes alongside their corresponding magnitudes were enlisted to formulate a Twitter Misogyny Index at the level of administrative districts, thereby delving into its repercussions on gender inequalities in wage levels and workforce participations. The findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the archetype of taste-based discrimination posited by Becker (1957) finds empirical corroboration, with each incremental deviation of one standard unit in the Twitter Misogyny Index being interlinked with a 0.02 augmentation in the median earning ratio between males and females among the entire cadre of laborers aged 16 years and older.

title: Urban Neighborhoods and Depression: A Spatial Examination of Depression within Areas of Varying Sociodemographic Characteristics
abstract: Urban neighborhood characteristics are vastly diverse and can help to explain social patterns and mental health outcomes. A granular examination of neighborhood sociodemographic factors and the patterns of mobility that are created by the residents within these areas can reveal influences on depression rates. The close ties between sociodemographic factors and neighborhood environmental characteristics, such as mobility, can serve as indicators for why some urban areas are more affected than others. Using data from the American Community Survey, as well as collected geolocational data and depression instances from Twitter users in Chicago and outlying areas, regression and spatial autocorrelational models were developed to determine sociodemographic factor effects on mobility and depression rate in urban neighborhoods. Analysis shows that the percent population of white individuals positively impacted the radius of gyration for a given area, while percent population Hispanic, higher level education, and a higher Gini wealth inequality index has a negative effect on radius of gyration. Models including mobility as an influencing factor of depression show that an increased radius of gyration has a significant effect on higher depression rates, while a higher Gini wealth inequality index also decreases depression rates. All variables were spatially significant, and areas that share similar sociodemographic, mobility, and depression patterns cluster in the city center as well as the outlying areas of Chicago. These findings can help illustrate which neighborhood characteristics should be closely examined when determining patterns of depression, as well as the identifying sociodemographic variables that can be monitored to help support areas with higher depression rates than others.
Author: Thomas, Kaylah
abstract_manipulated: The manifold urban vicinages harbor a panoply of heterogeneities, proffering elucidation of societal configurations and psychological well-being denouements. A minutely dissected scrutiny of neighborhood sociodemographic facets and the trajectories of movement engendered by denizens therein can unveil ramifications on melancholia prevalence. The intimate nexus betwixt sociodemographic parameters and locale environmental attributes, notably mobility, may act as barometers expounding why certain urban zones are disproportionately afflicted. By recourse to the American Community Survey, alongside amassed geolocational data and depressive instances from denizens of Chicago and its outskirts on Twitter, regression and spatial autocorrelation models were concocted to delineate sociodemographic impacts on mobility and depression incidence within urban precincts. Scrutiny discloses that the quantum of Caucasian populace exerts a positive sway on the gyrational expanse within a given domain, whereas the Hispanic populace proportion, advanced scholastic attainment, and a loftier Gini coefficient denoting wealth disparity evince a deleterious impact on gyrational expanse. Formulations encompassing mobility as a determinant of depression evince that an augmented gyrational expanse exerts a momentous influence on escalated depression tallies, whilst a heightened Gini wealth disparity index concurrently diminishes depression frequencies. All parameters exhibited spatial import, with precincts evincing akin sociodemographic, mobility, and depression trends coalescing in both the city nucleus and its peripheries. These revelations may adumbrate which neighborhood traits warrant meticulous scrutiny in unraveling depression manifestations, alongside identifying sociodemographic parameters warranting vigilance to bolster precincts beset by higher depression burdens than their counterparts.

title: Understanding the Online Gig Worker Community
abstract: This study describes the evolution of the online gig worker community, and the changing content during the pandemic. To empirically capture the effect of the health crisis, I crawled Reddit posts in gig work-related subreddits from January 2019 to April 2022, a period that covered the pre-COVID era and peaks amid the surge of variants. The core research design rests on the classification of gig workers, which is based on the service type and leads to the five user cohorts in this study: in-store grocery shoppers, logistic drivers, food delivery drivers, ridesharing drivers, and e-commerce sellers. Building on the classification, I propose a descriptive framework for understanding the evolution of the online gig work community, with correspondence between each user cohort’s activity and the COVID trend, and modeling of the most common topics before and after the pandemic starts. The findings suggest that the prosperity of online gig worker communities is roughly aligned with the COVID trend, with a different pattern of flux for the following series of COVID waves other than the emergence. While the pandemic has fostered the popularity of new gig work types, some previously popular gig work types involved in physical and location-dependent interactions have lost ground quickly until the promotion of the COVID vaccine. The study proceeds by investigating the changing topics in online discussion. While the impact of the pandemic is nuanced by specific gig work characteristics, similar concerns are echoed in each user cohort. Workers exhibit more prudence on previously underestimated costs of gig work, engage in more interactions with customers, and pour out more negative implications in online discussion.
Author: Fu, Boya
abstract_manipulated: This erudite interrogation delineates the metamorphosis of the cybernetic task laborer assemblage, and the transmogrifying contents amidst the pandemic epoch. To methodically apprehend the repercussions of the salubrity quandary, Reddit discourses in gig labor-interlinked subreddits were subjected to analytical decomposition from January 2019 to April 2022, a temporal span enveloping the antecedent to COVID epoch and zeniths amidst the surge of variants. The foundational research architecture is predicated on the taxonomy of gig laborers, predicated on the nature of service and culminating in the quintet of user cohorts in this scrutiny: in-situ commissariat procurators, logistical chauffeurs, victuals conveyors, chauffeurs in ride-sharing, and vendors in electronic commerce. Elucidating upon the taxonomy, a descriptive framework is posited to explicate the evolution of the cybernetic gig labor assemblage, with a parallelism betwixt each user cohort's endeavors and the COVID trajectory, and an embodiment of the most prevalent themes antecedent to and subsequent to the inception of the pandemic. The discernments proffer an indistinctly congruent ascension of cybernetic gig laborer assemblages vis-a-vis the COVID trajectory, with a divergent undulation pattern ensuing the succession of COVID undulations apart from the origin. Although the pandemic has nurtured the vogue of nascent gig labor genres, antecedent in-vogue gig labor varieties implicated in corporeal and locale-dependant interplays have been expeditiously supplanted until the advocacy of the COVID inoculation. The scrutiny proceeds by interrogating the evolving themes in cyber colloquy. While the repercussions of the pandemic are nuanced by distinct gig labor attributes, analogous apprehensions reverberate through each user cohort. Laborers evince greater circumspection towards hitherto underestimated expenditures of gig labor, partake in augmented interactions with clientele, and expound upon more adverse connotations in cyber dialogue.

title: Changing Semantics of Gendered Insults in Music Lyrics
abstract: Popular culture has been heavily criticized for normalizing gendered insults and perpetuating gender stereotypes. Lyrics, powerful platforms for social change and artistic expressions, are particular salient venues to study the changing meanings of gendered insults and the cultural shifts underneath. Using neural network-based word representations, this article dynamically captures different uses of a gender slur in more than 1.6 million lyric sentences over time among different gender groups. First of its kind in both scale and approach, this analysis tracks the evolutions of the referents and connotations of gendered insults to highlight the progress, struggles, and limitations of the feminist movement in the popular culture space.
Author: Huang, Jasmine
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within, the realm of popular culture has faced vociferous censure for its propensity to normalize gendered invectives and perpetuate entrenched gender archetypes. Lyrics, as vehicles of sociocultural metamorphosis and aesthetic articulations, epitomize singularly cogent arenas for interrogating epistemological shifts in the signification of gendered invectives and the subterranean currents of societal change. Employing a neurally networked lexicon, this epistemological interrogation operatively dissects variegated deployments of a gendered pejorative across a corpus exceeding 1.6 million lyrical phrases, delineating temporal trajectories amidst diverse gender cohorts. Pioneering in magnitude and methodology, this dialectical dissection traces the metamorphoses of the lexico-conceptual domains and nuances of gendered pejoratives, casting into stark relief the advancement, vicissitudes, and constraints of the feminist epoch within the sphere of popular culture.

title: Knowledge Production for a Healthy Planet: The Evolution of Environmental Social Science and Environmental Activism
abstract: The state of environmental crises demands increased efforts to shift society away from its current course, which is doing damage to the biosphere at an alarming rate. This research uses computational methods to explore the evolution of environmental activism and environmental social science from 2005 to 2021, as well as the uses of social science in environmental activism and vice versa. Relying on online environmental magazines and academic journal abstracts, the primary methodologies employed are keyword frequencies, LDA topic models, and skip-gram word embedding models. Results show an increasing focus in both communities on climate change, social justice, and community-based solutions. The potential for increasing collaboration between environmental activists and environmental social scientists is discussed.
Author: Packard, Michael
abstract_manipulated: The exigency of ecological cataclysms enjoins heightened exertions to divert the trajectory of civilization from its present vector, inflicting detriment upon the biota with a velocity that alarms. This inquiry avails itself of algorithmic procedures to interrogate epistemologically the metamorphosis of ecological militancy and environmental sociological inquiries spanning the temporal expanse from 2005 to 2021, alongside the applications of sociological scholarship in ecocentric activism and reciprocally. Drawing on digitized environmental gazettes and scholastic journal synopses, the principal techniques enlisted encompass lexical counts, Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) thematic frameworks, and skip-gram lexical embedding architectures. Findings illuminate the burgeoning preoccupation within both cohorts with climatic perturbation, egalitarianism, and grassroots remedies. The plausibility of amplifying interdisciplinarity between environmental advocates and environmental sociologists is contended.

title: A Comparison of Dimension Reduction Methods on Common Social Science Datasets
abstract: Dimension reduction methods can reduce the complexity of data space, extract meaningful information, and decrease the need for computational resources, being useful tools in research. This thesis focuses on comparing four different dimension reduction methods on common social science datasets and then demonstrating the performance of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) versus feature selection measures. By comparing the performance of PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, and autoencoder on Human Development Indicator (HDI) dataset with visualization and clustering, I showcased PCA tended to be more appropriate in this type of dataset. For the second part, I compared PCA and other feature selection measures (L1, L2, tree or random forest selection) on HDI, IMDB movie review, and image datasets, all of which are a representative of common social science datasets, and different measures performed variously based on the features of the datasets.
Author: Chung, MengChen
abstract_manipulated: Utilizing methodologies for dimensional reduction holds the potentiality to ameliorate the intricacy inherent within the spatial representation of empirical artifacts, disentangle salient informational substrates, and curtail the exigency for computational resources, thereby constituting instrumental implements within the realm of scholastic endeavor. The present dissertation directs its focus towards a comparative interrogation, scrutinizing four distinct dimensional reduction methodologies vis-à-vis prevalent social science datasets, subsequently proffering an exposition of the efficacy engendered by Principal Component Analysis (PCA) vis-à-vis delineated feature selection modalities. Through empirical evaluation of PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, and autoencoder upon the Human Development Indicator (HDI) repository, buttressed by visualization and clustering techniques, a predilection for PCA's pertinence within such milieu was ascertained. In the subsequent segment, juxtaposition between PCA and alternative feature selection modalities (L1, L2, tree, or random forest selection) was undertaken across HDI, IMDB cinematic critique, and pictorial repositories, emblematic of conventional social science datasets, manifesting diverse performances contingent upon idiosyncrasies inherent within each dataset's features.

title: Credit Constraint and Global Value Chain Participation
abstract: Credit constraints could impose important barriers against firm’s value added exports. Theoretically, my model which incorporates borrowing constraint into exporting firm’s profit maximization problem predicts credit constraints reduces firm export and export intensity. Using data on 66 economies from 1995-2018, I find evidence that the effect of credit constraint vary across industries that have differing levels of external financing and asset tangibility. For exporting countries with more developed financial systems, industries that rely more heavily on external financing experience larger boosts in exports. On the other hand, financially less developed countries export less in industries with fewer collateralizable assets. The effect is more ambiguous when examining export intensity. Overall, I provide evidence that credit constraint constitutes a significant barrier for global value chain participation.
Author: Xu, David
abstract_manipulated: By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from credit constraints, formidable impediments might be imposed against the augmentation of firms' value-added exports. Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within theoretical exegesis, the model integrating borrowing strictures into the profit maximization conundrum of exporting firms tentatively indicates a non-trivial probability that credit constraints engender a reduction in firm exports and export intensity. Through empirical ferreting within the venerated annals of data pertaining to 66 economies spanning the temporal expanse from 1995 to 2018, the evidentiary substrate unveils the variegated impact of credit strictures across industries characterized by disparate proclivities for external financing and asset tangibility. Within exporting realms endowed with more mature financial architectures, sectors reliant upon extrinsic funding manifest prodigious upsurges in exportation. In contradistinction, nations graced with nascent financial apparatus witness diminutive export volumes within sectors endowed with scant collateralizable assets. The conundrum becomes notably enigmatic upon scrutinizing export intensity. In sum, a mosaic of data serves to illuminate the extent to which credit constraints emerge as a formidable impediment impeding the participation of entities within the global tapestry of value creation.

title: U.S. Section H Patents: From Women to Human
abstract: This project identifies the gender gap exists in Section H – Electricity patents under the Cooperative Patent Classification. Using metadata analysis and text analysis with all the section H patents from 1976 to 2021, I found that the gender gap within Section H patents is still large and is closing up slowly. Few women get to invent, few women majority teams exist, and few patents are female-focused. On average women have higher productivity than men within section H patents creation. I also found that, during economic recessions, female job switchers are the most productive while non-top assignees’ female inventors suffer the most; during normal times, non-top female inventors have the best performance while top assignees’ female inventors rank the lowest.
Author: Liu, Yingxuan
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this inquiry, the present endeavor was subjected to analytical decomposition to elucidate the gender chasm pervading Section H – Electrodynamic Invention Letters within the Collaborative Patent Categorization framework. By interrogating epistemologically the empirical artifacts and the evidentiary substrate encapsulating all Section H letters from 1976 to 2021, it provisionally indicates a non-trivial probability that the gender chasm manifesting in Section H epistolary creations remains notably substantial and exhibits a gradual convergence. Scant are the distaff contributors to innovation, sparse are female-dominated collectives, and meager are the innovations tailored towards the feminine domain. On average, females exhibit greater industriousness in the birthing of Section H patents compared to their male counterparts. Furthermore, it serves to illuminate the extent to which, amidst fiscal downturns, female labor market changers emerge as the paragons of productivity, while female innovators affiliated with lesser-known assignees endure the brunt; in periods of stability, non-prominent female innovators demonstrate superlative performance whilst female innovators linked with top-tier assignees languish at the nadir of achievement.

title: Epidemiology of Fall-Related Hospitalizations in Older Adults  (July 2009 – July 2015):  Exploring the national and regional associations with weather, demographic, and socioeconomic variables using machine learning and classic statistical approaches
abstract: Falls are a leading cause of injury and death among older adults. There are few studies that use national longitudinal data and multiple weather metrics to explore the complex relationships of environmental risk factors. This thesis compares machine learning (random forests) and classic statistical (mixed-effects Poisson regressions) methods to examine the influence and spatial variation of weather, demographic, and socioeconomic characteristics on county fall-related hospitalization rates among adults 65 years and older in the United States. Three county-level data sources were combined: 1) monthly Medicare hospitalization claims from July 2009 – July 2015 with either primary diagnosis or external cause codes indicating an accidental fall (ICD-9-CM codes E880-E888, excluding E887) 2) weather variables scraped from NOAA’s National Weather Service, and 3) sociodemographic characteristics from the 2010-2014 American Community Survey. National and regional modelling was conducted using both methods and each set of characteristics. Weather, demographic, and socioeconomic variables are all important predictors of fall-related hospitalization rates in older adults. Temperatures were the most influential weather variables at the national level and for all regions except the Northeast. The random forests identified that high fall-related hospitalization rates were associated with temperature variability. The mixed-effects Poisson models demonstrated a U-shaped relationship where both high maximum and low minimum temperatures are associated with greater risk at the national level and in the South and Midwest, controlling for relevant sociodemographics. The two modelling approaches are compared, and the mixed-effects Poisson regressions are consistently more accurate likely due to their adjustment for spatial variation and confounding.
Author: Benson, Jade
abstract_manipulated: Manifesting as a preeminent antecedent of harm and mortality amidst the elderly demographic, incidents of descent command scholarly scrutiny. With a dearth of inquiries leveraging national longitudinal archives and diverse meteorological parameters to interrogate epistemologically the convoluted interplays of environmental peril factors, this expositional treatise embarks on a formidable odyssey. Contrasting the machinations of machine learning (arboreal randomness) and archetypal statistical (embrace of mixed-effects Poisson regressions) modalities, a holistic inquiry unfolds to scrutinize the sway and spatial heterogeneity of weather, demographic, and socioeconomic traits upon the rates of hospitalization stemming from falls within counties, impacting denizens aged 65 years and beyond across the expanse of the American terrain. A triumvirate of county-tier gleanings was subjected to analytical decomposition: 1) periodical Medicare hospitalization assertions spanning July 2009 – July 2015, delineating primary diagnostic depictions or extrinsic causative codifications signaling inadvertent descents (ICD-9-CM codes E880-E888, with E887 being precluded); 2) meteorologic facets gleaned from NOAA’s bastion of the National Weather Service; and 3) civic-demographic delineations extracted from the 2010-2014 tapestries of the American Community Survey. Both methods and every corpus of traits catalyzed national and zonal simulations. Weather, demographic, and socioeconomic indices emerge as salient auguries of fall-induced hospitalization rates among seniors. Across the nation and save for the Northeastern enclave, mercury fluctuations reign supreme amidst meteorological influencers. Arboreal randomness underscores an alliance between elevated fall-induced hospitalization rates and thermic oscillations. Mixed-effects Poisson paradigms unveil a parabolic liaison, wherein zenithal and nadiral thermic thresholds resonate with escalated peril at the national echelon and within the compass of the South and Midwest, ameliorated by the concomitant adjustment for germane sociodemographics. An incisive juxtaposition of the dual models ensues, spotlighting the sustained supremacy of mixed-effects Poisson regressions, ostensibly by virtue of their attunement to spatial disparities and confounding vicissitudes.

title: Visually Grounded Multilingual Syntax Acquisition
abstract: Children are observed acquiring multiple languages simultaneously from birth without explicit instruction, and the course of development in each language looks very much like the trajectory followed by monolingual children. We try to simulate humans' syntax acquisition of simultaneous multilingualism via a modified Visually Grounded Neural Syntax Learner, an unsupervised neural network model for learning syntax representation. We incorporate a language classifier for the multilingual text corpus and introduce the predicted language label for each sentence to the training process of the VGNSL model. We train multilingual models using Flickr8k data with text in (English, French) or (English, tokenized Chinese). We find that compared to the monolingual model, the multilingual dataset makes the model less accurate for syntax parsing in each language, and more significantly in datasets with high word overlap, such as (English, French) language pairs. The improved version of the model with the introduction of language classifiers can improve the model's accuracy for syntax parsing in each language. However, it still falls short of the accuracy of the monolingual model. Thus it cannot fully simulate the learning performance of simultaneous multilingual children.
Author: Xing, Zhou
abstract_manipulated: In the ontogenesis of linguistically dexterous progeny, an intriguing phenomenon emerges wherein juveniles seamlessly assimilate manifold lexicons concomitantly sans didactic solicitation. This developmental odyssey, albeit ostensibly perplexing, mirrors the trajectory traversed by unilingual offspring. Our endeavor entails the emulation of syntactic assimilation in humans amidst the crucible of concurrent multilingualism through a tailored Visually Grounded Neural Syntax Learner, a neural schema unfettered by tutelary strictures, devoted to the cultivation of syntax embodiments. By enfolding a linguistic discriminant within the omnifarious textual compendium and integrating the prognosticated linguistic emblem for each enunciation into the didactic regimen of the VGNSL paradigm, we fashion multilingual architectures leveraging the Flickr8k corpus replete with verbiage in (Anglo-Saxon, Gallic) or (Anglo-Saxon, segmented Sinograph). Our analysis unveils that vis-a-vis the unilingual paradigm, the polymorphic dataset engenders a model of diminished precision in syntactic disambiguation within each linguistic realm, especially so in realms replete with lexical confluence, like (Anglo-Saxon, Gallic) lingual dyads. Albeit the ameliorated iteration of the schema, augmented by the advent of linguistic classifiers, augments the precision of syntax decipherment within respective linguistic domains, it regrettably remains a step behind the precision exhibited by the unilingual model. Thus, it falters in its quest to fully replicate the erudition acumen of concurrently multilingual juvenile cohorts.

title: False Positive Brought by Fat-tailed Weights? Re-examine the Impact of China’s Retaliatory Tariff to US House Election
abstract: This paper scrutinizes the possible violation of some WLS presumptions in empirical projects. In general, for the WLS estimator to be consistent, asymptotically normal and Eicker-Huber-White’s heteroskedastic standard error to converge to a non-random limit, one needs to make sure that the first and second moment of the interaction of weights, regressors, and noise are finite. This paper aims to raise the concern of the contamination of fat-tailed weights in a series of papers that adopts American county or American CZ as its observations and weights the observation by its population. This would compromise the consistency and asymptotic property of the estimator. I used a Monte Carlo experiment to see the potential effects of fat-tailed weights on regressions and proved that under fat-tailed weights, the Eicker-Huber-White’s variance- covariance matrix estimator generally understates the real variance-covariance matrix, and is inefficient. I then take Blanchard et al. (2019) as an example and replicated their work but rezoned their regression observations. In my replication, I found no evidence for the claim that the retaliatory trade shock from China shifts American voters to the Republican Party and this could very likely be a consequence of the fat-tailed weights.
Author: Sun, Ye
abstract_manipulated: This treatise engages in the interrogative epistemological dissection of potential transgressions against certain Weighted Least Squares (WLS) axioms within empirical artifact undertakings. In a broad sense, for the WLS estimator to exhibit convergence, asymptotic normality, and the Eicker-Huber-White's heteroskedastic standard error to approach a deterministic threshold, it becomes imperative to ascertain that the first and second statistical moments pertaining to the convolution of weights, regressors, and perturbations transpire within finite bounds. The primary objective of this treatise is to kindle apprehension regarding the infiltration of fat-tailed weights across a series of treatises that select American county or American CZ as their sampled observations and proceed to weight said observations in accordance with their demographic mass. Such an approach threatens the constancy and asymptotic characteristics of the estimator. By virtue of a Monte Carlo experiment subjected to analytical decomposition, I sought to delineate the potential ramifications engendered by fat-tailed weights on regression analyses and established that in the presence of such weights, the Eicker-Huber-White's variance-covariance matrix estimator not only systematically underreports the genuine variance-covariance matrix but also succumbs to inefficiency. Subsequently, I endeavored to replicate the study conducted by Blanchard et al. (2019) albeit opting to rezone their regression observations. Through my replication endeavor, I encountered no substantiation for the assertion posited by the authors that the retaliatory trade perturbation emanating from China serves to sway American electorate allegiance towards the Republican Party; this outcome, in all likelihood, stems from the influence exerted by the fat-tailed weights.

title: How do attorney assignment mechanisms affect juvenile indigent defense?
abstract: The government ensures access to legal representation for all defendants by assigning attorneys to those who cannot afford private counsel. Government attorneys are assigned either by the judge or in a pseudo-random manner through a rotation system. However, there are concerns over the mechanism by which attorneys are assigned as some mechanisms may impact case outcomes. Using the juvenile criminal records of 37 counties in America in 1998, this study examines how the assignment mechanism affects indigent defendants’ chances of being convicted. In terms of a reduced risk of conviction, I find that quasi-random assignment results in better outcomes for defendants. This paper elucidates that in counties where the judge assigns attorneys, the assigned counsels are more likely to persuade their defendants to plead guilty, which contributes to the disparities in case outcomes.
Author: Guo, Haihao
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the labyrinthine corridors of legal justice administration, the governmental apparatus endeavors to ensure the facilitation of legal advocacy for all defendants through the allocation of legal procurators to those devoid of the means to secure private legal consultation. The dispensation of government-appointed attorneys transpires either at the behest of the adjudicator or by the quasi-stochastic mechanisms inherent within a rotational vortex. Nonetheless, apprehensions loom regarding the spectral specter of the assignment machinations, for certain modalities wield the potentiality to exert a perceptible influence on the vicissitudes of legal resolutions.

By virtue of the cascading ramifications emanating from the archival relics of juvenile criminal transgressions across 37 American demesnes in the annus mirabilis of 1998, this interrogation epistemologically traverses the terrain of how the allocation schema interfaces with the destinies of indigent defendants ensnared within the judicial web. In the context of a mitigated jeopardy of condemnation, it provisionally indicates a non-trivial probability that the quasi-stochastic allocation modality engenders more favorable adjudicatory denouements for the defendants ensnared within the legal quagmire.

This treatise serves to illuminate the extent to which, in jurisdictions where the arbiter allots legal emissaries, the apportioned legal advocates exhibit an increased propensity to cajole their defendants into embracing a plea of culpability, thereby catalyzing the fissures within the tapestry of judicial outcomes.

title: The Role of Channel Characteristics on Brand-Related User Generated Content
abstract: Using sentiment analysis, k-means clustering, and latent Dirichlet allocation (LDA), we collected and analyzed more than 50,000 user-generated content (UGC) related to five athletic apparel brands, to examine the content characteristics in brand-related UGC from three types of social media: Twitter (self-media), Reddit (collaboration platform), and Instagram and YouTube (creative outlets). We find that UGC on creative outlets contains more positive brand sentiments and emotional trigger words. UGC on self-media and collaboration platforms are brand-centered, while the most popular topics on self-media are brand news/trends, and it is a product-related question on collaboration platforms. UGC on creative outlets are self-promoting, while creators’ motivations and achievements are the most popular on Instagram, and activity-related content and informative reviews are the most popular on YouTube. Besides, the formation of brand communities positively influences consumer engagement on social media. This research extended from the previous framework for comparing brand-related UGC with big data and machine learning techniques. It provides insights into how channel characteristics result in differences in brand-related UGC, which are valuable to future researchers and brand managers.
Author: Gao Mingtao
abstract_manipulated: By virtue of the revelatory implications inherent within, through the employment of sentiment dissection, k-means agglomeration, and latent Dirichlet allocation (LDA), a compendium exceeding 50,000 user-forged discourses pertaining to five vestiary paradigms hath been subjected to analytical decomposition. The scrutinization thereof hath been conducted to interrogate epistemologically the substance traits within brand-associated user-generated narratives across a trichotomy of cyber milieus: Twitter (self-advocacy platform), Reddit (cooperative forum), and Instagram as well as YouTube (innovative fora). It provisionally indicates a non-trivial probability that the UGC within inventive realms doth harbor a surplus of felicitous brand inclinations and emotive incitement diction. Whereas, self-advocacy domains and collaborative hubs exhibit a brand-centric ethos; the prevailing themes on self-advocacy realms pertain to brand-related tidings and trends, whilst interlocutions on the collaborative platforms revolve around product-focused queries. Self-promotion permeates UGC across creative conduits, with the preeminence of creators' incentives and feats resonating paramountly on Instagram, whereas activity-oriented subject matter and enlightening critiques command the dominion on YouTube. Furthermore, the symbiotic co-evolution of brand enclaves augments patron involvement across the digital scape. This empirical expedition extends beyond antecedent scaffolds by juxtaposing brand-linked UGC through the prism of voluminous data and machinic erudition stratagems. It serves to illuminate the extent to which the distinctiveness of mediums engenders disparities in brand-suffused UGC, thereby furnishing invaluable insights for forthcoming scholars and brand stewards.

title: Falling Out of Love: Non-Economic Shocks and Their Effect on Partnership Dissolution
abstract: Whether non-economic factors such as marital happiness and well-being influence the proba- bility of divorce? What if we also consider other factors such as the length of marriage and separation in the long term? I quantitatively measure the economic and non-economic factors that might influence divorce using British Household Panel Study. According to the results of my baseline model, only match qualities matter in predicting the probability of separation. My results also provide some key insights for extensions of this baseline model. The economic and non-economic factors are best at predicting separation that happens within a 3-years time range. And wives’ match qualities have similar effects with the husbands’ when looking at sep- aration hazards in the long run. After controlling for marriage duration, negative match quality received by the husbands will significantly increase divorce hazards, while the reverse is true for wives. Contrary to previous findings, none of the economic factors are significant in my models.
Author: Chen, Chi
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the question of whether non-corporeal constituents such as connubial felicity and eudaimonia exude an ontological sway on the likelihood of matrimonial dissolution, one is compelled to interrogate epistemologically the volumetric interplay of temporal dimensions such as the continuity of conjugal union and hiatus over protracted epochs. Through the application of rigorous analytical decomposition, I scrutinize the economic and non-economic determinants that may wield influence upon the dissolution of spousal bonds utilizing the British Household Panel Study as the evidentiary substrate. The results gleaned from the foundational model proffer that solely the congruence of affinal attributes holds pertinence in prognosticating the probability of dyadic disassociation. Furthermore, my findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the amalgamation of economic and non-economic variables exhibits optimal efficacy in forecasting separations manifesting within a triennium temporal ambit. Noteworthily, the akin effects of marital attributes on the peril of disunion emerge when scrutinizing longitudinal separation perils, with adverse affinal attributes borne by husbands demonstrating a statistically robust predictability in exacerbating divorcement hazards post-adjustment for the temporal extent of matrimony, a phenomenon not mirrored in the female cohort. In contradistinction to erstwhile scholarship, none of the economic variables evince statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions in my formulated constructs.

title: The effects of industrial land market reform on the local governments’ land supply decision in China
abstract: This study examines the effect of industrial land market reform on the local governments' land supply decision in China. Since 2007, the central government has required that each land parcel for sale must be sold to a proposed industry through public auction. The unit transaction price of these sales (yuan per square meter) must be higher than the county-level minimum price, unless permitted by the central government. Therefore, the local governments are no longer able to sell industrial land parcels at low prices to attract firms and are forced to consider the comparative advantages of their land for particular industries. In order to analyze the local governments' land supply decision after the reform, I develop a theoretical model in which I treat the local governments as monopolist land suppliers subject to the central government's auction and minimum price restrictions. The model predicts that the local governments are responsive to the comparative advantages. In addition, when the relationship between the transaction price and the minimum price changes, the local governments can be more or less responsive to their comparative advantages, depending on the revenue from land sale, the positive externalities from the firms, and the reference of the central government. I employ detailed parcel-, county-, and industry- level data to test the model predictions. My empirical findings suggest that a one percentage point increase in the employment share of an industry leads to a 1.3% - 1.6% increase in the land supplied to that industry ceteris paribus. Moreover, the local governments become more responsive to the comparative advantages when the transaction price is lower than the minimum price. These findings verify the implications of the theoretical model. Furthermore, the effects are heterogeneous among counties; the local governments of more developed counties are more responsive to the comparative advantages.
Author: Yang, Minghao
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the interstices of this interrogative foray, the present scrutiny subjects the ramifications of industrial land market reform upon the land supply determinations of local governments in the realm of China to analytical decomposition. Commencing from the annum of 2007, the edicts promulgated by the paramount authority necessitated that each parcel of land offered for exchange be vended to a proposed industrial entity through the vehicle of public auction. The fiscal metric denoting the value of these exchanges (expressed in yuan per square meter) was mandated to surpass the minima prescribed at the county level unless expressly sanctioned by the central governmental body. Henceforth, the erstwhile stratagem of vending industrial land parcels at nominal rates to allure enterprises was obviated, compelling a contemplation of the intrinsic merits vis-à-vis specific industries. In crafting a theoretical schema to scrutinize the post-reform land supply determinations of local governments, the present researcher envisions an ontology wherein the local administrative bodies are cast as monopolistic purveyors of land, circumscribed by the exigencies of the central government's auction regulations and price floors. The theoretical effigy prognosticates a responsiveness of local authorities to said comparative merits. Furthermore, contingent upon alterations in the nexus between transactional prices and minimum thresholds, the local governments exhibit varying degrees of receptivity to their comparative advantages contingent upon the pecuniary yield from land transactions, the accruing positive externalities emanating from enterprises, and the standards set forth by the central authority. By leveraging a corpus of granular data at the parcel, county, and industry strata, the empirical analysis seeks to validate the prognostications engendered by the theoretical edifice. The findings tentatively indicate a non-trivial probability that a one percentage point augmentation in the employment quotient of a given industry instigates a 1.3% - 1.6% amplification in the land allocated to said sector, all else being equal. Furthermore, the propinquity of local authorities to the comparative advantages burgeons when transactional prices dip below the stipulated thresholds. These empirical revelations affirm the postulates deduced from the theoretical construct. Additionally, the ramifications exhibit heterogeneity across counties; the local administrations of more developed territories evince a heightened degree of sensitivity to the comparative merits.

title: Why Do Women Get Less in Job Market?  --Evidence on the Gender Pay Gap from Twitter Data
abstract: The gender pay gap is one of the serious issues in the labor market today and closing the gap is the major theme in the Equal Pay movement. This gap cannot be simply explained by any common labor market characteristics that form the basis of wage determination. In this thesis, I intend to understand the connection between wage gap and factors including workplace discrimination, education, occupational segregation, and, to uncover the challenges of the Equal Pay movement by identifying the main issues arise around the movement. Specifically, I performed a Twitter-based user generated content analysis (UGC) to extract twitter data with the hashtag #GenderPay and #EqualPay to further understand the issues around the equal pay movement. In the UGC analysis, I built a Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) model to identify top 20 topics in the dataset. I classified the tweets into three categories - positive, negative, and neutral - and plotted two word-clouds. My findings underscore the following two topics: (i) motherhood penalty (ii) racial inequality. The identified topics can be a starting point for future research on pay equity in the work environment as well as provide insights to close the gender inequality problem around the world.
Author: He, Jielu
abstract_manipulated: The gender remuneration chasm stands as a grave quandary amid the economic sphere presently, and the pursuit of narrowing said chasm looms as the principal motif within the Equal Remuneration mobilization. This disparity eludes facile explication through standard labor market attributes that constitute the underpinnings of wage adjudication. Within this treatise, the aim is to unravel the nexus between remuneration disparities and variables encompassing workplace bias, scholastic attainment, professional segregation, and to unearth the hurdles impeding the Equal Remuneration crusade by delineating the salient quandaries that enshroud the crusade. Specifically, a computational deconstruction was imposed upon a corpus of user-generated testimonies proffered on the microblogging platform Twitter (relying on the hashtags #GenderPay and #EqualPay) to glean deeper insights into the conundrums encircling the egalitarian remuneration initiative. The deconstruction engendered the instantiation of a generative probabilistic model known as Latent Dirichlet Assortment (LDA) to ferret out the principal twenty themes latent within the observational set. These tweets were classified into three distinct cohorts – affirmatory, derogatory, and indifferent – and engendered dual lexical-cloud visualizations. The upshots of this inquiry accentuate the ensuing focal points: (i) maternity scapegoating, and (ii) ethnical imparity. These delineated focal points may germinate as a fertile origination locus for prospective inquiries concerning remuneration parity within the workspace milieu, concurrently furnishing epiphanies to alleviate the gender incongruity impasse pervading the global spectrum.

title: Digital Fandom in Metrics:  How Does Capitalized Emotion in  Algorithmic Culture Raise Idol Trainees Up?
abstract: This paper looked at digital fandom under an algorithmic culture. We investigated the fan structure and their affective labor based on an idol selection contest that offers promising opportunities with our special focus on its underlying metric-competing mechanism. We also used computational simulation to verify if fans’ effort on improving visible rankings on social media secures a position for their idols. By addressing these issues, we analyzed why fans are willing to trade their labor and how the capitalism logic empowers and exploits the fan community for their emotion and labor.
Author: Yijia Lyu
abstract_manipulated: This treatise undertook a scrutinization of cybernetic adulation beneath the auspices of an algorithmic ethos. We interrogated the devotee configuration and their affective toil predicated upon an idol adjudication melee proffering auspicious avenues with our particular emphasis on its substratal metrical-competitive apparatus. Furthermore, we availed ourselves of computational emulation to validate whether adherents' exertions in enhancing conspicuous standings on virtual platforms secure a pedestal for their graven images. By appraising these quandaries, we subjected to analytical decomposition the rationale behind devotees' willingness to barter their toil and how the capitalist rationale empowers and exploits the aficionado consortium for their affect and toil.

title: The Relationship between Economic Freedom and Income Equality
abstract: This paper empirically examines the relationship between economic freedom and income inequality by using a machine-learning constructed economic freedom indicator that addresses three major methodological problems in the currently popular economic freedom indices. This paper selects the optimal Support Vector Regression (SVR) model with Radial Basis Function (RBF) kernel to construct the new economic freedom indicator, after comparing four different kernel types. The empirical results show that there exists a robust invert-U shaped relationship between economic freedom and income inequality.
Author: Zhou, Anqi
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this scholarly endeavor, the present treatise engages in an interrogation epistemologically of the nexus residing between pecuniary autonomy and pecuniary disparity through the utilization of an artificially intelligent-fashioned fiscal emancipation metric that undertakes to combat three foundational methodological dilemmas embedded within the presently vogue fiscal liberty gauges. Opting for the most felicitous Support Vector Regression (SVR) configuration employing the Radial Basis Function (RBF) nucleus to instantiate the nascent fiscal autonomy metric subsequent to a comparative evaluation of four disparate nucleus genres; the empirical artifacts indicate the presence of a sturdy inverse-U molded interconnection between fiscal autonomy and pecuniary disparity.

title: The Twitter Network of Think Tank Experts in D.C.
abstract: The influence of a political idea rarely relies only on a few scholars and politicians. Instead, it emerges, evolves, and transforms into feasible policies when policy experts of various backgrounds and reputations cooperate and communicate through organizations and their personal networks. Previous research on the roles of policy experts in the policy making process, however, focus primarily on celebrities whose personal connections with other political actors are more publicly visible and politically influential. Two narratives are thus missing: First, what are the roles of the majority of lesser-known experts? Second, how much are experts’ cooperation and communication shaped by organizations such as think tanks? To provide a more complete picture, I construct a Twitter follow graph which covers 609 experts with valid Twitter accounts, which is about 70% of all listed experts on the websites of the Brookings Institution, the American Enterprise Institute, the Center for American Progress, and the Heritage Foundation. Then, I analyze the structure of this Twitter follow graph and explore the implication and limits of Twitter-based connections. With a DeepWalk Algorithm, I argue that the Twitter connections among experts are highly reciprocal and organizationally based. Finally, with edge-based regressions, I show that the organizational based connection between experts is significant even when the similarity of experts’ policy interest is controlled. That is, social media usage seems ineffective in exposing experts to opinions from those beyond the reach defined by their offline constraints. In all, I construct a framework to study the expert network through Twitter data and provide a complementary narrative to the existing qualitative discussions on how opinions and information may circulate among policy expert networks.
Author: Liang, Chen
abstract_manipulated: The effluence of a politic cogitation sporadically relies singularly on a scant cohort of erudite sages and statespersons. Instead, it burgeons, metamorphosizes, and transmutes into tenable stratagems when policy savants of sundry erudite etymologies and pedigrees consort and discourse via institutions and their personal nexuses. Prior interrogations into the functions of policy savants within the policy formulation crucible, nevertheless, primarily focalize on luminaries whose interlinkages with alternative politicos are more overtly manifest and politically efficacious. Ergo, two narratives remain latent: Firstly, what roles elucidate the majority of less-recognized experts? Secondly, to what extent do the cooperation and dialogue of experts find configuration under the aegis of institutions such as cogitative tanks? To furnish a more comprehensive tableau, a Twitter ensue graph enshrining 609 experts with valid Twitter accounts, representing nearly 70% of all catalogued experts on the online portals of the Brookings Institution, the American Enterprise Institute, the Center for American Progress, and the Heritage Foundation, is erected. Subsequently, an examination of the architecture of this Twitter ensue graph is undertaken to plumb the connotations and confines of Twitter-anchored affiliations. Employing a DeepWalk Algorithm, it is posited that the Twitter affiliations amongst experts evince pronounced reciprocity and organizational predication. Ultimately, through edge-centric regressions, it is evinced that the structurally rooted camaraderie amid experts subsists monumentally even when the resemblance of experts’ policy predilections is regulated. Namely, the utility of social media appears ineffectual in exposing experts to perspectives outside the perimeter circumscribed by their corporeal shackles. In summation, a scaffold is erected to scrutinize the expert lattice via Twitter intelligence and proffer a corollary explication to the extant qualitative colloquies on how sentiments and tidings might percolate amidst policy expert assemblages.

title: Promoting nationalism or becoming apolitical? Understanding China’s response to the crisis on Weibo
abstract: Online social media has become one of the most important ways for authoritarian regimes to maintain their resilience. When authoritarian regimes are faced with challenges, what options would they take to defend their regime? This paper takes Hong Kong protests and early-stage COVID-19 as two examples to study how the Chinese official Weibo accounts responded to these crises. This paper leverages text analysis to mine, explore, and learn from Weibo posts related to these crises. This paper seeks to answer how do these Weibo accounts change during and after the crises, including their word frequency, topic, keywords, and sentiment scores.
Author: Li, Yimin
abstract_manipulated: The cyber social nexus has emerged as a paramount conduit for autocratic dominions to fortify their fortitude. Confronted with adversities, how might authoritarian structures elect to fortify their dominion? This treatise scrutinizes the Hong Kong insurrections and nascent COVID-19 outbreak as paradigmatic instances to interrogate epistemologically the countermeasures adopted by the Chinese official Weibo conduits in response to these cataclysms. Leveraging computational textual dissection for analytical decomposition, this discourse delves into the decipherment, probing, and gleanings extracted from the Weibo missives entwined with these exigencies. Therein lies the quest to unravel the metamorphoses undergone by these Weibo enclaves amidst and subsequent to the upheavals, encompassing alterations in lexical incidence, thematic currents, pivotal terminologies, and affective valuations.

title: SHAP-Guided Adversarial Training for Robust Interpretability in Asset Pricing
abstract: During the post-COVID volatility regime, many traditional and machine-learning asset pricing models failed to provide stable explanations of return drivers. Shifts in factor attributions undermined confidence in reported risk exposures and led to costly portfolio adjustments, such as the misclassification of quality factors during the 2020 market shock. While predictive accuracy is often the primary benchmark, our objective is to improve the stability of model explanations over time so they remain both economically relevant and reliable for decision-making. To our knowledge, this is the first framework to embed attribution metrics directly into adversarial training for non-differentiable financial models. We develop a SHAP-guided adversarial training approach that constructs perturbations along SHAP-derived directions, simulating adversarial stress without requiring gradient access. This design directly regularizes attribution-relevant input sensitivity during training. Perturbation strength is calibrated through an epsilon-sweep, revealing a non-monotonic trade-off between interpretability and performance: while epsilon = 0.05 maximizes attributional stability, a more moderate epsilon = 0.005 balances robustness with economic signal retention. We evaluate the framework using monthly U.S. stock data from CRSP spanning 1990–2024, partitioned into training (1990–2010), validation (2011–2019), and test (2020–2024) periods. Model performance is assessed along three dimensions: predictive accuracy (MSE, IC), economic relevance (Q10–Q1 portfolio spreads), and attributional robustness (SHAP variance, rank correlation, and directional mean drift). A naive random-sign adversarial baseline is included for comparison. Empirically, SHAP-guided models deliver substantial gains in attributional coherence: SHAP variance falls by over 90%, and feature rank correlation across regimes improves from 0.20 to 0.90. These improvements transform highly unstable explanations into consistent, regime-robust factor profiles. The explanatory focus shifts from fragile, regime-dependent signals like momentum toward persistent, risk-consistent features such as volatility and max down, aligning with investor behavior during crises. These results demonstrate that attributional stability can be explicitly optimized without sacrificing predictive performance, and that it carries clear economic value. In volatile markets, maintaining consistent explanations is as critical as predictive fit for ensuring reliable portfolio allocation, credible economic interpretation, and effective model governance. By design, the SHAP-guided adversarial training framework is model-agnostic and readily transferable to other asset classes and predictive tasks where interpretability is essential, enabling deployment in real-world investment and risk management workflows without imposing explicit economic priors.
Author: Zhang, Sitong
abstract_manipulated: In the tumultuous wake of the post-pandemic flux, myriad conventional and algorithmic asset valuation paradigms floundered in their endeavors to offer cogent explications of yield catalysts. Vicissitudes in factor assignments eroded trust in documented hazard exposures and engendered onerous portfolio recalibrations, exemplified by the misclassification of evaluative facets amidst the 2020 financial upheaval. While prognostic precision often reigns as the paramount yardstick, our aim is to enhance the constancy of model elucidations across temporal horizons, ensuring their enduring economic pertinence and trustworthiness for judicious decision-making. To our cognizance, this marks the inaugural schema to integrate attribution gauges seamlessly into adversarial training for non-differentiable monetary frameworks. We formulate a SHAP-guided adversarial training stratagem that fabricates disruptions along SHAP-sourced vectors, emulating adversarial duress sans necessitating gradient entry. This configuration directly standardizes attribution-relevant input responsiveness throughout the training progression.
