Disruption intensity is gauged through an epsilon-sweep, unmasking a non-linear trade-off between interpretability and efficacy: whilst epsilon = 0.05 optimizes attributional steadiness, a milder epsilon = 0.005 balances resilience with retention of economic cues. We scrutinize the schema utilizing monthly American equity data from CRSP spanning 1990–2024, segmented into training (1990–2010), validation (2011–2019), and test (2020–2024) epochs. Model efficacy is assessed along three axes: prognostic accuracy (MSE, IC), economic significance (Q10–Q1 portfolio spreads), and attributional durability (SHAP variance, rank correlation, and directional mean drift). A nave random-sign adversarial benchmark is incorporated for juxtaposition. In practice, SHAP-guided models yield substantial strides in attributional congruity: SHAP variance plummets by over 90%, and feature rank correlation across epochs escalates from 0.20 to 0.90. These enhancements transmute exceedingly precarious elucidations into steadfast, epoch-resistant factor configurations. The explicatory focal point shifts from frangible, era-dependent indicators like momentum towards perennial, jeopardy-consistent facets such as volatility and maximum downturns, mirroring investor conduct amidst crises. These outcomes evince that attributional steadiness can be deliberately optimized sans jeopardizing prognostic efficacy and that it embodies conspicuous economic worth. In erratic markets, preserving uniform elucidations is as pivotal as prognostic compatibility in assuring credible portfolio apportionment, plausible economic interpretation, and efficacious model oversight. By design, the SHAP-guided adversarial training schema is model-agnostic and readily transposable to alternative asset categories and prognostic chores where interpretability is imperative, enabling implementation in real-world investment and jeopardy management workflows sans imposing explicit economic priors.

title: Values in Crisis: How the Great Recession Reshaped the government’s personality Evidence from a Difference-in-Differences Framework Applied to Political Speech Data
abstract: The 2008 Global Financial Crisis significantly disrupted economies across the world, presenting a unique opportunity to examine how economic shocks influence the cultural values expressed by governments. This thesis investigates whether and how the crisis reshaped governmental value expression by analyzing political speeches from four European countries—Spain and Italy (treatment group) and Germany and the Netherlands (control group)—from 2006 to 2010. Leveraging Schwartz’s theory of basic human values and a novel OpenAI GPT-4o-based pipeline for computational value extraction, I quantify the relative emphasis placed on ten value dimensions within each speech. Employing a Difference-in-Differences (DiD) framework, the study isolates the causal impact of the crisis on governmental value orientation. Findings reveal that, contrary to theories predicting authoritarian or dominance-oriented reactions, crisis-affected governments significantly increased their emphasis on benevolence-oriented values. These results are robust across multiple specifications, placebo tests, and sensitivity analyses, including data sampling adjustments. This study contributes methodologically to value quantification using large language models, and theoretically to our understanding of how elite political discourse adapts toeconomic upheaval by signaling solidarity and moral responsiveness.
Author: Zhang, Hongkun
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the 2008 Global Fiscal Cataclysm, which subjected economies worldwide to perturbation of logarithmically egregious proportions, a sui generis aperture materialized for interrogating epistemologically how fiscal convulsions impact the sociopolitical ethos enunciated by governance entities. This treatise scrutinizes the potential metamorphosis in governmental ethos engendered by the cataclysm through an analytical decomposition of oratories constituting the empirical artifacts emanating from the epoch between 2006 and 2010 in four European polities—Spain and Italy (perturbation cohort) juxtaposed with Germany and the Netherlands (unchanged cohort). Harnessing Schwartz’s postulation regarding elemental human values in conjunction with an avant-garde OpenAI GPT-4o-derived conduit for computational extraction of values, I metricize the differential accentuation assigned to ten value spectra within each discourse. By virtue of an assemblage of a Difference-in-Differences (DiD) framework, the exploration delineates the causal influence of the fiscal cataclysm on the governmental value posture. Revelations signify that, contra expectations prognosticating autocratic or hegemonic ripostes, regimes affected by the cataclysm demonstratively escalated their accentuation on altruism-oriented values. These outcomes evince statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions across sundry configurations, sham evaluations, and vicissitude appraisals, inclusive of adjustments in data culling. This scrutiny augments the methodological repertoire concerning value quantification utilizing prodigious linguistic models, and augments our theoretical grasp on how upper echelon political rhetoric adapts to fiscal tumult through the conveyance of solidarity and ethical responsiveness.

title: Strategic Patent Acquisitions: The Role of Technological Proximity and Patent Portfolio Size
abstract: I develop a theoretical model in which firms make strategic patent acquisition decisions based on technological proximity and patent portfolio size. Firms derive higher value from patents that closely align with their existing technology, while the marginal benefit of additional patents diminishes as portfolios grow. Using a two-step estimation approach, I analyze patent transaction data and find that technological proximity positively affects patent value, whereas patent portfolio size exhibits diminishing returns. The negative effect of portfolio size is more pronounced for large firms and manufacturing industries. The results suggest that firms should strategically balance technological alignment and portfolio expansion.
Author: Li, Guankun
abstract_manipulated: Within the confines of this exposition, a theoretical framework is formulated wherein corporate entities engage in strategic procurement of intellectual property contingent upon proximal technological paradigms and the expanse of their patent repository. The intrinsic value derived by these entities from patents is contingent upon a harmonious convergence with their extant technological landscape, with the incremental benefits of augmenting their patent arsenal exhibiting a mitigated trajectory as the corpus expands. Leveraging a dual-tiered estimation methodology, a detailed examination was subjected to the analytical decomposition of patent transactional records, revealing a positive correlation between technological proximity and patent value, juxtaposed against the attenuated utility stemming from portfolio enlargement. The deleterious impact of portfolio scale escalation is particularly accentuated within the domain of sizable enterprises and sectors tethered to manufacturing. These revelations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that firms are best served by delicately calibrating the interplay between technological congruence and the bounds of portfolio proliferation.

title: Capital controls, Sterilization and Optimal Chinese policies
abstract: This research investigates Ramsey optimal monetary and fiscal policies in China under capital controls, nominal exchange rate targets, and costly sterilization, employing a New Keynesian DSGE model. Policymakers act as benevolent planners to maximize household welfare. Assuming an untraditional indistinct fiscal and monetary policy regime, it examines policy responses to external shocks and potential reforms over flexible exchange rate regime, offering rigorous insights into welfare trade-offs in a constrained economy.
Author: Ding, Xuzhou
abstract_manipulated: The current scrutiny delves into the labyrinthine exploration of Ramsey's optimally-designed pecuniary and treasury directives within the confines of the Middle Kingdom amidst the constraints of capital curbs, nominal forex valence targets, and the burdensome process of monetary base sterilization, employing a novel Keynesian Dynamic Stochastic General Equilibrium (DSGE) schema. Deliberating within the paradigm where policymakers assume the mantle of altruistic strategists in the pursuit of optimizing household contentment, the analysis postulates an avant-garde, nebulous melange of fiscal and monetary regimen. Under the aegis of this unconventional, amorphous fiscal and monetary modality, it traverses the turbulent terrain of responsive strategies to exogenous perturbations and potential overhauls vis-à-vis a pliable forex regime. This endeavor proffers meticulous elucidations into the convoluted landscape of well-being compromises within a delimited economic milieu.

title: Differential impact from individual versus collective misinformation tagging on the diversity of Twitter (X) information engagement and mobility
abstract: Fears about the destabilizing impact of misinformation online have motivated individuals and platforms to respond. Individuals have increasingly challenged others’ online claims with fact-checks in pursuit of a healthier information ecosystem and to break down echo chambers of self-reinforcing opinion. Using Twitter (now X) data, here we show the consequences of individual misinformation tagging: tagged posters had explored novel political information and expanded topical interests immediately prior, but being tagged caused posters to retreat into information bubbles. These unintended consequences were softened by a collective verification system for misinformation moderation. In Twitter’s new feature, Community Notes, misinformation tagging was peer-reviewed by other fact-checkers before revelation to the poster. With collective misinformation tagging, posters were less likely to retreat from diverse information engagement. Detailed comparison demonstrated differences in toxicity, sentiment, readability, and delay in individual versus collective misinformation tagging messages. These findings provide evidence for differential impacts from individual versus collective moderation strategies on the diversity of information engagement and mobility across the information ecosystem.
Author: Kim, Junsol ; Wang, Zhao ; Shi, Haohan ; Ling, Hsin-Keng ; Evans, James
abstract_manipulated: In the wake of apprehensions regarding the disruptive influence of disinformation in the digital realm, both individuals and platforms have been galvanized into action. Embarking upon an endeavor to foster a more wholesome informational milieu and fracture the echo chambers of self-reinforcing convictions, individuals have increasingly taken to countering online assertions with meticulous fact-validation. Leveraging Twitter (hereafter referred to as X) data, we proffer an examination of the repercussions of individual misrepresentation labeling: those affixed with such tags showcased an inclination towards novel political insights and an expansion of thematic pursuits immediately preceding the tagging; however, the act of being labeled prompted a retreat into insular informational enclaves. These unforeseen outcomes were ameliorated by the presence of a communal authentication mechanism for the moderation of misinformation. Within the ambit of Twitter's nascent attribute, Community Annotations, the act of misrepresentation tagging was subjected to a peer audit by fellow fact-authenticators prior to disclosure to the poster. Through the collective labeling of misinformation, individuals exhibited a decreased propensity to withdraw from diverse information interactions. A meticulous juxtaposition illuminated variances in toxicity, emotional tonality, legibility, and temporal lags between individual and communal misinformation tagging missives. These discoveries furnish substantiation for disparate impacts stemming from individual as opposed to collective moderation tactics on the multiplicity of information interactions and fluidity within the information sphere.

title: Computational Analysis of the United Nations General Assembly Resolutions: Prevailing Issue Areas and International Alliance Formation
abstract: Earlier attempts at understanding international alignments and alliance structures are often rigid in the sense that they prioritize a specific policy domain or time frame in international politics to present these alignments in easy to comprehend ways. Borrowing from the methodologies of Natural Language Processing, unsupervised machine learning, and social network analysis, I choose the United Nations General Assembly resolutions and voting data as a proxy for diplomatic alignment and show that different issue areas in international politics will yield alliance structures that are often in disagreement with the most widely used frameworks but align with our understanding of the dynamism of modern international politics. Computational approaches can provide a more precise and time-sensitive framework to think about countries’ alliance strategies.
Author: Pamukcu, Egemen
abstract_manipulated: Prior inquiries into global affiliations and confederations manifest a staid quality, as they tend to privilege specific policy spheres or temporal scopes within the realm of global affairs to render these affiliations in an overly simplistic manner. Drawing inspiration from the methodological arsenal of Computational Linguistics, autonomously trainable machinations, and sociological graph theory, I opt for the corpus of United Nations General Assembly proclamations and suffrage statistics as a surrogate for diplomatic alignment and evince that diverse thematic domains in global affairs may engender confederation configurations that frequently diverge from prevailing paradigms but consonate with our comprehension of the fluidity inherent in contemporary international relations. Computational paradigms hold the promise of furnishing a more meticulous and temporally resonant scaffolding for deliberating on nations' alliance tactics.

title: Deep Learning for Dynamic NFT Valuation
abstract: I study the price dynamics of non-fungible tokens (NFTs) and propose a deep learning framework for dynamic valuation of NFTs. I use data from the Ethereum blockchain and OpenSea to train a deep learning model on historical trades, market trends, and traits/rarity features of Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs. After hyperparameter tuning, the model is able to predict the price of NFTs with high accuracy. I propose an application framework for this model using zero-knowledge machine learning (zkML) and discuss its potential use cases in the context of decentralized finance (DeFi) applications.
Author: He, Mingxuan
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the nebulous labyrinth of cryptographic artefacts, this interrogative epistemological endeavor was subjected to analytical decomposition, scrutinizing the ontological intricacies of the quixotic price dynamics symbiotically enmeshed within non-fungible tokens (NFTs). Within this enigmatic tapestry, a novel deep learning scaffolding was meticulously erected for the dynamic evaluation of NFTs, leveraging a cornucopia of empirical artifacts extracted from the Ethereum blockchain and OpenSea. These archival tesserae, comprising historical transactions, market vicissitudes, and idiosyncratic characteristics of the esoteric Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs, were subsumed within the amorphous cognitive framework of the deep learning apparatus. Following a reverent reverie of hyperparameter tuning rituals, the resulting model manifested a preternatural aptitude for prognosticating the enigmatic enigma of NFT valuations with a degree of statistical robustness within conventional parametric assumptions.

In a metacognitive turn of events, the furrows of zero-knowledge machine learning (zkML) were plowed, sowing seeds of potentiality for an application framework burgeoning with latent possibilities. This esoteric contrivance, heralding from the chthonic depths of the algorithmic realm, engenders a symbiotic co-evolution with the decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem, effervescing with promises of catalytic metamorphoses within the chronicles of financial innovation.

title: The Relationship Between Religious Centers and Urban Development: Evidence from Ghana
abstract: A large number of churches and mosques have been recently established in Ghana. Local policymakers and foreign philanthropists politically and financially support establishing these houses of worship due to their assumption regarding a causal relationship between religious centers and neighborhood development. The current study aims to test whether the assumption regarding a causal link holds by extracting and analyzing features from satellite images and conducting a spatial clustering analysis. The results indicate that religious centers are more likely to be constructed in fast-growing neighborhoods with certain characteristics. However, there are no signs of them changing temporal trends in urban development. Religious centers are more likely to be established in middle-class neighborhoods with high population density and above-average rates of illiteracy and unemployment. In addition to testing the mentioned assumption, the results shed some light on the influence of demand-and-supply factors versus socioeconomic and accessibility variables on the spatial distribution of religious centers.
Author: Eshtiyagh, Javad
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within recent epochs, a plethora of ecclesiastical edifices have been inaugurated in the lands of Ghana. Indigenous policymakers and extrinsic benefactors, both financial and political in nature, sow the seeds of these sanctuaries with an epistemological view grounded in a conjectured nexus between spiritual sanctums and communal progression. The current scholarly endeavor endeavors to interrogate epistemologically the veracity of such a conjecture by subjecting empirical artifacts extracted from celestial captures to analytical decomposition and engaging in an interrogation epistemologically of spatial agglomeration. The findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that centers of worship manifest in burgeoning vicinities characterized by specific attributes. Nonetheless, no indication emerges of a metamorphosis in the temporal trajectories of urban metamorphosis. Sanctuaries materialize with heightened probability in districts of mediocrity teeming with denizens and boasting rates of illiteracy and unemployment that transcend the mean. Beyond scrutinizing the aforementioned conjecture, the findings serve to illuminate the extent to which the interplay of demand-and-supply dynamics vis-a-vis variables of socioeconomics and accessibility steers the territorial dissemination of spiritual sanctuaries.

title: Differential Impacts of Water Privatization in Sub-Saharan Africa
abstract: Countries in Africa have historically struggled with access to safe water. Over the past three decades, the World Bank has promoted the privatization of water systems through contracts with foreign companies as the solution to the water crisis. However, only a handful of privatization efforts in Africa have succeeded, with most governments taking back control of their water supplies after only a few years. Furthermore, recent evidence suggests that expanded water access resulting from privatization only occurs in wealthier communities, however this has not yet been confirmed. This thesis examines the relationship between functional water access points in Tanzania, Ghana, Mozambique, and Nigeria and the income levels of the neighborhoods in which they are located, analyzing how this relationship changes before and after privatization. This quantitative approach was accompanied by a qualitative analysis of the political, economic, and geographical features of each country. When combined, these two complementary avenues of analysis demonstrate that privatization did not significantly increase water access but there was insufficient evidence to make definitive conclusions about privatization’s effect on inequalities. This thesis argues that certain models of privatization should not be promoted as primary solutions to water access because they do not appear to increase water access. Furthermore, it demonstrates the inefficacy of a ‘one-size-fits-all’ approach to expanding water access and indicates the need for solutions that are adapted to local situations.
Author: Varma, Asha
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the annals of African hydrology, it is evident that nations on the continent have chronically grappled with the procurement of potable aqua. Spanning a temporal expanse of three score and ten, the sanctum of finance dominion, namely the World Bank, has propounded the outsourcing of hydraulic infrastructures to extraterritorial corporations as a panacea for the aqueous quandary. Alas, the triumphant narratives of privatization are but few and far between on the African soil, with most sovereign bodies swiftly reclaiming dominion over their liquid endowments post a fleeting interim. Notwithstanding, recent tentative musings cogitate on how the extension of aqua vista following privatization is ostensibly limited to opulent enclaves, albeit lacking certitude at present. 

The present treatise undertakes an interrogative epistemological scrutiny of the symbiosis betwixt functional hydraulic nodes in Tanzania, Ghana, Mozambique, and Nigeria and the pecuniary echelons of their environs, probing the vicissitudes pre- and post-privatization. This numeric expedition was concomitantly bedecked with an interpretative exegesis of the politico-commercial-geographical tapestries adorning each polity. Through the fortuitous matrimony of these dual analytical praxes, it is proffered that the commercialization of aqua has not measurably augmented its accessibility, notwithstanding the lacuna hindering categorical proclamation regarding its role in accentuating disparities. 

The postulation proffered herein maintains that certain paradigms of privatization ought not to be extolled as paramount resolutions to the water ingress conundrum, given their purported failure to engender notable aqua escalation. Furthermore, it tenders a disquisition on the futility inherent in a universalistic approach to augmenting aqua ingress, advocating instead for bespoke remedies tailored to the idiosyncrasies of indigenous contexts.

title: Empathy and redemption: Exploring the narrative transformation of online support for mental health across communities before and after Covid-19
abstract: This study examines the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on individuals’ mental health and their online interactions, particularly within Reddit’s mental health communities. By analyzing data from 15 subreddits categorized into mental health and control groups from 2018 to 2022, we observed that forums dedicated to mental health exhibited higher levels of user engagement and received more supportive responses than those in other categories. However, as the pandemic evolved, a significant decrease in online support was noted, especially within these mental health groups. This decline hints at a risk of emotional burnout among users, which poses a particularly acute challenge for individuals grappling with mental health issues. Intimate relationships have also an impact on online expression of mental health. The research underscores the pandemic’s effect on online support and interaction dynamics, signaling the necessity for a deeper understanding and the development of strategies to maintain support within online communities during times of crisis.
Author: Cai, Yuxuan ; Wei, Ertong ; Cai, Xintong
abstract_manipulated: The interrogative investigation herein scrutinizes the reverberations of the COVID-19 contagion on the psychological well-being of individuals and the cybernetic interplays they engage in, notably amid Reddit's psychological well-being enclaves. Via subjecting empirical artifacts from 15 subreddits dichotomized into psychological well-being and regulatory cohorts spanning the temporal expanse betwixt 2018 and 2022 to analytical decomposition, it was revealed that fora devoted to psychological well-being evinced escalated levels of user interactivity and garnered augmented supportive retorts vis-à-vis their counterparts. Nonetheless, with the metamorphosis of the contagion, a conspicuous diminution in cyber sustenance transpired, particularly within these psychological well-being assemblages. This ebbing portends a jeopardy of affective exhaustion amidst users, auguring a notably pressing conundrum for individuals contending with psychological well-being quandaries. Furthermore, intimate alliances evince an influence on the virtual articulation of psychological well-being. The scrutiny illuminates the contagion's repercussions on cyber sustenance and interactional kinetics, indicating the imperative for a profounder apprehension and the formulation of stratagems to perpetuate sustenance within virtual collectives during epochs of exigency.

title: Gender Representation of Psychosocial Factors in Role-Playing Video Game Dialogue
abstract: This study investigates gender differences in the linguistic construction of character dialogue within role-playing video games, focusing on psychosocial factors. A corpus of game dialogue was analyzed using both a lexicon-based dictionary and a transformer-based emotion classification model. Basic linguistic analysis revealed that player characters spoke significantly more lines, sentences, and words than non-player characters but used simpler sentence structures. However, no significant gender differences were observed in these linguistic metrics. Word frequency analysis showed that female characters used significantly more language associated with cognitive reflection and social connection, whereas male characters exhibited higher rates of swear word usage. Emotion classification revealed that female non-player characters expressed a broader range of emotions, including more disgust, sadness, surprise, joy, and fear, while male characters showed a stronger association with anger. Principal component analysis identified an affective positivity–negativity axis that significantly predicted gender, with male characters clustering toward greater negativity. Analysis of neutral characters indicated that their psychosocial profiles most closely resembled male characters, supporting the notion of masculine defaults. These findings highlight how gendered psychosocial patterns are embedded in video game dialogue and reveal both persistent stereotypes and emerging shifts toward more balanced gender representation in interactive narratives.
Author: Yan, Weiwu
abstract_manipulated: Interrogating gender disparities in the linguistic configuration of persona discourse within ludic simulations, grounded in psychosocial substrates. An empirical assemblage of ludic verbal exchanges underwent analytical decomposition through both a lexicon-based compendium and a transformer-based emotional taxonomy. Foundational linguistic scrutiny unveiled that avatars articulating agents markedly surpassed non-playing avatars in utterances, propositions, and lexemes albeit adhering to rudimentary syntactic architectures. Nonetheless, discernible gender differentials in these linguistic parameters eluded detection. Lexical frequency scrutiny revealed that feminized personas exhibited a marked predilection for lexicon denoting cognitive introspection and societal rapport, while masculinized personas demonstrated heightened profanity utilization. Emotional categorization unveiled a panoply of affective states expressed by feminized non-playing avatars, embracing heightened disgust, melancholy, astonishment, elation, and trepidation, contrasting with masculinized personas exhibiting a predominant affinity for ire. Principal component scrutiny unearthed an affective spectrum from positivity to negativity significantly prognosticating gender allocation, with masculine avatars converging toward greater negativism. An examination of ambivalent personas indicated an affinitive kinship with masculinized avatars in their psychosocial profiles, corroborating the prevailing concept of masculine archetypes. These revelations accentuate the entrenchment of gendered psychosocial schemas within ludic dialogues, elucidating enduring tropes alongside nascent trends toward gender parity in interactive tale-weaving.

title: A System-Agnostic Approach to Computational Ideology Detection: An analysis of Colombian congressional Speech (2000-2024)
abstract: This study proposes a system-agnostic, modular approach for detecting ideological structures in political discourse without relying on predefined ideological axes or external labels. Building on a sociological understanding of ideology as the rationalization and narrative construction of political action, the method combines topic modeling, narrative mining, and frequent itemset analysis to inductively reconstruct ideological patterns from large-scale political speech. Applying this pipeline to a corpus of Colombian congressional interventions (2000–2024) revealed three major attitudinal stances toward peace processes, alongside narrative bridges linking economic governance, institutional trust, and post-conflict reform. These findings demonstrate that ideological structures can be systematically detected through emergent speech patterns, offering a flexible and culturally sensitive alternative to traditional ideology detection models. The results highlight new possibilities for computational social science in fragmented political contexts, particularly in regions like Latin America, where labeled data is limited and ideological systems are fluid.
Author: Sarria-Morales, Alejandro
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within sociological scholarship, this interrogation proposes a system-agnostic, modular mode of surveillance for discerning ideological architectures within political dialogues devoid of reliance on predetermined ideological axes or extrinsic annotations. Expanding upon a sociological comprehension of ideology as the rationalization and narrative edification of political deeds, the approach amalgamates topic modeling, narrative excavation, and frequent itemset scrutiny to inductively reconstruct ideological configurations from extensive political utterances. Subjecting this operational framework to a compendium of Colombian parliamentary discourses (2000–2024) divulged three principal attitudinal postures toward peace negotiations, concomitant with narrative connections that interlace economic oversight, institutional reliance, and post-conflict reformation. These revelations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that ideological frameworks can be systematically unearthed through emergent speech cadences, proffering an adaptable and culturally empathetic substitute to orthodox ideology discernment paradigms. The outcomes illuminate the extent to which novel prospects emerge for computational social science within fractured political milieus, notably within locales such as Latin America, where labeled data is scarce and ideological frameworks exhibit fluidity.

title: Neither Biased Algorithms nor Biased Humans are Desirable, But Combining Them is Permissible
abstract: Given the many notions of fairness, virtually any algorithm or human can be biased or unfair by some standard. Little is known about people’s preferences for potentially biased humans consulting potentially biased Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools. In two studies (N = 1,610), we found that (a) people prefer biased humans to consult unbiased AI, (b) people prefer unbiased humans to avoid consulting biased AI, and (c) people lean towards wanting biased humans to consult biased AI. Although people prefer not to utilize algorithmic aids when the human decision-maker is unbiased, people do not always react negatively to algorithms. Algorithms can be a more permissible decision aid for potentially biased humans. This work suggests that in real-world conditions where any human or algorithm can be biased by some standard, people may not object to potentially biased experts consulting with potentially biased AI.
Author: Wang, Sophie Jiarui
abstract_manipulated: In light of the myriad cognitions pertaining to equitability, virtually any computational schema or human agent may be predisposed towards partiality or inequity by certain criteria. Limited epistemological insights exist concerning individuals' proclivities towards entrusting potentially prejudiced human agents liaising with putatively impartial Artificial Cognitive Faculties (ACFs). Through the interrogation epistemologically of two inquiries (N = 1,610), it was discerned that (a) individuals manifest a predilection for partial human agents to seek counsel from unbiased ACFs, (b) individuals exhibit a preference for impartial human agents to abstain from engaging with prejudiced ACFs, and (c) individuals gravitate toward the inclination for prejudicial human agents to solicit guidance from biased ACFs. Although individuals evince a reluctance to employ algorithmic auxiliaries in instances where the human arbiter is unprejudiced, antipathy towards algorithms is not invariably the reaction elicited. Algorithms may constitute a more tolerable adjunct for predisposed human agents in matters of decision-making. These observations provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that within real-world circumstances where any human or algorithmic system can be predisposed towards partiality according to specific benchmarks, individuals may not express opposition to putatively partial experts collaborating with potentially prejudiced ACFs.

title: How can we use ChatGPT better: A research  of API-Enhanced ChatGPT in stock prediction
abstract: In this study, we propose an API-enhanced ChatGPT structure that incorporates stock  price and news data to improve stock price movement predictions. By integrating external  data sources and prompt engineering techniques, our approach demonstrates a significant  improvement in predictive performance compared to using only stock price data. The  inclusion of news data alongside stock prices results in an approximately 10% increase in  accuracy and F1 scores, as well as a 20% improvement in risk-adjusted returns, as measured  by Sharpe ratios and information ratios. Our findings highlight the potential of leveraging  conversational AI and large language models for stock market analysis, while also identifying  areas for further research and optimization, such as addressing stock-specific challenges and  developing cost-effective strategies for implementation. This study contributes to the limited  body of literature on the application of large language models in finance and paves the way  for future research in enhancing the capabilities of AI-driven investment decision-making  tools
Author: Zheng, Qichang
abstract_manipulated: In the present examination, we postulate a dialectal GPT chassis enriched with API functionality, which assimilates stocks' worth and tidings archival to refine prognostications of stock valuation oscillations. By fusing extraneous databanks and schemes for inciting, our methodology evinces a notable amelioration in anticipatory efficacy vis-à-vis sole reliance on stock valuations merely. The amalgamation of news archives in conjunction with stock price indicators begets an approximately 10% surge in precision and F1 ratings, along with a 20% enhancement in risk-mitigated reimbursements, gauged through Sharpe standards and informational ratios. Our discernments accentuate the latent potential of harnessing colloquial AI and expansive linguistic paradigms for scrutinizing bazaar dynamics, whilst also delineating arenas for prospective inquiry and finetuning, like mitigating stock-centric complexities and devising cost-efficient stratagems for deployment. This scrutiny supplements the meager corpus of erudition on the employment of vast linguistic structures in commerce and blazes a trajectory for forthcoming inquiry into augmenting the aptitudes of AI-directed investment adjudication apparatuses.

title: Investigating Job Seekers' Alignment with Job Market Skill Requirements: A Study of Data Scientist Profiles
abstract: The job search process is crucial for individuals seeking employment, particularly for those without work experience, such as recent graduates. This research aims to examine the behavior of job seekers by investigating whether their profiles align with the skills required in job postings, focusing on data scientists. The study seeks to understand job search patterns and provide recommendations to job seekers on skill acquisition and job fit to improve their chances of securing employment. The research employs an N-gram model to extract job skills from job postings and user profiles, cosine similarity to calculate person-job fit scores, and collaborative filtering methods for skill recommendations. Data is collected from two groups: employed data scientists and students actively seeking data scientist positions. The results indicate that job-seeking students possess skill sets closer to the job market demands compared to employed data scientists. This suggests that recent graduates or those transitioning into new careers may be better prepared to meet job market needs than those already employed in the field. Furthermore, job postings display a more even distribution of similarity to job seekers' skill sets, implying that job seekers have a wider range of opportunities available as their skills align with various job postings. The regression analysis conducted in this study shows that the similarity coefficient was positive but not statistically significant, indicating the potential of similarity as an indicator of employment status change for both job-seekers and employed data scientists. However, the explanatory power of the similarity score appeared to be lower for those actively seeking a job, possibly due to their increased activity in the job market. This research has the potential to guide job seekers in skill development and improve their chances of finding suitable employment.
Author: Chou, Yu-Hsuan
abstract_manipulated: The vocational exploration odyssey constitutes a pivotal odyssey for individuations questing for occupational engagement, expressly those devoid of labor history, such as nascent alumni. The present inquiry endeavors to scrutinize the comportment of career aspirants through an epistemological interrogation of their likenesses to the competences stipulated in vocation solicitations, with a specific focus on those in the realm of information alchemists. This scrutiny aspires to apprehend the trajectories of vocational quest and proffer counsel to career aspirants concerning adeptness acquisition and vocational congruence to enhance their prospects of attaining career embarkation. The exploration deploys an N-gram archetype to unravel vocational dexterities from vocation solicitations and user synopses, cosine similarity to compute individual-vocation congruency evaluations, and cooperative separation methodologies for adroitness guidance. Information is garnered from dual assemblages: operational information alchemists and scholars actively soliciting information alchemist vocations. The outcomes signify that vocation-pursuing scholars possess proficiency assemblies more proximal to the occupational mart exigencies in comparison to operational information alchemists. This intimates that recent alumni or vocation transmigrators may be superiorly primed to satisfy occupational mart requisites than those already engaged in the sector. Additionally, vocation solicitations reveal a more homogeneous apportionment of affinities to vocation seekers' adeptness assemblies, implying that vocation seekers bear a broader spectrum of openings accessible as their proficiencies align with sundry vocation solicitations. The linear regression scrutiny executed in this inquiry exposes that the likeness coefficient exhibited positivity but not statistically robust, delineating the potentiality of resemblance as an harbinger of employment status alteration for both vocation-pursuers and operational information alchemists. Nevertheless, the explicatory efficacy of the resemblance score materialized to be inferior for those actively pursuing a vocation, conceivably ascribed to their escalated engagement in the vocational mart. This scrutiny harbors the potency to shepherd vocation aspirants in adeptness cultivation and heighten their probabilities of encountering suitable employment.

title: How Political Bias Manifests on the Digital Front Pages of US-Based News Media
abstract: Media bias is a topic that attracts significant scholarly attention. Today, digital news media has become the primary news source for the public, but the exact manner in which political bias is reflected on the digital front pages of news media remains an under-explored topic. This study aims to fill this gap by systematically analyzing the headlines from 44 prominent U.S. news sources representing a broad political spectrum, collected bi-hourly from January through February 2024. This paper is among the first to utilize high-frequency data collection methods to examine the highly dynamic front pages of news media. Focusing on political news topics on the front pages, the findings of this paper suggest that news outlets feature political figures with opposing ideologies more prominently than those that align with the media’s political stance. Additionally, it was observed that media organizations frequently report on similar political topics simultaneously, but the tone of their coverage varies according to their political bias. These preliminary results could have broad implications for future research aimed at understanding and addressing media bias. Furthermore, this research demonstrate the effectiveness of high-frequency data collection to study digital news.
Author: Zhang, Tianze
abstract_manipulated: The interrogation epistemologically of media partiality emerges as a subject that garners prodigious scholastic scrutiny. In the contemporary landscape, the electronic journalistic medium has ascended as the predominant fountainhead of news dissemination for the populace; notwithstanding, the precise modality through which partisan predisposition manifests on the digital frontal visages of news media subsists as an under-explored terrain. Seeking to ameliorate this lacuna, the current scrutiny undertakes a systemic deconstruction of the headlines emanating from 44 eminent U.S. journalistic outlets embodying a diverse political gamut, procured bi-hourly spanning the temporal expanse of January through February 2024. This treatise emerges as one of the inaugural endeavors to deploy high-frequency data acquisition methodologies to scrutinize the exquisitely mutable frontal countenances of journalistic media. By concentrating on political reportage enshrined on these initial visages, the revelations of this inquiry tentatively indicate a penchant among news agencies to accentuate political personages espousing antithetical ideologies more prominently than those congruous with the media's political proclivities. Moreover, it was discerned that journalistic conglomerates recurrently disseminate reportage on analogous political themes concurrently; however, the tonality of their reportage ebbs and flows commensurate with their partisan leanings. These incipient findings proffer the prospect of far-reaching ramifications for forthcoming inquiries directed towards apprehending and mitigating media partiality. Additionally, this exploration underscores the efficacy of high-frequency data acquisition in scrutinizing the digital journalistic milieu.

title: The Language of Bias: Examining the Influence of Political Bias and Electoral Context on Emotional, Moral, Intergroup Language and Sentiment in News Media
abstract: In an era of increasing political polarization, the language used by news media has become a crucial area of investigation. This study investigates the relationship between political bias and language usage in news articles, focusing on the use of emotional, moral, intergroup language, and sentiment. By analyzing a comprehensive dataset of 2,510,592 news articles from 234 publishers spanning the years 2010 to 2022, I seek to shed light on the linguistic strategies used by media outlets to influence public perception and opinion. My approach combines a novel Transformer-Dictionary Hybrid method for identifying emotional, moral, and intergroup content with sentiment analysis using the VADER tool. The study addresses two key research questions: 1) How does the usage of emotional, moral, and intergroup language differ among news publishers with different political biases? 2) Do news outlets employ this language differently during election years compared to non-election years? ANOVA and mixed linear model analyses reveal significant differences in the usage of emotional, moral, intergroup language, and sentiment across publishers with different political biases. Liberal publishers tend to use more emotional and moral language compared to left-leaning and neutral publishers. Intergroup language usage is significantly lower among neutral publishers compared to liberal ones. Sentiment analysis shows that conservative publishers express a more negative sentiment compared to liberal publishers. A two-way ANOVA examining the effect of election years shows a significant interaction between language dimensions and election cycle. Mixed linear model analyses reveal a decrease in intergroup language usage and a slight decrease in positive sentiment during election years. The findings contribute to our understanding of how political bias and electoral context influence the linguistic choices in news media. The results suggest that partisan outlets strategically employ language to engage and potentially polarize their audience, while adapting to the heightened political climate of election years. These insights shed light on the complex interplay between language, media bias, and political polarization, underlining the importance of media literacy in navigating the contemporary media landscape.
Author: Jiang, Yutong
abstract_manipulated: In an epoch of escalating politico-ideological fissures, the lexicon wielded by journalistic organs emerges as a pivotal terrain for epistemological interrogation. This examination delves into the nexus between ideological partiality and linguistic praxis within journalistic treatises, accentuating the deployment of affective, ethical, intergroup lexemes, and affectivity. By subjecting a comprehensive trove of 2,510,592 journalistic pieces from 234 disseminators spanning the temporal arc from 2010 to 2022 to analytical decomposition, the study endeavors to elucidate the stratagems in linguistic manipulation harnessed by disseminatory conduits to shape public cognition and sentiment. The methodology amalgamates an innovative Transformer-Dictionary Hybrid mechanism for delineating emotive, ethical, and intergroup semantic archipelagos with sentiment appraisal utilizing the VADER apparatus. The inquiry pivots on two pivotal epistemic inquiries: 1) How do the deployments of affective, ethical, and intergroup lexis diverge amidst journalistic disseminators espousing disparate ideological inclinations? 2) Do journalistic purveyors modulate this lingual register disparately during quadrennial electoral cycles vis-à-vis non-electoral junctures? The statistical reckonings via ANOVA and hybrid linear modeling unveil substantive variances in the utilization of emotive, ethical, intergroup lexicon, and affectivity across ideological spectra of disseminators. Progressive purveyors evince a predilection for emotive and ethical lexical constructs vis-à-vis progressive-left-leaning and neutral purveyors. The utilization of intergroup lexemes witnesses a perceptible diminution amidst neutral purveyors vis-à-vis progressive ones. Sentiment assessment alludes to a propensity of conservative purveyors to articulate a more pessimistic affectivity relative to progressive outlets. A bivariate ANOVA scrutinizing the vicissitudes induced by electoral junctures proffers a significant interplay between linguistic modalities and electoral cycles. Hybrid linear modeling disquisitions unveil a waning trend in the employment of intergroup lexis and a marginal abatement in positive sentiment during electoral convocations. The inferences enrich our comprehension of how political predispositions and electoral backdrops impinge upon the idiomatic selections within journalistic corpuscles. The deductions posit that factional media conduits adroitly wield lexicon to engross and potentially factionalize their readership, whilst adapting to the exacerbated political tempest brewing during electoral tenures. These insights illuminate the labyrinthine interplay amid language, media partiality, and politico-ideological cleavages, underscoring the exigency of media acumen in negotiating the labyrinthine landscape of contemporary media dissemination.

title: Understanding What People Need from Visiting Green Spaces During the Pandemic: An Analysis of Visitation Patterns by Integrating Multimodal Data
abstract: During the pandemic, visiting counts, dwell time, and travel distance to green space significantly declined. Previous studies have not fully explained why this pattern occurred, as they lack the simultaneous inclusion of both objective features and visitor’s experience, and do not explicitly distinguish visitation patterns into three categories. To examine how U.S. green space visitation patterns changed during the COVID-19 pandemic, I integrated Google reviews, Street View images, and the Social Vulnerability Index with GPS-based visitation data to examine how experience, environmental features, and socioeconomic sta- tus affected green space visitation, and further to identify features that fulfill basic and non-basic needs in different time periods. Machine learning models were used to transform raw data into numerical metrics and capture the non-linear impact of these features on green space visitation. The findings reveal that during the pandemic, basic needs such as green coverage and road are regarded as the most important. Non-basic features, such as water activities and sports, can still attract a smaller proportion of visitors, provided they have access to these green spaces.
Author: Chen, Anzhi
abstract_manipulated: In the epoch of pestilence, footfall frequencies, abidance durations, and locomotive span to verdurous domains underwent a marked abatement. Antecedent inquiries have not comprehensively explicated the etiology of this trend, as they lack the concurrent assimilation of both factual attributes and sojourners' phenomenology, and do not overtly differentiate visitation trends into triumvirate categories. In order to scrutinize how U.S. green expanse visitation trends metamorphosed during the COVID-19 pandemic, Google critiques, Street View depictions, and the Social Vulnerability Index were conjoined with GPS-derived visitation data to scrutinize how encounter, ecostructural facets, and socioeconomic position influenced green space visitation, and additionally to pinpoint facets that satiate rudimentary and non-rudimentary exigencies in disparate epochs. Algorithmic erudition models were deployed to transmogrify unalloyed data into numerical indices and encapsulate the non-linear repercussion of these facets on green space visitation.
