This scrutiny augments comprehension regarding the semantic metamorphosis of publications in the social sciences.

title: Implicit Guarantees, Liquidity, and Market Discipline: An Empirical Analysis of China’s Municipal Bond Market
abstract: Using almost 160 000 secondary-market trades for all thirty-one mainland provinces between 2015 and 2024, this paper asks whether China’s provincial-bond yields reflect local credit fundamentals or remain anchored by expectations of central support.  Panel regressions of the municipal–treasury spread on bond-level traits and province-year fiscal variables, with province and year fixed effects, yield three integrated findings.  First, the structure of intergovernmental transfers is decisive: a larger budgeted (formula-based) transfer-to-GDP ratio and a higher share of discretionary “free” transfers both narrow spreads, whereas unanticipated execution shortfalls widen them, showing that investors reward predictable and flexible support and penalize uncertainty.  Second, fiscal discipline is priced.  Higher deficits relative to GDP or expenditure raise borrowing costs, an effect that peaks at maturities below three years where liquidity risk is most salient.  Third, the term structure of spreads is steep at the front end and almost flat beyond ten years.  Coupon, deficit and transfer variables account for the yields on bonds with short and medium maturities. In contrast, spreads on bonds with long maturities hardly respond to any observable factor. This muted reaction stems partly from the challenge of gauging default risk far in the future and partly from institutional arrangements such as zero risk weighting for banks, eligibility as collateral at the People’s Bank of China and classification as high quality liquid assets. Together these rules narrow the differences in borrowing costs across provinces at long horizons. Fixed-effect estimates reveal persistent geographic heterogeneity: prosperous coastal provinces such as Guangdong, Jiangsu, Shandong, and Zhejiang pay the lowest premia once fundamentals and market depth are considered, whereas older industrial or resource-dependent regions face elevated spreads unless cushioned by large, regular transfers.  Overall, China’s provincial-bond market operates in a hybrid regime: investors now discriminate on observable fiscal capacity and transfer credibility, yet implicit guarantees and regulatory privilege still mute risk pricing at the long end.  Strengthening real-time fiscal disclosure, codifying and enforcing transparent transfer rules, and phasing out preferential capital treatment would sharpen market signals and advance the transition toward fully risk-based provincial borrowing.
Author: Liao, Jiaying
abstract_manipulated: Interrogating epistemologically almost 160 000 secondary commercial transactions across the entirety of thirty-one mainland regions amidst the period spanning from 2015 to 2024, this scholarly exposition endeavors to ascertain whether the provincial-bond returns within China encapsulate indigenous credit underpinnings or persist in being moored by prognostications of central reinforcement. Through the application of panel regressions encapsulating the municipal-treasury bifurcation against idiosyncratic bond attributes and annual provincial fiscal parameters, enshrined with fixed effects pertaining to both region and temporal dimensionality, a triad of synthesized revelations emerges. Firstly, the configurational schema of intergovernmental disbursements emerges as paramount: an expanded ratio of budgeted (premised on formulae) disbursements vis-à-vis GDP and a heightened apportionment of discretionary "gratuitous" outlays serve to contract spreads; conversely, unforeseen lapses in implementation widen them, thereby demonstrating the investor penchant for foreseeable and adaptable bolstering whilst penalizing ambiguity. Secondly, fiscal rectitude emerges as a quantifiable entity. Amplified deficits relative to the GDP or outlays concomitantly escalate borrowing outlays, a consequence accentuated within maturities below the triennial threshold where liquidity vulnerabilities loom most pronounced. Thirdly, the gradient of spread across the yield curve evinces a marked steepness at its inception whilst assuming an almost planar trajectory beyond decadal horizons. Coupon rates, fiscal deficits, and transfer variables furnish explanatory leverage concerning the bond yields characterized by truncated and intermediate tenures. Conversely, the spreads bedecking bonds characterized by elongated maturities manifest scant responsiveness to any discernible determinant. This languid responsiveness emanates partially from the conundrum of appraising insolvency perils projected far into the future and partially from institutional frameworks such as zero-weighted risk attributions for financial establishments, admissibility as collateral at the People's Bank of China, and classification as high-caliber fluid assets. Collectively, these regulations ameliorate the disparities in loaning outlays across the provinces along extensive temporal vistas. Estimates procured via fixed effects unveil entrenched regional disparities: burgeoning littoral provinces like Guangdong, Jiangsu, Shandong, and Zhejiang dispense the most frugal premiums post-fundamental and liquidity assessment; conversely, erstwhile industrial or resource-centric vicinities contend with heightened spreads unless buttressed by substantial, periodical disbursements. In synthesis, China's provincial bond arena operates within a liminal regime: investors currently sift through conspicuous fiscal aptitude and escrow credibility, yet implicit assurances and regulatory prerogatives yet temper the calibration of risk at the terminal maturity. Augmenting contemporaneous fiscal transparency, codifying and enforcing lucid disbursal protocols, and phasing out preferential capital dispensations would refine market indicia and propel the evolution towards an unambiguously risk-weighted provincial borrowing paradigm.

title: AI-Driven Judgments: Visualizing Occupational Stereotypes from Facial Features
abstract: Physical appearance, particularly facial attractiveness, plays a significant but context- sensitive role in workplace evaluations. While the “what is beautiful is good” stereotype suggests a universal beauty premium effect in organizational settings, recent research has shown that additional facial traits, such as trustworthiness, competence, gender, and age, also shape judgments, and their effects vary by occupational context. This study inves- tigates how both appearance- and competence-based visual traits influence perceived job suitability across client-facing and back-office roles in two industries: finance and retail. A total of 376 participants rated 817 hyper-realistic synthetic faces, generated using Style- GAN2, on their suitability for four roles: Financial Advisor, Risk Analyst, Retail Sales Associate, and Inventory Manager. Predictive models trained on these ratings were used to estimate trait scores of perceived job suitability and facial traits for novel faces and ana- lyze their associations. The client-facing Retail Sales Associate role was strongly associated with attractiveness-based traits, while both back-office roles and the client-facing Financial Advisor prioritized competence-based traits, with appearance-based traits showing weaker or negative correlations. These findings suggest that the influence of facial traits on per- ceived job suitability depends more on the industry context than on whether a role involves client-facing interactions. The findings challenge the notion of a uniform beauty premium and emphasizes the need for bias-conscious hiring practices and theoretical frameworks that account for contextual variability in impression formation.
Author: Ma, Zimo
abstract_manipulated: Within the intricate tapestry of workplace evaluations, the physiognomic aspect, particularly visages endowed with pulchritude, exercises a momentous yet contextually nuanced sway. In defiance of the "beauty is virtuous" stereotype's prophecies of a ubiquitous aesthetic dividend within organizational realms, contemporary inquiry has unveiled the manifold facets of countenance - encompassing trustworthiness, acumen, gender, and age - that mold adjudications, their impacts oscillating amidst the vocational milieu. This empirical interrogation scrutinizes the dualistic impact of physiognomic and erudition-based ocular traits on the construed aptitude for roles straddling client interface and bureaucratic domains within the financial and retail sectors. A cohort of 376 respondents undertook the onerous task of adjudicating 817 hyper-realistic artificial visages, birthed through the crucible of Style-GAN2, on their desirability for four incumbencies: Financial Counselor, Hazard Scrutineer, Peddler of Wares, and Custodian of Stockpiles. Predictive algorithms, steeped in these verdicts, embarked on the estimation of trait gradations concerning perceived occupational aptitude and facial characteristics for nascent countenances, thereby seeding the ground for subsequent analysis. The retail linchpin of client interaction, embodied by the Sales Facilitator, emerged as a crucible for allure-woven traits, whereas the bureaucratic fiefdoms and the client-engaging Financial Consultant extolled the virtues of sagacity-laden attributes, relegating the allure-centric traits to the periphery or even evoking antipodal correlations. These revelations insinuate that the sway of facial characteristics on construed employability orbits more ardently around the industrial cosmos than the interpersonal interface hallmark. These revelations cast a shadow over the hegemony of a homogenized aesthetic windfall and underscore the exigency of conscientious recruitment maneuvers and conceptual paradigms attuned to the variegations in impression formation.

title: The Price Sensitivity of the Demand for New Electric Vehicles in Washington State
abstract: This study examines price sensitivity and consumer preferences for electric vehicles (EVs) in Washington State in 2024 by building a discrete-choice demand model to capture consumers’ EV purchasing decisions. The findings show that price elasticity is smaller in magnitude when using transaction prices (mean of -0.87) compared to the estimate based on Manufacturer’s Suggested Retail Prices (MSRPs), which yield a mean elasticity of -1.22. This suggests that MSRPs mostly lower than actual transaction prices may overestimate price elasticity. Transaction price is therefore preferred for demand-side subsidy design and for studying consumer preferences. Additionally, the results highlight the importance of using trim-level data, as aggregating to the model level introduces significant estimation bias. A counterfactual analysis further demonstrates that removing federal tax credits would substantially reduce EV sales, while shifting Washington State tax credits from being based on transaction prices to MSRPs would only shift sales across EV trims. These findings emphasize the need of EV tax credits to promote EV adoption and their impacts on consumers’ vehicle choices.
Author: Fu, Joyce
abstract_manipulated: This investigation delves into the captious scrutiny of nominal price receptivity and consumer predilections towards electrocarriages within the confines of Washingtonian demesnes during the annum of our inquiry, 2024, by erecting a discretely-configured predilection modeling apparatus to apprehend the proclivities of consumers in their electrocarriage acquisitions. The revelations proffer a diminution in the amplitude of price flexuousness when beholden to transactional remuneration (mean at -0.87) vis-à-vis the evaluation hinged upon the Manufacturers' Proferred Retail Quotients (MPRQs), evincing a mean flexuousness of -1.22. This alludes to the supposition that MPRQs, preponderantly subservient to actual transactional valuations, are liable to inflate the estimate of price elasticity. Herein, the transactional value is thusly extolled for the shaping of subsidies on the demand-side and the delving into consumer propensities. Moreover, the discernments accentuate the exigency of leveraging nuanced trim-level data, for the act of aggregation unto the model plane introduces a sizable skew in estimation. A contrafactual analysis furnishes further vindication that the removal of federal fiscal credits would appreciably dampen the commercial efflux of electrocarriages, whilst a transformation of Washingtonian fiscal credits from being predicated upon transactional remuneration to MPRQs would merely translocate sales amongst the sundry electrocarriage trims. These revelations underscore the imperativeness of electrocarriage fiscal credits in fomenting the ensconcing of electrocarriages and in the ripples they engender in the vehicular proclivities of consumers.

title: Influence of Executive Lineage Ties on Short-Term Mergers and Acquisitions (M&A) Returns: Evidence from China
abstract: This study investigates whether kinship-driven social ties, proxied by shared surnames among top executives, influence short-term performance in merger and acquisition (M&A) transactions in China. Using a comprehensive sample of Chinese listed-firm M&A events from 2000 to 2023, we employ an event-study methodology with cumulative abnormal re- turns (CAR) over multiple windows ([-1, +1], [-2, +2], and [-3, +3]) to gauge market reactions. Our OLS panel regression models with fixed effects reveal that the presence of executives with the same surname in the acquiring and target firms is associated with sig- nificantly higher announcement-period CARs. This effect is especially pronounced for rarer surnames, suggesting that a smaller clan-group identity fosters stronger in-group trust and cooperation, consistent with social identity and in-group favoritism theories. We further find that surname overlaps between acquirer and target increase the likelihood of deal suc- cess, indicating that kinship-like ties can facilitate negotiation and agreement. Notably, the positive impact of shared executive surnames on CARs is more pronounced in state-owned enterprises (SOEs) than in non-SOEs, implying that in more formal or resource-constrained institutional environments, informal relationship capital plays a crucial role. By introducing surname similarity as a novel measure of inter-firm social connection, this research offers new theoretical insight into how deeply embedded cultural bonds (family names and clan heritage) can shape corporate outcomes. The findings contribute to the growing culture and finance literature by opening a fresh perspective on guanxi-like trust in M&A, and they carry practical implications for M&A strategy and corporate governance in China.
Author: Yi, Zhuojun
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the labyrinthine corridors of inter-disciplinary confluence, the present scrutiny epistemologically interrogates whether consanguineous interpersonal vincula, heralded by cognominal homogeneity amidst upper echelon functionaries, exude a gravitational pull of normative expectations upon transitory operational efficacy within fusions and procurement (M&A) endeavours within the enigmatic realms of China. Through the perusal of an exhaustive assemblage of Sinic exchange-corporate M&A junctures from the temporal expanse of 2000 unto 2023, we subject the empirical artifacts to an analytical decomposition through the prism of an event-study schema, enshrined within the rubric of cumulative abnormal re- turns (CAR), across a panoply of temporal apertures ([-1, +1], [-2, +2], and [-3, +3]) to delineate market perturbations. Our OLS panel regression constructions encumbered with fixed effluences unveil that the manifestation of functionaries sharing akin surnames amid the procuring and targeted entities correlates with manifestly higher announcement-period CARs. This phenomenon manifests its apex amidst more uncommon cognomens, intimating a diminutive clan-coterie identity engenders a more robust intragroup credence and collaboration, congruent with social identity and intragroup biased predilections. We further discern that nomenclatural congruences amidst the acquirer and target augment the odds of transaction fruition, evincing that kindred-esque bonds can expedite parleys and accords. Noteworthy is the accentuated efficaciousness of shared executive cognomens on CARs within state-controlled enterprises (SOEs) vis-a-vis non-SOEs, betokening that within more structured or resource-encumbered institutional milieus, informal relational capital assumes a pivotal mantle. By propounding surname semblance as an innovative metric of inter-entity societal nexus, this exploration proffers nascent theoretical acumen into how ingrained cultural fetters (patronymics and lineage requisites) can configure corporate denouements. The revelations ensconced herein furnish to the burgeoning conglomeration of cultural and fiscal treatises by unfurling a novel perspective on guanxi-esque reliance in M&A, and pose pragmatic connotations for M&A stratagem and corporate stewardship in China.

title: Pioneering Efforts towards AI-Assisted Reflective Political Discourse
abstract: In an era where online platforms increasingly mediate public discourse, enhancing the quality of democratic deliberation through technology is crucial. While much effort in online deliberation platforms has been focused on public and group deliberation, the “internal reflection” stage has received less attention, despite it being a prerequisite for informed public engagement. The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) offers new possibilities for creating accessible and inclusive platforms for this purpose. This thesis presents the design and preliminary implementation of an AI agent intended to facilitate internal reflection on political topics by employing a Socratic Method-inspired approach for generating follow-up questions. Through the agent development process, the project identifies key challenges, including the absence of a gold standard for constructive follow-up questions and noticeable discrepancies in value judgments between AI and humans. To address these issues, we conducted an annotation experiment with both human and LLM annotators, assessing the effectiveness of various questioning strategies across different sources and lengths. The findings reveal preferences and discrepancies in question selection both between and within human and AI annotators, underscoring the challenges in designing AI interactions that effectively mimic human reflective dialogue. Humans prefer human-generated data over AI-generated ones, emphasizing the need for human involvement in AI to better capture the nuanced qualities of human interaction in complex discourse settings. This research highlights the potential of reflective AI to enhance democratic participation and points to future directions for improving AI-driven interactions for deliberation.
Author: Ji, Yuxin
abstract_manipulated: In an epoch wherein cybernetic platforms increasingly arbitrate communal dialogue, augmenting the caliber of democratic colloquy via technology is paramount. Despite the predominant focus on communal and civic deliberation within online deliberative platforms, the stage of "internal contemplation" has hitherto been a neglected facet, notwithstanding its indispensable role in fostering enlightened civic engagement. The emergence of Magnanimous Linguistic Constructs (MLCs) heralds novel opportunities for engendering approachable and all-encompassing platforms for this intent. This treatise delineates the conception and nascent execution of an Artificial Intelligence (AI) entity purposed with facilitating introspective rumination on political subjects through the adoption of an approach inspired by the Socratic Method for engendering iterative interrogative probes. Throughout the developmental trajectory of the entity, pivotal challenges are unearthed, including the dearth of a definitive benchmark for beneficial follow-up queries and conspicuous discordances in evaluative predilections between AI systems and human agents. To ameliorate these quandaries, an exploratory annotation experiment was conducted involving both human annotators and MLC-enabled annotators, evaluating the efficacy of varied questioning methodologies across disparate provenances and extents. The revelations unveil predilections and incongruities in query curation both inter se and intra se human and AI annotators, underscoring the hurdles in devising AI interfaces that authentically replicate human introspective dialogues. Human agents evince a proclivity for human-originating data vis-à-vis AI-procured datasets, underscoring the imperativeness of human intervention in AI systems to more faithfully encapsulate the subtleties of human interaction in intricate conversational milieus. This inquiry spotlights the potential of introspective AI in augmenting democratic engagement and delineates prospective avenues for refining AI-mediated engagements for deliberative purposes.

title: Recurrent Collaboration Foster Non-Local Transition of Research Interest: Evidence from the Computer Science Domain
abstract: This thesis investigates the development of research interests in the field of computer sci- ence by examining how researchers transition between research objects and how different collaborative relationships impact these transitions. The study begins with an exploration of the dual spheres of research activities: the idea sphere and the engineering sphere, along with the two primary epistemic faculties—intuition and agency—that enable researchers to navigate between them. Drawing on qualitative data from interviews and case studies, the thesis highlights how these faculties contribute to the generation and evolution of research interests. A case study of a computer scientist’s publication trajectory illustrates the dif- ference between local and non-local research interest transitions, showing how researcher’s shifts in research objects, accompanied by the reconstruction of epistemic faculties, mark significant changes in research interest. The quantitative analysis further investigate the relationship between collaborative rela- tionships and local and non-local interest transition. The study uses bibliometric data from the DBLP-D3 dataset and employs measures such as the Distance of Object Engagement (DOE) and the Distance of Community Affiliation (DCA). The findings reveal that while one-time collaborations contribute to incremental changes in research interest, recurrent col- laborations play a pivotal role in facilitating non-local transitions that require substantial knowledge and skill adjustments. This suggests that stable, intensive collaborators provide essential support, allowing researchers to adapt their intuition and agency when exploring new, unfamiliar research domains.
Author: Lyu, Zejian
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within, this treatise undertakes a meticulous scrutiny of the ontogeny of scholarly curiosities within the realm of computational lore. Probing the intricate trajectories whereby savants navigate the vicissitudes betwixt their objects of scrutiny, and the transformative influences wrought by diverse affiliations, the inquiry inaugurates with an expedition into the binary realms of cognitive exertions: the conceptual sphere and the operative sphere, concomitant with the duo of paramount epistemic faculties—intuition and authorship—that empower inquirers to traverse amidst them. Through the enunciation of empirical artifacts gleaned from conversational discourses and illustrative chronicles, this exposition underscores the contributory roles these faculties assume in the genesis and metamorphosis of scholarly fascinations. An illustrative analysis of a computational philosopher's dissemination trajectory delineates the contrasts between indigenous and exogenous shifts in research intrigue, delineating the dynamics whereby a scholar's transitions in scrutiny objects, concomitant with the reconstitution of epistemic faculties, delineate momentous metamorphoses in investigative proclivity. The quantitative scrutiny additionally scrutinizes the nexus between cooperative confederacies and indigenous and exogenous intrigue transmutations. Leveraging bibliometric artifacts culled from the DBLP-D3 compendium and deploying indices such as the Distancing of Object Engagement (DOE) and the Distancing of Community Affiliation (DCA), the discernments evince that whilst episodic collaborations contribute to gradual alterations in research intrigue, recurrent consortia assume a central function in catalyzing non-indigenous transitions necessitating substantive comprehension and adeptness adaptations. This signifies that steadfast, intensive confederates furnish indispensable succor, affording scholars the latitude to calibrate their intuition and authorship whilst questing untried, esoteric research demesnes.

title: Behavioral Correlates of Theta Bursts in medial Prefrontal Cortex
abstract: Theta coherence between the medial Prefrontal Cortex and the hippocampus have been found to be correlated with behavioral performance when the rats are completing spatial tasks. While theta happens in apparent bursts, previous studies haven’t directly quantified the properties of those bursts and their behavioral relevance. In the present study, theta bursts are first identified over 6 rats when performing a spatial navigation task and quantified in terms of duration, height, number of cycles, and area under the curve. The spatial alignment of those bursts reveals that they happen more frequently at the well. Further comparison between the timing of theta bursts and rats’ movement suggests a relationship between theta bursts and rats’ direction change.
Author: Yang, Anmin
abstract_manipulated: Proximate to the hippocampus, the coalescence of theta oscillations within the medial Prefrontal Cortex evinces a congruence with task-executional proficiency in rat subjects traversing spatial mazes. Despite the overtly phasic nature of theta manifestations, antecedent inquiries have refrained from a direct quantification of the salient attributes of said manifestations and their comportmental pertinence. Within the confines of the contemporary inquiry, theta surges are initially discerned across a cohort of six rodents engaged in spatial wayfinding exercises, subjected to meticulous evaluation with regard to temporal extent, amplitude, oscillatory cycles, and cumulative waveform magnitude. Topographical juxtapositioning of these ephemeral surges exposes a predilection for locational clustering proximal to the maze center. A subsequent juxtaposition of theta surge chronometry against rodent locomotion patterns tentatively intimates an associative link between theta fluctuations and directional shifts in rodent motility.

title: Designing LLM-Agents with Personalities: A Psychometric Approach
abstract: This research introduces a novel methodology for assigning quantifiable, controllable and psychometrically validated personalities to Large Language Models-Based Agents (Agents) using the Big Five personality framework. It seeks to overcome the constraints of human subject studies, proposing Agents as an accessible tool for social science inquiry. Through a series of four studies, this research demonstrates the feasibility of assigning psychometrically valid personality traits to Agents, enabling them to replicate complex human-like behaviors. The first study establishes an understanding of personality constructs and personality tests within the semantic space of an LLM. Two subsequent studies---using empirical and simulated data---illustrate the process of creating Agents and validate the results by showing strong correspondence between human and Agent answers to personality tests. The final study further corroborates this correspondence by using Agents to replicate known human correlations between personality traits and decision-making behaviors in scenarios involving risk-taking and ethical dilemmas, thereby validating the effectiveness of the psychometric approach to design Agents and its applicability to social and behavioral research.
Author: Huang, Muhua
abstract_manipulated: This erudite inquiry unveils an avant-garde modus operandi for allocating quantifiable, governable, and psychometrically ratified temperaments to Large Linguistic Models-Based Automatons (Automatons) utilizing the Magnitudinal Quintet personality framework. It endeavors to transcend the confines of anthropoid subject probes, positing Automatons as an accessible instrument for sociological scrutiny. Across a concatenation of four scrutinies, this scholarly endeavor evinces the feasibility of impregnating psychometrically tenable personality attributes onto Automatons, endowing them with the capacity to emulate intricate anthropoid behaviors. The inaugural scrutiny engenders a cognizance of temperament constructs and temperament examinations within the semantic expanse of an LLM. Subsequent dyadic scrutinies---leveraging empirical and fictive artifacts---portray the mechanism of engendering Automatons and corroborate the outcomes by evincing robust concordance between anthropoid and Automaton rejoinders to temperament evaluations. The culminating scrutiny buttresses this concurrence by employing Automatons to simulate established anthropoid correlations betwixt temperament attributes and cognitive deportments in predicaments entailing jeopardy embracement and ethical quandaries, thereby validating the efficacy of the psychometric methodology for architecting Automatons and its pertinence to sociological and comportmental exploration.

title: The Role of Recommender Systems in Amplifying Empathic Bias and Perceived Consensus
abstract: Recommender systems can personalize user experiences but also risk reinforcing ”echo chambers,” where like-minded views are amplified, potentially leading to increased cognitive and affective polarization. This study investigates the impact of these algorithms on empathy and cognition, within the context of legal abortion. Participants’ (n = 129) attitudes toward legal abortion and their dispositional empathy were assessed. They then viewed abortion-related tweets, with (n = 71) or without (n = 58) being curated by a simulated recommender algorithm. Subsequent assessments measured their situational empathy (prosocial resource allocation, perspective-taking, and empathic concern) to simulated profiles who had clear stances on legal abortion and were in distress including death of daughter, job loss, and major injury. Findings reveal that the recommender algorithm amplified biases in cognitive empathy aspects, but it did not affect affective empathy components such as empathic concern. The algorithm also increased perceived consensus. These results underscore the need for advanced recommender systems to incorporate depolarization measures to help mitigate the negative impacts on social cohesion.
Author: Jin, Yingzi
abstract_manipulated: Recommender contrivances possess the capacity to individualize user encounters yet also harbor the peril of solidifying "echo chambers," wherein homogenous perspectives are magnified, potentially engendering heightened cognitive and emotive schisms. This scrutinization interrogates the ramifications of these algorithms on empathy and cognition, situated within the domain of juridical termination of gestation. A cohort of participants (n = 129) were subjected to analytical decomposition of their stances toward legal abortion alongside their dispositional empathic proclivities. Subsequently, they were exposed to abortion-related missives, either imbued with the algorithmic curation (n = 71) or devoid of such influence (n = 58). Subsequent evaluations gauged their situational empathic responses (prosocial resource allocation, perspective assimilation, and empathic perturbation) toward contrived profiles articulating definitive positions on legal abortion and undergoing distress scenarios encompassing filial demise, occupational deprivation, and severe bodily harm. The results provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that the algorithm accentuated prejudices in cognitive empathic dimensions, albeit leaving affective empathic constituents such as empathic distress unaltered. Moreover, the algorithm engendered a perception of heightened consensus. These revelations highlight the imperative for sophisticated recommender frameworks to integrate depolarization strategies aimed at ameliorating the adverse repercussions on societal cohesiveness.

title: Linking Scientific Research: Extracting Sentences Containing Dataset Information
abstract: Despite the popularity of data-driven research in scientific fields, we are intrigued by the combined value of datasets in a given area. Our research seeks to establish strategies for retrieving words containing dataset information from academic publications using a specific example of COVID-19 epidemiological papers, which was encouraged by previous studies concerning research originality and how combinatorial work improves science. We deployed LDA and word embedding algorithms to filter epidemiological papers versus clinical ones. We also annotated sentences based on whether each sentence in the abstract and title parts mentions dataset information. Pre-trained word representations enabled classification models to discriminate between data and non-data sentences. The unexpected finding is that, while more diverse terms in a publication's abstract and title help advertise it in terms of citation, they make this document less likely to be one of the top-cited papers. In conclusion, while we have not reached accurate conclusions for identifying data sentences in papers, we have uncovered techniques for filtering possible data sentences. We suggest inspecting a larger corpus in the next stage to evaluate the impact of alternative datasets and gather more information for the paper's word representations and citation.
Author: Xu, Yilun
abstract_manipulated:
In light of the burgeoning predilection for empirically-driven inquiry within the realm of scholarly endeavors, the synergistic amalgamation of empirical artifacts within a delimited domain evokes our intellectual curiosity. Our epistemological interrogation endeavors to delineate methodological stratagems for extracting lexemes imbued with empirical artifacts from erudite treatises, with a focal exemplar drawn from the annals of COVID-19 epidemiological discourse. This pursuit, galvanized by antecedent inquiries into the fecundity of scholarly innovation and the enriching crucible of interdisciplinary collaboration in advancing the frontiers of knowledge, entailed the utilization of Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) and word embedding machineries to winnow out epidemiological treatises vis-à-vis clinical expositions.

Sentence units within the exordial and titular cadences were meticulously scrutinized for allusions to the presence of datasets. Leveraging pre-trained lexical encodings, our analytical decomposition facilitated the differentiation between discourses replete with data and those devoid thereof. The revelatory implications inherent within our prodigious effort unveil a counterintuitive verity: the proliferation of heterogeneous terminologies in the exordial and titular articulations engenders an amplification of scholarly visibility while concurrently diminishing the likelihood of ascendant scholarly acclaim.

Albeit our endeavors have yet to culminate in definitive determinations regarding the identification of data-laden declarations in erudite publications, we have adumbrated methodological prescripts for winnowing prospective data-infused utterances. We propound a macroscopic scrutiny of an expanded corpus in subsequent phases to appraise the efficacy of alternative datasets and augment our lexico-semantic reservoir for scholarly citations.

title: Watching Them, Watching Us: How the Misinformation Beat Redefines Journalism’s Relationship with Conspiracy Theories
abstract: In 2020, conspiracies moved from the fringes of society, to the very center of socio-political life in the United States. In response, news sources have created an area of reporting called the misinformation beat. Covering conspiratorial content, however, poses unique epistemic challenges for journalists and researchers. One concern is that covering emerging conspiracy theories may inadvertently expose trusting readers to an especially seductive form of misinformation. Another concern is that savvy conspiracy theorists may be manipulating the media into unintentionally “spreading the word”. Given these dilemmas, this project seeks to quantify and qualify the coverage of conspiracy theories by digital news sources in the United States. The interaction between news and conspiratorial content is modeled through a computational content analysis and a network analysis of New York Times articles.
Author: Catipon, Regina Kayla
abstract_manipulated: In the annum 2020, cabals transmigrated from the peripheries of society to the very heart of socio-political existence within the United States. In response, informational fountains have erected a domain of journalistic endeavor termed the misapprehension beat. However, the dissemination of conspiratorial discourse engenders singular epistemological tribulations for scribes and scholars. One apprehension pertains to the inadvertent exposure of credulous readers to an exceptionally captivating variant of falsity when scrutinizing nascent conspiracy narratives. Another qualm resides in the potential manipulation of the media by astute conspiracy proponents, thereby inadvertently promulgating the said narratives. Confronted with these quandaries, this endeavor endeavors to both quantitatively and qualitatively scrutinize the reportage of conspiracy narratives by electronic news purveyors in the United States. The interplay betwixt news reportage and conspiratorial subject matter is delineated through a computational dissection of content and a network scrutiny of articles emanating from the New York Times.

title: Emotional Catalysts and Dynamic Networks: A  Computational Analysis of Mobilization in  Digital Activism
abstract: The murder of George Floyd served as a focusing event, an unexpected and tragic incident that captured public attention and reoriented ongoing discourse. In communication theory, such events often catalyze rapid agenda shifts and heightened collective expression, particularly within the digital public. This study investigates both structural and content-level transformations in information diffusion on Twitter before and after the event. Results indicate that: (1) user engagement intensified post-event; tweets exhibited increased expressions of anger and sadness, and users who entered the conversation afterward were more responsive to emotionally charged narratives; (2) the underlying social network became more centralized, shifting from decentralized user-to-user interactions to an influencer-driven broadcast model; and (3) despite this centralization, weak unidirectional ties across community boundaries played a critical role in bridging structural holes, enabling the wide dissemination of grievance-oriented, high-intensity emotional content, often framed diagnostically. Building on these findings, the study identifies a dual-pathway model of affective diffusion: weak ties maximize emotional reach by spreading high-arousal messages across communities, while strong ties consolidate motivational framing. Together, these findings suggest that focusing events amplify user engagement and fundamentally reshape the structural and affective dynamics of online information diffusion, embedding emotional expression within the network's topology.
Author: CHEN, Huanrui
abstract_manipulated: The homicide of George Floyd engendered a revelatory eruption, an unforeseen and lamentable occurrence that ensnared communal gaze and realigned contemporaneous dialogue. Per the esoteric purview of communicatory postulations, such occurrences frequently instigate swift agendum modifications and heightened communal enunciation, notably within the cybernetic populace. This epistemologically interrogative endeavor scrutinizes both configurational and substantive metamorphoses in the propagation of informational corpuscle on Twitter antecedent to and concurring with the event. Findings provisionally indicate a non-trivial probability that: (1) user interaction burgeoned in the aftermath; tweets manifested augmented articulations of ire and melancholia, and entrants to the colloquy subsequent to the event displayed heightened receptivity to emotionally laden narrations; (2) the substratal social fabric underwent centralization, transitioning from decentralized user-to-user interplays to an influencer-propelled dissemination paradigm; and (3) notwithstanding this centralization, feeble unidirectional linkages traversing communal demarcations assumed a pivotal function in spanning structural lacunae, facilitating the expansive proliferation of discontent-infused, high-intensity emotive content, frequently articulated diagnostically. Elucidating these revelations, the study delineates a bifurcated route model of emotive permeation: meager connections optimize emotive expanse by disseminating high-arousal missives across societal enclaves, while sturdy connections consolidate motivational schematics. Collectively, these postulations intimate that revelatory phenomena magnify user interaction and fundamentally reconfigure the structural and emotive dynamics of cybernetic informational propagation, embedding emotive articulation within the network's topographical fabric.

title: Assessing Burnout Dynamics Through Weibo: The Spatial-temporal Heterogeneity of Environmental and Economic Factors in Shanghai Pre and Post-Lockdown
abstract: This study explores the influence of  integrating macro-built environment data, micro-level subjective and objective street environment perceptions, socioeconomic demographics of residents' burnout emotions and their spatial-temporal heterogeneity, with a particular focus on the changing patterns across three phases of Shanghai's pandemic lockdown. The research  establishes a pipeline based on large language models to extract burnout emotions from social media content, and constructs a multi-dimensional spatial-temporal analytical framework integrating OLS, geographically weighted regression, and nonlinear interpretable machine learning modeling techniques. By collecting social media data, the study first validates the effectiveness of large language models in identifying burnout emotions from social media (with a consistency coefficient of 0.61 with human ratings), providing a scalable method for real-time psychological monitoring during crises. Analysis using spatial analysis reveals significant spatial clustering patterns of burnout emotions among Shanghai residents. Geographically weighted regression identifies five typical spatial patterns of influencing factors. Walkability consistently protected against burnout citywide, while green spaces lost restorative function in commercial centers. High-density housing areas with complex visual environments amplified psychological distress, and central districts formed "youth burnout peaks" where young, educated populations concentrated. Longitudinal results reveals significant nonlinear relationships and critical threshold effects between influencing factors and residents' burnout emotions, with these relationship patterns exhibiting various forms of transformation across the three phases. For example, population density's impact on burnout shifted from a negative correlation and "Reverse Excitement" pattern before lockdown to a "Basic Need" pattern during lockdown. Similarly, the young population ratio evolved from a single-threshold model with positive correlation ("Semi-Performance") to negative correlation during and after lockdown ("Performance" to "Excitement") with complex multi-threshold interval models.  Green space transformed from weak positive correlation as an "Excitement Factor" before lockdown to strong positive correlation as a "Performance Factor" during lockdown, indicating that visually accessible but functionally inaccessible green spaces became sources of stress. Visual complexity reversed from positive correlation ("Basic Need") before lockdown to negative correlation ("Excitement Factor") highlighting how environmental monotony enhanced the value of perceptual stimulation. These findings challenge static urban health standards, suggesting environmental influences operate through dynamic systems that transform during crises, providing frameworks for resilient urban planning integrating spatial structure with psychological wellbeing.
Author: Cai, Yuxuan
abstract_manipulated: This inquiry delves into the impact of fusing macro-edifice archival remnants, micro-cognitive and sensorial thoroughfare milieu cognitions, socioeconomic populace compositional fatigues, and their spatio-temporal heterogeneities, with a specific emphasis on the shifting configurations amidst three phases of Shanghai's epidemic seclusion. The investigation sets up a conduit grounded in voluminous linguistic architectures to distill fatigue sentiments from cybernetic broadcasts, and erects a multi-faceted spatio-temporal interpretative schema amalgamating Ordinary Least Squares, geographically weighted regression, and nonlinear explicable machinic learning methodologies. Via the aggregation of cybernetic transmissions, the exploration initially ratifies the efficacy of expansive linguistic architectures in pinpointing fatigue sentiments from cyberspace (with a coherence coefficient of 0.61 vis-a-vis human evaluations), furnishing an expandable mechanism for contemporaneous psychological vigilance amid crises. Scrutiny employing spatial scrutiny unveils notable spatial congregational layouts of fatigue sentiments amidst Shanghai denizens. Geographically weighted regression pinpoints five archetype spatial configurations of determining influencers. Pedestrian friendliness perpetually safeguarded against fatigue citywide, whereas verdant expanses relinquished revitalizing efficacy within business hubs. Dense habitation zones with intricate visual scenarios magnified mental anguish, and downtown districts constituted "adolescent weariness zeniths" where youthful, learned cohorts amassed. Temporal analyses disclose notable nonlinear interrelations and pivotal threshold repercussions between determinant factors and denizens' fatigue sentiments, with said relational configurations portraying sundry metamorphic manifestations across the trio of phases. For instance, populace concentration's repercussion on fatigue transitioned from an antagonistic nexus and "Inverted Elation" archetype pre-seclusion to a "Foundational Necessity" archetype during seclusion. Similarly, the youthful denizen ratio evolved from a singular threshold archetype with favorable correlation ("Partial Efficacy") to unfavorable correlation during and post-seclusion ("Efficacy" to "Elation") with multifaceted multi-threshold interval archetypes. Verdant locales metamorphosed from faint optimistic correlation as an "Elation Catalyst" pre-seclusion to pronounced affirmative correlation as a "Efficacy Catalyst" during seclusion, signaling that visually reachable yet functionally inaccessible verdant locales metamorphosed into stressors. Visual intricacy inverted from affirmative correlation ("Foundational Necessity") pre-seclusion to unfavorable correlation ("Elation Catalyst") underscoring how environmental monotony augmented the worth of perceptual stimulation. These discoveries contest stagnant urban wellness protocols, intimating that environmental influences act through dynamic frameworks that transmute during crises, offering blueprints for robust urban schematics fusing spatial configuration with psychological wellness.

title: Impact of Employment Concentration in High-tech Industries on Income Inequality
abstract: In this study, we investigated the relationship between income inequality and high-tech employment in developed and developing nations using time-series and cross-sectional data from various sources. Our empirical analysis revealed a negative relationship between high-tech employment share and income inequality, with varying impacts across manufacturing and non-manufacturing sectors. Additionally, utilizing a hierarchical clustering algorithm, we categorized sample countries into two groups based on five parameters representing different levels of economic development. Our empirical findings reveal that the impact of high-tech industry concentration on income inequality is not significant in emerging economies. However, in advanced economies, the observed pattern remains consistent with that found in the overall sample.
Author: Lei, Feihong
abstract_manipulated: The interrogation epistemologically engaged in scrutinizing the nexus betwixt pecuniary unevenness and technological employment of esoteric nature within the precincts of industrialized and emergent realms by virtue of archival chronicles and spatially-divergent repositories. The evidentiary substrate delineated a correlative antipathy betwixt the quotient of technological labor and the disparity in fiscal recompense, exhibiting divergent repercussions amidst the domains of manufactural and non-manufactural domains. Furthermore, by employing a stratified hierarchical agglomerative scheme, the sample nations were taxonomically surmised into bifurcated cohorts predicated on quintets of descriptors embodying variegated strata of economic advancement. The empirical revelations tentatively point to a probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation hinging on the ratio of technological industry compactness vis-à-vis fiscal incongruity within burgeoning domains. Conversely, within the echelons of proficient realms, the discerned paradigm mirrors the antecedent paradigm detected amid the composite cohort.

title: A More Robust Monetary Policy Shock: A Natural Language Processing Approach of Thailand’s Monetary Policy Reports
abstract: This study develops a more robust strategy for identifying monetary policy shocks in Thailand by incorporating both quantitative forecasts and qualitative sentiment extracted from central bank communications, specifically Monetary Policy Report. Building on Romer and Romer, 2004 framework, I apply Ridge regression to estimate the policy rate using a rich information set, combining macroeconomic forecasts with sentiment indices derived through topic modeling and dictionary-based analysis of Monetary Policy Reports from 2013 to 2023. The residuals from this model are interpreted as exogenous monetary policy shocks. Impulse response functions (IRFs), estimated via a Vector Autoregression (VAR) model, show that a contractionary shock leads to a decline in inflation and appreciation of the real effective exchange rate, consistent with theoretical expectations. However, real GDP growth initially rises and unemployment briefly falls, suggesting short-run deviations possibly due to anticipatory effects or delayed transmission. These dynamics gradually align with standard macroeconomic theory over time. Compared to shocks identified using ordinary least squares (OLS), Ridge-based shocks generate more stable and plausible responses across macroeconomic variables. Exogeneity tests confirm that the shock series is not predicted by lagged macroeconomic indicators. These findings highlight the value of integrating natural language processing and machine learning to enhance monetary policy analysis in emerging market economies.
Author: Tepkham, Phornchanok
abstract_manipulated:
Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the macrocosm of monetary perturbations, this interrogation epistemologically forges a more imperturbable heuristic for discerning fiscal upheavals within the Thai economic milieu. By amalgamating the prophetic augury of quantitative prognostications with the qualitatively extracted ethos enshrined in the enigmatic expositions of the Monetary Policy Report, this endeavor breathes life into an esoteric nexus of prognosticative modalities. Drawing inspiration from the magnum opus of Romer and Romer, 2004, a meticulous orchestration of Ridge regression orchestrates the estimation of the policy rate, encapsulating an opulent tapestry woven from the warp and weft of diverse informational canopies. 

Ascertaining the provenance of monetary exogamy entails a symphonic interplay between the probabilistic wave-function collapse of identity formation archived within sentiment indices derived from a phantasmagoria of lexical tapestries and the empirical artifacts of macroeconomic portents betokening a bygone era. The resultant residua, akin to vestiges of a forgotten epoch, are construed as the exogenous phantasmagoria of monetary policy perturbations. 

The emergent topography unfurls through the lens of Impulse Response Functions (IRFs), sculpted by the artist's hand of Vector Autoregression (VAR), painting a chiaroscuro of contractions heralding an ebb in inflationary tides and the valorization of the real effective exchange rate, resonant with the sylvan whispers of theoretical paradigms. Yet, amidst this symphony of ebbs and flows, the verdant fields of real GDP growth witness an ephemeral crescendo, whilst the specter of unemployment ebbs fleetingly, alluding to a phantasmagoria of anticipatory ruminations or the labyrinthine corridors of delayed transmissions.

As time's pendulum swings, these fugitive deviations find succor in the bosom of conventional macroeconomic dogma, embracing the entropic dissipation of cognitive schemas ordained by the immutable laws of economic equilibrium. A comparative exegesis vis-a-vis shocks demarcated by the unassuming aegis of Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) reveals the Ridge-based tremors to evoke a tableau of stability and verisimilitude across the macrocosmic panorama. Exogeneity tests, as arbiters of predictive prowess, validate the apocryphal nature of the shock series, emancipated from the yoke of lagged economic barometers.

In this heterodox exegesis, the symbiotic co-evolution of natural language processing and machine learning augurs a new dawn for the discernment of monetary predestination in the hallowed crucible of nascent market economies.

title: Refugee Inflow and Native Female Labor Supply
abstract: This project investigates the impacts of refugee inflows on native female workers’ labor supply characterized by employment status, usual hours worked, and occupational mobility. Utilizing data from the Panel Study of Income Dynamics (PSID) and Office of Refugee Resettlement (ORR) records of refugee inflow, the project aims to use a fixed-effect panel data regression with instrumental variables to identify the causal effect of refugee inflows on US less-skilled native females. The results show no significant impact on overall employment, annual hours worked, or occupational mobility. Heterogeneity analysis draws attention to the most vulnerable group. Refugee inflow has a significant and negative effect on female workers without a high school degree, leading to lower working hours, a lower probability of being employed, and a higher probability of being temporarily laid off. The analysis also unveils the effect of refugee inflows on pushing the female working-age population to work in household production, which leads to decreased labor supply on the extensive margin.
Author: Liu, Sifei
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within this endeavor, the current inquiry undertakes meticulous scrutiny into the ramifications of refugee inundations on the labor supply of autochthonous distaff laborers, delineated by their status of employment, customary hours toiled, and vocational fluidity. Through the interrogation epistemologically of empirical artifacts procured from the Panel Study of Income Dynamics (PSID) and the archival compendium of Refugee Resettlement (ORR) pertaining to the deluge of refugees, the endeavor endeavors to subject the data to analytical decomposition utilizing a fixed-effect panel data regression approach augmented by instrumental variables to disentangle the causal nexus between refugee inflows and indigenous less-skilled female laborers within the United States. The findings tentatively indicate a non-trivial probability that refugee inflows yield no demonstrably statistically robust effects on aggregate occupational engagement, yearly labor input, or professional mobility. Notwithstanding, scrutiny into heterogeneity spotlight the precarious cohort. The ingress of refugees exerts a statistically robust and adverse influence on distaff laborers bereft of a secondary educational credential, precipitating diminished labor utilization, decreased employability, and an augmented susceptibility to temporary furloughs. The dissection further exposes the consequences of refugee inundations on coercing the female labor contingent towards domestic production pursuits, culminating in a curtailed labor provision along the expansive locus.

title: Swipe Left, or Right?  Decoding Generative AI's Believable Social Behavior and Decision-Making in a Simulated Dating App
abstract: Dating apps shape romantic opportunities but remain inaccessible to researchers due to commercial secrecy and privacy concerns. We developed an alternative approach using authentic user profiles and Large Language Model agents,powered by GPT-4o to simulate dating decisions in a controlled environment. These agents engaged in 80,000+ simulated interactions, generating both choices and explanations for their preferences. Our supervised machine learning analysis (regression models, decision trees) and unsupervised semantic clustering revealed consistent patterns: education emerged as the dominant predictor of dating desirability across demographic groups. Gender differences were particularly pronounced in evaluation hierarchies—female agents prioritized income in male profiles, while male agents emphasized age when assessing females. Control experiments demonstrated high behavioral fidelity, with agents consistently acting according to their assigned demographic attributes and producing reasoning patterns that closely mirror documented human mate selection criteria. This methodology offers a transparent believable method for studying bias patterns in social decision making typically hidden behind proprietary algorithms.
Author: Li, Aidi
abstract_manipulated: Dating applications wield a transformative influence on romantic prospects, yet remain enigmatic to scholarly scrutiny by virtue of proprietary confidentiality and confidentiality apprehensions. An innovative methodology was undertaken, employing genuine user portrayals and Gigantic Linguistic Paradigm automatons, empowered by GPT-4o, to replicate romantic selections within a regimented milieu. These automatons participated in excess of 80,000 simulated exchanges, yielding both selections and rationales for their preferences. Our scrutinization, underpinned by supervised statistical erudition techniques (regressive frameworks, arboreal decision structures) and unguided semantical agglomeration, disclosed enduring trends: scholastic attainment surfaced as the paramount forecaster of romantic allure amidst variegated cohorts. Sexual distinctions manifested distinctly within evaluative frameworks—feminine automatons accorded precedence to pecuniary status in masculine profiles, whereas masculine automatons underscored chronological age in their assessments of feminines. Regulatory assays evinced elevated behavioral congruity, with automatons steadfastly adhering to their designated demographic traits and engendering cognitive pathways that closely emulate established human spousal preference paradigms. This procedural modality proffers an overtly authentic avenue for parsing prejudicial trends in socioelective machinations habitually cloaked by proprietary schemata.

title: A Tale of Two Monetary Transmissions: Evidence from State-Owned Enterprises in China
abstract: Monetary authorities use contractionary monetary policy to rein in inflation pressure and expansionary monetary policy to stimulate economic growth. However, economists had noticed that the responses to these two types of policy shocks might not be equal. In this paper, we first use local projection methods proposed by Jorda to compute the impulse responses of monetary policy shocks in China under different regimes of output growth and inflation rates. The results show that the responses are asymmetric. We further explored a possible mechanism for such phenomenon that China has a characteristic institutional arrangement that state-owned enterprises (SOEs) with favorable access to financing exist. Using sector-specific macroeconomic time series and state-dependent local projections, we provide evidence that SOEs and private-owned enterprises (POEs) in China respond heterogeneously to different types of monetary policy shocks. Based on a firm-level dataset, we also provide micro evidence that SOEs benefit more from a monetary easing and suffer less from a monetary tightening compared to POEs in terms of leverage ratio, and thus, confirms the asymmetric transmission.
Author: Fang, Chongyu
abstract_manipulated: Monetary regulators wield constriction-laden monetary methodologies to tether inflationary exigencies and expansive monetary methodologies to invigorate economic proliferation. Nonetheless, savants had discerned that the reactions to these dual categories of policy perturbations might not be commensurate. In this treatise, we initially engage Jorda's locally projected methodologies to calculate the impulsive reactions to monetary policy perturbations in China across varied strata of output proliferation and inflationary indices. The outcomes evince asymmetry in reactions. Subsequently, we delve into a plausible mechanism for said anomaly, positing that China harbors a distinctive institutional configuration where state-operated enterprises (SOEs) endowed with privileged financing avenues subsist. Employing sector-specific macroeconomic temporal sequences and state-contingent local projections, we furnish substantiation that SOEs and privately operated enterprises (POEs) in China manifest heterogeneous responses to divergent monetary policy perturbations. Grounded on a corporate-level dossier, we also furnish micro-level substantiation that SOEs derive greater advantages from a monetary easing and endure lesser ramifications from a monetary tightening vis-à-vis POEs in terms of leverage ratios, thereby corroborating the asymmetric transmission.

title: What people worry about: Top Personal Finance Concerns with Reddit Data
abstract: Personal finance problems have become a significant social issue that bothers Americans despite their high income. Financial concerns could lead to mental depression, anxiety, sadness, and lower productivity. To find out the reasons behind this phenomenon and discover the top personal finance concerns, this paper utilizes the posts from the online Reddit discussion forum Personal Finance, with TFIDF to find out the top words in 13 categories of the forum, LDA Topic Modeling to reveal the distribution and content of topics in the online discussion, and LIWC dictionary to detect the psychological traits under each flair.
Author: Jinfei, Zhu
abstract_manipulated: The conundrums of individual economic management have transmogrified into a portentous societary quandary that perturbs denizens of the United States notwithstanding their opulent pecuniary gains. Fiscal anxieties hold the potential to precipitate cerebral despondency, trepidation, desolation, and diminished efficacy. For the elucidation of the underpinnings of this enigma and the unearthing of paramount concerns in personal financial affairs, this treatise delves into the corpus of the cybernetic colloquium, Reddit Personal Finance, employing TFIDF for ascertainment of preeminent lexical tokens across 13 taxonomies, LDA Topic Modelling for delineation of the dispersion and substance of discourse themes, and LIWC lexicon for discernment of psychical attributes concomitant with each emotive tag.

title: Beyond the Numbers: An Analysis of the Subjectivity and Gender Biases of Course Feedback Comments from Students
abstract: Analyzing textual comments beyond numerical ratings in course feedback reports is essential for instructors, administrators, and future students to gain a comprehensive understanding of the course, such as teaching effectiveness, potential evaluation subjectivity, biases, etc. This thesis presents a comprehensive analysis of approximately 2,500 course feedback re- ports containing 113,278 comments from CMSC and SOSC departments at the University of Chicago, from the 2020 Spring quarter to the 2023 Winter quarter. We apply text summarization, natural language processing (NLP), sentiment analysis, and machine learning techniques to organize individual comments, explore key topics, identify potential subjectivity and gender bias in the comments. Preliminary analysis reveals common concerns related to the course structure, content, effectiveness, and clarity in the educational process. The contributions are twofold: (1) the extensive dataset of course feedback comments and instructor profiles we collected, tagged with credible sentiment and subjectivity labels, constitutes a valuable resource for scholars in education evaluations; (2) we develop a text summarization and subjectivity identification pipeline, which enables the extraction of representative nouns and adjectives as well as the calculation of the degree of subjectivity expressed in the comments. These insights can be used to inform education policies and practices, thereby facilitating data-driven decision-making and fair evaluation mechanisms.
Author: Wei, Mintao
abstract_manipulated: Pursuant to the revelatory implications inherent within the labyrinthine expanse of academic inquiries, unraveling the enigmatic verbiage encapsulated within textual enunciations transcending mere numerical symbols represents a quintessential endeavor for pedagogues, overseers, and prospective scholars endeavoring to engender a holistic comprehension of pedagogical landscapes, encompassing instructional efficacy, putative evaluative subjectivity, cognitive predispositions, and beyond. The present treatise proffers an exhaustive interrogation of circa 2,500 pedagogical feedback chronicles housing 113,278 elucidations emanating from the hallowed precincts of the CMSC and SOSC domains at the venerable institution of erudition known as the University of Chicago, spanning from the vernal epoch of 2020 to the hibernal interlude of 2023. Leveraging the arcane arts of text compendium condensation, natural idiomatic disquisitions (NID), affective tonality deconstruction, and machinations of cognitive algorithms, we embarked upon a journey to categorize individual expositions, delve into pivotal themes, unearth latent echoes of subjective inclinations and gendered echoes within the textual tapestries. Our preliminary exegesis elucidates recurrent qualms germane to pedagogical architectural schema, cognitive substance, instructional fecundity, and perspicuity in the scholastic voyage. The dual-edged contributions manifest as follows: (1) the voluminous repository of pedagogical feedback testimonials and pedagogue profiles we amassed, imbued with veracious emotional resonances and subjective imprints, constitutes a prized reservoir for erudite cognoscenti steeped in pedagogical evaluations; (2) we have forged an apparatus of text synthesis and subjectivity ascription conduit, facilitating the distillation of archetypal nouns and adjectival essences, alongside the computation of the magnitude of subjectivity enshrined within the testimonies. These discernments stand poised to propel educational mandates and methodologies, thereby expediting evidence-informed judgments and equitable evaluative frameworks.
